Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 And the earth was formless and void
Genesis1:3 Then God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
Genesis1:5 And God called the light day
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 So God made the expanse and separated the waters which were below the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse; and it was so.
Genesis1:8 And God called the expanse heaven. And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 And God called the dry land earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 And the earth brought forth vegetation
Genesis1:13 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:14 Then God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be for lights in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth”; and it was so.
Genesis1:16 So God made the two great lights
Genesis1:17 And God placed them in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth
Genesis1:18 and to rule the day and the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:20 Then God said
Genesis1:21 And God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves
Genesis1:22 Then God blessed them
Genesis1:23 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:24 Then God said
Genesis1:25 God made the beasts of the earth after their kind
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 And God created man in His own image
Genesis1:28 God blessed them
Genesis1:29 Then God said
Genesis1:30 and to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the sky and to every thing that creeps on the earth which has life
Genesis1:31 And God saw all that He had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth were completed
Genesis2:2 And on the seventh day God completed His work which He had done
Genesis2:3 Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it
Genesis2:4 These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created
Genesis2:5 Now no shrub of the field was yet in the earth
Genesis2:6 But a stream would rise from the earth and water the whole surface of the ground.
Genesis2:7 Then Yahweh God formed man of dust from the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and so the man became a living being.
Genesis2:8 And Yahweh God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 And out of the ground Yahweh God caused to grow every tree that is desirable in appearance and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden
Genesis2:10 Now a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from there it divided and became four rivers.
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one that went around the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 Now the gold of that land is good; the bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 And the name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one that went around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 And the name of the third river is Tigris; it is the one that went east of Asshur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 Then Yahweh God took the man and set him in the garden of Eden to cultivate it and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And Yahweh God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil
Genesis2:18 Then Yahweh God said
Genesis2:19 And out of the ground Yahweh God had formed every beast of the field and every bird of the sky
Genesis2:20 And the man gave names to all the cattle and to the birds of the sky and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found a helper suitable for him.
Genesis2:21 So Yahweh God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man
Genesis2:22 And Yahweh God fashioned the rib
Genesis2:23 Then the man said
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked and were not ashamed.

Genesis3:2 And the woman said to the serpent
Genesis3:3 but from the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden
Genesis3:4 And the serpent said to the woman
Genesis3:5 For God knows that in the day you eat from it your eyes will be opened
Genesis3:6 Then the woman saw that the tree was good for food
Genesis3:7 And the eyes of both of them were opened
Genesis3:8 Then they heard the sound of Yahweh God walking in the garden in the cool of the day

Genesis3:20 Now the man called his wife’s name Eve

Genesis3:24 So He drove the man out; and at the east of the garden of Eden He stationed the cherubim and the flaming sword which turned every direction to guard the way to the tree of life.

Genesis4:2 And again

Genesis4:20 And Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the father of those who live in tents and have livestock.

Genesis5:1 This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day when God created man
Genesis5:2 He created them male and female
Genesis5:3 When Adam had lived 130 years
Genesis5:4 Then the days of Adam after he became the father of Seth were 800 years
Genesis5:5 So all the days that Adam lived were 930 years
Genesis5:6 And Seth lived 105 years and became the father of Enosh.
Genesis5:7 Then Seth lived 807 years after he became the father of Enosh
Genesis5:8 So all the days of Seth were 912 years
Genesis5:9 And Enosh lived 90 years and became the father of Kenan.
Genesis5:10 Then Enosh lived 815 years after he became the father of Kenan
Genesis5:11 So all the days of Enosh were 905 years
Genesis5:12 And Kenan lived 70 years and became the father of Mahalalel.
Genesis5:13 Then Kenan lived 840 years after he became the father of Mahalalel
Genesis5:14 So all the days of Kenan were 910 years
Genesis5:15 And Mahalalel lived 65 years and became the father of Jared.
Genesis5:16 Then Mahalalel lived 830 years after he became the father of Jared
Genesis5:17 So all the days of Mahalalel were 895 years
Genesis5:18 And Jared lived 162 years and became the father of Enoch.
Genesis5:19 Then Jared lived 800 years after he became the father of Enoch
Genesis5:20 So all the days of Jared were 962 years
Genesis5:21 And Enoch lived 65 years and became the father of Methuselah.
Genesis5:22 Then Enoch walked with God 300 years after he became the father of Methuselah
Genesis5:23 So all the days of Enoch were 365 years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch walked with God; and he was not
Genesis5:25 And Methuselah lived 187 years and became the father of Lamech.
Genesis5:26 Then Methuselah lived 782 years after he became the father of Lamech
Genesis5:27 So all the days of Methuselah were 969 years
Genesis5:28 And Lamech lived 182 years and became the father of a son.
Genesis5:29 Now he called his name Noah
Genesis5:30 Then Lamech lived 595 years after he became the father of Noah
Genesis5:31 So all the days of Lamech were 777 years
Genesis5:32 And Noah was 500 years old

Genesis6:2 that the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were good in appearance; and they took wives for themselves
Genesis6:3 Then Yahweh said

Genesis6:8 But Noah found favor in the eyes of Yahweh.

Genesis6:11 Now the earth was corrupt before God

Genesis6:17 As for Me
Genesis6:18 But I will establish My covenant with you; and you shall enter the ark—you and your sons and your wife and your sons’ wives with you.

Genesis7:19 And the water prevailed more and more upon the earth
Genesis7:20 The water prevailed fifteen cubits higher
Genesis7:21 And all flesh that moved on the earth breathed its last
Genesis7:22 All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life—of all that was on the dry land—died.
Genesis7:23 Thus He blotted out every living thing that was upon the face of the land
Genesis7:24 And the water prevailed upon the earth 150 days.

Genesis8:12 Then he waited yet another seven days and sent out the dove; but it did not return to him again.

Exodus22:25 “If you lend money to My people
Exodus22:26 If you ever take your neighbor’s cloak as a pledge
Exodus22:27 for that is his only covering; it is his cloak for his body. What else shall he sleep in? And it shall be that when he cries out to Me
Exodus22:28 “You shall not curse God
Exodus22:29 “You shall not delay the offering from the fullness of your harvest and the juice of your wine vat. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to Me.
Exodus22:30 And you shall do the same with your oxen and with your sheep. It shall be with its mother seven days; on the eighth day you shall give it to Me.
Exodus22:31 “You shall be holy men to Me
Exodus23:1 “You shall not bear a false report; do not join your hand with a wicked man to be a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 You shall not follow the masses in doing evil
Exodus23:3 nor shall you be partial to a poor man in his case.
Exodus23:4 “If you meet your enemy’s ox or his donkey wandering away
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of one who hates you lying helpless under its load
Exodus23:6 “You shall not cause the justice due to your needy brother to be turned aside in his case.
Exodus23:7 Keep far from a false charge
Exodus23:8 “And you shall not take a bribe
Exodus23:9 “And you shall not oppress a sojourner
Exodus23:10 “Now you shall sow your land for six years and gather in its produce
Exodus23:11 but on the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow
Exodus23:12 “Six days you are to do your work
Exodus23:13 Now concerning everything which I have said to you
Exodus23:14 “Three times a year you shall celebrate a feast to Me.
Exodus23:15 You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread; for seven days you are to eat unleavened bread
Exodus23:16 Also you shall keep the Feast of the Harvest of the first fruits of your labors from what you sow in the field; also the Feast of the Ingathering at the end of the year when you gather in the fruit of your labors from the field.
Exodus23:17 Three times a year all your males shall appear before the Lord Yahweh.
Exodus23:18 “You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread; nor is the fat of My feast to remain overnight until morning.
Exodus23:19 “You shall bring the choice first fruits of your ground into the house of Yahweh your God. “You shall not boil a young goat in the milk of its mother.
Exodus23:20 “Behold
Exodus23:21 Keep watch of yourself before him and listen to his voice; do not be rebellious toward him
Exodus23:22 But if you truly listen to his voice and do all that I speak
Exodus23:23 For My angel will go before you and bring you in to the land of the Amorites
Exodus23:24 You shall not worship their gods
Exodus23:25 But you shall serve Yahweh your God
Exodus23:26 There shall be no one miscarrying or barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will send My terror ahead of you and throw into confusion all the people among whom you come
Exodus23:28 And I will send hornets ahead of you so that they will drive out the Hivites
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out before you in a single year
Exodus23:30 I will drive them out before you little by little
Exodus23:31 And I will set your boundary from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 You shall cut no covenant with them or with their gods.
Exodus23:33 They shall not live in your land
Exodus24:1 Then He said to Moses
Exodus24:2 Moses alone
Exodus24:3 Then Moses came and recounted to the people all the words of Yahweh and all the judgments; and all the people answered with one voice and said
Exodus24:4 And Moses wrote down all the words of Yahweh. Then he arose early in the morning and built an altar at the foot of the mountain with twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.
Exodus24:5 And he sent young men of the sons of Israel
Exodus24:6 And Moses took half of the blood and put it in basins
Exodus24:7 Then he took the book of the covenant and read it in the hearing of the people; and they said
Exodus24:8 So Moses took the blood and sprinkled it on the people and said
Exodus24:9 Then Moses went up with Aaron
Exodus24:10 and they saw the God of Israel; and under His feet there appeared to be a pavement of sapphire
Exodus24:11 Yet He did not stretch out His hand against the nobles of the sons of Israel; and they beheld God
Exodus24:12 Now Yahweh said to Moses
Exodus24:13 So Moses arose with Joshua his attendant
Exodus24:14 But to the elders he said
Exodus24:15 Then Moses went up to the mountain
Exodus24:16 And the glory of Yahweh dwelt on Mount Sinai
Exodus24:17 And the appearance of the glory of Yahweh was like a consuming fire on the mountain top
Exodus24:18 Then Moses entered the midst of the cloud as he went up to the mountain; and Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.
Exodus25:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Exodus25:2 “Speak with the sons of Israel so that they take a contribution for Me; from every man whose heart is willing you shall take My contribution.
Exodus25:3 And this is the contribution which you shall take from them: gold
Exodus25:4 blue
Exodus25:5 rams’ skins dyed red
Exodus25:6 oil for lighting
Exodus25:7 onyx stones
Exodus25:8 And let them make a sanctuary for Me
Exodus25:9 According to all that I am going to show you
Exodus25:10 “And they shall make an ark of acacia wood two and a half cubits long
Exodus25:11 You shall overlay it with pure gold
Exodus25:12 You shall cast four gold rings for it and fasten them on its four feet
Exodus25:13 You shall make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus25:14 You shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark
Exodus25:15 The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be removed from it.
Exodus25:16 You shall put into the ark the testimony which I shall give you.
Exodus25:17 “You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold
Exodus25:18 You shall make two cherubim of gold; make them of hammered work at the two ends of the mercy seat.
Exodus25:19 Make one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end; from one piece you shall make the mercy seat with the cherubim at its two ends.
Exodus25:20 The cherubim shall have their wings spread upward
Exodus25:21 You shall put the mercy seat on top of the ark
Exodus25:22 There I will meet with you; and from above the mercy seat
Exodus25:23 “You shall make a table of acacia wood
Exodus25:24 You shall overlay it with pure gold and make a gold border around it.
Exodus25:25 You shall make for it a rim of a handbreadth around it; and you shall make a gold border for the rim around it.
Exodus25:26 You shall make four gold rings for it and put rings on the four corners which are on its four feet.
Exodus25:27 The rings shall be close to the rim as holders for the poles to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold
Exodus25:29 You shall make its dishes and its pans and its jars and its offering bowls with which to pour drink offerings; you shall make them of pure gold.
Exodus25:30 You shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before Me at all times.
Exodus25:31 “Then you shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand
Exodus25:32 Six branches shall go out from its sides; three branches of the lampstand from its one side and three branches of the lampstand from its other side.
Exodus25:33 Three cups shall be shaped like almond blossoms in the one branch
Exodus25:34 and in the lampstand four cups shaped like almond blossoms
Exodus25:35 And a bulb shall be under the first pair of branches coming out of it
Exodus25:36 Their bulbs and their branches shall be of the same piece; all of it shall be one piece of hammered work of pure gold.
Exodus25:37 Then you shall make its lamps seven in number; and they shall mount its lamps so as to shed light on the space in front of it.
Exodus25:38 And its tongs and their trays shall be of pure gold.
Exodus25:39 It shall be made from a talent of pure gold
Exodus25:40 And see that you make them after the pattern for them
Exodus26:1 “Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet material; you shall make them with cherubim
Exodus26:2 The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits
Exodus26:3 Five curtains shall be joined to one another
Exodus26:4 And you shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the one set
Exodus26:5 You shall make fifty loops in the one curtain
Exodus26:6 You shall make fifty clasps of gold
Exodus26:7 “Then you shall make curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; you shall make eleven curtains in all.
Exodus26:8 The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits
Exodus26:9 And you shall join five curtains by themselves and the other six curtains by themselves
Exodus26:10 You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the one set
Exodus26:11 “You shall make fifty clasps of bronze
Exodus26:12 The overlapping part that is left over in the curtains of the tent
Exodus26:13 The cubit on one side and the cubit on the other
Exodus26:14 You shall make a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red and a covering of porpoise skins above.
Exodus26:15 “Then you shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood
Exodus26:16 Ten cubits shall be the length of each board
Exodus26:17 There shall be two tenons for each board
Exodus26:18 You shall make the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side.
Exodus26:19 You shall make forty bases of silver under the twenty boards
Exodus26:20 and for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:21 and their forty bases of silver; two bases under one board and two bases under another board.
Exodus26:22 For the rear of the tabernacle
Exodus26:23 You shall make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear.
Exodus26:24 They shall be separated beneath
Exodus26:25 There shall be eight boards with their bases of silver
Exodus26:26 “Then you shall make bars of acacia wood
Exodus26:27 and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:28 The middle bar in the center of the boards shall pass through from end to end.
Exodus26:29 You shall overlay the boards with gold and make their rings of gold as holders for the bars; and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
Exodus26:30 Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to its plan which you have been shown in the mountain.
Exodus26:31 “You shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet material and fine twisted linen; it shall be made with cherubim
Exodus26:32 You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold
Exodus26:33 You shall hang up the veil under the clasps
Exodus26:34 You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the Holy of Holies.
Exodus26:35 You shall set the table outside the veil and the lampstand opposite the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south; and you shall put the table on the north side.
Exodus26:36 “You shall make a screen for the doorway of the tent of blue and purple and scarlet material and fine twisted linen
Exodus26:37 You shall make five pillars of acacia for the screen and overlay them with gold
Exodus27:1 “And you shall make the altar of acacia wood
Exodus27:2 You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of the same piece
Exodus27:3 You shall make its pots for removing its ashes
Exodus27:4 You shall make for it a grating
Exodus27:5 You shall put it beneath
Exodus27:6 You shall make poles for the altar
Exodus27:7 Its poles shall be inserted into the rings
Exodus27:8 You shall make it hollow with planks; as it was shown to you in the mountain
Exodus27:9 “You shall make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side there shall be hangings for the court of fine twisted linen one hundred cubits long for one side;
Exodus27:10 and its pillars shall be twenty
Exodus27:11 Likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long
Exodus27:12 For the width of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits with their ten pillars and their ten bases.
Exodus27:13 The width of the court on the east side toward the sunrise shall be fifty cubits.
Exodus27:14 The hangings for the one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits with their three pillars and their three bases.
Exodus27:15 And for the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits with their three pillars and their three bases.
Exodus27:16 And as for the gate of the court there shall be a screen of twenty cubits
Exodus27:17 All the pillars around the court shall be furnished with silver bands with their hooks of silver and their bases of bronze.
Exodus27:18 The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits
Exodus27:19 All the utensils of the tabernacle used in all its service

Exodus27:21 In the tent of meeting
Exodus28:1 “Now as for you
Exodus28:2 You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother
Exodus28:3 You shall speak to all those wise at heart whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom
Exodus28:4 These are the garments which they shall make: a breastpiece and an ephod and a robe and a tunic of checkered work
Exodus28:5 They shall take the gold and the blue and the purple and the scarlet material and the fine linen.
Exodus28:6 “They shall also make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder pieces joined to its two ends
Exodus28:8 The skillfully woven band
Exodus28:9 You shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel
Exodus28:10 six of their names on the one stone and the names of the remaining six on the second stone
Exodus28:11 As a jeweler engraves a signet
Exodus28:12 You shall put the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:13 You shall make filigree settings of gold
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold; you shall make them a twisted work of cords
Exodus28:15 “You shall make a breastpiece of judgment
Exodus28:16 It shall be square
Exodus28:17 You shall mount on it four rows of stones; the first row shall be a row of ruby
Exodus28:18 and the second row a turquoise
Exodus28:19 and the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row a beryl and an onyx and a jasper; they shall be set in gold settings.
Exodus28:21 The stones shall be according to the names of the sons of Israel: twelve
Exodus28:22 You shall make on the breastpiece chains of a twisted work of cords in pure gold.
Exodus28:23 You shall make on the breastpiece two rings of gold
Exodus28:24 You shall put the two cords of gold on the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:25 You shall put the two ends of the two cords on the two filigree settings
Exodus28:26 You shall make two rings of gold and shall place them on the two ends of the breastpiece
Exodus28:27 You shall make two rings of gold and put them on the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:28 They shall bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus28:29 Aaron shall carry the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment over his heart when he comes into the holy place
Exodus28:30 You shall put in the breastpiece of judgment the Urim and the Thummim
Exodus28:31 “You shall make the robe of the ephod entirely of blue.
Exodus28:32 There shall be an opening at its top in the middle of it; around the edge of its opening there shall be a binding of woven work
Exodus28:33 You shall make on its hem pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet material
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 It shall be on Aaron when he ministers; and its sound shall be heard when he comes into the holy place before Yahweh and when he goes out
Exodus28:36 “You shall also make a plate of pure gold and shall engrave on it
Exodus28:37 You shall fasten it on a blue cord
Exodus28:38 It shall be on Aaron’s forehead
Exodus28:39 “You shall weave the tunic of checkered work of fine linen
Exodus28:40 “For Aaron’s sons you shall make tunics; you shall also make sashes for them
Exodus28:41 You shall put them on Aaron your brother and on his sons with him; and you shall anoint them and ordain them and set them apart as holy
Exodus28:42 You shall make for them linen undergarments to cover their bare flesh; they shall reach from the loins even to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 They shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they come into the tent of meeting or when they approach the altar to minister in the holy place
Exodus29:1 “Now this is what you shall do to them to set them apart as holy to minister as priests to Me: take one bull from the herd and two rams without blemish
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread and unleavened cakes mixed with oil
Exodus29:3 And you shall put them in one basket and bring them near in the basket along with the bull and the two rams.
Exodus29:4 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons near to the doorway of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 And you shall take the garments
Exodus29:6 and you shall set the turban on his head and put the holy crown on the turban.
Exodus29:7 Then you shall take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 And you shall bring his sons near and put tunics on them.
Exodus29:9 You shall gird them with sashes
Exodus29:10 “Then you shall bring the bull near before the tent of meeting
Exodus29:11 You shall slaughter the bull before Yahweh at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:12 You shall take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; and you shall pour out all the blood at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails and the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull and its hide and its refuse
Exodus29:15 “You shall also take the one ram
Exodus29:16 and you shall slaughter the ram
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram into its pieces
Exodus29:18 You shall offer up in smoke the whole ram on the altar; it is a burnt offering to Yahweh: it is a soothing aroma
Exodus29:19 “Then you shall take the second ram
Exodus29:20 You shall slaughter the ram
Exodus29:21 Then you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil
Exodus29:22 “You shall also take the fat from the ram and the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails and the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them and the right thigh (for it is a ram of ordination)
Exodus29:23 and one cake of bread and one cake of bread mixed with oil and one wafer from the basket of unleavened bread which is set before Yahweh;
Exodus29:24 and you shall put all these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons
Exodus29:25 You shall take them from their hands and offer them up in smoke on the altar on the burnt offering for a soothing aroma before Yahweh; it is an offering by fire to Yahweh.
Exodus29:26 “Then you shall take the breast of Aaron’s ram of ordination and wave it as a wave offering before Yahweh; and it shall be your portion.
Exodus29:27 You shall set apart as holy the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution offering which was waved and which was raised up as a contribution from the ram of ordination
Exodus29:28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual statute from the sons of Israel
Exodus29:29 “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him
Exodus29:30 For seven days the one of his sons who is priest in his stead shall put them on when he comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
Exodus29:31 “You shall take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket
Exodus29:33 Thus they shall eat those things by which atonement was made to ordain them
Exodus29:34 If any of the flesh of ordination or any of the bread remains until morning
Exodus29:35 “Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons
Exodus29:36 Each day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement
Exodus29:37 For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and set it apart as holy; then the altar shall be most holy
Exodus29:38 “Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two one year old lambs each day
Exodus29:39 The one lamb you shall offer in the morning
Exodus29:40 and there shall be one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 The second lamb you shall offer at twilight
Exodus29:42 It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the doorway of the tent of meeting before Yahweh
Exodus29:43 I will meet there with the sons of Israel
Exodus29:44 I will set the tent of meeting and the altar apart as holy; I will also set Aaron and his sons apart as holy to minister as priests to Me.
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the sons of Israel and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 They shall know that I am Yahweh their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt

Exodus30:10 Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once a year; he shall make atonement on it with the blood of the sin offering of atonement once a year throughout your generations. It is most holy to Yahweh.”

Exodus30:15 The rich shall not pay more and the poor shall not pay less than the half shekel when you give the contribution to Yahweh to make atonement for your souls.
Exodus30:16 And you shall take the atonement money from the sons of Israel and shall give it for the service of the tent of meeting

Exodus32:4 And he took this from their hand and fashioned it with a graving tool and made it into a molten calf; and they said
Exodus32:5 And Aaron looked and built an altar before it; and Aaron made a proclamation and said
Exodus32:6 So the next day they rose early and offered burnt offerings and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink and rose up to play.
Exodus32:7 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Exodus32:8 They have quickly turned aside from the way which I commanded them. They have made for themselves a molten calf and have worshiped it and have sacrificed to it and said
Exodus32:9 And Yahweh said to Moses
Exodus32:10 Now then let Me alone
Exodus32:11 Then Moses entreated the favor of Yahweh his God and said
Exodus32:12 Why should the Egyptians speak
Exodus32:13 Remember Abraham

Exodus32:16 Now the tablets were the work of God

Exodus32:19 Now it happened

Exodus32:22 And Aaron said
Exodus32:23 Indeed they said to me
Exodus32:24 And I said to them
Exodus32:25 Now Moses saw that the people were out of control—for Aaron had let them get out of control to be a derision among their enemies—

Exodus32:34 But now go

Exodus33:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Exodus33:2 And I will send an angel before you
Exodus33:3 Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey; for I will not go up in your midst because you are a stiff-necked people

Exodus33:6 So the sons of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments

Exodus33:9 And it happened whenever Moses entered the tent

Exodus34:6 Then Yahweh passed by in front of him and called out
Exodus34:7 who keeps lovingkindness for thousands

Exodus34:12 Beware lest you cut a covenant with the inhabitants of the land into which you are going
Exodus34:13 But rather
Exodus34:14 —for you shall not worship any other god
Exodus34:15 lest you cut a covenant with the inhabitants of the land
Exodus34:16 and you take some of his daughters for your sons
Exodus34:17 You shall make for yourself no molten gods.
Exodus34:18 “You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread
Exodus34:19 “The first offspring from every womb belongs to Me
Exodus34:20 And you shall redeem with a lamb the first offspring from a donkey; and if you do not redeem it
Exodus34:21 “You shall work six days
Exodus34:22 And you shall celebrate the Feast of Weeks
Exodus34:23 Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord Yahweh
Exodus34:24 For I will dispossess nations before you and enlarge your borders
Exodus34:25 “You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread
Exodus34:26 “You shall bring the very first of the first fruits of your ground into the house of Yahweh your God. “You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
Exodus34:27 Then Yahweh said to Moses
Exodus34:28 So he was there with Yahweh forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread or drink water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant
Exodus34:29 Now it happened when Moses was coming down from Mount Sinai (and the two tablets of the testimony were in Moses’ hand as he was coming down from the mountain)
Exodus34:30 Then Aaron and all the sons of Israel saw Moses
Exodus34:31 Then Moses called to them
Exodus34:32 And afterward all the sons of Israel came near
Exodus34:33 Then Moses finished speaking with them and put a veil over his face.
Exodus34:34 But whenever Moses went in before Yahweh to speak with Him
Exodus34:35 and the sons of Israel would see the face of Moses
Exodus35:1 Then Moses assembled all the congregation of the sons of Israel and said to them
Exodus35:2 “Six days work may be done
Exodus35:3 You shall not kindle a fire in any of your places of habitation on the sabbath day.”
Exodus35:4 And Moses spoke to all the congregation of the sons of Israel
Exodus35:5 ‘Take from among you a contribution to Yahweh; whoever is of a willing heart
Exodus35:6 and blue
Exodus35:7 and rams’ skins dyed red
Exodus35:8 and oil for lighting
Exodus35:9 and onyx stones and setting stones for the ephod and for the breastpiece.
Exodus35:10 ‘And let everyone wise at heart among you come and make all that Yahweh has commanded:
Exodus35:11 the tabernacle
Exodus35:12 the ark and its poles
Exodus35:13 the table and its poles
Exodus35:14 the lampstand also for the light and its utensils and its lamps and the oil for the light;
Exodus35:15 and the altar of incense and its poles
Exodus35:16 the altar of burnt offering with its bronze grating
Exodus35:17 the hangings of the court
Exodus35:18 the pegs of the tabernacle and the pegs of the court and their cords;
Exodus35:19 the woven garments for ministering in the holy place
Exodus35:20 Then all the congregation of the sons of Israel went out from Moses’ presence.
Exodus35:21 And everyone whose heart stirred him and everyone whose spirit was willing came and brought the contribution to Yahweh for the work of the tent of meeting and for all its service and for the holy garments.
Exodus35:22 Then all whose hearts were willing
Exodus35:23 And every man
Exodus35:24 Everyone who could raise up a contribution of silver and bronze brought the contribution to Yahweh; and every man who had in his possession acacia wood for any work of the service brought it.
Exodus35:25 All the women wise at heart spun with their hands and brought what they had spun
Exodus35:26 And all the women whose heart stirred with wisdom spun the goats’ hair.
Exodus35:27 And the rulers brought the onyx stones and the stones for setting for the ephod and for the breastpiece;
Exodus35:28 and the spice and the oil for the light and for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense.
Exodus35:29 The sons of Israel—all the men and women whose heart was willing to bring material for all the work
Exodus35:30 Then Moses said to the sons of Israel
Exodus35:31 And He has filled him with the Spirit of God
Exodus35:32 to devise designs for working in gold and in silver and in bronze
Exodus35:33 and in the cutting of stones for settings and in the carving of wood
Exodus35:34 He also has put in his heart to teach
Exodus35:35 He has filled them with wisdom in their heart to do every work of an engraver and of a designer and of an embroiderer
Exodus36:1 “Now Bezalel and Oholiab and every person wise at heart
Exodus36:2 Then Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every person wise at heart to whom Yahweh had given wisdom
Exodus36:3 And they received from Moses the entire contribution which the sons of Israel had brought to do the work in the service of the sanctuary. And they still continued bringing to him freewill offerings every morning.
Exodus36:4 And all the wise men who were doing all the work of the sanctuary came
Exodus36:5 and they said to Moses
Exodus36:6 So Moses commanded and a proclamation was passed throughout the camp
Exodus36:7 Indeed
Exodus36:8 And all those wise at heart among those who were doing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet material
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains had the same measurements.
Exodus36:10 He joined five curtains to one another
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set; he did likewise on the edge of the curtain that was outermost in the second set.
Exodus36:12 He made fifty loops in the one curtain
Exodus36:13 He made fifty clasps of gold and joined the curtains to one another with the clasps
Exodus36:14 Then he made curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains in all.
Exodus36:15 The length of each curtain was thirty cubits and four cubits the width of each curtain; the eleven curtains had the same measurements.
Exodus36:16 He joined five curtains by themselves and the other six curtains by themselves.
Exodus36:17 Moreover
Exodus36:18 He made fifty clasps of bronze to join the tent together so that it would be a unit.
Exodus36:19 He made a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red and a covering of porpoise skins above.
Exodus36:20 Then he made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood
Exodus36:21 Ten cubits was the length of each board and one and a half cubits the width of each board.
Exodus36:22 There were two tenons for each board
Exodus36:23 And he made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side;
Exodus36:24 and he made forty bases of silver under the twenty boards; two bases under one board for its two tenons and two bases under another board for its two tenons.
Exodus36:25 Then for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 and their forty bases of silver; two bases under one board and two bases under another board.
Exodus36:27 For the rear of the tabernacle
Exodus36:28 And he made two boards for the corners of the tabernacle at the rear.
Exodus36:29 And they were separated beneath
Exodus36:30 And there were eight boards with their bases of silver
Exodus36:31 Then he made bars of acacia wood
Exodus36:32 and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:33 And he made the middle bar to pass through in the center of the boards from end to end.
Exodus36:34 He also overlaid the boards with gold and made their rings of gold as holders for the bars and overlaid the bars with gold.
Exodus36:35 Moreover
Exodus36:36 And he made four pillars of acacia for it and overlaid them with gold
Exodus36:37 And he made a screen for the doorway of the tent
Exodus36:38 and he made its five pillars with their hooks
Exodus37:1 And Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood; its length was two and a half cubits
Exodus37:2 and he overlaid it with pure gold inside and out
Exodus37:3 He cast four rings of gold for it on its four feet; even two rings on one side of it
Exodus37:4 He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:5 He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry it.
Exodus37:6 He made a mercy seat of pure gold
Exodus37:7 He made two cherubim of gold; he made them of hammered work at the two ends of the mercy seat;
Exodus37:8 one cherub at the one end and one cherub at the other end; from one piece he made the mercy seat with the cherubim at its two ends.
Exodus37:9 The cherubim had their wings spread upward
Exodus37:10 Then he made the table of acacia wood
Exodus37:11 He overlaid it with pure gold and made a gold molding for it all around.
Exodus37:12 He made a rim for it of a handbreadth all around and made a gold molding for its rim all around.
Exodus37:13 He cast four gold rings for it and put the rings on the four corners that were on its four feet.
Exodus37:14 Close to the rim were the rings
Exodus37:15 He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold
Exodus37:16 He made the utensils which were on the table
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand of pure gold. He made the lampstand of hammered work
Exodus37:18 There were six branches going out of its sides; three branches of the lampstand from the one side of it and three branches of the lampstand from the other side of it;
Exodus37:19 three cups shaped like almond blossoms
Exodus37:20 In the lampstand there were four cups shaped like almond blossoms
Exodus37:21 and a bulb was under the first pair of branches coming out of it
Exodus37:22 Their bulbs and their branches were of one piece with it; the whole of it was a single hammered work of pure gold.
Exodus37:23 He made its seven lamps with its tongs and its trays of pure gold.
Exodus37:24 He made it and all its utensils from a talent of pure gold.

Exodus39:1 Moreover
Exodus39:2 He made the ephod of gold
Exodus39:3 Then they hammered out gold sheets and cut them into threads to be woven in with the blue and the purple and the scarlet material and the fine linen
Exodus39:4 They made joining shoulder pieces for the ephod; it was joined at its two upper ends.
Exodus39:5 The skillfully woven band which was on it was like its workmanship
Exodus39:6 They made the onyx stones
Exodus39:7 And he placed them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus39:8 He made the breastpiece
Exodus39:9 It was square; they made the breastpiece
Exodus39:10 And they mounted four rows of stones on it. The first row was a row of ruby
Exodus39:11 and the second row
Exodus39:12 and the third row
Exodus39:13 and the fourth row
Exodus39:14 The stones were corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel; they were twelve
Exodus39:15 They made on the breastpiece chains of a twisted work of cords in pure gold.
Exodus39:16 They made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings
Exodus39:17 Then they put the two gold cords in the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece.
Exodus39:18 They put the other two ends of the two cords on the two filigree settings and put them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod at the front of it.
Exodus39:19 They made two gold rings and placed them on the two ends of the breastpiece
Exodus39:20 Furthermore
Exodus39:21 They bound the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus39:22 Then he made the robe of the ephod of woven work entirely of blue;
Exodus39:23 and the opening of the robe was at the top in the center
Exodus39:24 They made pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet material and twisted linen on the hem of the robe.
Exodus39:25 They also made bells of pure gold and put the bells between the pomegranates all around on the hem of the robe
Exodus39:26 alternating a bell and a pomegranate all around on the hem of the robe for the ministry
Exodus39:27 They made the tunics of finely woven linen for Aaron and his sons
Exodus39:28 and the turban of fine linen
Exodus39:29 and the sash of fine twisted linen
Exodus39:30 They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold and wrote upon it with engraved writing as found on a signet
Exodus39:31 They fastened a blue cord to it
Exodus39:32 Thus all the service of constructing the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was completed; and the sons of Israel did according to all that Yahweh had commanded Moses; so they did.
Exodus39:33 They brought the tabernacle to Moses
Exodus39:34 and the covering of rams’ skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the ark of the testimony and its poles and the mercy seat;
Exodus39:36 the table

Exodus39:40 the hangings for the court
Exodus39:41 the woven garments for ministering in the holy place and the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments of his sons
Exodus39:42 Thus
Exodus39:43 And Moses saw all the work
Exodus40:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Exodus40:2 “On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 You shall place the ark of the testimony there
Exodus40:4 You shall bring in the table and arrange what belongs on it; and you shall bring in the lampstand and mount its lamps.
Exodus40:5 Moreover
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of burnt offering in front of the doorway of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:7 You shall set the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.
Exodus40:8 You shall set up the court all around and hang up the screen for the gateway of the court.
Exodus40:9 Then you shall take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it and set it apart as holy and all its furnishings; and it shall be holy.
Exodus40:10 You shall anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 You shall anoint the laver and its stand
Exodus40:12 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the doorway of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 You shall put the holy garments on Aaron and anoint him and set him apart as holy
Exodus40:14 You shall bring his sons and put tunics on them;
Exodus40:15 and you shall anoint them even as you have anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Thus Moses did; according to all that Yahweh had commanded him
Exodus40:17 Now it happened
Exodus40:18 Moses erected the tabernacle and laid its bases and set up its boards and inserted its bars and erected its pillars.
Exodus40:19 He spread the tent over the tabernacle and placed the covering of the tent on top of it
Exodus40:20 Then he took the testimony and put it into the ark and attached the poles to the ark and put the mercy seat on top of the ark.
Exodus40:21 He brought the ark into the tabernacle and placed the veil of the screen and screened off the ark of the testimony
Exodus40:22 Then he put the table in the tent of meeting on the north side of the tabernacle
Exodus40:23 He set the arrangement of bread in order on it before Yahweh
Exodus40:24 Then he placed the lampstand in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 He lighted the lamps before Yahweh
Exodus40:26 Then he placed the gold altar in the tent of meeting in front of the veil;
Exodus40:27 and he burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 Then he placed the screen at the doorway of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 He placed the altar of burnt offering before the doorway of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 He placed the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 From it Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
Exodus40:32 When they entered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 He erected the court all around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 And Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud had dwelt on it
Exodus40:36 Now throughout all their journeys whenever the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 but if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For throughout all their journeys
Leviticus1:1 Then Yahweh called to Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting
Leviticus1:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus1:3 If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 Then he shall slaughter the young bull before Yahweh; and Aaron’s sons the priests shall bring near the blood and splash the blood around on the altar that is at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus1:6 And he shall then skin the burnt offering and cut it into its pieces.
Leviticus1:7 And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 Then Aaron’s sons the priests shall arrange the pieces
Leviticus1:9 Its entrails
Leviticus1:10 ‘But if his offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 And he shall slaughter it on the side of the altar northward before Yahweh
Leviticus1:12 He shall then cut it into its pieces with its head and its suet
Leviticus1:13 The entrails
Leviticus1:14 ‘But if his offering to Yahweh is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 And the priest shall bring it near to the altar and wring off its head and offer it up in smoke on the altar; and its blood is to be drained out on the side of the altar.
Leviticus1:16 He shall also take away its crop with its feathers and cast it beside the altar eastward
Leviticus1:17 Then he shall tear it by its wings
Leviticus2:1 ‘Now when anyone brings near a grain offering as an offering to Yahweh
Leviticus2:2 He shall then bring it to Aaron’s sons the priests; and he shall take from it his handful of its fine flour and of its oil with all of its frankincense. And the priest shall offer it up in smoke as its memorial portion on the altar
Leviticus2:3 And the remainder of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons: a thing most holy of the offerings to Yahweh by fire.
Leviticus2:4 ‘Now when you bring near an offering of a grain offering baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 And if your offering is a grain offering made on the griddle
Leviticus2:6 you shall break it into bits and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 Now if your offering is a grain offering made in a pan
Leviticus2:8 And you will bring in the grain offering which is made of these things to Yahweh
Leviticus2:9 The priest then shall raise up from the grain offering its memorial portion
Leviticus2:10 And the remainder of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons: a thing most holy of the offerings to Yahweh by fire.
Leviticus2:11 ‘No grain offering
Leviticus2:12 As an offering of first fruits you shall bring them near to Yahweh
Leviticus2:13 Every grain offering of yours
Leviticus2:14 ‘Also if you bring near a grain offering of early ripened things to Yahweh
Leviticus2:15 You shall then put oil on it and place frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 And the priest shall offer up in smoke its memorial portion
Leviticus3:1 ‘Now if his offering is a sacrifice of peace offerings
Leviticus3:2 Then he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it at the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:3 From the sacrifice of the peace offerings he shall bring an offering by fire near to Yahweh
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron’s sons shall offer it up in smoke on the altar on the burnt offering
Leviticus3:6 But if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offerings to Yahweh is from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he is going to bring near a lamb for his offering
Leviticus3:8 and he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:9 And from the sacrifice of peace offerings he shall bring near as an offering by fire to Yahweh its fat
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest shall offer it up in smoke on the altar as food
Leviticus3:12 ‘Moreover
Leviticus3:13 and he shall lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:14 And from it he shall bring near his offering as an offering by fire to Yahweh the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus3:16 And the priest shall offer them up in smoke on the altar as food
Leviticus3:17 It is a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your places of habitation: you shall not eat any fat or any blood.’”
Leviticus4:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus4:3 if the anointed priest sins so as to bring guilt on the people
Leviticus4:4 Then he shall bring the bull to the doorway of the tent of meeting before Yahweh
Leviticus4:5 Then the anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it to the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:6 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before Yahweh in front of the veil of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 The priest shall also put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense which is before Yahweh in the tent of meeting; and all the blood of the bull he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering which is at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:8 Then he shall raise up from it all the fat of the bull of the sin offering: the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat which is on the entrails
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus4:10 (just as it is raised up from the ox of the sacrifice of peace offerings)
Leviticus4:11 But the hide of the bull and all its flesh with its head and its legs and its entrails and its refuse
Leviticus4:12 that is
Leviticus4:13 ‘Now if the whole congregation of Israel commits error
Leviticus4:14 when the sin which they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 Then the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before Yahweh
Leviticus4:16 Then the anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull to the tent of meeting;
Leviticus4:17 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before Yahweh in front of the veil.
Leviticus4:18 And he shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is before Yahweh in the tent of meeting; and all the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering which is at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:19 He shall then raise up all its fat from it and offer it up in smoke on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He shall also do with the bull just as he did with the bull of the sin offering; thus he shall do with it. So the priest shall make atonement for them
Leviticus4:21 Then he shall bring out the bull to a place outside the camp and burn it as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering for the assembly.
Leviticus4:22 ‘When a leader sins and unintentionally does any one of all the things which Yahweh his God has commanded not to be done
Leviticus4:23 or if his sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:24 Then he shall lay his hand on the head of the male goat and slaughter it in the place where they slaughter the burnt offering before Yahweh; it is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:26 And all its fat he shall offer up in smoke on the altar as in the case of the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings. Thus the priest shall make atonement for him in regard to his sin
Leviticus4:27 ‘Now if anyone of the common people sins unintentionally in doing any of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done
Leviticus4:28 or if his sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:29 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter the sin offering at the place of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and all the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 Then he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus4:32 ‘But if he brings a lamb as his offering for a sin offering
Leviticus4:33 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it for a sin offering in the place where they slay the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:34 And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering
Leviticus4:35 Then he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus5:1 ‘Now if a person sins after he hears a public oath to testify when he is a witness
Leviticus5:2 Or a person who touches any unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human uncleanness
Leviticus5:4 Or if a person swears thoughtlessly with his lips to do evil or to do good
Leviticus5:5 So it shall be
Leviticus5:6 He shall also bring his guilt offering to Yahweh for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:7 ‘But if he cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus5:8 And he shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 He shall also sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 The second he shall then prepare as a burnt offering according to the legal judgment. So the priest shall make atonement on his behalf for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:11 ‘But if his means are insufficient for two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 He shall then bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 So the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin which he has committed from one of these
Leviticus5:14 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 “If a person acts unfaithfully and sins unintentionally against the holy things of Yahweh
Leviticus5:16 And he shall make restitution for that which he has sinned against the holy thing
Leviticus5:17 “Now if a person sins and does any one of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done
Leviticus5:18 He is then to bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; he was certainly guilty before Yahweh.”
Leviticus6:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 “If a person sins and acts unfaithfully against Yahweh and deals falsely with his companion in regard to a deposit or a security entrusted to him or through robbery
Leviticus6:3 or has found what was lost and dealt falsely about it and sworn falsely
Leviticus6:4 then it shall be
Leviticus6:5 or anything about which he swore falsely; he shall make restitution for it in full and add to it one-fifth more. He shall give it to the one to whom it belongs on the day he presents his guilt offering.
Leviticus6:6 Then he shall bring to the priest his guilt offering to Yahweh
Leviticus6:7 and the priest shall make atonement for him before Yahweh
Leviticus6:8 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:9 “Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 And the priest shall put on his linen robe
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall take off his garments and put on other garments and carry the ashes outside the camp to a clean place.
Leviticus6:12 And the fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it. It shall not go out
Leviticus6:13 Fire shall be kept burning continually on the altar; it shall not go out.
Leviticus6:14 ‘Now this is the law of the grain offering: the sons of Aaron shall bring it near before Yahweh in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 Then one of them shall raise up from it a handful of the fine flour of the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 What is left of it Aaron and his sons shall eat. It shall be eaten as unleavened cakes in a holy place; they shall eat it in the court of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus6:17 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their share from My offerings by fire; it is most holy
Leviticus6:18 Every male among the sons of Aaron may eat it; it is a perpetual statute throughout your generations
Leviticus6:19 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 “This is the offering which Aaron and his sons shall bring near to Yahweh on the day when he is anointed; the tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It shall be prepared with oil on a griddle. When it is well stirred
Leviticus6:22 And the anointed priest
Leviticus6:23 So every grain offering of the priest shall be burned entirely. It shall not be eaten.”
Leviticus6:24 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 “Speak to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it. It shall be eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Anyone who touches its flesh will be set apart as holy; and when any of its blood sprinkles on a garment
Leviticus6:28 Also the earthenware vessel in which it was boiled shall be broken; and if it was boiled in a bronze vessel
Leviticus6:29 And every male among the priests may eat of it; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering
Leviticus7:1 ‘Now this is the law of the guilt offering; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:2 In the place where they slaughter the burnt offering they are to slaughter the guilt offering
Leviticus7:3 Then he shall bring near from it all its fat: the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus7:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them
Leviticus7:5 And the priest shall offer them up in smoke on the altar as an offering by fire to Yahweh; it is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Every male among the priests may eat of it. It shall be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 The guilt offering is like the sin offering
Leviticus7:8 Also the priest who brings near any man’s burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 Likewise
Leviticus7:10 And every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 ‘Now this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings which shall be brought near to Yahweh.
Leviticus7:12 If he brings it near for thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 With the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving
Leviticus7:14 Of this he shall bring near
Leviticus7:15 ‘Now as for the flesh of the sacrifice of his thanksgiving peace offerings
Leviticus7:16 But if the sacrifice of his offering is a votive or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 but what is left over from the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burned with fire.
Leviticus7:18 So if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings should ever be eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 ‘Also the flesh that touches anything unclean shall not be eaten; it shall be burned with fire. As for other flesh
Leviticus7:20 But the person who eats the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings which belong to Yahweh
Leviticus7:21 When anyone touches anything unclean
Leviticus7:22 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:23 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus7:24 Also the fat of an animal which dies and the fat of an animal torn by beasts may be put to any other use
Leviticus7:25 For whoever eats the fat of the animal from which an offering by fire is brought near to Yahweh
Leviticus7:26 And you shall not eat any blood
Leviticus7:27 Any person who eats any blood
Leviticus7:28 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:29 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus7:30 His own hands are to bring offerings by fire to Yahweh. He shall bring the fat with the breast
Leviticus7:31 And the priest shall offer up the fat in smoke on the altar
Leviticus7:32 And you shall give the right thigh to the priest as a contribution from the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
Leviticus7:33 The one among the sons of Aaron who brings near the blood of the peace offerings and the fat
Leviticus7:34 For I have taken the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution from the sons of Israel from the sacrifices of their peace offerings
Leviticus7:35 ‘This is the share for Aaron and the share for his sons from the offerings by fire to Yahweh
Leviticus7:36 These Yahweh had commanded to be given them from the sons of Israel in the day that He anointed them. It is a perpetual statute throughout their generations.’”
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which Yahweh commanded Moses at Mount Sinai in the day that He commanded the sons of Israel to bring their offerings near to Yahweh in the wilderness of Sinai.
Leviticus8:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus8:2 “Take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 and assemble all the congregation at the doorway of the tent of meeting.”
Leviticus8:4 So Moses did just as Yahweh commanded him. Then the congregation was assembled at the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus8:5 and Moses said to the congregation
Leviticus8:6 Then Moses had Aaron and his sons come near and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 And he put the tunic on him and girded him with the sash and clothed him with the robe and put the ephod on him; and he girded him with the skillfully woven band of the ephod
Leviticus8:8 He then placed the breastpiece on him
Leviticus8:9 He also placed the turban on his head
Leviticus8:10 Moses then took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it and set them apart as holy.
Leviticus8:11 And he sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times and anointed the altar and all its utensils and the laver and its stand
Leviticus8:12 Then he poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him
Leviticus8:13 Next Moses brought Aaron’s sons near and clothed them with tunics and girded them with sashes and bound caps on them
Leviticus8:14 Then he brought the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 Next Moses slaughtered it and took the blood and with his finger put some of it around on the horns of the altar and purified the altar. Then he poured out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar and set it apart as holy
Leviticus8:16 He also took all the fat that was on the entrails
Leviticus8:17 But the bull and its hide and its flesh and its refuse he burned in the fire outside the camp
Leviticus8:18 Then he brought near the ram of the burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 Then Moses slaughtered it and splashed the blood around on the altar.
Leviticus8:20 And he cut the ram into its pieces. Then Moses offered up the head and the pieces and the suet in smoke.
Leviticus8:21 And he washed the entrails and the legs with water. Then Moses offered up the whole ram in smoke on the altar. It was a burnt offering for a soothing aroma; it was an offering by fire to Yahweh
Leviticus8:22 Then he brought near the second ram
Leviticus8:23 And Moses slaughtered it and took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.
Leviticus8:24 He also brought Aaron’s sons near; and Moses put some of the blood on the lobe of their right ear and on the thumb of their right hand and on the big toe of their right foot. Moses then splashed the rest of the blood around on the altar.
Leviticus8:25 And he took the fat
Leviticus8:26 From the basket of unleavened bread that was before Yahweh
Leviticus8:27 He then put all these on the hands of Aaron and on the hands of his sons
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took them from their hands and offered them up in smoke on the altar with the burnt offering. They were an ordination offering for a soothing aroma; it was an offering by fire to Yahweh.
Leviticus8:29 Moses also took the breast and waved it as a wave offering before Yahweh; it was Moses’ portion of the ram of ordination
Leviticus8:30 So Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood which was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aaron
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus8:32 And the remainder of the flesh and of the bread you shall burn in the fire.
Leviticus8:33 And you shall not go outside the doorway of the tent of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 Yahweh has commanded to do as has been done this day
Leviticus8:35 At the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus8:36 Thus Aaron and his sons did all the things which Yahweh had commanded through Moses.
Leviticus9:1 Now it happened on the eighth day that Moses called Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel;

Leviticus9:3 Then to the sons of Israel you shall speak
Leviticus9:4 and an ox and a ram for peace offerings
Leviticus9:5 So they took what Moses had commanded to the front of the tent of meeting
Leviticus9:6 And Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Moses then said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron came near to the altar and slaughtered the calf of the sin offering which was for himself.
Leviticus9:9 Then Aaron’s sons brought the blood near to him; and he dipped his finger in the blood and put some on the horns of the altar and poured out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus9:10 The fat and the kidneys and the lobe of the liver of the sin offering he then offered up in smoke on the altar just as Yahweh had commanded Moses.
Leviticus9:11 The flesh and the skin
Leviticus9:12 Then he slaughtered the burnt offering; and Aaron’s sons handed the blood to him
Leviticus9:13 And they handed the burnt offering to him in pieces
Leviticus9:14 He also washed the entrails and the legs and offered them up in smoke with the burnt offering on the altar.
Leviticus9:15 Then he brought near the people’s offering and took the goat of the sin offering which was for the people and slaughtered it and offered it for sin
Leviticus9:16 He also brought near the burnt offering and offered it according to the legal judgment.
Leviticus9:17 Next he brought near the grain offering and filled his hand with some of it and offered it up in smoke on the altar
Leviticus9:18 Then he slaughtered the ox and the ram
Leviticus9:19 As for the portions of fat from the ox and from the ram
Leviticus9:20 they now placed the portions of fat on the breasts; and he offered them up in smoke on the altar.
Leviticus9:21 But the breasts and the right thigh Aaron waved as a wave offering before Yahweh
Leviticus9:22 Then Aaron lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them
Leviticus9:23 And Moses and Aaron went into the tent of meeting. Then they came out and blessed the people. And the glory of Yahweh appeared to all the people.
Leviticus9:24 Then fire came out from before Yahweh and consumed the burnt offering and the portions of fat on the altar. And all the people saw it and shouted and fell on their faces.

Leviticus11:1 Yahweh spoke again to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus11:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus11:3 Whatever divides a hoof
Leviticus11:4 Nevertheless
Leviticus11:5 Likewise
Leviticus11:6 the rabbit also
Leviticus11:7 and the pig
Leviticus11:8 You shall not eat of their flesh
Leviticus11:9 ‘These you may eat
Leviticus11:10 But whatever is in the seas and in the rivers that does not have fins and scales among all the swarming life of the water
Leviticus11:11 and they shall be detestable to you; you may not eat of their flesh
Leviticus11:12 Whatever in the water does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 ‘These
Leviticus11:14 and the kite and the falcon in its kind
Leviticus11:15 every raven in its kind
Leviticus11:16 and the ostrich and the owl and the gull and the hawk in its kind
Leviticus11:17 and the little owl and the cormorant and the great owl
Leviticus11:18 and the white owl and the pelican and the carrion vulture
Leviticus11:19 and the stork
Leviticus11:20 ‘All the swarming things that fly and that walk on all fours are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 Yet these you may eat among all the swarming things that fly and that walk on all fours: those which have above their feet jointed legs with which to jump on the earth.
Leviticus11:22 These of them you may eat: the locust in its kinds and the devastating locust in its kinds and the cricket in its kinds and the grasshopper in its kinds.
Leviticus11:23 But all other swarming things that fly and that are four-footed are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 ‘By these
Leviticus11:25 and whoever picks up any of their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:26 Concerning all the animals which divide the hoof but do not make a split hoof or which do not chew cud
Leviticus11:27 Also whatever walks on its paws
Leviticus11:28 and the one who picks up their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 ‘Now these are to you the unclean among the swarming things which swarm on the earth: the mole and the mouse and the great lizard in its kinds
Leviticus11:30 and the gecko and the crocodile and the lizard and the sand reptile and the chameleon.
Leviticus11:31 These are to you the unclean among all the swarming things; whoever touches them when they are dead becomes unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 Also anything on which one of them may fall when they are dead becomes unclean
Leviticus11:33 As for any earthenware vessel into which one of them may fall
Leviticus11:34 Any of the food which may be eaten
Leviticus11:35 Everything
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless a spring or a cistern collecting water shall be clean
Leviticus11:37 And if a part of their carcass falls on any seed for sowing which is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 Though if water is put on the seed and a part of their carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 ‘Also if one of the animals dies which you have for food
Leviticus11:40 He too
Leviticus11:41 ‘Now every swarming thing that swarms on the earth is detestable; it shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever goes on its belly
Leviticus11:43 Do not render yourselves detestable through any of the swarming things that swarm; and you shall not make yourselves unclean with them so that you become unclean.
Leviticus11:44 For I am Yahweh your God. Therefore
Leviticus11:45 For I am Yahweh who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God; thus you shall be holy
Leviticus11:46 This is the law regarding the animal and the bird and every living thing that moves in the waters and everything that swarms on the earth
Leviticus11:47 to separate between the unclean and the clean

Leviticus12:3 Now on the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
