Leviticus12:5 But if she bears a female child
Leviticus12:6 ‘When the days of her cleansing are fulfilled
Leviticus12:7 Then he shall bring it near before Yahweh and make atonement for her
Leviticus12:8 But if she cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus13:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus13:2 “When a man has on the skin of his body a swelling or a scab or a bright spot
Leviticus13:3 Then the priest shall look at the mark on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:4 But if the bright spot is white on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:5 Then the priest shall look at him on the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 And the priest shall look at him again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 “But if the scab spreads farther on the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing
Leviticus13:8 And the priest shall look
Leviticus13:9 “When the infection of leprosy is on a man
Leviticus13:10 The priest shall then look
Leviticus13:11 it is a chronic leprosy on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:12 But if the leprosy breaks out farther on the skin
Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall look
Leviticus13:14 But whenever raw flesh appears on him
Leviticus13:15 And the priest shall look at the raw flesh
Leviticus13:16 Or if the raw flesh turns again and is changed to white
Leviticus13:17 and the priest shall look at him
Leviticus13:18 “When the body has a boil on its skin and it is healed
Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there is a white swelling or a reddish-white bright spot
Leviticus13:20 and the priest shall look
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest looks at it
Leviticus13:22 and if it spreads farther on the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the bright spot remains in its place and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 “Or if the body sustains in its skin a burn by fire
Leviticus13:25 then the priest shall look at it. And if the hair in the bright spot has turned white and it appears to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest looks at it
Leviticus13:27 and the priest shall look at him on the seventh day. If it spreads farther in the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot remains in its place and has not spread in the skin but is faded
Leviticus13:29 “Now if a man or woman has an infection on the head or on the beard
Leviticus13:30 then the priest shall look at the infection
Leviticus13:31 But if the priest looks at the infection of the scale
Leviticus13:32 Now on the seventh day the priest shall look at the infection
Leviticus13:33 then he shall shave himself
Leviticus13:34 Then on the seventh day the priest shall look at the scale
Leviticus13:35 But if the scale spreads farther in the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 then the priest shall look at him
Leviticus13:37 If in his sight the scale has remained
Leviticus13:38 “When a man or a woman has bright spots on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:39 then the priest shall look
Leviticus13:40 “Now if a man loses the hair of his head
Leviticus13:41 And if his head becomes bald at the front and sides
Leviticus13:42 But if on the bald head or the bald forehead
Leviticus13:43 Then the priest shall look at him; and if the swelling of the infection is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead
Leviticus13:44 he is a leprous man; he is unclean. The priest shall surely pronounce him unclean; his infection is on his head.
Leviticus13:45 “As for the leper who has the infection
Leviticus13:46 He shall remain unclean all the days during which he has the infection; he is unclean. He shall live alone; his place of habitation shall be outside the camp.
Leviticus13:47 “When a garment has a mark of leprosy in it
Leviticus13:48 whether in warp or woof
Leviticus13:49 if the mark is greenish or reddish in the garment or in the leather or in the warp or in the woof or in any article of leather
Leviticus13:50 Then the priest shall look at the mark and shall isolate the article with the mark for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 He shall then look at the mark on the seventh day; if the mark has spread in the garment
Leviticus13:52 So he shall burn the garment
Leviticus13:53 “But if the priest shall look
Leviticus13:54 then the priest shall command them to wash the thing in which the mark occurs
Leviticus13:55 After the article with the mark has been washed
Leviticus13:56 “Then if the priest looks
Leviticus13:57 and if it appears again in the garment
Leviticus13:58 Now the garment
Leviticus13:59 This is the law for the mark of leprosy in a garment of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus14:2 “This shall be the law of the leper in the day of his cleansing. Now he shall be brought to the priest
Leviticus14:3 and the priest shall go out to the outside of the camp. Thus the priest shall look
Leviticus14:4 then the priest shall give a command to take two live clean birds and cedar wood and a scarlet string and hyssop for the one who is to be cleansed.
Leviticus14:5 The priest shall also give a command to slaughter the one bird in an earthenware vessel over running water.
Leviticus14:6 As for the live bird
Leviticus14:7 He shall then sprinkle seven times the one who is to be cleansed from the leprosy and shall pronounce him clean
Leviticus14:8 The one to be cleansed shall then wash his clothes and shave off all his hair and bathe in water and be clean. Now afterward
Leviticus14:9 And it will be on the seventh day that he shall shave off all his hair: he shall shave his head and his beard and his eyebrows
Leviticus14:10 “Now on the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish
Leviticus14:11 and the priest who pronounces him clean shall present the man to be cleansed as well as these things before Yahweh at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus14:12 Then the priest shall take the one male lamb and bring it near for a guilt offering
Leviticus14:13 Next he shall slaughter the male lamb in the place where they slaughter the sin offering and the burnt offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest shall then take some of the blood of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:15 The priest shall also take some of the log of oil and pour it into his left palm;
Leviticus14:16 the priest shall then dip his right-hand finger into the oil that is in his left palm
Leviticus14:17 Of the remaining oil which is in his palm
Leviticus14:18 but the rest of the oil that is in the priest’s palm
Leviticus14:19 The priest shall next offer the sin offering and make atonement for the one to be cleansed from his uncleanness. Then afterward
Leviticus14:20 And the priest shall offer up the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. Thus the priest shall make atonement for him
Leviticus14:21 “But if he is poor and his means are insufficient
Leviticus14:22 and two turtledoves or two young pigeons which are within his means
Leviticus14:23 Then the eighth day he shall bring them for his cleansing to the priest
Leviticus14:24 And the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log of oil
Leviticus14:25 Next he shall slaughter the lamb of the guilt offering; and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.
Leviticus14:26 The priest shall also pour some of the oil into his left palm;
Leviticus14:27 and with his right-hand finger the priest shall sprinkle some of the oil that is in his left palm seven times before Yahweh.
Leviticus14:28 The priest shall then put some of the oil that is in his palm on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot
Leviticus14:29 Moreover
Leviticus14:30 He shall then offer one of the turtledoves or young pigeons
Leviticus14:31 He shall offer what he can afford
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for him in whom there is an infection of leprosy
Leviticus14:33 Yahweh further spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus14:34 “When you enter the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 then the one who owns the house shall come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 The priest shall then command that they empty the house before the priest goes in to look at the mark
Leviticus14:37 So he shall look at the mark
Leviticus14:38 then the priest shall come out of the house
Leviticus14:39 And the priest shall return on the seventh day and look again. If the mark has indeed spread in the walls of the house
Leviticus14:40 then the priest shall command them to tear out the stones with the mark in them and throw them away at an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 And he shall have the house scraped all around inside
Leviticus14:42 Then they shall take other stones and replace those stones
Leviticus14:43 “If
Leviticus14:44 then the priest shall come in and look again. If he sees that the mark has indeed spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 He shall therefore tear down the house
Leviticus14:46 Moreover
Leviticus14:47 Likewise
Leviticus14:48 “If
Leviticus14:49 To cleanse the house then
Leviticus14:50 and he shall slaughter the one bird in an earthenware vessel over running water.
Leviticus14:51 Then he shall take the cedar wood and the hyssop and the scarlet string
Leviticus14:52 He shall thus cleanse the house with the blood of the bird and with the running water
Leviticus14:53 However
Leviticus14:54 This is the law for any mark of leprosy—even for a scale
Leviticus14:55 and for the leprous garment or house
Leviticus14:56 and for a swelling and for a scab and for a bright spot—
Leviticus14:57 to instruct when they are unclean and when they are clean. This is the law of leprosy.
Leviticus15:1 Yahweh also spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus15:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus15:3 This
Leviticus15:4 Every bed on which the person with the discharge lies becomes unclean
Leviticus15:5 Anyone
Leviticus15:6 and whoever sits on the thing on which the man with the discharge has been sitting shall wash his clothes and bathe in water and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:7 Also whoever touches the person with the discharge shall wash his clothes and bathe in water and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:8 Or if the man with the discharge spits on one who is clean
Leviticus15:9 Every saddle on which the person with the discharge rides becomes unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Whoever then touches any of the things which were under him shall be unclean until evening
Leviticus15:11 Likewise
Leviticus15:12 However
Leviticus15:13 ‘Now when the man with the discharge becomes cleansed from his discharge
Leviticus15:14 Then on the eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two young pigeons and come before Yahweh to the doorway of the tent of meeting and give them to the priest;
Leviticus15:15 and the priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 ‘Now if a man has a seminal emission
Leviticus15:17 As for any garment or any leather on which there is seminal emission
Leviticus15:18 If there is a woman with whom a man lies so that there is a seminal emission
Leviticus15:19 ‘If a woman has a discharge
Leviticus15:20 Everything also on which she lies during her menstrual impurity shall be unclean
Leviticus15:21 And anyone who touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe in water and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:22 And whoever touches any thing on which she sits shall wash his clothes and bathe in water and be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:23 Whether it be on the bed or on the thing on which she is sitting
Leviticus15:24 If a man actually lies with her so that her menstrual impurity is on him
Leviticus15:25 ‘Now if a woman has a discharge of her blood many days
Leviticus15:26 Any bed on which she lies all the days of her discharge shall be to her like her bed at menstruation; and every thing on which she sits shall be unclean
Leviticus15:27 Likewise
Leviticus15:28 Now if she becomes clean from her discharge
Leviticus15:29 Then on the eighth day she shall take for herself two turtledoves or two young pigeons and bring them in to the priest
Leviticus15:30 And the priest shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering. So the priest shall make atonement on her behalf before Yahweh because of her unclean discharge.’
Leviticus15:31 “Thus you shall keep the sons of Israel separated from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for the one with a discharge
Leviticus15:33 and for the woman who is ill because of menstrual impurity
Leviticus16:1 Now Yahweh spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron
Leviticus16:2 And Yahweh said to Moses: “Tell your brother Aaron that he shall not enter at any time into the holy place inside the veil
Leviticus16:3 Aaron shall enter the holy place with this: with a bull from the herd for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He shall put on the holy linen tunic
Leviticus16:5 And he shall take from the congregation of the sons of Israel two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Then Aaron shall bring near the bull for the sin offering which is for himself
Leviticus16:7 And he shall take the two goats and present them before Yahweh at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus16:8 And Aaron shall cast lots for the two goats
Leviticus16:9 Then Aaron shall bring near the goat on which the lot for Yahweh fell
Leviticus16:10 But the goat on which the lot for the scapegoat fell shall be presented alive before Yahweh
Leviticus16:11 “Then Aaron shall bring near the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus16:12 And he shall take a firepan full of coals of fire from upon the altar before Yahweh and two handfuls of finely ground fragrant incense
Leviticus16:13 And he shall put the incense on the fire before Yahweh
Leviticus16:14 Moreover
Leviticus16:15 “Then he shall slaughter the goat of the sin offering which is for the people and bring its blood inside the veil and do with its blood as he did with the blood of the bull. And he shall sprinkle it on the mercy seat and in front of the mercy seat.
Leviticus16:16 So he shall make atonement for the holy place because of the uncleanness of the sons of Israel and because of their transgressions in regard to all their sins; and thus he shall do for the tent of meeting which abides with them in the midst of their uncleanness.
Leviticus16:17 Now when he goes in to make atonement in the holy place
Leviticus16:18 Then he shall go out to the altar that is before Yahweh and make atonement for it. And he shall take some of the blood of the bull and of the blood of the goat
Leviticus16:19 With his finger he shall sprinkle some of the blood on it seven times and cleanse it and set it apart as holy from the uncleanness of the sons of Israel.
Leviticus16:20 “And when he finishes making atonement for the holy place and the tent of meeting and the altar
Leviticus16:21 Then Aaron shall lay both of his hands on the head of the live goat
Leviticus16:22 And the goat shall bear on itself all their iniquities to an isolated land; and he shall send out the goat in the wilderness.
Leviticus16:23 “Then Aaron shall come into the tent of meeting and take off the linen garments which he put on when he went into the holy place and shall leave them there.
Leviticus16:24 And he shall bathe his body with water in a holy place and put on his clothes and come forth
Leviticus16:25 Then he shall offer up in smoke the fat of the sin offering on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 And the one who sent the goat out as the scapegoat shall wash his clothes and bathe his body with water; then afterward he shall come into the camp.
Leviticus16:27 But the bull of the sin offering and the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 Then the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body with water
Leviticus16:29 “And this shall be a perpetual statute for you: in the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 for it is on this day that atonement shall be made for you to cleanse you; you will be clean from all your sins before Yahweh.
Leviticus16:31 It is to be a sabbath of solemn rest for you
Leviticus16:32 So the priest who is anointed and ordained to minister as a priest in his father’s place shall make atonement: he shall thus put on the linen garments
Leviticus16:33 and make atonement for the holy sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 Now you shall have this as a perpetual statute
Leviticus17:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus17:2 “Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus17:3 “Any man from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox or a lamb or a goat in the camp
Leviticus17:4 and has not brought it to the doorway of the tent of meeting to bring it near as an offering to Yahweh before the tabernacle of Yahweh
Leviticus17:5 The reason is so that the sons of Israel may bring their sacrifices which they were sacrificing in the open field
Leviticus17:6 And the priest shall splash the blood on the altar of Yahweh at the doorway of the tent of meeting and offer up the fat in smoke as a soothing aroma to Yahweh.
Leviticus17:7 And they shall no longer sacrifice their sacrifices to the goat demons with which they play the harlot. This shall be a perpetual statute to them throughout their generations.”’
Leviticus17:8 “Then you shall say to them
Leviticus17:9 and does not bring it to the doorway of the tent of meeting to offer it to Yahweh
Leviticus17:10 ‘And any man from the house of Israel
Leviticus17:11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood
Leviticus17:12 Therefore I said to the sons of Israel
Leviticus17:13 So any man from the sons of Israel
Leviticus17:14 “For as for the life of all flesh
Leviticus17:15 And when any person eats an animal which dies or is torn by beasts
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash them or bathe his body
Leviticus18:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus18:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus18:3 You shall not do according to what is done in the land of Egypt where you lived
Leviticus18:4 You are to do My judgments and keep My statutes
Leviticus18:5 So you shall keep My statutes and My judgments
Leviticus18:6 ‘None of you shall approach any blood relative of his to uncover nakedness; I am Yahweh.
Leviticus18:7 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father
Leviticus18:8 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife; it is your father’s nakedness.
Leviticus18:9 The nakedness of your sister
Leviticus18:10 The nakedness of your son’s daughter or your daughter’s daughter
Leviticus18:11 The nakedness of your father’s wife’s daughter
Leviticus18:12 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s sister; she is your father’s blood relative.
Leviticus18:13 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s brother; you shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law; she is your son’s wife; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Leviticus18:16 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife; it is your brother’s nakedness.
Leviticus18:17 You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and of her daughter
Leviticus18:18 And you shall not marry a woman in addition to her sister as a rival while she is alive
Leviticus18:19 ‘Also you shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness during her menstrual impurity.
Leviticus18:20 And you shall not lie sexually with your neighbor’s wife
Leviticus18:21 And you shall not give any of your seed to pass them over to Molech
Leviticus18:22 And you shall not lie with a male as one lies with a female; it is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 Also you shall not lie with any animal to be defiled with it
Leviticus18:24 ‘So do not defile yourselves by any of these things; for by all these the nations which I am casting out before you have become defiled.
Leviticus18:25 So the land has become defiled
Leviticus18:26 But as for you
Leviticus18:27 (for the men of the land who have been before you have done all these abominations
Leviticus18:28 so that the land will not vomit you out
Leviticus18:29 For whoever does any of these abominations
Leviticus18:30 Thus you are to keep My charge
Leviticus19:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus19:2 “Speak to all the congregation of the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus19:3 Every one of you shall fear his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves molten gods; I am Yahweh your God.
Leviticus19:5 ‘Now when you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to Yahweh
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten the same day you offer it and the next day; but what remains until the third day shall be burned with fire.
Leviticus19:7 So if it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 And everyone who eats it will bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 ‘Now when you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 Nor shall you glean your vineyard
Leviticus19:11 ‘You shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 And you shall not swear falsely by My name
Leviticus19:13 ‘You shall not oppress your neighbor nor rob him. The wages of a hired man shall not remain with you overnight until morning.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse a deaf man nor place a stumbling block before the blind
Leviticus19:15 ‘You shall do no injustice in judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor nor defer to the great
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go about as a slanderer among your people
Leviticus19:17 ‘You shall not hate your brother in your heart; you may surely reprove your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take vengeance
Leviticus19:19 ‘You are to keep My statutes. You shall not breed together two kinds of your cattle; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed nor wear a garment upon you of two kinds of material mixed together.
Leviticus19:20 ‘Now if a man lies sexually with a woman who is a slave assigned to another man
Leviticus19:21 And he shall bring his guilt offering to Yahweh to the doorway of the tent of meeting
Leviticus19:22 The priest shall also make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before Yahweh for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus19:23 ‘When you enter the land and plant all kinds of trees for food
Leviticus19:24 But in the fourth year all its fruit shall be holy
Leviticus19:25 Now in the fifth year you shall eat of its fruit
Leviticus19:26 ‘You shall not eat anything with the blood nor interpret omens or soothsaying.
Leviticus19:27 You shall not round off the side-growth of your heads nor harm the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 And you shall not make any cuts in your body for the dead nor make any tattoo marks on yourselves: I am Yahweh.
Leviticus19:29 ‘Do not profane your daughter by making her a harlot
Leviticus19:30 You shall keep My sabbaths and fear My sanctuary; I am Yahweh.
Leviticus19:31 ‘Do not turn to mediums or spiritists; do not seek them out to be defiled by them. I am Yahweh your God.
Leviticus19:32 ‘You shall rise up before the gray-haired and honor the aged
Leviticus19:33 ‘And when a sojourner sojourns with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 The sojourner who sojourns with you shall be to you as the native among you
Leviticus19:35 ‘You shall do no wrong in judgment
Leviticus19:36 You shall have just balances
Leviticus19:37 You shall thus keep all My statutes and all My judgments and do them; I am Yahweh.’”
Leviticus20:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus20:2 “You shall also say to the sons of Israel: ‘Any man from the sons of Israel or from the sojourners sojourning in Israel who gives any of his seed to Molech shall surely be put to death; the people of the land shall stone him with stones.
Leviticus20:3 I will also set My face against that man and will cut him off from among his people because he has given some of those who are his seed to Molech
Leviticus20:4 If the people of the land
Leviticus20:5 then I Myself will set My face against that man and against his family
Leviticus20:6 ‘As for the person who turns to mediums and to spiritists
Leviticus20:7 Therefore
Leviticus20:8 And you shall keep My statutes and do them; I am Yahweh who makes you holy.
Leviticus20:9 ‘If there is anyone who curses his father or his mother
Leviticus20:10 ‘If there is a man who commits adultery with another man’s wife
Leviticus20:11 If there is a man who lies with his father’s wife
Leviticus20:12 If there is a man who lies with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If there is a man who lies with a male as those who lie with a woman
Leviticus20:14 If there is a man who marries a woman and her mother
Leviticus20:15 If there is a man who lies with an animal
Leviticus20:16 If there is a woman who approaches any animal to mate with it
Leviticus20:17 ‘If there is a man who takes his sister
Leviticus20:18 If there is a man who lies with a menstruous woman and uncovers her nakedness
Leviticus20:19 You shall also not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister or of your father’s sister
Leviticus20:20 If there is a man who lies with his aunt
Leviticus20:21 If there is a man who takes his brother’s wife
Leviticus20:22 ‘You shall therefore keep all My statutes and all My judgments and do them
Leviticus20:23 Moreover
Leviticus20:24 Hence I have said to you
Leviticus20:25 You are therefore to separate between the clean animal and the unclean
Leviticus20:26 Thus you shall be holy to Me
Leviticus20:27 ‘Now a man or a woman who is a medium or a spiritist shall surely be put to death. They shall be stoned with stones. Their bloodguiltiness is upon them.’”
Leviticus21:1 Then Yahweh said to Moses
Leviticus21:2 except for his blood relatives who are nearest to him
Leviticus21:3 also for his virgin sister
Leviticus21:4 He shall not defile himself as a relative by marriage among his people
Leviticus21:5 They shall not make any baldness on their heads nor shave off the edges of their beards nor make any cuts in their flesh.
Leviticus21:6 They shall be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God
Leviticus21:7 They shall not take a woman who is profaned by harlotry
Leviticus21:8 Therefore
Leviticus21:9 Also the daughter of any priest
Leviticus21:10 ‘And the priest who is the highest among his brothers
Leviticus21:11 nor shall he approach any dead person nor defile himself even for his father or his mother;
Leviticus21:12 nor shall he go out of the sanctuary nor profane the sanctuary of his God
Leviticus21:13 And he shall take a wife in her virginity.
Leviticus21:14 A widow or a divorced woman or one who is profaned by harlotry
Leviticus21:15 so that he will not profane his seed among his people; for I am Yahweh who makes him holy.’”

Leviticus21:19 or a man who has a broken foot or broken hand
Leviticus21:20 or a hunchback or a dwarf

Leviticus21:22 He may eat the food of his God

Leviticus22:6 a person who touches any such thing shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat of the holy gifts unless he has bathed his body in water.
Leviticus22:7 But the sun will set
Leviticus22:8 He shall not eat an animal which dies or is torn by beasts
Leviticus22:9 They shall therefore keep My charge so that they will not bear sin because of it and die thereby because they profane it; I am Yahweh who makes them holy.
Leviticus22:10 ‘No layman
Leviticus22:11 But if a priest buys a person as his property with his money
Leviticus22:12 If a priest’s daughter is married to a layman
Leviticus22:13 But if a priest’s daughter becomes a widow or divorced
Leviticus22:14 But if a man eats a holy gift unintentionally
Leviticus22:15 They shall not profane the holy gifts of the sons of Israel which they raise up to Yahweh
Leviticus22:16 and so cause them to bear punishment for guilt by eating their holy gifts; for I am Yahweh who makes them holy.’”

Leviticus22:18 “Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus22:19 for you to be accepted—it must be a male without blemish from the cattle
Leviticus22:20 Whatever has a defect
Leviticus22:21 And when a man brings a sacrifice of peace offerings near to Yahweh to fulfill a special vow or for a freewill offering
Leviticus22:22 Those that are blind or fractured or maimed or have a running sore or eczema or scabs
Leviticus22:23 Now in respect to an ox or a lamb which has an overgrown or stunted member
Leviticus22:24 Also anything with its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut
Leviticus22:25 nor shall you bring near any such thing from the hand of a foreigner for offering as the food of your God; for their corruption is in them

Leviticus22:28 But
Leviticus22:29 And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to Yahweh
Leviticus22:30 It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until morning; I am Yahweh.
Leviticus22:31 So you shall keep My commandments and do them; I am Yahweh.
Leviticus22:32 “And you shall not profane My holy name
Leviticus22:33 who brought you out from the land of Egypt to be your God; I am Yahweh.”
Leviticus23:1 And Yahweh spoke again to Moses
Leviticus23:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus23:3 ‘For six days work may be done
Leviticus23:4 ‘These are the appointed times of Yahweh
Leviticus23:5 In the first month
Leviticus23:6 Then on the fifteenth day of the same month there is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to Yahweh; for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not do any laborious work.
Leviticus23:8 But for seven days you shall bring near an offering by fire to Yahweh. On the seventh day is a holy convocation; you shall not do any laborious work.’”
Leviticus23:9 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:10 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus23:11 And he shall wave the sheaf before Yahweh for you to be accepted; on the day after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.
Leviticus23:12 Now on the day when you wave the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 Its grain offering shall then be two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 Until this same day
Leviticus23:15 ‘You shall also count for yourselves from the day after the sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You shall count fifty days to the day after the seventh sabbath; then you shall bring a new grain offering near to Yahweh.
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring in from your places of habitation two loaves of bread for a wave offering
Leviticus23:18 Along with the bread you shall bring near seven one year old male lambs without blemish and a bull from the herd and two rams; they shall be a burnt offering to Yahweh
Leviticus23:19 You shall also offer one male goat for a sin offering and two male lambs one year old for a sacrifice of peace offerings.
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall then wave them with the bread of the first fruits for a wave offering with two lambs before Yahweh; they shall be holy to Yahweh for the priest.
Leviticus23:21 On this same day you shall make a proclamation as well; it shall be a holy convocation for you. You shall do no laborious work. It is to be a perpetual statute in all your places of habitation throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 ‘When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 Again Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus23:25 You shall not do any laborious work
Leviticus23:26 And Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 “On exactly the tenth day of this seventh month is the day of atonement; it shall be a holy convocation for you
Leviticus23:28 And you shall not do any work on this same day
Leviticus23:29 If there is any person who will not humble himself on this same day
Leviticus23:30 And as for any person who does any work on this same day
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no work at all. It is to be a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your places of habitation.
Leviticus23:32 It is to be a sabbath of complete rest to you
Leviticus23:33 Again Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus23:35 On the first day is a holy convocation; you shall do no laborious work of any kind.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you shall bring an offering by fire near to Yahweh. On the eighth day you shall have a holy convocation and bring an offering by fire near to Yahweh; it is a solemn assembly. You shall do no laborious work.
Leviticus23:37 ‘These are the appointed times of Yahweh which you shall proclaim as holy convocations
Leviticus23:38 besides those of the sabbaths of Yahweh and besides your gifts and besides all your votive and freewill offerings
Leviticus23:39 ‘On exactly the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 And on the first day you shall take for yourselves the foliage of beautiful trees
Leviticus23:41 You shall thus celebrate it as a feast to Yahweh for seven days in the year. It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations; you shall celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You shall live in booths for seven days; all the native-born in Israel shall live in booths
Leviticus23:43 so that your generations may know that I had the sons of Israel live in booths when I brought them out from the land of Egypt. I am Yahweh your God.’”
Leviticus23:44 So Moses spoke to the sons of Israel the appointed times of Yahweh.
Leviticus24:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 “Command the sons of Israel that they bring to you clear oil from beaten olives for the light

Leviticus24:5 “Then you shall take fine flour and bake twelve cakes with it; two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each cake.
Leviticus24:6 And you shall set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 And you shall put pure frankincense on each row that it may be a memorial portion for the bread
Leviticus24:8 Every sabbath day he shall set it in order before Yahweh continually; it is an everlasting covenant for the sons of Israel.
Leviticus24:9 And it shall be for Aaron and his sons

Leviticus24:12 And they put him in custody so that the command of Yahweh might be made clear to them.

Leviticus24:15 And you shall speak to the sons of Israel
Leviticus24:16 Moreover

Leviticus24:18 And the one who strikes down the life of an animal shall make restitution for it
Leviticus24:19 If a man injures his neighbor

Leviticus24:21 Thus the one who strikes down an animal shall make restitution for it
Leviticus24:22 There shall be one standard of judgment for you; it shall be for the sojourner as well as the native

Leviticus25:1 Yahweh then spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus25:3 Six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but during the seventh year the land shall have a sabbath rest
Leviticus25:5 What grows of its own accord from your harvest you shall not reap
Leviticus25:6 And the sabbath produce of the land shall be for food; for you and your male and female slaves and your hired man and your foreign resident
Leviticus25:7 Even your cattle and the beasts that are in your land shall have all its produce to eat.
Leviticus25:8 ‘You are also to count off seven sabbaths of years for yourself
Leviticus25:9 You shall then sound a ram’s horn abroad on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the day of atonement you shall sound a horn all through your land.
Leviticus25:10 You shall thus set apart as holy the fiftieth year and proclaim a release through the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you
Leviticus25:11 You shall have the fiftieth year as a jubilee; you shall not sow; you shall not reap what grows of its own accord; you shall not gather in from its untrimmed vines.
Leviticus25:12 For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you. You shall eat its produce out of the field.
Leviticus25:13 ‘On this year of jubilee
Leviticus25:14 If you make a sale
Leviticus25:15 Corresponding to the number of years after the jubilee
Leviticus25:16 In proportion to the extent of the years you shall increase its price
Leviticus25:17 So you shall not mistreat one another
Leviticus25:18 ‘You shall thus observe My statutes and keep My judgments
Leviticus25:19 Then the land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 But if you say
Leviticus25:21 then I will command My blessing for you in the sixth year that it will bring forth the produce for three years.
Leviticus25:22 So you shall sow the eighth year and eat old things from that produce
Leviticus25:23 ‘The land
Leviticus25:24 Thus for every piece of land of your possession
Leviticus25:25 ‘If a brother of yours becomes so poor he has to sell part of his possession of land
Leviticus25:26 Or in case a man has no kinsman redeemer
Leviticus25:27 then he shall calculate the years since its sale and return the balance to the man to whom he sold it
Leviticus25:28 But if he has not found sufficient means to return it to himself
Leviticus25:29 ‘Likewise
Leviticus25:30 But if it is not redeemed for him within the space of a full year
Leviticus25:31 The houses of the villages
Leviticus25:32 As for cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 What
Leviticus25:34 But pasture fields of their cities shall not be sold
Leviticus25:35 ‘Now if a brother of yours becomes poor and his means with regard to you falter
Leviticus25:36 Do not take usurious interest from him
Leviticus25:37 You shall not give him your silver at interest nor your food for gain.
Leviticus25:38 I am Yahweh your God
Leviticus25:39 ‘If a brother of yours becomes so poor with regard to you that he sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 He shall be with you as a hired man
Leviticus25:41 He shall then go out from you
Leviticus25:42 For they are My slaves whom I brought out from the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold in a slave sale.
Leviticus25:43 You shall not have dominion over him with brutality
Leviticus25:44 As for your male and female slaves whom you may have—you may acquire male and female slaves from the nations that are around you.
Leviticus25:45 And also you may acquire from the sons of the foreign residents who sojourn among you
Leviticus25:46 You may even give them as an inheritance to your sons after you
Leviticus25:47 ‘Now if the means of a sojourner or of a foreign resident with you becomes sufficient
Leviticus25:48 then he shall have redemption right after he has been sold. One of his brothers may redeem him
Leviticus25:49 or his uncle or his uncle’s son may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 He then with his purchaser shall calculate from the year when he sold himself to him up to the year of jubilee; and the price of his sale shall correspond to the number of years. It is like the days of a hired man that he shall be with him.
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years
Leviticus25:52 and if few years remain until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:53 Like a man hired year by year he shall be with him; he shall not have dominion over him with brutality in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 Even if he is not redeemed by these means
Leviticus25:55 For the sons of Israel are My slaves; they are My slaves whom I brought out from the land of Egypt. I am Yahweh your God.
Leviticus26:1 ‘You shall not make for yourselves idols
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep My sabbaths and fear My sanctuary; I am Yahweh.
Leviticus26:3 If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments so as to do them
Leviticus26:4 then I shall give you rains in their season
Leviticus26:5 Indeed
Leviticus26:6 I shall also give you peace in the land so that you may lie down
Leviticus26:7 But you will pursue your enemies
Leviticus26:8 and five of you will pursue one hundred
Leviticus26:9 So I will turn toward you and make you fruitful and multiply you
Leviticus26:10 And you will eat the old supply and clear out the old because of the new.
Leviticus26:11 Moreover
Leviticus26:12 I will also walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am Yahweh your God
Leviticus26:14 ‘But if you do not obey Me and do not do all these commandments
Leviticus26:15 if
Leviticus26:16 I
Leviticus26:17 And I will set My face against you so that you will be defeated before your enemies; and those who hate you will have dominion over you
Leviticus26:18 If also after these things you do not obey Me
Leviticus26:19 I will also break down your pride of strength; I will also give your sky over to become like iron and your earth like bronze.
Leviticus26:20 And your power will be spent uselessly
Leviticus26:21 ‘If then
Leviticus26:22 And I will send out among you the beasts of the field
Leviticus26:23 ‘And if by these things you do not accept My discipline
Leviticus26:24 then I will walk in hostility against you; and I
Leviticus26:25 I will also bring upon you a sword which will execute vengeance for the covenant; and when you gather together into your cities
Leviticus26:26 When I break your staff of bread
Leviticus26:27 ‘Yet
Leviticus26:28 then I will walk in wrathful hostility against you
Leviticus26:29 Further
Leviticus26:30 I then will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars and give your corpses to lie on the corpses of your idols
Leviticus26:31 And I will give your cities over as a waste and will make your sanctuaries desolate
Leviticus26:32 And I will make the land desolate
Leviticus26:33 You
Leviticus26:34 ‘Then the land will make up for its sabbaths all the days of the desolation
Leviticus26:35 All the days of its desolation it will observe the rest which it did not observe on your sabbaths
Leviticus26:36 As for those of you who may remain
Leviticus26:37 They will therefore stumble over each other as if running from the sword
Leviticus26:38 But you will perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 So those of you who may remain will rot away in their iniquity in the lands of your enemies; and also in the iniquities of their fathers they will rot away with them.
Leviticus26:40 ‘If they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers
Leviticus26:41 I also was walking in hostility against them
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember My covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 For the land will be forsaken by them and will make up for its sabbaths while it is made desolate without them. They
Leviticus26:44 Yet in spite of this
Leviticus26:45 But I will remember for them the covenant with their ancestors
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and judgments and laws which Yahweh has given to be between Himself and the sons of Israel by the hand of Moses at Mount Sinai.
Leviticus27:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Leviticus27:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Leviticus27:3 If your valuation is of the male from twenty years even to sixty years old
Leviticus27:4 Or if it is a female
Leviticus27:5 If it be from five years even to twenty years old then your valuation for the male shall be twenty shekels and for the female ten shekels.
Leviticus27:6 But if they are from a month even up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 If they are from sixty years old and upward
Leviticus27:8 But if he is poorer than your valuation
Leviticus27:9 ‘Now if it is an animal of the kind which men can bring near as an offering to Yahweh
Leviticus27:10 He shall not replace it or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 If
Leviticus27:12 And the priest shall value it as either good or bad; as you
Leviticus27:13 But if he should ever wish to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 ‘Now if a man sets his house apart as holy to Yahweh
Leviticus27:15 Yet if the one who sets it apart as holy should wish to redeem his house
Leviticus27:16 ‘Again
Leviticus27:17 If he sets apart his field as holy from the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:18 If he sets apart his field as holy after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If the one who sets it apart as holy should ever wish to redeem the field
Leviticus27:20 Yet if he will not redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 and it will be that when it reverts in the jubilee
Leviticus27:22 Or if he sets apart as holy to Yahweh a field which he has bought
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of your valuation up to the year of jubilee; and he shall on that day give your valuation as holy to Yahweh.
Leviticus27:24 In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom he bought it
Leviticus27:25 Every valuation of yours
Leviticus27:26 ‘However
Leviticus27:27 But if it is among the unclean animals
Leviticus27:28 ‘Nevertheless
Leviticus27:29 No one who may have been devoted among men shall be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 ‘Thus all the tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 If
Leviticus27:32 For every tenth part of herd or flock
Leviticus27:33 He shall not inquire whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which Yahweh commanded Moses for the sons of Israel at Mount Sinai.

Numbers1:2 “Take a census of all the congregation of the sons of Israel
Numbers1:3 from twenty years old and upward

Numbers1:5 These then are the names of the men who shall stand with you: of Reuben
Numbers1:6 of Simeon
Numbers1:7 of Judah
Numbers1:8 of Issachar

Numbers1:11 of Benjamin
Numbers1:12 of Dan
Numbers1:13 of Asher
Numbers1:14 of Gad
Numbers1:15 of Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These are they who were called upon by the congregation
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled all the congregation together on the first of the second month. Then they registered by genealogy in their families

Numbers1:54 Thus the sons of Israel did; according to all which Yahweh had commanded Moses

Numbers3:1 Now these are the generations of Aaron and Moses at the time when Yahweh spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These then are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died before Yahweh when they offered strange fire before Yahweh in the wilderness of Sinai; and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priests in the lifetime of their father Aaron.
Numbers3:5 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 “Bring the tribe of Levi near and have them stand before Aaron the priest
Numbers3:7 And they shall keep his responsibility and the responsibility for the whole congregation before the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 They shall also keep all the furnishings of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 You shall thus give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the sons of Israel.
Numbers3:10 So you shall appoint Aaron and his sons that they may keep their priesthood
Numbers3:11 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 “Now
Numbers3:13 For all the firstborn are Mine; on the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 “Number the sons of Levi by their fathers’ households
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them according to the word of Yahweh
Numbers3:17 These then are the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon and Kohath and Merari.
Numbers3:18 And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei;
Numbers3:19 and the sons of Kohath by their families: Amram and Izhar
Numbers3:20 and the sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their fathers’ households.
Numbers3:21 Of Gershon was the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimeites; these were the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Their numbered men
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle westward
Numbers3:24 and the leader of the fathers’ households of the Gershonites was Eliasaph the son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 Now the responsibility of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting involved the tabernacle and the tent
Numbers3:26 and the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 Of Kohath was the family of the Amramites and the family of the Izharites and the family of the Hebronites and the family of the Uzzielites; these were the families of the Kohathites.
Numbers3:28 In the numbering of every male from a month old and upward
Numbers3:29 The families of the sons of Kohath were to camp on the southward side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 and the leader of the fathers’ households of the Kohathite families was Elizaphan the son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Now their responsibility involved the ark
Numbers3:32 and Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest was the chief of the leaders of Levi and had the oversight of those who keep the responsibility of the sanctuary.
Numbers3:33 Of Merari was the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites; these were the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 And their numbered men
Numbers3:35 And the leader of the fathers’ households of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail. They were to camp on the northward side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 Now the appointed responsibility of the sons of Merari involved the boards of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 and the pillars around the court with their bases and their pegs and their cords.
Numbers3:38 Now those who were to camp before the tabernacle eastward
Numbers3:39 All the numbered men of the Levites
Numbers3:40 Then Yahweh said to Moses
Numbers3:41 And you shall take the Levites for Me
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered all the firstborn among the sons of Israel
Numbers3:43 and all the firstborn males by the number of names from a month old and upward
Numbers3:44 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 “Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel and the cattle of the Levites. And the Levites shall be Mine; I am Yahweh.
Numbers3:46 For the redemption price of the 273 of the firstborn of the sons of Israel who are in excess beyond the Levites
Numbers3:47 you shall take five shekels apiece
Numbers3:48 and give the money
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the money of the redemption price from those who were in excess
Numbers3:50 from the firstborn of the sons of Israel he took the money in terms of the shekel of the sanctuary
Numbers3:51 Then Moses gave the money of the redemption price to Aaron and to his sons
Numbers4:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers4:2 “Take a census of the descendants of Kohath from among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years and upward
Numbers4:4 This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting
Numbers4:5 “And when the camp sets out
Numbers4:6 and they shall put a covering of porpoise skin on it and shall spread over it a cloth of pure blue and shall insert its poles.
Numbers4:7 Over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall also spread a cloth of blue and put on it the dishes and the pans and the offering bowls and the jars for the drink offering
Numbers4:8 And they shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet material and cover the same with a covering of porpoise skin
Numbers4:9 Then they shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand for the light
Numbers4:10 and they shall put it and all its utensils in a covering of porpoise skin and shall put it on the carrying bars.
Numbers4:11 And over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth and cover it with a covering of porpoise skin and shall insert its poles;
Numbers4:12 and they shall take all the utensils of ministry
Numbers4:13 Then they shall take away the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
Numbers4:14 They shall also put on it all its utensils by which they minister in connection with it: the firepans
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the holy objects and all the furnishings of the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 “And the assignment of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest is the oil for the light and the fragrant incense and the continual grain offering and the anointing oil—the assignment of all the tabernacle and of all that is in it
Numbers4:17 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers4:18 “Do not let the tribe of the families of the Kohathites be cut off from among the Levites.
Numbers4:19 But do this to them that they may live and not die when they approach the most holy objects: Aaron and his sons shall go in and set each of them to his service and to his load;
Numbers4:20 but they shall not go in to see the holy objects even for a moment
Numbers4:21 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses

Numbers4:30 from thirty years and upward even to fifty years old
Numbers4:31 Now this is the responsibility of their loads
Numbers4:32 and the pillars around the court and their bases and their pegs and their cords
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari

Numbers5:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers5:2 “Command the sons of Israel that they send away from the camp every leper and everyone having a discharge and everyone who is unclean because of a dead person.
Numbers5:3 You shall send away both male and female; you shall send them outside the camp so that they will not defile their camp where I dwell in their midst.”
Numbers5:4 And the sons of Israel did so and sent them outside the camp; just as Yahweh had spoken to Moses
Numbers5:5 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers5:6 “Speak to the sons of Israel
Numbers5:7 then he shall confess his sins which he has committed
Numbers5:8 But if the man has no kinsman redeemer to whom restitution may be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 Also every contribution pertaining to all the holy gifts of the sons of Israel
Numbers5:10 So every man’s holy gifts shall be his; whatever any man gives to the priest
Numbers5:11 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers5:12 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Numbers5:13 and a man lies sexually with her
Numbers5:14 if a spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife and she has defiled herself
Numbers5:15 the man shall then bring his wife to the priest and shall bring as an offering for her one-tenth of an ephah of barley meal; he shall not pour oil on it nor put frankincense on it
Numbers5:16 ‘Then the priest shall bring her near and have her stand before Yahweh
Numbers5:17 and the priest shall take holy water in an earthenware vessel; and the priest shall take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.
Numbers5:18 The priest shall then have the woman stand before Yahweh and let the hair of the woman’s head go loose
Numbers5:19 Then the priest shall have her swear an oath and shall say to the woman
Numbers5:20 if you
Numbers5:21 (then the priest shall have the woman swear with the oath of the curse
Numbers5:22 and this water that brings curses shall go into your stomach and make your abdomen swell and your thigh fall away.” And the woman shall say
Numbers5:23 ‘The priest shall then write these curses on a scroll
Numbers5:24 Then he shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings curses
Numbers5:25 And the priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy from the woman’s hand
Numbers5:26 and the priest shall take a handful of the grain offering as its memorial offering and offer it up in smoke on the altar
Numbers5:27 So he will have her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean
Numbers5:29 ‘This is the law of jealousy: when a wife
Numbers5:30 or when a spirit of jealousy comes over a man and he is jealous of his wife
Numbers5:31 Moreover
Numbers6:1 Again Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Numbers6:3 he shall abstain as a Nazirite from wine and strong drink; he shall drink no vinegar
Numbers6:4 All the days of his Nazirite vow he shall not eat anything that is produced by the grape vine
Numbers6:5 ‘All the days of his vow as a Nazirite no razor shall pass over his head. He shall be holy until the days are fulfilled for which he dedicated himself as a Nazirite to Yahweh; he shall let the locks of hair on his head grow long.
Numbers6:6 ‘All the days of his dedication as a Nazirite to Yahweh he shall not go near to a dead person.
Numbers6:7 He shall not defile himself for his father or for his mother
Numbers6:8 All the days of his Nazirite vow he is holy to Yahweh.
Numbers6:9 ‘But if a man dies very suddenly beside him and he defiles the head of hair during his Nazirite vow
Numbers6:10 Then on the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest
Numbers6:11 And the priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering and make atonement for him concerning his sin because of the dead person. And that same day he shall set apart his head as holy
Numbers6:12 and shall dedicate to Yahweh the days of his Nazirite vow and shall bring a male lamb a year old for a guilt offering; but the former days will be void because his Nazirite vow was defiled.
Numbers6:13 ‘Now this is the law of the Nazirite when the days of his Nazirite vow are fulfilled: he shall bring the offering to the doorway of the tent of meeting.
Numbers6:14 And he shall bring his offering near to Yahweh: one male lamb a year old without blemish for a burnt offering and one ewe-lamb a year old without blemish for a sin offering and one ram without blemish for a peace offering
Numbers6:15 and a basket of unleavened cakes of fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil
Numbers6:16 Then the priest shall bring them near before Yahweh and shall offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 He shall also offer with the ram a sacrifice of peace offerings to Yahweh
Numbers6:18 The Nazirite shall then shave the head of hair for his Nazirite vow at the doorway of the tent of meeting and take the hair of his head of his Nazirite vow and put it on the fire which is under the sacrifice of peace offerings.
Numbers6:19 And the priest shall take the ram’s shoulder when it has been boiled
Numbers6:20 Then the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before Yahweh. It is holy for the priest
Numbers6:21 “This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to Yahweh according to his Nazirite vow
Numbers6:22 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers6:23 “Speak to Aaron and to his sons
Numbers6:24 Yahweh bless you
Numbers6:25 Yahweh make His face shine on you
Numbers6:26 Yahweh lift up His face on you
Numbers6:27 So they shall invoke My name on the sons of Israel

Numbers8:13 And you shall have the Levites stand before Aaron and before his sons so as to present them as a wave offering to Yahweh.

Numbers8:17 For every firstborn among the sons of Israel is Mine

Numbers19:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 “This is the statute of the law which Yahweh has commanded
Numbers19:3 And you shall give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 Next Eleazar the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle some of its blood toward the front of the tent of meeting seven times.
Numbers19:5 Then the heifer shall be burned in his sight; its hide and its flesh and its blood
Numbers19:6 And the priest shall take cedar wood and hyssop and scarlet material and cast it into the midst of the burning heifer.
Numbers19:7 The priest shall then wash his clothes and bathe his body in water and afterward come into the camp
Numbers19:8 The one who burns it shall also wash his clothes in water and bathe his body in water and shall be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:9 Now a man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place
Numbers19:10 And the one who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening; and it shall be a perpetual statute to the sons of Israel and to the sojourner who sojourns among them.
Numbers19:11 ‘The one who touches the corpse of any person shall be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 That one shall purify himself from uncleanness with the water on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Anyone who touches a corpse
Numbers19:14 ‘This is the law when a man dies in a tent: everyone who comes into the tent and everyone who is in the tent shall be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:15 And every open vessel
Numbers19:16 Also
Numbers19:17 Then for the unclean person they shall take some of the ashes of the burnt purification from sin and flowing water shall be added to them in a vessel.
Numbers19:18 And a clean person shall take hyssop and dip it in the water and sprinkle it on the tent and on all the furnishings and on the persons who were there and on the one who touched the bone or the one slain or the one dying naturally or the grave.
Numbers19:19 Then the clean person shall sprinkle on the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day; and on the seventh day he shall purify him from uncleanness
Numbers19:20 ‘But the man who is unclean and does not purify himself from uncleanness
Numbers19:21 So it shall be a perpetual statute for them. And he who sprinkles the water for impurity shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 Furthermore

Numbers28:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers28:2 “Command the sons of Israel and say to them

Numbers28:5 also a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering
Numbers28:6 It is a continual burnt offering which was ordained in Mount Sinai as a soothing aroma
Numbers28:7 Then the drink offering with it shall be a fourth of a hin for each lamb
Numbers28:8 And the other lamb you shall offer at twilight; as the grain offering of the morning and as its drink offering
Numbers28:9 ‘Then on the sabbath day two male lambs one year old without blemish
Numbers28:10 This is the burnt offering of every sabbath in addition to the continual burnt offering and its drink offering.
Numbers28:11 ‘Then at the beginning of each of your months you shall bring near a burnt offering to Yahweh: two bulls from the herd and one ram
Numbers28:12 and three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering

Numbers28:15 And one male goat for a sin offering to Yahweh; it shall be offered with its drink offering in addition to the continual burnt offering.

Numbers32:41 And Jair the son of Manasseh went and took its towns and called them Havvoth-jair.
Numbers32:42 And Nobah went and took Kenath and its towns and called it Nobah after his own name.
Numbers33:1 These are the journeys of the sons of Israel
Numbers33:2 And Moses wrote down their starting places according to their journeys by the command of Yahweh
Numbers33:3 They journeyed from Rameses in the first month
Numbers33:4 but the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn whom Yahweh had struck down among them. Yahweh had also executed judgments on their gods.
Numbers33:5 Then the sons of Israel journeyed from Rameses and camped in Succoth.
Numbers33:6 They journeyed from Succoth and camped in Etham
Numbers33:7 They journeyed from Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth
Numbers33:8 They journeyed from before Hahiroth and passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness; and they went three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham and camped at Marah.
Numbers33:9 They journeyed from Marah and came to Elim; and in Elim there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees
Numbers33:10 They journeyed from Elim and camped by the Red Sea.
Numbers33:11 They journeyed from the Red Sea and camped in the wilderness of Sin.
Numbers33:12 They journeyed from the wilderness of Sin and camped at Dophkah.
Numbers33:13 They journeyed from Dophkah and camped at Alush.
Numbers33:14 They journeyed from Alush and camped at Rephidim; now it was there that the people had no water to drink.
Numbers33:15 They journeyed from Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers33:16 They journeyed from the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
Numbers33:17 They journeyed from Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.
Numbers33:18 They journeyed from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.
Numbers33:19 They journeyed from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez.
Numbers33:20 They journeyed from Rimmon-perez and camped at Libnah.
Numbers33:21 They journeyed from Libnah and camped at Rissah.
Numbers33:22 They journeyed from Rissah and camped in Kehelathah.
Numbers33:23 They journeyed from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.
Numbers33:24 They journeyed from Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah.
Numbers33:25 They journeyed from Haradah and camped at Makheloth.
Numbers33:26 They journeyed from Makheloth and camped at Tahath.
Numbers33:27 They journeyed from Tahath and camped at Terah.
Numbers33:28 They journeyed from Terah and camped at Mithkah.
Numbers33:29 They journeyed from Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah.
Numbers33:30 They journeyed from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.
Numbers33:31 They journeyed from Moseroth and camped at Bene-jaakan.
Numbers33:32 They journeyed from Bene-jaakan and camped at Hor-haggidgad.
Numbers33:33 They journeyed from Hor-haggidgad and camped at Jotbathah.
Numbers33:34 They journeyed from Jotbathah and camped at Abronah.
Numbers33:35 They journeyed from Abronah and camped at Ezion-geber.
Numbers33:36 They journeyed from Ezion-geber and camped in the wilderness of Zin
Numbers33:37 They journeyed from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor
Numbers33:38 Then Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor at the command of Yahweh
Numbers33:39 Now Aaron was 123 years old when he died on Mount Hor.
Numbers33:40 Now the Canaanite
Numbers33:41 Then they journeyed from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah.
Numbers33:42 They journeyed from Zalmonah and camped at Punon.
Numbers33:43 They journeyed from Punon and camped at Oboth.
Numbers33:44 They journeyed from Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim
Numbers33:45 They journeyed from Iyim and camped at Dibon-gad.
Numbers33:46 They journeyed from Dibon-gad and camped at Almon-diblathaim.
Numbers33:47 They journeyed from Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim
Numbers33:48 They journeyed from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.
Numbers33:49 They camped by the Jordan
Numbers33:50 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho
Numbers33:51 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Numbers33:52 then you shall dispossess all the inhabitants of the land from before you and destroy all their carved stones and destroy all their molten images and demolish all their high places;
Numbers33:53 and you shall take possession of the land and inhabit it
Numbers33:54 And you shall inherit the land by lot according to your families; to the larger you shall give a larger inheritance
Numbers33:55 But if you do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land from before you
Numbers33:56 And it will be as I plan to do to them
Numbers34:1 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers34:2 “Command the sons of Israel and say to them
Numbers34:3 And your southern sector shall extend from the wilderness of Zin along the side of Edom
Numbers34:4 Then your border shall turn from the south to the ascent of Akrabbim and continue to Zin
Numbers34:5 And the border shall turn from Azmon to the brook of Egypt
Numbers34:6 ‘And as for the west border
Numbers34:7 ‘And this shall be your north border: you shall draw your border line from the Great Sea to Mount Hor.
Numbers34:8 You shall draw a line from Mount Hor to Lebo-hamath
Numbers34:9 and the border shall proceed to Ziphron
Numbers34:10 ‘For your east border you shall also draw a line from Hazar-enan to Shepham
Numbers34:11 and the border shall go down from Shepham to Riblah on the east side of Ain; and the border shall go down and reach to the slope on the east side of the Sea of Chinnereth.
Numbers34:12 And the border shall go down to the Jordan
Numbers34:13 So Moses commanded the sons of Israel
Numbers34:14 For the tribe of the sons of Reuben have received theirs according to their fathers’ households
Numbers34:15 The two and a half tribes have received their inheritance across the Jordan opposite Jericho to the east toward the sunrise.”
Numbers34:16 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers34:17 “These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land to you for inheritance: Eleazar the priest and Joshua the son of Nun.
Numbers34:18 And you shall take one leader of every tribe to apportion the land for inheritance.
Numbers34:19 Now these are the names of the men: Of the tribe of Judah
Numbers34:20 Of the tribe of the sons of Simeon
Numbers34:21 Of the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers34:22 Of the tribe of the sons of Dan a leader
Numbers34:23 Of the sons of Joseph: of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh a leader
Numbers34:24 Of the tribe of the sons of Ephraim a leader
Numbers34:25 Of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun a leader
Numbers34:26 Of the tribe of the sons of Issachar a leader
Numbers34:27 Of the tribe of the sons of Asher a leader
Numbers34:28 Of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali a leader
Numbers34:29 These are those whom Yahweh commanded to apportion the inheritance to the sons of Israel in the land of Canaan.
Numbers35:1 Now Yahweh spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho
Numbers35:2 “Command the sons of Israel that they give to the Levites from the inheritance of their possession cities to live in; and you shall give to the Levites pasture lands around the cities.
Numbers35:3 And the cities shall be theirs to live in; and their pasture lands shall be for their cattle and for their herds and for all their beasts.
Numbers35:4 “And the pasture lands of the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall extend from the wall of the city outward one thousand cubits around.
Numbers35:5 You shall also measure outside the city on the east side two thousand cubits
Numbers35:6 And the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall be the six cities of refuge
Numbers35:7 All the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall be forty-eight cities
Numbers35:8 Now as for the cities which you shall give from the possession of the sons of Israel
Numbers35:9 Then Yahweh spoke to Moses
Numbers35:10 “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them
Numbers35:11 then you shall select for yourselves cities to be your cities of refuge
Numbers35:12 And the cities shall be for you as a refuge from the avenger
Numbers35:13 And the cities which you are to give shall be your six cities of refuge.
Numbers35:14 You shall give three cities across the Jordan and three cities in the land of Canaan; they are to be cities of refuge.
Numbers35:15 These six cities shall be for refuge for the sons of Israel and for the sojourner and for the foreign resident among them; that anyone who strikes a person down unintentionally may flee there.
Numbers35:16 ‘But if he struck him down with an iron object
Numbers35:17 And if he struck him down with a stone in the hand
Numbers35:18 Or if he struck him with a wooden object in the hand
Numbers35:19 The blood avenger himself shall put the murderer to death; he shall put him to death when he meets him.
Numbers35:20 And if he pushed him of hatred or threw something at him lying in wait and as a result he died
Numbers35:21 or if he struck him down with his hand in enmity
Numbers35:22 ‘But if he pushed him suddenly without enmity or threw something at him without lying in wait
Numbers35:23 or with any stone—by which one might die
Numbers35:24 then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the blood avenger according to these legal judgments.
Numbers35:25 And the congregation shall deliver the manslayer from the hand of the blood avenger
Numbers35:26 But if the manslayer at any time goes beyond the border of his city of refuge to which he may flee
Numbers35:27 and the blood avenger finds him outside the border of his city of refuge
Numbers35:28 because he should have remained in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. But after the death of the high priest the manslayer shall return to the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 ‘And these things shall be for a statutory judgment to you throughout your generations in all your places of habitation.
Numbers35:30 ‘If anyone strikes down a person
Numbers35:31 Moreover
Numbers35:32 And you shall not take ransom for him who has fled to his city of refuge
Numbers35:33 So you shall not pollute the land in which you are; for blood pollutes the land
Numbers35:34 And you shall not defile the land in which you live

Numbers36:2 and they said
Numbers36:3 But if they marry one of the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel

Numbers36:7 Thus no inheritance of the sons of Israel shall be transferred from tribe to tribe
Numbers36:8 And every daughter who comes into possession of an inheritance of any tribe of the sons of Israel shall be wife to one of the family of the tribe of her father
Numbers36:9 Thus no inheritance shall be transferred from one tribe to another tribe
Numbers36:10 Just as Yahweh had commanded Moses
Numbers36:11 And Mahlah

Numbers36:13 These are the commandments and the judgments which Yahweh commanded to the sons of Israel by the hand of Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.

Deuteronomy5:6 ‘I am Yahweh your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy5:7 ‘You shall have no other gods before Me.
Deuteronomy5:8 ‘You shall not make for yourself an idol—any likeness of what is in heaven above or on the earth beneath or in the water under the earth.
Deuteronomy5:9 You shall not worship them or serve them; for I
Deuteronomy5:10 but showing lovingkindness to thousands
Deuteronomy5:11 ‘You shall not take the name of Yahweh your God in vain
Deuteronomy5:12 ‘Observe the sabbath day to keep it holy
Deuteronomy5:13 Six days you shall labor and do all your work
Deuteronomy5:14 but the seventh day is a sabbath of Yahweh your God; in it you shall not do any work
Deuteronomy5:15 You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy5:16 ‘Honor your father and your mother
Deuteronomy5:17 ‘You shall not murder.
Deuteronomy5:18 ‘You shall not commit adultery.
Deuteronomy5:19 ‘You shall not steal.
Deuteronomy5:20 ‘You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
Deuteronomy5:21 ‘You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife

Deuteronomy5:26 For who is there of all flesh who has heard the voice of the living God speaking from the midst of the fire

Deuteronomy5:30 Go
Deuteronomy5:31 But as for you

Deuteronomy7:1 “When Yahweh your God brings you into the land where you are entering to possess it
Deuteronomy7:2 and when Yahweh your God gives them over before you and you strike them down

Deuteronomy7:6 For you are a holy people to Yahweh your God; Yahweh your God has chosen you to be a people for His own treasured possession out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.
Deuteronomy7:7 “Yahweh did not set His affection on you nor choose you because you were more in number than any of the peoples
Deuteronomy7:8 but because Yahweh loved you and kept the oath which He swore to your fathers
Deuteronomy7:9 You shall know therefore that Yahweh your God
Deuteronomy7:10 but repays those who hate Him to their faces
Deuteronomy7:11 Therefore
Deuteronomy7:12 “Then it will be
Deuteronomy7:13 And He will love you and bless you and multiply you; He will also bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground
Deuteronomy7:14 You shall be blessed above all peoples; there will be no male or female barren among you or among your cattle.
Deuteronomy7:15 And Yahweh will take away from you all sickness; and He will not put on you any of the harmful diseases of Egypt which you have known
Deuteronomy7:16 And you shall consume all the peoples whom Yahweh your God will give over to you; your eye shall not pity them
Deuteronomy7:17 “If you should say in your heart
Deuteronomy7:18 you shall not be afraid of them; you shall well remember what Yahweh your God did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt:
Deuteronomy7:19 the great trials which your eyes saw and the signs and the wonders and the mighty hand and the outstretched arm by which Yahweh your God brought you out. So shall Yahweh your God do to all the peoples of whom you are afraid.
Deuteronomy7:20 Moreover
Deuteronomy7:21 You shall not dread them
Deuteronomy7:22 And Yahweh your God will clear away these nations before you little by little; you will not be able to put an end to them quickly
Deuteronomy7:23 But Yahweh your God will give them over before you and will throw them into great confusion until they are destroyed.
Deuteronomy7:24 And He will give their kings into your hand so that you will make their name perish from under heaven; no man will be able to stand before you until you have destroyed them.
Deuteronomy7:25 The graven images of their gods you are to burn with fire; you shall not covet the silver or the gold that is on them
Deuteronomy7:26 And you shall not bring an abomination into your house and become devoted to destruction like it; you shall utterly detest it
Deuteronomy8:1 “The entire commandment that I am commanding you today you shall be careful to do
Deuteronomy8:2 And you shall remember all the way which Yahweh your God has led you in the wilderness these forty years
Deuteronomy8:3 And He humbled you and let you be hungry and fed you with manna which you did not know
Deuteronomy8:4 Your clothing did not wear out on you
Deuteronomy8:5 Thus you shall know in your heart that Yahweh your God was disciplining you just as a man disciplines his son.
Deuteronomy8:6 So you shall keep the commandments of Yahweh your God
Deuteronomy8:7 For Yahweh your God is bringing you into a good land
Deuteronomy8:8 a land of wheat and barley
Deuteronomy8:9 a land where you will eat food without scarcity
Deuteronomy8:10 And so you will eat and be satisfied

Deuteronomy9:12 Then Yahweh said to me
Deuteronomy9:13 Yahweh spoke further to me
Deuteronomy9:14 Let Me alone

Deuteronomy9:16 And I saw that you had indeed sinned against Yahweh your God. You had made for yourselves a molten calf; you had turned aside quickly from the way which Yahweh had commanded you.

Deuteronomy9:19 For I was afraid of the anger and the wrath with which Yahweh was provoked against you in order to destroy you

Deuteronomy9:24 You have been rebellious against Yahweh from the day I knew you.

Deuteronomy13:1 “If a prophet or a dreamer of dreams arises among you and gives you a sign or a wonder
Deuteronomy13:2 and the sign or the wonder comes true
Deuteronomy13:3 you shall not listen to the words of that prophet or that dreamer of dreams; for Yahweh your God is testing you to find out if you love Yahweh your God with all your heart and with all your soul.
Deuteronomy13:4 You shall walk after Yahweh your God and fear Him; and you shall keep His commandments
Deuteronomy13:5 But that prophet or that dreamer of dreams shall be put to death because he has counseled rebellion against Yahweh your God who brought you from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slavery
Deuteronomy13:6 “If your brother
Deuteronomy13:7 of the gods of the peoples who are around you

Deuteronomy13:9 But you shall surely kill him; your hand shall be first against him to put him to death
Deuteronomy13:10 So you shall stone him to death because he has sought to drive you from Yahweh your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy13:11 Thus all Israel will hear and be afraid and will never again do such an evil thing among you.
Deuteronomy13:12 “If you hear in one of your cities
Deuteronomy13:13 some vile men have gone out from among you and have driven the inhabitants of their city astray
Deuteronomy13:14 then you shall inquire and search out and ask thoroughly. Behold
Deuteronomy13:15 you shall surely strike the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword
Deuteronomy13:16 Then you shall gather all its spoil into the middle of its open square and burn the city and all its spoil with fire as a whole burnt offering to Yahweh your God; and it shall be a ruin forever. It shall never be rebuilt.
