Deuteronomy13:18 if you will listen to the voice of Yahweh your God
Deuteronomy14:1 “You are the sons of Yahweh your God; you shall not gash yourselves nor shave your forehead for the sake of the dead.
Deuteronomy14:2 For you are a holy people to Yahweh your God
Deuteronomy14:3 “You shall not eat any abominable thing.
Deuteronomy14:4 These are the animals which you may eat: the ox
Deuteronomy14:5 the deer
Deuteronomy14:6 And any animal that divides the hoof and has the hoof split in two and chews the cud
Deuteronomy14:7 Nevertheless
Deuteronomy14:8 And the pig
Deuteronomy14:9 “These you may eat of all that are in water: anything that has fins and scales you may eat
Deuteronomy14:10 but anything that does not have fins and scales you shall not eat; it is unclean for you.
Deuteronomy14:11 “You may eat any clean bird.
Deuteronomy14:12 But these are the ones which you shall not eat: the eagle and the vulture and the buzzard
Deuteronomy14:13 and the red kite
Deuteronomy14:14 and every raven in its kind
Deuteronomy14:15 and the ostrich
Deuteronomy14:16 the little owl
Deuteronomy14:17 the pelican
Deuteronomy14:18 the stork
Deuteronomy14:19 And all the teeming life with wings are unclean to you; they shall not be eaten.
Deuteronomy14:20 You may eat any clean bird.
Deuteronomy14:21 “You shall not eat anything which dies of itself. You may give it to the sojourner who is within your gates
Deuteronomy14:22 “You shall surely tithe all the produce from what you sow
Deuteronomy14:23 And you shall eat in the presence of Yahweh your God
Deuteronomy14:24 And if the distance is so great for you that you are not able to bring the tithe
Deuteronomy14:25 then you shall exchange it for money and bind the money in your hand and go to the place which Yahweh your God chooses.
Deuteronomy14:26 And you may spend the money for whatever your heart desires: for oxen or sheep or wine or strong drink or whatever your heart desires; and there you shall eat in the presence of Yahweh your God and be glad
Deuteronomy14:27 Also you shall not forsake the Levite who is within your gates

Deuteronomy15:1 “At the end of every seven years you shall grant a remission of debts.
Deuteronomy15:2 And this is the manner of remission: every creditor shall release what he has loaned to his neighbor; he shall not exact it of his neighbor and his brother because the remission from Yahweh has been proclaimed.
Deuteronomy15:3 From a foreigner you may exact it
Deuteronomy15:4 However
Deuteronomy15:5 if only you listen obediently to the voice of Yahweh your God
Deuteronomy15:6 For Yahweh your God will bless you as He has promised you
Deuteronomy15:7 “If there is a needy one among you
Deuteronomy15:8 but you shall freely open your hand to him and shall generously lend him sufficient for his need in whatever he lacks.
Deuteronomy15:9 Beware lest there be a vile thought in your heart
Deuteronomy15:10 You shall generously give to him
Deuteronomy15:11 For the needy will never cease to be in the land; therefore I am commanding you
Deuteronomy15:12 “If your brother
Deuteronomy15:13 When you let him go to be free from you

Deuteronomy15:15 And you shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy15:16 And it will be that
Deuteronomy15:17 then you shall take an awl and pierce it through his ear into the door
Deuteronomy15:18 “It shall not seem hard in your sight when you let him go to be free from you

Deuteronomy15:20 You and your household shall eat it every year before Yahweh your God in the place which Yahweh chooses.
Deuteronomy15:21 But if it has any defect
Deuteronomy15:22 You shall eat it within your gates; the unclean and the clean alike may eat it
Deuteronomy15:23 Only you shall not eat its blood; you are to pour it out on the ground like water.

Deuteronomy19:14 “You shall not move your neighbor’s boundary mark

Deuteronomy19:16 If a malicious witness rises up against a man to accuse him of wrongdoing
Deuteronomy19:17 then both the men who have the dispute shall stand before Yahweh
Deuteronomy19:18 And the judges shall inquire thoroughly
Deuteronomy19:19 then you shall do to him just as he had intended to do to his brother. Thus you shall purge the evil from among you.
Deuteronomy19:20 And the rest will hear and be afraid and will never again do such an evil thing among you.
Deuteronomy19:21 Thus your eye shall not show pity: life for life

Deuteronomy34:1 Then Moses went up from the plains of Moab to Mount Nebo
Deuteronomy34:2 and all Naphtali and the land of Ephraim and Manasseh
Deuteronomy34:3 and the Negev and the plain in the valley of Jericho
Deuteronomy34:4 Then Yahweh said to him
Deuteronomy34:5 So Moses the servant of Yahweh died there in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy34:6 And He buried him in the valley in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy34:7 Now Moses was 120 years old when he died; his eye was not dim
Deuteronomy34:8 So the sons of Israel wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days. Then the days of weeping and mourning for Moses came to an end.
Deuteronomy34:9 Now Joshua the son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom
Deuteronomy34:10 And there has not yet arisen a prophet in Israel like Moses
Deuteronomy34:11 in regard to all the signs and wonders which Yahweh sent him to do in the land of Egypt against Pharaoh
Deuteronomy34:12 and in regard to all the mighty power and in regard to all the great terror which Moses did in the sight of all Israel.
Joshua1:1 Now it came about after the death of Moses the servant of Yahweh
Joshua1:2 “Moses My servant is dead; so now arise
Joshua1:3 Every place on which the sole of your foot treads
Joshua1:4 From the wilderness and this Lebanon
Joshua1:5 No man will be able to stand before you all the days of your life. Just as I have been with Moses
Joshua1:6 Be strong and courageous
Joshua1:7 Only be strong and very courageous to be careful to do according to all the law which Moses My servant commanded you; do not turn aside from it to the right or to the left
Joshua1:8 This book of the law shall not depart from your mouth
Joshua1:9 Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous! Do not be in dread or be dismayed
Joshua1:10 Then Joshua commanded the officers of the people
Joshua1:11 “Pass through the midst of the camp and command the people
Joshua1:12 To the Reubenites and to the Gadites and to the half-tribe of Manasseh

Joshua1:16 And they answered Joshua
Joshua1:17 Just as we listened to Moses in all things

Joshua2:1 Then Joshua the son of Nun sent two men as spies secretly from Shittim
Joshua2:2 And it was told to the king of Jericho

Joshua2:4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them
Joshua2:5 Now it happened when it was time to shut the gate at dark
Joshua2:6 But she had brought them up to the roof and concealed them in the stalks of flax which she had laid in order on the roof.
Joshua2:7 So the men pursued them on the road to the Jordan to the fords; and as soon as those who were pursuing them had gone out
Joshua2:8 Now before they lay down
Joshua2:9 and said to the men

Joshua2:12 So now
Joshua2:13 and preserve my father and my mother and my brothers and my sisters alive

Joshua2:20 But if you tell this business of ours
Joshua2:21 And she said
Joshua2:22 And they went and came to the hill country and remained there for three days until the pursuers returned. The pursuers had sought them all along the road but had not found them.

Joshua6:10 But Joshua commanded the people
Joshua6:11 So he had the ark of Yahweh taken around the city
Joshua6:12 Then Joshua rose early in the morning
Joshua6:13 And the seven priests carrying the seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark of Yahweh went on continually and blew the trumpets; and the armed men went before them
Joshua6:14 Thus the second day they marched around the city once and returned to the camp; they did so for six days.
Joshua6:15 Then it happened that on the seventh day they rose early at the breaking of dawn and marched around the city in the same manner seven times; only on that day they marched around the city seven times.
Joshua6:16 Now it happened that on the seventh time
Joshua6:17 And the city shall be devoted to destruction
Joshua6:18 But as for you
Joshua6:19 But all the silver and gold and articles of bronze and iron are holy to Yahweh; they shall go into the treasury of Yahweh.”

Joshua8:2 So you shall do to Ai and its king just as you did to Jericho and its king; you shall take only its spoil and its cattle as plunder for yourselves. Set an ambush for the city behind it.”
Joshua8:3 So Joshua rose with all the people of war to go up to Ai; and Joshua chose 30
Joshua8:4 And he commanded them
Joshua8:5 Then I and all the people who are with me will come near the city. And it will be that when they come out to meet us as at the first
Joshua8:6 And they will come out after us until we have drawn them away from the city
Joshua8:7 And you shall rise from your ambush and take possession of the city
Joshua8:8 Then it will be that when you have seized the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them away
Joshua8:10 Now Joshua rose early in the morning and mustered the people
Joshua8:11 Then all the people of war who were with him went up and drew near and arrived in front of the city and camped on the north side of Ai. Now there was a valley between him and Ai.
Joshua8:12 And he took about 5
Joshua8:13 So they set the people
Joshua8:14 Now it happened that when the king of Ai saw it
Joshua8:15 And Joshua and all Israel pretended to be beaten before them and fled by the way of the wilderness.
Joshua8:16 And all the people who were in the city were called together to pursue them
Joshua8:17 So not a man remained in Ai or Bethel who had not gone out after Israel
Joshua8:18 Then Yahweh said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 Then the men in ambush rose quickly from their place
Joshua8:20 Then the men of Ai turned back and looked
Joshua8:21 But Joshua and all Israel saw that the men in ambush had captured the city and that the smoke of the city ascended. So they turned back and struck down the men of Ai.
Joshua8:22 And the others came out from the city to meet them
Joshua8:23 But they seized the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 Now it happened that when Israel had finished killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the field in the wilderness where they pursued them
Joshua8:25 So all who fell that day
Joshua8:26 For Joshua did not withdraw his hand with which he stretched out the javelin until he had devoted to destruction all the inhabitants of Ai.
Joshua8:27 Israel took only the cattle and the spoil of that city as plunder for themselves
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a heap forever
Joshua8:29 And he hanged the king of Ai on a tree until evening; and at sunset Joshua gave a command
Joshua8:30 Then Joshua built an altar to Yahweh
Joshua8:31 just as Moses the servant of Yahweh had commanded the sons of Israel
Joshua8:32 And he wrote there on the stones a copy of the law of Moses

Joshua8:34 Then afterward he read all the words of the law
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded which Joshua did not read before all the assembly of Israel with the women and the little ones and the sojourners who were going among them.

Joshua10:29 Then Joshua and all Israel with him passed on from Makkedah to Libnah and fought against Libnah.

Joshua10:31 And Joshua and all Israel with him passed on from Libnah to Lachish
Joshua10:32 And Yahweh gave Lachish into the hands of Israel; and he captured it on the second day and struck it and every person who was in it with the edge of the sword
Joshua10:33 Then Horam king of Gezer came up to help Lachish
Joshua10:34 And Joshua and all Israel with him passed on from Lachish to Eglon
Joshua10:35 And they captured it on that day and struck it with the edge of the sword; and every person who was in it
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua and all Israel with him went up from Eglon to Hebron
Joshua10:37 And they captured it and struck it and its king and all its cities and all the persons who were in it with the edge of the sword. He left no survivor remaining
Joshua10:38 Then Joshua and all Israel with him returned to Debir
Joshua10:39 And he captured it and its king and all its cities

Joshua10:42 And Joshua captured all these kings and their lands at one time because Yahweh
Joshua10:43 So Joshua and all Israel with him returned to the camp at Gilgal.

Joshua14:1 Now these are the territories which the sons of Israel inherited in the land of Canaan
Joshua14:2 by the lot of their inheritance
Joshua14:3 For Moses had given the inheritance of the two tribes and the half-tribe beyond the Jordan; but he did not give an inheritance to the Levites among them.
Joshua14:4 For the sons of Joseph were two tribes
Joshua14:5 Thus the sons of Israel did just as Yahweh had commanded Moses
Joshua14:6 Then the sons of Judah drew near to Joshua in Gilgal

Joshua14:8 Nevertheless
Joshua14:9 So Moses swore on that day
Joshua14:10 So now behold
Joshua14:11 I am still as strong today as I was in the day Moses sent me; as my strength was then
Joshua14:12 So now

Joshua14:14 Therefore
Joshua14:15 Now the name of Hebron was formerly Kiriath-arba; for Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim. Then the land was quiet from war.
Joshua15:1 Now the lot for the tribe of the sons of Judah according to their families reached the border of Edom
Joshua15:2 And their south border was from the lower end of the Salt Sea
Joshua15:3 Then it went out southward to the ascent of Akrabbim and passed on to Zin and went up by the south of Kadesh-barnea and continued to Hezron and went up to Addar and turned about to Karka.
Joshua15:4 And it passed on to Azmon and went out to the brook of Egypt

Judges1:9 And afterward the sons of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanites living in the hill country and in the Negev and in the Shephelah.
Judges1:10 So Judah went against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron (now the name of Hebron formerly was Kiriath-arba); and they struck Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai.

Judges1:22 Likewise the house of Joseph went up against Bethel
Judges1:23 And the house of Joseph spied out Bethel (now the name of the city was formerly Luz).

Judges1:25 So he showed them the entrance to the city
Judges1:26 So the man went into the land of the Hittites and built a city and named it Luz which is its name to this day.
Judges1:27 But Manasseh did not take possession of Beth-shean and its towns or Taanach and its towns or the inhabitants of Dor and its towns or the inhabitants of Ibleam and its towns or the inhabitants of Megiddo and its towns; so the Canaanites persisted to live in that land.
Judges1:28 And it happened when Israel became strong
Judges1:29 Also Ephraim did not dispossess the Canaanites who were living in Gezer; so the Canaanites lived in Gezer among them.

Judges1:31 Asher did not dispossess the inhabitants of Acco or the inhabitants of Sidon
Judges1:32 So the Asherites lived among the Canaanites

Judges1:35 and the Amorites persisted in living in Mount Heres
Judges1:36 Now the border of the Amorites ran from the ascent of Akrabbim

1Samuel5:3 Then the Ashdodites arose early the next morning
1Samuel5:4 But they arose early the next morning
1Samuel5:5 Therefore neither the priests of Dagon nor all who enter Dagon’s house tread on the threshold of Dagon in Ashdod to this day.
1Samuel5:6 Now the hand of Yahweh was glorious against the Ashdodites
1Samuel5:7 Thus the men of Ashdod saw that it was so and said
1Samuel5:8 So they sent and gathered all the lords of the Philistines to them and said
1Samuel5:9 Now it happened after they had brought it around
1Samuel5:10 So they sent the ark of God to Ekron. And as the ark of God came to Ekron the Ekronites cried out
1Samuel5:11 They sent therefore and gathered all the lords of the Philistines and said
1Samuel5:12 Now the men who did not die were struck with tumors and the cry of the city went up to heaven.
1Samuel6:1 Now the ark of Yahweh had been in the fields of the Philistines seven months.
1Samuel6:2 And the Philistines called for the priests and the diviners
1Samuel6:3 So they said
1Samuel6:4 Then they said
1Samuel6:5 So you shall make likenesses of your tumors and likenesses of your mice that bring the land to ruin
1Samuel6:6 Why then do you harden your hearts as the Egyptians and Pharaoh hardened their hearts? When He had abused them
1Samuel6:7 So now
1Samuel6:8 And take the ark of Yahweh and place it on the cart; and put the articles of gold which you return to Him as a guilt offering in a box by its side. Then send it away that it may go.
1Samuel6:9 See
1Samuel6:10 Then the men did so and took two milch cows and hitched them to the cart and shut up their calves at home.
1Samuel6:11 And they put the ark of Yahweh on the cart
1Samuel6:12 And the cows took the straight way in the direction of Beth-shemesh; they went along the highway
1Samuel6:13 Now the people of Beth-shemesh were reaping their wheat harvest in the valley
1Samuel6:14 And the cart came into the field of Joshua the Beth-shemite and stood there
1Samuel6:15 And the Levites took down the ark of Yahweh and the box that was with it
1Samuel6:16 So the five lords of the Philistines saw it and returned to Ekron that day.
1Samuel6:17 Now these are the golden tumors which the Philistines returned for a guilt offering to Yahweh: one for Ashdod
1Samuel6:18 and the golden mice

1Samuel6:20 And the men of Beth-shemesh said

1Samuel7:1 And the men of Kiriath-jearim came and brought the ark of Yahweh up and brought it into the house of Abinadab on the hill and set apart Eleazar his son as holy in order to keep the ark of Yahweh.
1Samuel7:2 Now it happened from the day when the ark remained at Kiriath-jearim
1Samuel7:3 Then Samuel spoke to all the house of Israel
1Samuel7:4 So the sons of Israel removed the Baals and the Ashtaroth and served Yahweh alone.
1Samuel7:5 Then Samuel said
1Samuel7:6 And they gathered to Mizpah and drew water and poured it out before Yahweh and fasted on that day and said there
1Samuel7:7 Then the Philistines heard that the sons of Israel had gathered to Mizpah. And the lords of the Philistines went up against Israel. Then the sons of Israel heard it and were afraid of the Philistines.
1Samuel7:8 So the sons of Israel said to Samuel
1Samuel7:9 And Samuel took a suckling lamb and offered it for a whole burnt offering to Yahweh; and Samuel cried out to Yahweh for Israel
1Samuel7:10 Now Samuel was offering up the burnt offering
1Samuel7:11 So the men of Israel went out of Mizpah and pursued the Philistines and struck them down as far as below Beth-car.
1Samuel7:12 Then Samuel took a stone and set it between Mizpah and Shen
1Samuel7:13 So the Philistines were subdued
1Samuel7:14 And the cities which the Philistines had taken from Israel were restored to Israel

1Samuel8:10 So Samuel spoke all the words of Yahweh to the people who had asked of him a king.

1Samuel8:15 He will take a tenth of your seed and of your vineyards and give it to his officers and to his servants.
1Samuel8:16 He will also take your male slaves and your female slaves and your best young men and your donkeys and use them for his work.
1Samuel8:17 He will take a tenth of your flocks
1Samuel8:18 Then you will cry out in that day because of your king whom you have chosen for yourselves
1Samuel8:19 Nevertheless
1Samuel8:20 that we also may be like all the nations

2Samuel22:1 And David spoke to Yahweh the words of this song in the day that Yahweh delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 He said
2Samuel22:3 My God
2Samuel22:4 I call upon Yahweh
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death encompassed me; The torrents of vileness terrorized me;
2Samuel22:6 The cords of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon Yahweh
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth shook and quaked; The foundations of heaven were trembling And were shaken
2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up out of His nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heavens and came down With thick darkness under His feet.
2Samuel22:11 He rode on a cherub and flew; And He appeared upon the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 And He made darkness canopies around Him
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness before Him Coals of fire were kindled.
2Samuel22:14 Yahweh thundered from heaven
2Samuel22:15 And He sent out arrows
2Samuel22:16 Then the channels of the sea appeared
2Samuel22:17 He sent from on high
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my disaster
2Samuel22:20 He brought me forth also into a broad place; He rescued me
2Samuel22:21 Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of Yahweh
2Samuel22:23 For all His judgments were before me
2Samuel22:24 I was also blameless toward Him
2Samuel22:25 Therefore Yahweh has recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the kind You show Yourself kind
2Samuel22:27 With the pure You show Yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 And You save an afflicted people; But Your eyes are on the haughty whom You bring down.
2Samuel22:29 For You are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For by You I can run upon a troop; By my God I can leap over a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong fortress; And He sets the blameless in His way.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like hinds’ feet
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for battle
2Samuel22:36 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation
2Samuel22:37 You enlarge my steps under me
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them
2Samuel22:39 And I have consumed them and crushed them
2Samuel22:40 For You have girded me with strength for battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
2Samuel22:41 You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 Then I beat them fine as the dust of the earth; I pulverized and stamped them as the mire of the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You have also delivered me from the contentions of my people; You have kept me as head of the nations; A people whom I have not known serve me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners cower before me; As soon as they hear
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners fade away
2Samuel22:47 Yahweh lives
2Samuel22:48 The God who executes vengeance for me
2Samuel22:49 Who also brings me out from my enemies; You even lift me above those who rise up against me; You rescue me from the violent man.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will give thanks to You
2Samuel22:51 He gives great salvation to His king

1Kings5:1 Then Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon
1Kings5:2 And Solomon sent word to Hiram
1Kings5:3 “You know that David my father was unable to build a house for the name of Yahweh his God because of the wars which surrounded him
1Kings5:4 But now Yahweh my God has given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary nor calamity.
1Kings5:5 So behold
1Kings5:6 So now
1Kings5:7 Now it happened
1Kings5:8 So Hiram sent word to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants will bring them down from Lebanon to the sea; and I will make them into rafts to go by sea to the place where you direct me
1Kings5:10 So Hiram gave Solomon as much as he desired of the cedar and cypress timber.
1Kings5:11 Solomon then gave Hiram 20
1Kings5:12 Now Yahweh gave wisdom to Solomon
1Kings5:13 And King Solomon raised up forced laborers from all Israel; and the forced laborers numbered 30
1Kings5:14 And he sent them to Lebanon
1Kings5:15 And Solomon had 70
1Kings5:16 besides Solomon’s 3
1Kings5:17 Then the king commanded
1Kings5:18 So Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders and the Gebalites carved them out
1Kings6:1 Now it happened in the four hundred and eightieth year after the sons of Israel came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 As for the house which King Solomon built for Yahweh
1Kings6:3 And the porch in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:4 Also for the house he made windows with artistic frames.
1Kings6:5 And he built
1Kings6:6 The lowest story was five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 The house
1Kings6:8 The doorway for the lowest side chamber was on the right side of the house; and they would go up by winding stairs to the middle story
1Kings6:9 So he built the house and completed it; and he paneled the house with beams and planks of cedar.
1Kings6:10 He also built the stories against the whole house
1Kings6:11 And the word of Yahweh came to Solomon saying
1Kings6:12 “Concerning this house which you are building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the sons of Israel
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house and completed it.
1Kings6:15 Then he built the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar; from the floor of the house to the ceiling he overlaid the walls on the inside with wood
1Kings6:16 And he built twenty cubits on the rear part of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the ceiling; he built them for it on the inside as an inner sanctuary
1Kings6:17 And the house
1Kings6:18 And there was cedar on the house within
1Kings6:19 Then he prepared an inner sanctuary within the house in order to place there the ark of the covenant of Yahweh.
1Kings6:20 Now the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold. And he drew chains of gold across the front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:22 So he overlaid the whole house with gold
1Kings6:23 Also in the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood
1Kings6:24 And five cubits was the one wing of the cherub and five cubits the other wing of the cherub; from the end of one wing to the end of the other wing were ten cubits.
1Kings6:25 The other cherub was also ten cubits; both the cherubim were of the same measure and the same form.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 And he put the cherubim in the midst of the inner house
1Kings6:28 He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 Then he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved engravings of cherubim
1Kings6:30 And he overlaid the floor of the house with gold
1Kings6:31 For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood
1Kings6:32 So he made two doors of olive wood
1Kings6:33 So also he made for the entrance of the nave four-sided doorposts of olive wood
1Kings6:34 and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door turned on pivots
1Kings6:35 And he carved on it cherubim
1Kings6:36 And he built the inner court with three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the house of Yahweh was laid
1Kings6:38 Now in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 Now Solomon built his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 And he built the house of the forest of Lebanon; its length was 100 cubits and its width 50 cubits and its height 30 cubits
1Kings7:3 And it was paneled with cedar above the side chambers which were on the 45 pillars
1Kings7:4 Now there were artistic window frames in three rows
1Kings7:5 And all the doorways and doorposts had squared artistic frames
1Kings7:6 Then he made the hall of pillars; its length was 50 cubits and its width 30 cubits
1Kings7:7 And he made the hall of the throne where he was to judge
1Kings7:8 Now his house where he was to live
1Kings7:9 All these were of precious stones
1Kings7:10 And the foundation was of precious stones
1Kings7:11 And above were precious stones
1Kings7:12 So the great court all around had three rows of cut stone and a row of cedar beams even as the inner court of the house of Yahweh
1Kings7:13 Then King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was a widow’s son from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 And he fashioned the two pillars of bronze; eighteen cubits was the height of one pillar
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of molten bronze to set on the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
1Kings7:17 There were nets of network and twisted threads of chainwork for the capitals which were on the top of the pillars; seven for the one capital and seven for the other capital.
1Kings7:18 So he made the pillars
1Kings7:19 Now the capitals which were on the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily design
1Kings7:20 And there were capitals on the two pillars
1Kings7:21 Thus he set up the pillars at the porch of the nave; and he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin
1Kings7:22 And on the top of the pillars was lily design. So the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 And he made the sea of cast metal ten cubits from brim to brim
1Kings7:24 Now under its brim gourds went around encircling it ten to a cubit
1Kings7:25 It stood on twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 And it was a handbreadth thick
1Kings7:27 Then he made the ten stands of bronze; the length of each stand was four cubits and its width four cubits and its height three cubits.
1Kings7:28 Now this was the workmanship of the stands: they had borders
1Kings7:29 and on the borders which were between the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Now each stand had four bronze wheels with bronze axles
1Kings7:31 And its opening inside the capital at the top was a cubit
1Kings7:32 And the four wheels were underneath the borders
1Kings7:33 Now the workmanship of the wheels was like the workmanship of a chariot wheel. Their axles
1Kings7:34 Now there were four supports at the four corners of each stand; its supports were part of the stand itself.
1Kings7:35 And on the top of the stand there was a circular form half a cubit high
1Kings7:36 And he engraved on the plates of its stays and on its borders
1Kings7:37 He made the ten stands like this: all of them had one casting
1Kings7:38 He also made ten lavers of bronze
1Kings7:39 Then he set the stands
1Kings7:40 And Hiram made the lavers and the shovels and the bowls. So Hiram completed doing all the work that he did for King Solomon in the house of Yahweh:
1Kings7:41 the two pillars and the two bowls of the capitals which were on the top of the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks
1Kings7:43 and the ten stands with the ten lavers on the stands;
1Kings7:44 and the one sea and the twelve oxen under the sea;
1Kings7:45 and the pots and the shovels and the bowls; and all these utensils which Hiram made for King Solomon in the house of Yahweh were of polished bronze.
1Kings7:46 On the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
1Kings7:47 And Solomon left all the utensils unweighed
1Kings7:48 Solomon also made all the furniture which was in the house of Yahweh: the golden altar and the golden table on which was the bread of the Presence;
1Kings7:49 and the lampstands
1Kings7:50 and the cups and the snuffers and the bowls and the spoons and the firepans
1Kings7:51 Thus all the work that King Solomon did in the house of Yahweh was finished. And Solomon brought in the things set apart as holy by his father David
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel assembled themselves to King Solomon at the feast
1Kings8:3 Then all the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 And they brought up the ark of Yahweh and the tent of meeting and all the holy utensils
1Kings8:5 And King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel
1Kings8:6 Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of Yahweh to its place
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread their wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 But the poles were so long that the ends of the poles could be seen from the holy place before the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone which Moses laid there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 Now it happened that when the priests came out of the holy place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said
1Kings8:13 I have surely built You a lofty house
1Kings8:14 Then the king turned his face around and blessed all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:15 And he said
1Kings8:16 ‘Since the day that I brought My people Israel from Egypt
1Kings8:17 And it was in the heart of my father David to build a house for the name of Yahweh
1Kings8:18 But Yahweh said to my father David
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless you shall not build the house
1Kings8:20 And Yahweh has established His word which He spoke; and I have been established in place of my father David and sit on the throne of Israel
1Kings8:21 And there I have set a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of Yahweh before all the assembly of Israel and spread out his hands toward heaven.
1Kings8:23 And he said
1Kings8:24 who have kept with Your servant
1Kings8:25 So now
1Kings8:26 So now
1Kings8:27 “But will God truly dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have regard to the prayer of Your slave and to his supplication
1Kings8:29 that Your eyes may be open toward this house night and day
1Kings8:30 And listen to the supplication of Your slave and of Your people Israel
1Kings8:31 “If a man sins against his neighbor and is made to take an oath
1Kings8:32 then listen in heaven and act and judge Your slaves
1Kings8:33 “When Your people Israel are defeated before an enemy
1Kings8:34 then listen in heaven
1Kings8:35 “When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain
1Kings8:36 then listen in heaven and forgive the sin of Your slaves and of Your people Israel; indeed
1Kings8:37 “If there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer or supplication is made by any man or by all Your people Israel
1Kings8:39 then listen in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:40 that they may fear You all the days that they live upon the face of the land which You have given to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 “Also concerning the foreigner who is not of Your people Israel
1Kings8:42 (for they will hear of Your great name and Your strong hand
1Kings8:43 listen in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:44 “When Your people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then listen in heaven to their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 “When they sin against You (for there is no man who does not sin) and You are angry with them and give them over to an enemy
1Kings8:47 and if they cause these things to return to their heart in the land where they have been taken captive
1Kings8:48 and if they return to You with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies who have taken them captive
1Kings8:49 then listen in heaven Your dwelling place to their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:50 and forgive Your people who have sinned against You and all their transgressions which they have transgressed against You
1Kings8:51 (for they are Your people and Your inheritance which You have brought forth from Egypt
1Kings8:52 that Your eyes may be open to the supplication of Your slave and to the supplication of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You have separated them from all the peoples of the earth as Your inheritance
1Kings8:54 Now it happened that when Solomon had finished praying this entire prayer and supplication to Yahweh
1Kings8:55 And he stood and blessed all the assembly of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be Yahweh
1Kings8:57 May Yahweh our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that He may incline our hearts to Himself
1Kings8:59 And may these words of mine
1Kings8:60 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that Yahweh is God; there is no one else.
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be wholly devoted to Yahweh our God
1Kings8:62 Now the king and all Israel with him were offering sacrifices before Yahweh.
1Kings8:63 And Solomon offered for the sacrifice of peace offerings
1Kings8:64 On the same day the king set apart as holy the middle of the court that was before the house of Yahweh
1Kings8:65 So Solomon celebrated the feast at that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away and they blessed the king. Then they went to their tents with gladness and goodness of heart because of all the goodness that Yahweh had shown to David His servant and to Israel His people.
1Kings9:1 Now it happened when Solomon had completed building the house of Yahweh
1Kings9:2 that Yahweh appeared to Solomon a second time
1Kings9:3 And Yahweh said to him
1Kings9:4 As for you
1Kings9:5 then I will establish the throne of your kingdom over Israel forever
1Kings9:6 “But if you or your sons indeed turn away from following Me
1Kings9:7 then I will cut off Israel from the land which I have given them
1Kings9:8 And this house will become a heap of ruins; everyone who passes by will feel desolate and hiss and say
1Kings9:9 And they will say
1Kings9:10 Now it happened that at the end of twenty years in which Solomon had built the two houses
1Kings9:11 (Hiram king of Tyre had supplied Solomon with cedar and cypress timber and gold according to all his desire)
1Kings9:12 So Hiram came out from Tyre to see the cities which Solomon had given him
1Kings9:13 And he said
1Kings9:14 And Hiram sent to the king 120 talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 Now this is the account of the forced labor which King Solomon raised up to build the house of Yahweh
1Kings9:16 For Pharaoh king of Egypt had gone up and captured Gezer and burned it with fire
1Kings9:17 So Solomon rebuilt Gezer and the lower Beth-horon
1Kings9:18 and Baalath and Tamar in the wilderness
1Kings9:19 and all the storage cities which Solomon had
1Kings9:20 As for all the people who were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 namely
1Kings9:22 But Solomon did not make slaves of the sons of Israel; for they were men of war
1Kings9:23 These were the chief deputies who were over Solomon’s work
1Kings9:24 As soon as Pharaoh’s daughter came up from the city of David to her house which Solomon had built for her
1Kings9:25 And three times in a year Solomon offered burnt offerings and peace offerings on the altar which he built to Yahweh
1Kings9:26 King Solomon also made a fleet of ships in Ezion-geber
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent his servants with the fleet of ships
1Kings9:28 And they went to Ophir and took from there 420 talents of gold and brought it to King Solomon.
1Kings10:1 Now the queen of Sheba heard the report about Solomon concerning the name of Yahweh. So she came to test him with riddles.
1Kings10:2 She came to Jerusalem with a very glorious retinue
1Kings10:3 And Solomon declared to her the answer to all her matters; there was not a matter which was hidden from the king which he did not declare to her.
1Kings10:4 Then the queen of Sheba saw all the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings10:5 the food of his table
1Kings10:6 Then she said to the king
1Kings10:7 Nevertheless I did not believe those words
1Kings10:8 How blessed are your men
1Kings10:9 Blessed be Yahweh your God who delighted in you
1Kings10:10 Then she gave the king 120 talents of gold and a very great amount of spices and precious stones. Never again did such abundance of spices come in as that which the queen of Sheba gave King Solomon.
1Kings10:11 Also
1Kings10:12 And the king made of the almug trees supports for the house of Yahweh and for the king’s house
1Kings10:13 Thus King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire which she asked
1Kings10:14 Now the weight of gold which came to Solomon in one year was 666 talents of gold
1Kings10:15 besides that from the traders and the wares of the merchants and all the kings of the Arabs and the governors of the country.
1Kings10:16 And King Solomon made 200 large shields of beaten gold
1Kings10:17 And he made 300 shields of beaten gold
1Kings10:18 Moreover
1Kings10:19 There were six steps to the throne and a round top to the throne at its rear
1Kings10:20 Twelve lions were also standing there on the six steps on the one side and on the other; nothing like it was made for all the other kingdoms.
1Kings10:21 Now all King Solomon’s drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had at sea the ships of Tarshish with the ships of Hiram; once every three years the ships of Tarshish came carrying gold and silver
1Kings10:23 So King Solomon became greater than all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.
1Kings10:24 And all the earth was seeking the presence of Solomon
1Kings10:25 And they brought every man his present
1Kings10:26 And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen. And he had 1
1Kings10:27 The king also made silver as plentiful as stones in Jerusalem
1Kings10:28 Solomon’s import of horses was from Egypt and Kue; the king’s merchants procured them from Kue for a price.
1Kings10:29 And a chariot was imported from Egypt for 600 shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 Now King Solomon loved many foreign women along with the daughter of Pharaoh: Moabite
1Kings11:2 from the nations concerning which Yahweh had said to the sons of Israel
1Kings11:3 And he had 700 wives—princesses—and 300 concubines
1Kings11:4 Now it happened at the time that Solomon was old
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians and after Milcom the detestable idol of the Ammonites.
1Kings11:6 And Solomon did what was evil in the sight of Yahweh
1Kings11:7 Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh the detestable idol of Moab
1Kings11:8 Thus also he did for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 Now Yahweh was angry with Solomon because his heart was turned away from Yahweh
1Kings11:10 and had commanded him concerning this thing
1Kings11:11 So Yahweh said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 Nevertheless I will not do it in your days for the sake of your father David
1Kings11:13 However
1Kings11:14 Then Yahweh raised up an adversary to Solomon
1Kings11:15 Now it happened that when David was in Edom
1Kings11:16 (for Joab and all Israel stayed there six months
1Kings11:17 that Hadad fled to Egypt
1Kings11:18 Then they arose from Midian and came to Paran; and they took men with them from Paran and came to Egypt
1Kings11:19 And Hadad found great favor in the sight of Pharaoh. So he gave him as a wife the sister of his own wife
1Kings11:20 And the sister of Tahpenes bore his son Genubath. And Tahpenes weaned him in Pharaoh’s house; and Genubath was in Pharaoh’s house among the sons of Pharaoh.
1Kings11:21 But Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers and that Joab the commander of the army had died. So Hadad said to Pharaoh
1Kings11:22 Then Pharaoh said to him
1Kings11:23 And God raised up another adversary to him
1Kings11:24 And he gathered men to himself and became commander of a marauding band
1Kings11:25 So he was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon
1Kings11:26 Now Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 Now this was the reason why he raised his hand against the king: Solomon built the Millo
1Kings11:28 Now the man Jeroboam was a mighty man of valor. And Solomon saw that the young man was industrious and appointed him over all the load-bearers of the house of Joseph.
1Kings11:29 Now it happened at that time
1Kings11:30 Then Ahijah took hold of the new cloak which was on him and tore it into twelve pieces.
1Kings11:31 And he said to Jeroboam
1Kings11:32 but he will have one tribe
1Kings11:33 because they have forsaken Me
1Kings11:34 Nevertheless I will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand
1Kings11:35 but I will take the kingdom from his son’s hand and give it to you—the ten tribes.
1Kings11:36 But to his son I will give one tribe
1Kings11:37 And I will take you
1Kings11:38 Then it will be
1Kings11:39 Thus I will afflict the seed of David for this
1Kings11:40 Solomon sought therefore to put Jeroboam to death; but Jeroboam arose and fled to Egypt to Shishak king of Egypt
1Kings11:41 Now the rest of the acts of Solomon and whatever he did
1Kings11:42 Thus the time that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.
1Kings11:43 And Solomon slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of his father David
1Kings12:1 Then Rehoboam went to Shechem
1Kings12:2 And it happened

1Kings12:4 “Your father made our yoke harsh; but you
1Kings12:5 Then he said to them
1Kings12:6 Then King Rehoboam took counsel with the elders who had stood before his father Solomon while he was still alive
1Kings12:7 And they spoke to him
1Kings12:8 But he forsook the counsel of the elders which they had counseled him
1Kings12:9 So he said to them
1Kings12:10 Then the young men who grew up with him spoke to him
1Kings12:11 So now
1Kings12:12 So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day as the king had spoken
1Kings12:13 And the king answered the people harshly
1Kings12:14 and he spoke to them according to the counsel of the young men
1Kings12:15 So the king did not listen to the people; for it was a turn of events from Yahweh
1Kings12:16 Then all Israel saw that the king did not listen to them. So the people responded to the king with this word
1Kings12:17 But as for the sons of Israel who lived in the cities of Judah
1Kings12:18 Then King Rehoboam sent Adoram
1Kings12:19 So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day.

1Kings12:24 ‘Thus says Yahweh

1Kings13:1 Now behold
1Kings13:2 And he cried against the altar by the word of Yahweh
1Kings13:3 Then he gave a miraculous sign the same day
1Kings13:4 Now it happened that when the king heard the word of the man of God
1Kings13:5 The altar also was torn apart and the ashes were poured out from the altar
1Kings13:6 Then the king answered and said to the man of God
1Kings13:7 Then the king said to the man of God
1Kings13:8 But the man of God said to the king
1Kings13:9 For so it was commanded me by the word of Yahweh
1Kings13:10 So he went another way and did not return by the way which he came to Bethel.
1Kings13:11 Now an old prophet was living in Bethel; and his sons came and recounted to him all the work which the man of God had done that day in Bethel; the words which he had spoken to the king
1Kings13:12 And their father said to them
1Kings13:13 Then he said to his sons
1Kings13:14 So he went after the man of God and found him sitting under an oak; and he said to him
1Kings13:15 Then he said to him
1Kings13:16 And he said
1Kings13:17 For a word came to me by the word of Yahweh
1Kings13:18 And he said to him
1Kings13:19 So he went back with him
1Kings13:20 Now it happened as they were sitting down at the table
1Kings13:21 and he called out to the man of God who came from Judah
1Kings13:22 but have returned and eaten bread and drunk water in the place of which He said to you
1Kings13:23 Now it happened after he had eaten bread and after he had drunk
1Kings13:24 Then he went and on the way a lion met him and put him to death
1Kings13:25 And behold
1Kings13:26 Then the prophet
1Kings13:27 Then he spoke to his sons
1Kings13:28 And he went and found his body thrown on the road with the donkey and the lion standing beside the body; the lion had not eaten the body nor mauled the donkey.
1Kings13:29 So the prophet took up the body of the man of God and laid it on the donkey and brought it back
1Kings13:30 He laid his body in his own grave
1Kings13:31 Now it happened after he had buried him
1Kings13:32 For the word shall surely happen which he cried by the word of Yahweh against the altar in Bethel and against all the houses of the high places which are in the cities of Samaria.”
1Kings13:33 After this event Jeroboam did not return from his evil way
1Kings13:34 And this event became sin to the house of Jeroboam
1Kings14:1 At that time Abijah the son of Jeroboam became sick.
1Kings14:2 And Jeroboam said to his wife

1Kings14:4 Then Jeroboam’s wife did so
1Kings14:5 Now Yahweh had said to Ahijah
1Kings14:6 Now it happened as Ahijah heard the sound of her feet coming in the doorway
1Kings14:7 Go
1Kings14:8 and tore the kingdom away from the house of David and gave it to you—yet you have not been like My servant David
1Kings14:9 you also have done more evil than all who were before you
1Kings14:10 therefore behold
1Kings14:11 Anyone belonging to Jeroboam who dies in the city the dogs will eat. And he who dies in the field the birds of the sky will eat; for Yahweh has spoken it.”’
1Kings14:12 But you
1Kings14:13 And all Israel shall mourn for him and bury him
1Kings14:14 Moreover
1Kings14:15 “So Yahweh will strike Israel
1Kings14:16 And He will give Israel over on account of the sins of Jeroboam

1Kings14:19 Now the rest of the acts of Jeroboam
1Kings14:20 Now the time that Jeroboam reigned was twenty-two years; and he slept with his fathers
1Kings14:21 Now Rehoboam the son of Solomon became king in Judah. Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he became king
1Kings14:22 And Judah did what was evil in the sight of Yahweh
1Kings14:23 They also built for themselves high places and sacred pillars and Asherim on every high hill and beneath every green tree.
1Kings14:24 There were also male cult prostitutes in the land. They did according to all the abominations of the nations which Yahweh dispossessed before the sons of Israel.
1Kings14:25 Now it happened in the fifth year of King Rehoboam
1Kings14:26 And he took the treasures of the house of Yahweh and the treasures of the king’s house. And he took everything; he even took all the shields of gold which Solomon had made.
1Kings14:27 Then King Rehoboam made shields of bronze in their place and committed them into the hand of the commanders of the guard who kept the door of the king’s house.
1Kings14:28 Now it happened as often as the king entered the house of Yahweh

1Kings14:30 Now there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all their days.
1Kings14:31 And Rehoboam slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of David; and his mother’s name was Naamah the Ammonitess. And Abijam his son became king in his place.
1Kings15:1 Now in the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam
1Kings15:2 He reigned three years in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Maacah the daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:3 And he walked in all the sins of his father which he had committed before him; and his heart was not wholly devoted to Yahweh his God

1Kings15:16 Now there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.

2Kings16:5 Then Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah
2Kings16:6 At that time Rezin king of Aram restored Elath for Aram and cleared the Judeans out of Elath entirely; and the Arameans came to Elath and have lived there to this day.
2Kings16:7 So Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria
2Kings16:8 Ahaz also took the silver and gold that was found in the house of Yahweh and in the treasuries of the king’s house
2Kings16:9 So the king of Assyria listened to him; and the king of Assyria went up against Damascus and seized it and took the people of it away into exile to Kir
2Kings16:10 Now King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria and saw the altar which was at Damascus; and King Ahaz sent to Urijah the priest the likeness of the altar and its pattern
2Kings16:11 So Urijah the priest built an altar; according to all that King Ahaz had sent from Damascus
2Kings16:12 So the king came from Damascus
2Kings16:13 and offered his burnt offering and his meal offering up in smoke
2Kings16:14 Now as for the bronze altar
2Kings16:15 Then King Ahaz commanded Urijah the priest
2Kings16:16 So Urijah the priest did according to all that King Ahaz commanded.
2Kings16:17 Then King Ahaz cut off the borders of the stands and removed the laver from them; he also took down the sea from the bronze oxen which were under it and put it on a pavement of stone.
2Kings16:18 And the covered way for the sabbath which they had built in the house
2Kings16:19 Now the rest of the acts of Ahaz which he did
2Kings16:20 So Ahaz slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of David; and Hezekiah his son became king in his place.
2Kings17:1 In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah
2Kings17:2 And he did what was evil in the sight of Yahweh
2Kings17:3 Shalmaneser king of Assyria went up against him
2Kings17:4 But the king of Assyria found conspiracy in Hoshea
2Kings17:5 Then the king of Assyria went up against the whole land and he went up to Samaria and besieged it three years.
2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea
2Kings17:7 Now this happened because the sons of Israel had sinned against Yahweh their God—who had brought them up from the land of Egypt from under the hand of Pharaoh
2Kings17:8 and walked in the statutes of the nations whom Yahweh had dispossessed from before the sons of Israel
2Kings17:9 And the sons of Israel did things secretly which were not right against Yahweh their God. Moreover
2Kings17:10 And they set for themselves sacred pillars and Asherim on every high hill and under every green tree
2Kings17:11 and there they burned incense on all the high places as the nations did which Yahweh had taken away into exile before them; and they did evil things provoking Yahweh to anger.
2Kings17:12 And they served idols
2Kings17:13 Yet Yahweh warned Israel and Judah by the hand of all His prophets and every seer
2Kings17:14 However
2Kings17:15 They also rejected His statutes and His covenant which He cut with their fathers and His warnings with which He warned them. And they followed vanity and became vain
2Kings17:16 And they forsook all the commandments of Yahweh their God and made for themselves molten images
2Kings17:17 Then they made their sons and their daughters pass through the fire
2Kings17:18 So Yahweh was very angry with Israel and caused them to depart from His presence; none was left except the tribe of Judah alone.
2Kings17:19 Also Judah did not keep the commandments of Yahweh their God
2Kings17:20 So Yahweh rejected all the seed of Israel and afflicted them and gave them into the hand of plunderers
2Kings17:21 When He had torn Israel from the house of David
2Kings17:22 And the sons of Israel walked in all the sins of Jeroboam which he did; they did not depart from them
2Kings17:23 until Yahweh caused Israel to depart from His presence
2Kings17:24 And the king of Assyria brought men from Babylon and from Cuthah and from Avva and from Hamath and Sepharvaim
2Kings17:25 Now it happened at the beginning of their settlement there
2Kings17:26 So they spoke to the king of Assyria
2Kings17:27 Then the king of Assyria commanded
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests whom they had taken away into exile from Samaria came and settled at Bethel
2Kings17:29 But each nation was still making gods of its own and put them in the houses of the high places which the people of Samaria had made
2Kings17:30 And the men of Babylon made Succoth-benoth
2Kings17:31 and the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak; and the Sepharvites burned their children in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech
2Kings17:32 They were also fearing Yahweh and appointed from among all of themselves priests of the high places
2Kings17:33 They were fearing Yahweh and serving their own gods according to the custom of the nations from among whom they had been taken away into exile.
2Kings17:34 To this day they are acting according to the earlier customs: they are not fearing Yahweh; they are not acting according to their statutes or their judgments or the law
2Kings17:35 with whom Yahweh cut a covenant and commanded them
2Kings17:36 But Yahweh
2Kings17:37 And the statutes and the judgments and the law and the commandment which He wrote for you
2Kings17:38 And the covenant that I have cut with you
2Kings17:39 But Yahweh your God you shall fear; and He will deliver you from the hand of all your enemies.”
2Kings17:40 However
2Kings17:41 So while these nations were fearing Yahweh
2Kings18:1 Now it happened in the third year of Hoshea

2Kings23:1 Then the king sent

1Chronicles10:6 Thus Saul died with his three sons

1Chronicles10:8 Now it happened on the next day
1Chronicles10:9 So they stripped him and took his head and his weapons and sent them all around the land of the Philistines
1Chronicles10:10 And they placed his weapons in the house of their gods and fastened his head in the house of Dagon.

1Chronicles10:13 Thus Saul died for his unfaithfulness
1Chronicles10:14 and did not inquire of Yahweh. Therefore He put him to death and turned the kingdom to David the son of Jesse.

2Chronicles3:9 Now the weight of the nails was fifty shekels of gold. He also covered the upper rooms with gold.

2Chronicles29:34 But the priests were too few

2Chronicles29:36 Then Hezekiah and all the people were glad over what God had prepared for the people
2Chronicles30:1 And Hezekiah sent to all Israel and Judah and wrote letters also to Ephraim and Manasseh
2Chronicles30:2 And the king and his princes and all the assembly in Jerusalem had taken counsel to celebrate the Passover in the second month
2Chronicles30:3 since they could not celebrate it at that time
2Chronicles30:4 Thus the thing was right in the eyes of the king and in the eyes of all the assembly.
2Chronicles30:5 So they established a decree to make a proclamation pass throughout all Israel from Beersheba even to Dan
2Chronicles30:6 And the couriers went throughout all Israel and Judah with the letters from the hand of the king and his princes
2Chronicles30:7 Now do not be like your fathers and your brothers
2Chronicles30:8 Now do not stiffen your neck like your fathers
2Chronicles30:9 For when you return to Yahweh
2Chronicles30:10 So the couriers passed from city to city through the country of Ephraim and Manasseh
2Chronicles30:11 Nevertheless some men of Asher
2Chronicles30:12 The hand of God was also on Judah to give them one heart to do what the king and the princes commanded by the word of Yahweh.
2Chronicles30:13 Now many people were gathered at Jerusalem to celebrate the Feast of Unleavened Bread in the second month
2Chronicles30:14 And they arose and took away the altars which were in Jerusalem; they also took away all the incense altars and cast them into the brook Kidron.
2Chronicles30:15 Then they slaughtered the Passover lambs on the fourteenth of the second month. And the priests and Levites were ashamed of themselves
2Chronicles30:16 And they stood at their stations according to the legal judgment for them
2Chronicles30:17 For there were many in the assembly who had not set themselves apart as holy; therefore
2Chronicles30:18 For a multitude of the people
2Chronicles30:19 for everyone who prepares his heart to seek God
2Chronicles30:20 So Yahweh heard Hezekiah and healed the people.
2Chronicles30:21 And the sons of Israel present in Jerusalem celebrated the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days with great gladness
2Chronicles30:22 Then Hezekiah spoke to the hearts of all the Levites who showed good insight in the things of Yahweh. So they ate for the appointed seven days
2Chronicles30:23 Then the whole assembly took counsel to determine to celebrate the feast another seven days
2Chronicles30:24 For Hezekiah king of Judah had contributed to the assembly 1
2Chronicles30:25 And all the assembly of Judah were glad
2Chronicles30:26 So there was great gladness in Jerusalem
2Chronicles30:27 Then the Levitical priests arose and blessed the people; and their voice was heard and their prayer came to His holy habitation
2Chronicles31:1 Now when all this was completed
2Chronicles31:2 And Hezekiah set up the divisions of the priests and the Levites by their divisions
2Chronicles31:3 He also appointed the king’s portion of his goods for the burnt offerings
2Chronicles31:4 Also he said to the people who lived in Jerusalem to give the portion due to the priests and the Levites
2Chronicles31:5 As soon as the word spread forth
2Chronicles31:6 Now the sons of Israel and Judah who lived in the cities of Judah also brought in the tithe of oxen and sheep
2Chronicles31:7 In the third month they began to make the heaps
2Chronicles31:8 Then Hezekiah and the rulers came and saw the heaps
2Chronicles31:9 Then Hezekiah inquired of the priests and the Levites concerning the heaps.
2Chronicles31:10 And Azariah the chief priest of the house of Zadok said to him
2Chronicles31:11 Then Hezekiah ordered them to prepare rooms in the house of Yahweh
2Chronicles31:12 And they faithfully brought in the contribution and the tithe and the holy things; and Conaniah the Levite was the officer over them and his brother Shimei was second.
2Chronicles31:13 Now Jehiel
2Chronicles31:14 And Kore the son of Imnah the Levite
2Chronicles31:15 Now assisting by his hand were Eden
2Chronicles31:16 without regard to their genealogical record
2Chronicles31:17 as well as the priests who were recorded genealogically according to their fathers’ households
2Chronicles31:18 The genealogical record included all their little ones
2Chronicles31:19 Also for the sons of Aaron the priests who were in the pasture lands of their cities
2Chronicles31:20 Thus Hezekiah did throughout all Judah; and he did what was good
2Chronicles31:21 And every work which he began in the service of the house of God in law and in commandment—to seek his God—he did with all his heart and succeeded.
2Chronicles32:1 After these acts of faithfulness Sennacherib king of Assyria came and invaded Judah and encamped against the fortified cities
2Chronicles32:2 Then Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come and that he had set his face to make war on Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 and he took counsel with his commanders and his warriors to stop up the supply of water from the springs which were outside the city
2Chronicles32:4 So many people gathered and stopped up all the springs and the stream which flowed in the midst of the land
2Chronicles32:5 And he strengthened himself and rebuilt all the wall that had been broken down and erected towers on it
2Chronicles32:6 He also put military commanders over the people and gathered them to him in the square at the city gate
2Chronicles32:7 “Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be dismayed because of the king of Assyria nor because of all the multitude that is with him; for the one with us is greater than the one with him.
2Chronicles32:8 With him is only an arm of flesh
2Chronicles32:9 After this Sennacherib king of Assyria sent his servants to Jerusalem while he was besieging Lachish with all his forces with him
2Chronicles32:10 “Thus says Sennacherib king of Assyria
2Chronicles32:11 Is not Hezekiah inciting you to give yourselves over to die by hunger and by thirst
2Chronicles32:12 Has not the same Hezekiah taken away His high places and His altars
2Chronicles32:13 Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of the lands? Were the gods of the nations of the lands able at all to deliver their land from my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who was there among all the gods of these nations which my fathers devoted to destruction
2Chronicles32:15 So now
2Chronicles32:16 His servants spoke further against Yahweh God and against His servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 He also wrote letters to reproach Yahweh
2Chronicles32:18 And they called this out with a loud voice in the language of Judah to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall
2Chronicles32:19 And they spoke of the God of Jerusalem as of the gods of the peoples of the earth
2Chronicles32:20 But King Hezekiah and Isaiah the prophet
2Chronicles32:21 And Yahweh sent an angel who wiped out every mighty man of valor
2Chronicles32:22 So Yahweh saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib the king of Assyria and from the hand of all others
2Chronicles32:23 And many were bringing presents to Yahweh at Jerusalem and precious things to Hezekiah king of Judah
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah became sick to the point of death; and he prayed to Yahweh
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah gave no return for the benefit he received
2Chronicles32:26 However
2Chronicles32:27 Now Hezekiah had abundant riches and honor; and he made for himself treasuries for silver
2Chronicles32:28 storehouses also for the produce of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He also made cities for himself and acquired flocks and herds in abundance
2Chronicles32:30 And it was Hezekiah who stopped the upper outlet of the waters of Gihon and directed them to the west side of the city of David. And Hezekiah succeeded in all that he did.
2Chronicles32:31 Even in the matter of the envoys of the rulers of Babylon
2Chronicles32:32 Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah and his deeds of lovingkindness

2Chronicles36:5 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king
2Chronicles36:6 Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against him and bound him with bronze chains to lead him off to Babylon.

Ezra4:12 let it be known to the king that the Jews who came up from you have come to us at Jerusalem; they are rebuilding the rebellious and evil city and are completing the walls and repairing the foundations.
Ezra4:13 Now let it be known to the king
Ezra4:14 Now because we are in the service of the palace
Ezra4:15 that a search be made in the record books of your fathers. And you will find in the record books and come to know that that city is a rebellious city and damaging to kings and provinces
Ezra4:16 We make known to the king that if that city is rebuilt and the walls completed
Ezra4:17 Then the king sent an edict to Rehum the commander
Ezra4:18 the document which you sent to us has been plainly read before me.

Ezra4:20 that strong kings have been over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 Now issue a decree to make those men stop
Ezra4:22 And beware of being negligent in doing this matter; why should harm increase to damage the kings?”

Ezra7:9 For on the first of the first month he began to go up from Babylon; and on the first of the fifth month he came to Jerusalem
Ezra7:10 For Ezra had set his heart to study the law of Yahweh and to practice it
Ezra7:11 Now this is the copy of the letter which King Artaxerxes gave to Ezra the priest
Ezra7:12 “Artaxerxes
Ezra7:13 I have issued a decree that any of the people of Israel and their priests and the Levites in my kingdom who freely offer to go to Jerusalem
Ezra7:14 Forasmuch as you are sent from before the king and his seven counselors to inquire concerning Judah and Jerusalem according to the law of your God which is in your hand
Ezra7:15 and to bring the silver and gold
Ezra7:16 and all the silver and gold which you find in the whole province of Babylon
Ezra7:17 with this money
Ezra7:18 And whatever seems good to you and to your brothers to do with the rest of the silver and gold
Ezra7:19 Also the utensils which are given to you for the service of the house of your God
Ezra7:20 The rest of the needs for the house of your God
Ezra7:21 “So I
Ezra7:22 even up to 100 talents of silver
Ezra7:23 Whatever is decreed by the God of heaven
Ezra7:24 We also make known to you that it is not allowed to impose tribute
Ezra7:25 “And you
Ezra7:26 Whoever will not do the law of your God and the law of the king
Ezra7:27 Blessed be Yahweh
Ezra7:28 and has extended lovingkindness to me before the king and his counselors and before all the king’s mighty princes. Thus I was strengthened according to the hand of Yahweh my God upon me
Ezra8:1 Now these are the heads of their fathers’ households and the genealogical records of those who went up with me from Babylon in the reign of King Artaxerxes:
Ezra8:2 of the sons of Phinehas

Ezra8:4 of the sons of Pahath-moab

Ezra8:6 and of the sons of Adin
Ezra8:7 and of the sons of Elam

Ezra8:9 of the sons of Joab
Ezra8:10 and of the sons of Bani
Ezra8:11 and of the sons of Bebai
Ezra8:12 and of the sons of Azgad
Ezra8:13 and of the sons of Adonikam
Ezra8:14 and of the sons of Bigvai

Ezra8:18 So
Ezra8:19 and Hashabiah and Jeshaiah of the sons of Merari
Ezra8:20 and 220 of the temple servants
Ezra8:21 Then I called for a fast there at the river of Ahava
Ezra8:22 For I was ashamed to ask from the king for a military force and horsemen to help us against the enemy on the way
Ezra8:23 So we fasted and sought our God concerning this matter
Ezra8:24 Then I set apart twelve of the leading priests
Ezra8:25 and I weighed out to them the silver
Ezra8:26 Thus I weighed into their hands 650 talents of silver
Ezra8:27 and 20 gold bowls worth 1
Ezra8:28 Then I said to them
Ezra8:29 Watch and keep them until you weigh them before the officials of the priests
Ezra8:30 So the priests and the Levites accepted the weighed out silver and gold and the utensils
Ezra8:31 Then we set out from the river Ahava on the twelfth of the first month to go to Jerusalem; and the hand of our God was upon us
Ezra8:32 Thus we came to Jerusalem and remained there three days.
Ezra8:33 On the fourth day the silver and the gold and the utensils were weighed out in the house of our God into the hand of Meremoth the son of Uriah the priest
Ezra8:34 Everything was numbered and weighed
Ezra8:35 The exiles who had come from the captivity brought burnt offerings near to the God of Israel: 12 bulls for all Israel
Ezra8:36 Then they gave the king’s edicts to the king’s satraps and to the governors in the provinces beyond the River

Ezra9:6 and I said

Ezra9:14 shall we again break Your commandments and intermarry with the peoples who commit these abominations? Would You not be angry with us to the point of destruction

Nehemiah1:3 They said to me
Nehemiah1:4 Now it happened that when I heard these words
Nehemiah1:5 I said

Nehemiah1:7 We have worked in utter destruction against You and have not kept the commandments
Nehemiah1:8 Remember the word which You commanded Your servant Moses
Nehemiah1:9 but if you return to Me and keep My commandments and do them
Nehemiah1:10 They are Your slaves and Your people whom You redeemed by Your great power and by Your strong hand.
Nehemiah1:11 O Lord

Nehemiah2:10 Then Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite official heard about it
Nehemiah2:11 So I came to Jerusalem and was there three days.
Nehemiah2:12 And I arose in the night

Nehemiah2:14 Then I passed on to the Spring Gate and the King’s Pool
Nehemiah2:15 So I went up at night by the ravine and inspected the wall. Then I turned and entered the Valley Gate and turned around.

Nehemiah9:26 “But they became disobedient and rebelled against You
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore You gave them into the hand of the ones who distressed them
Nehemiah9:28 But as soon as they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 And testified to them in order to turn them back to Your law. Yet they acted presumptuously and did not listen to Your commandments but sinned against Your judgments

Esther1:1 Now it happened in the days of Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 in those days as King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne which was at the citadel in Susa
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign
Esther1:4 while he displayed the riches of his royal glory and the splendor of his great majesty for many days
Esther1:5 And when these days were fulfilled
Esther1:6 There were hangings of fine white and blue linen held by cords of fine purple linen on silver rings and marble pillars
Esther1:7 And drinks were served in golden vessels of various kinds
Esther1:8 And the drinking was done according to the law; there was no compulsion
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also held a feast for the women in the royal house which belonged to King Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king with her royal crown in order to display her beauty to the people and the princes
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the word of the king which was delivered by the hand of the eunuchs. Then the king became exceedingly furious
Esther1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who knew the times—for it was the custom of the king thus to speak before all who knew law and justice
Esther1:14 and were close to him: Carshena
Esther1:15 “According to law
Esther1:16 Then in the presence of the king and the princes
Esther1:17 For the word about the queen will get out to all the women causing them to despise their husbands in their eyes by saying
Esther1:18 This day the ladies of Persia and Media who have heard of the word about the queen will speak in the same way to all the king’s princes
Esther1:19 If it seems good to the king
Esther1:20 And the king’s sentence
Esther1:21 And this word was good in the eyes of the king and the princes
Esther1:22 So he sent letters to all the king’s provinces
Esther2:1 After these things when the wrath of King Ahasuerus had subsided
Esther2:2 Then the young men of the king

Esther2:4 Then let the young lady who is good in the eyes of the king be queen in place of Vashti.” And the word was good in the eyes of the king
Esther2:5 Now there was at the citadel in Susa a Jew. And his name was Mordecai
Esther2:6 who had been taken away into exile from Jerusalem with the exiles who had been taken away into exile with Jeconiah king of Judah
Esther2:7 And he was bringing up Hadassah
Esther2:8 Now it happened that when the word and law of the king were heard and many young ladies were gathered to the citadel of Susa into the hand of Hegai
Esther2:9 And the young lady was good in his eyes
Esther2:10 Esther did not tell anyone about her people or her kinsmen
Esther2:11 And every day Mordecai walked back and forth in front of the court of the harem to know the well-being of Esther and how she fared.
Esther2:12 Now when it reached the turn of each young lady to go in to King Ahasuerus
Esther2:13 then the young lady would go in to the king in this way: anything that she said she desired was given to her to come with her from the harem to the king’s house.
Esther2:14 In the evening she would go in
Esther2:15 Now when it reached the turn of Esther
Esther2:16 So Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus to his royal house in the tenth month which is the month Tebeth
Esther2:17 And the king loved Esther more than all the women
Esther2:18 Then the king held a great feast
Esther2:19 And when the virgins were gathered together the second time
Esther2:20 Esther had not yet told anyone about her kinsmen or her people
Esther2:21 In those days
Esther2:22 But the matter became known to Mordecai
