Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and that man was blameless
Job1:2 Seven sons and three daughters were born to him.
Job1:3 His possessions also were 7
Job1:4 His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one on his day
Job1:5 Now it happened when the days of feasting had completed their cycle
Job1:6 Now it was the day that the sons of God came to stand before Yahweh
Job1:7 And Yahweh said to Satan
Job1:8 Then Yahweh said to Satan
Job1:9 And Satan answered Yahweh and said
Job1:10 Have You not made a hedge about him and his house and all that he has
Job1:11 But send forth Your hand now and touch all that he has; he will surely curse You to Your face.”
Job1:12 Then Yahweh said to Satan
Job1:13 Now it happened that on the day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their brother
Job1:14 a messenger came to Job and said
Job1:15 and the Sabeans fell upon them and took them. They also struck down the young men with the edge of the sword
Job1:16 While this one was still speaking
Job1:17 While this one was still speaking
Job1:18 While this one was still speaking
Job1:19 and behold
Job1:20 Then Job arose and tore his robe and shaved his head
Job1:21 And he said
Job1:22 Through all this Job did not sin
Job2:1 Again it was the day that the sons of God came to stand before Yahweh
Job2:2 And Yahweh said to Satan
Job2:3 And Yahweh said to Satan
Job2:4 Satan answered Yahweh and said
Job2:5 However
Job2:6 So Yahweh said to Satan
Job2:7 Then Satan went out from the presence of Yahweh and struck Job with terrible boils from the sole of his foot to the top of his head.
Job2:8 And he took a potsherd to scrape himself while he was sitting among the ashes.
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 Then Job’s three friends heard of all this calamity that had come upon him. So they came each one from his own place
Job2:12 Then they lifted up their eyes at a distance and did not recognize him
Job2:13 Then they sat down on the ground with him for seven days and seven nights with no one speaking a word to him
Job3:1 Afterward Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
Job3:2 And Job answered and said
Job3:3 “Let the day perish on which I was to be born
Job3:4 May that day be darkness; Let not God seek it from above
Job3:5 Let darkness and shadow of death redeem it; Let a cloud dwell upon it; Let the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 As for that night
Job3:7 Behold
Job3:8 Let those curse it who curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of its twilight be darkened; Let it hope for light but have none
Job3:10 Because it did not shut the opening of my mother’s body
Job3:11 “Why did I not die from the womb
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me
Job3:13 For now I would have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept then; it would have been rest to me
Job3:14 With kings and with counselors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not like a miscarriage hidden away
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from raging
Job3:18 The prisoners are at ease together; They do not hear the voice of the taskmaster.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there
Job3:20 “Why is light given to him who is troubled
Job3:21 Who long for death
Job3:22 Who are glad with joy
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my groaning comes at the sight of my food
Job3:25 For the dread that I dread comes upon me
Job3:26 I am not complacent
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job4:2 “If one tries a word with you
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Your words have helped the stumbling to stand
Job4:5 But now it has come to you
Job4:6 Is not your fear of God your confidence
Job4:7 “Remember now
Job4:8 According to what I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion and the voice of the fierce lion
Job4:11 The lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 “Now a word was brought to me stealthily
Job4:13 Amid disquieting thoughts from the visions of the night
Job4:14 Dread came upon me
Job4:15 Then a spirit swept by my face; The hair of my flesh bristled up.
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 ‘Can mankind be right before God? Can a man be pure before his Maker?
Job4:18 He puts no trust even in His slaves; And against His angels He charges error.
Job4:19 How much more those who dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 Between morning and evening they are broken in pieces; Unobserved
Job4:21 Is not their tent‑cord pulled up within them? They die
Job5:1 “Call now
Job5:2 For vexation kills the ignorant fool
Job5:3 I have seen the ignorant fool taking root
Job5:4 His sons are far from salvation
Job5:5 His harvest the hungry devour And take it to a place of thorns
Job5:6 For wickedness does not come out from the dust
Job5:7 For man is born for trouble
Job5:8 “But as for me
Job5:9 Who does great and unsearchable things
Job5:10 He gives rain on the earth And sends water on the fields outside
Job5:11 So that He sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the thoughts of the crafty
Job5:13 He catches the wise by their own craftiness
Job5:14 By day they meet with darkness And grope at noon as in the night.
Job5:15 But He saves from the sword of their mouth
Job5:16 So the poor has hope
Job5:17 “Behold
Job5:18 For He inflicts pain and gives relief; He wounds
Job5:19 From six distresses He will deliver you
Job5:20 In famine He will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You will be hidden from the scourge of the tongue
Job5:22 You will laugh at devastation and starvation
Job5:23 For your covenant will be with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You will know that your tent is at peace
Job5:25 You will know also that your seed will be many
Job5:26 You will come to the grave in full vigor
Job5:27 Behold this; we have investigated it
Job6:1 Then Job answered and said
Job6:2 “Oh that my vexation were actually weighed And laid in the balances together with my destruction!
Job6:3 For then it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; Therefore my words have been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me
Job6:5 Does the wild donkey bray over his grass
Job6:6 Can something tasteless be eaten without salt
Job6:7 My soul refuses to touch them; They are like loathsome food to me.
Job6:8 “Oh that my request might come to pass
Job6:9 Would that God were willing to crush me
Job6:10 But it is still my comfort
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones
Job6:13 Is it that there is no help within me
Job6:14 “For the despairing man lovingkindness should be from his friend; But he forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My brothers have betrayed me like a wadi
Job6:16 Which grow dark because of ice And upon which the snow hides itself.
Job6:17 When they become waterless
Job6:18 The paths of their course wind along; They go up into a formless place and perish.
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema looked; The travelers of Sheba hoped for them.
Job6:20 They were ashamed for they had trusted; They came there and were humiliated.
Job6:21 Indeed
Job6:22 Have I said
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 “Instruct me
Job6:25 How painful are upright words! But what does your reproof prove?
Job6:26 Do you think to reprove my words
Job6:27 You would even cast lots for the orphans And bargain over your friend.
Job6:28 So now be willing to face me
Job6:29 Now turn from this
Job6:30 Is there unrighteousness on my tongue? Cannot my palate discern destruction?
Job7:1 “Is not man conscripted to labor on earth
Job7:2 As a slave who pants for the shade
Job7:3 So am I apportioned months of worthlessness
Job7:4 If I lie down I say
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and a crust of dirt; My skin scabs over and flows out again.
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle
Job7:7 “Remember that my life is but wind; My eye will not again see good.
Job7:8 The eye of him who sees me will behold me no longer; Your eyes will be on me
Job7:9 A cloud vanishes
Job7:10 He will not return again to his house
Job7:11 “Indeed I will not hold back my mouth; I will speak in the distress of my spirit; I will muse on the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I the sea or the sea monster
Job7:13 If I say
Job7:14 Then You frighten me with dreams And terrify me by visions
Job7:15 So that my soul would choose suffocation
Job7:16 I have rejected everything; I will not live forever. Leave me alone
Job7:17 What is man that You magnify him
Job7:18 That You examine him every morning And test him every moment?
Job7:19 Will You never turn Your gaze away from me
Job7:20 Have I sinned? What have I done to You
Job7:21 Why then do You not forgive my transgression And take away my iniquity? For now I will lie down in the dust; And You will seek me earnestly
Job8:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said
Job8:2 “How long will you say these things
Job8:3 Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert what is right?
Job8:4 If your sons sinned against Him
Job8:5 If you would seek God earnestly And plead for the grace of the Almighty
Job8:6 If you are pure and upright
Job8:7 Though your beginning was insignificant
Job8:8 “Please ask of past generations
Job8:9 For we are only of yesterday and know nothing
Job8:10 Will they not instruct you and tell you
Job8:11 “Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the rushes grow without water?
Job8:12 While it is still green and not cut down
Job8:13 So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the godless will perish
Job8:14 Whose confidence is fragile
Job8:15 He relies on his house
Job8:16 He thrives before the sun
Job8:17 His roots wrap around a rock pile; He looks upon a house of stones.
Job8:18 If He swallows him up from his place
Job8:19 Behold
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter And your lips with shouting.
Job8:22 Those who hate you will be clothed with shame
Job9:1 Then Job answered and said
Job9:2 “In truth I know that this is so; But how can a man be in the right before God?
Job9:3 If one desired to contend with Him
Job9:4 Wise in heart and mighty in power
Job9:5 God is the One who removes the mountains
Job9:6 The One who shakes the earth out of its place
Job9:7 The One who says for the sun not to shine
Job9:8 Who alone stretches out the heavens
Job9:9 Who makes the Bear
Job9:10 Who does great things
Job9:11 Were He to sweep by me
Job9:12 Were He to snatch away
Job9:13 “God will not turn back His anger; Beneath Him crouch the helpers of Rahab.
Job9:14 How then can I answer Him
Job9:15 For though I were right
Job9:16 If I called and He answered me
Job9:17 For He bruises me with a tempest And multiplies my wounds without cause.
Job9:18 He will not allow me to get my breath
Job9:19 If it is a matter of power
Job9:20 Though I am righteous
Job9:21 I am blameless; I do not know my soul; I reject my life.
Job9:22 It is all one; therefore I say
Job9:23 If the scourge puts to death suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; He covers the faces of its judges. If it is not He
Job9:25 “Now my days are swifter than a runner; They flee away; they do not see good.
Job9:26 They sweep by like reed boats
Job9:27 Though I say
Job9:28 I am afraid of all my pains; I know that You will not acquit me.
Job9:29 I am accounted wicked; Why then should I toil in vain?
Job9:30 If I should wash myself with snow And cleanse my hands with lye
Job9:31 Yet You would plunge me into the pit
Job9:32 For He is not a man as I am that I may answer Him
Job9:33 There is no adjudicator between us
Job9:34 Let Him remove His rod from me
Job9:35 Then I would speak and not fear Him; But I am not like that in myself.
Job10:1 “My soul is loathed by my life; I will abandon all restraint in myself to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God
Job10:3 Is it good to You that You oppress
Job10:4 Have You eyes of flesh? Or do You see as a mortal man sees?
Job10:5 Are Your days as the days of a mortal man
Job10:6 That You should seek for my guilt And search after my sin?
Job10:7 According to Your knowledge I am indeed not wicked
Job10:8 ‘Your hands fashioned and made me altogether
Job10:9 Remember now
Job10:10 Did You not pour me out like milk And curdle me like cheese
Job10:11 Clothe me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 You have made alongside me life and lovingkindness; And Your care has kept my spirit.
Job10:13 Yet these things You have concealed in Your heart; I know that this is within You:
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 If I am wicked
Job10:16 Should my head be set on high
Job10:17 You renew Your witnesses against me And increase Your vexation toward me; Hardship after hardship is with me.
Job10:18 ‘Why then have You brought me out of the womb? Would that I had breathed my last and no eye had seen me!
Job10:19 I should have been as though I had not been
Job10:20 Would He not cease for a few of my days? Withdraw from me that I may have a little cheer
Job10:21 Before I go—and I shall not return— To the land of darkness and shadow of death
Job10:22 The land of utter gloom as the thick darkness itself
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said
Job11:2 “Shall a multitude of words go unanswered
Job11:3 Shall your boasts silence men? And shall you mock and none rebuke?
Job11:4 You have said
Job11:5 But would that God might speak
Job11:6 And tell you the secrets of wisdom! For sound wisdom has two sides. Know then that God forgets a part of your iniquity.
Job11:7 “Can you find the depths of God? Can you find the limits of the Almighty?
Job11:8 They are high as the heavens
Job11:9 Its measure is longer than the earth And broader than the sea.
Job11:10 If He sweeps by or shuts up
Job11:11 For He knows worthless men
Job11:12 Yet an empty headed man will obtain a heart of wisdom
Job11:13 “If you would set your heart right And spread out your hand to Him
Job11:14 If wickedness is in your hand
Job11:15 Then
Job11:16 For you would forget your trouble
Job11:17 And your lifetime would arise brighter than noonday; Darkness would be like the morning.
Job11:18 Then you would trust
Job11:19 You would lie down and none would make you tremble
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked will come to an end
Job12:1 Then Job answered and said
Job12:2 “Truly then you are the people
Job12:3 But I have a heart of wisdom as well as you; I do not fall short of you. And who does not know such things as these?
Job12:4 I am a laughingstock to my friends
Job12:5 As for upheaval
Job12:6 The tents of the destroyers are complacent
Job12:7 “But now ask the beasts
Job12:8 Or muse to the earth
Job12:9 Who among all these does not know That the hand of Yahweh has done this
Job12:10 In whose hand is the life of every living thing
Job12:11 Does not the ear test words
Job12:12 Wisdom is with aged men
Job12:13 “With Him are wisdom and might; To Him belong counsel and discernment.
Job12:14 Behold
Job12:15 Behold
Job12:16 With Him are strength and sound wisdom; The misled and the misleader belong to Him.
Job12:17 He makes counselors walk barefoot And makes fools of judges.
Job12:18 He opens the bond of kings And binds their loins with a belt.
Job12:19 He makes priests walk barefoot And subverts the enduring ones.
Job12:20 He removes speech from the faithful And takes away the discerning taste of the elders.
Job12:21 He pours contempt on nobles And loosens the belt of the strong.
Job12:22 He reveals mysteries from the darkness And brings out the shadows of death into light.
Job12:23 He makes the nations great
Job12:24 He removes the heart of wisdom from the heads of the earth’s people And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
Job12:25 They grope in darkness with no light
Job13:1 “Behold
Job13:2 What you know I also know; I have not fallen short of you.
Job13:3 “But I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But you cover me with lies; You are all worthless physicians.
Job13:5 O that you would be completely silent
Job13:6 Please hear my argument And give heed to the contentions of my lips.
Job13:7 Will you speak what is unrighteous for God
Job13:8 Will you show partiality for Him? Will you contend for God?
Job13:9 Will it be well when He examines you? Or will you deceive Him as one deceives a man?
Job13:10 He will surely reprove you If you secretly show partiality.
Job13:11 Will not His exaltedness terrify you
Job13:12 Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes; Your defenses are defenses of clay.
Job13:13 “Be silent before me so that I may speak; Then let come on me what may.
Job13:14 Why should I take my flesh in my teeth And put my life in my hands?
Job13:15 Though He slay me
Job13:16 This also will be my salvation
Job13:17 Listen carefully to my words
Job13:18 Behold now
Job13:19 Who will contend with me? For now I am silent and will breathe my last.
Job13:20 “Only two things do not do to me
Job13:21 Move Your hand far from me
Job13:22 Then call
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and sins? Make known to me my transgression and my sin.
Job13:24 Why do You hide Your face And think of me as Your enemy?
Job13:25 Will You cause a driven leaf to tremble? Or will You pursue the dry chaff?
Job13:26 For You write bitter things against me And make me to possess the iniquities of my youth.
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks And kept watch over all my paths; You set a limit for the soles of my feet
Job13:28 While I am decaying like a rotten thing
Job14:1 “Man
Job14:2 Like a flower he comes forth and withers. He also flees like a shadow and does not stand.
Job14:3 You also open Your eyes on him And bring me into judgment with Yourself.
Job14:4 Who can make the clean out of the unclean? No one!
Job14:5 Since his days are determined
Job14:6 Turn Your gaze from him that he may cease from toil
Job14:7 “For there is hope for a tree
Job14:8 Though its roots grow old in the ground And its stump dies in the dry soil
Job14:9 At the scent of water it will flourish And put forth sprigs like a plant.
Job14:10 But man dies and lies prostrate. Man breathes his last
Job14:11 As water evaporates from the sea
Job14:12 So man lies down and does not rise. Until the heavens are no longer
Job14:13 “Oh that You would conceal me in Sheol
Job14:14 If a man dies
Job14:15 You will call
Job14:16 For now You number my steps
Job14:17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 “But the falling mountain crumbles away
Job14:19 Water wears away stones; Its torrents wash away the dust of the earth; So You make man’s hope perish.
Job14:20 You forever overpower him and he goes away; You alter his appearance and send him away.
Job14:21 His sons achieve honor
Job14:22 But his flesh pains him
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job15:2 “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge And fill his belly with the east wind?
Job15:3 Should he argue with a word that cannot be used
Job15:4 Indeed
Job15:5 For your iniquity teaches your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 “Were you the first man to be born
Job15:8 Do you hear the secret counsel of God
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not with us?
Job15:10 Both the gray‑haired and the aged are among us
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for you
Job15:12 Why does your heart take you away? And why do your eyes flash
Job15:13 That you should turn your spirit against God And allow such words to go out of your mouth?
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much less one who is abominable and corrupt
Job15:17 “I will tell you
Job15:18 What wise men have told
Job15:19 To whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man writhes in pain all his days
Job15:21 Sounds of dread are in his ears; While at peace the destroyer comes upon him.
Job15:22 He does not believe that he will return from darkness
Job15:23 He wanders about for food
Job15:24 Distress and anguish terrify him; They overpower him like a king ready for the attack
Job15:25 Because he has stretched out his hand against God And magnifies himself against the Almighty.
Job15:26 He rushes headlong at Him With his massive shield.
Job15:27 For he has covered his face with his fat And made his thighs heavy with flesh.
Job15:28 He has dwelt in desolate cities
Job15:29 He will not become rich
Job15:30 He will not be able to depart from darkness; The flame will wither his shoots
Job15:31 Let him not believe in emptiness
Job15:32 When his days are not yet fulfilled
Job15:33 He will drop off his unripe grape like the vine
Job15:34 For the company of the godless is barren
Job15:35 They conceive trouble and give birth to wickedness
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said
Job16:2 “I have heard many such things; Troublesome comforters are you all.
Job16:3 Is there no end to windy words? Or what pains you that you answer?
Job16:4 I too could speak like you
Job16:5 I could encourage you with my mouth
Job16:6 “If I speak
Job16:7 But now He has exhausted me; You have made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 You have shriveled me up
Job16:9 His anger has torn me and hunted me down; He has gnashed at me with His teeth; My adversary sharpens his eyes to look at me.
Job16:10 They have opened their mouth wide at me; They have struck me on the cheek in reproach; They have massed themselves against me.
Job16:11 God hands me over to ruffians And tosses me into the hands of the wicked.
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His arrows surround me. Without mercy He splits my kidneys open; He pours out my gall on the ground.
Job16:14 He breaks through me with breach after breach; He runs at me like a warrior.
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth over my skin And thrust my horn in the dust.
Job16:16 My face is flushed from weeping
Job16:17 Why?—because there is no violence in my hands
Job16:18 “O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends are my scoffers; My eye weeps to God.
Job16:21 O that a man might argue with God As a man with his neighbor!
Job16:22 For when a few years are past
Job17:1 “My spirit is broken; my days are extinguished; The grave is ready for me.
Job17:2 Surely mockers are with me
Job17:3 “Establish
Job17:4 For You have hidden their heart from insight
Job17:5 He who informs against friends for a share of the spoil
Job17:6 “But He has made me a byword of the people
Job17:7 My eye has also grown dim because of grief
Job17:8 The upright will be appalled at this
Job17:9 Nevertheless the righteous will hold to his way
Job17:10 But come again all of you now
Job17:11 My days are past; my plans are torn apart
Job17:12 They make night into day
Job17:13 If I hope for Sheol as my home
Job17:14 If I call to the pit
Job17:15 Where now is my hope? And who beholds my hope?
Job17:16 Will it go down with me to Sheol? Shall we together go down into the dust?”
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said
Job18:2 “How long until you put an end to your words? Show understanding and then we can talk.
Job18:3 Why are we regarded as beasts
Job18:4 O you who tear yourself in your anger— For your sake is the earth to be forsaken
Job18:5 “Indeed
Job18:6 The light in his tent is darkened
Job18:7 His vigorous stride is shortened
Job18:8 For he is thrown into the net by his own feet
Job18:9 A snare seizes him by the heel
Job18:10 A rope for him is hidden in the ground
Job18:11 All around terrors frighten him
Job18:12 His vigor is famished
Job18:13 The firstborn of death eats parts of his skin; It eats parts of him.
Job18:14 He is torn from the security of his tent
Job18:15 There dwells in his tent nothing of his; Brimstone is scattered on his abode.
Job18:16 His roots are dried below
Job18:17 Memory of him perishes from the earth
Job18:18 He is driven from light into darkness
Job18:19 He has neither offspring nor posterity among his people
Job18:20 Those in the west are appalled at his fate
Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the unjust
Job19:1 Then Job answered and said
Job19:2 “How long will you torment my soul And crush me with words?
Job19:3 These ten times you have dishonored me; You are not ashamed that you wrong me.
Job19:4 Even if I have truly erred
Job19:5 If truly you magnify yourselves against me And argue my disgrace to me
Job19:6 Know then that God has wronged me And has closed His net around me.
Job19:7 “Behold
Job19:8 He has walled up my way so that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He has stripped my honor from me And removed the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side
Job19:11 He has also kindled His anger against me And counted me as His adversary.
Job19:12 His troops come together
Job19:13 “He has removed my brothers far from me
Job19:14 My relatives have failed
Job19:15 Those who sojourn in my house and my maidservants count me a stranger. I am a foreigner in their sight.
Job19:16 I call to my servant
Job19:17 My breath is offensive to my wife
Job19:18 Even young children reject me; I rise up
Job19:19 All the men of my counsel abhor me
Job19:20 My bone clings to my skin and my flesh
Job19:21 Pity me
Job19:22 Why do you persecute me as God does
Job19:23 “Oh that my words were written! Oh that they were inscribed in a book!
Job19:24 That with an iron stylus and lead They were engraved in the rock forever!
Job19:25 As for me
Job19:26 Even after my skin is destroyed
Job19:27 Whom I myself shall behold
Job19:28 If you say
Job19:29 Then be afraid of the sword for yourselves
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said
Job20:2 “Therefore my disquieting thoughts make me respond
Job20:3 I listened to the discipline which dishonors me
Job20:4 Do you know this from of old
Job20:5 That the shouts of joy of the wicked are short
Job20:6 Though his loftiness goes up to the heavens
Job20:7 He perishes forever like his refuse; Those who have seen him will say
Job20:8 He flies away like a dream
Job20:9 The eye which saw him sees him no longer
Job20:10 His sons seek the favor of the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of his youthful vigor
Job20:12 “Though evil is sweet in his mouth And he hides it under his tongue
Job20:13 Though he desires it and will not forsake it
Job20:14 Yet his food in his stomach is changed To the venom of cobras within him.
Job20:15 He swallows up wealth
Job20:16 He sucks the poison of cobras; The viper’s tongue kills him.
Job20:17 He does not look at the streams
Job20:18 He returns what he has attained And cannot swallow it; As to the wealth of his trading
Job20:19 For he has crushed and forsaken the poor; He has seized a house which he has not built.
Job20:20 “Because he knew no ease within his belly
Job20:21 Nothing remains for him to devour; Therefore his prosperity does not endure.
Job20:22 In the fullness of his plenty he will be confined; The hand of everyone who is troubled will come against him.
Job20:23 So it will be that he fills his belly
Job20:24 He may flee from the iron weapon
Job20:25 It is drawn forth and comes out of his back
Job20:26 Complete darkness is held in reserve for his treasures; A fire unfanned will devour him; It will consume the survivor in his tent.
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The increase of his house will depart; His possessions will flow away in the day of His anger.
Job20:29 This is the wicked man’s portion from God
Job21:1 Then Job answered and said
Job21:2 “Listen carefully to my speech
Job21:3 Bear with me that I may speak; Then after I have spoken
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me
Job21:6 Even when I remember
Job21:7 Why do the wicked still live
Job21:8 Their seed is established with them in their presence
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from dread
Job21:10 His ox mates and does not fail; His cow calves and does not miscarry.
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like the flock
Job21:12 They lift up the tambourine and harp And are glad at the sound of the pipe.
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity
Job21:14 They say to God
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 Behold
Job21:17 “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out
Job21:18 Are they as straw before the wind
Job21:19 You say
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see his decay
Job21:21 For what desire does he have for his household after him
Job21:22 Can anyone teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in his full strength
Job21:24 His sides are filled out with fat
Job21:25 But another dies with a bitter soul
Job21:26 Together they lie down in the dust
Job21:27 “Behold
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked those who pass by along the way
Job21:30 That the wicked is reserved for the day of disaster; They will be led forth at the day of fury.
Job21:31 Who will declare to his face about his actions
Job21:32 While he is led forth to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley will gently cover him; Moreover
Job21:34 How then will you vainly comfort me
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said
Job22:2 “Can a mighty man be of use to God
Job22:3 Is there any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous
Job22:4 Is it because of your reverent fear that He reproves you
Job22:5 Is not your evil great
Job22:6 For you have taken pledges of your brothers without cause
Job22:7 To the weary you have given no water to drink
Job22:8 But the earth belongs to the mighty man
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares surround you
Job22:11 Or darkness
Job22:12 “Is not God in the height of heaven? Look also at the distant stars
Job22:13 You say
Job22:14 Clouds are a hiding place for Him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the ancient path Which wicked men have trod
Job22:16 Who were snatched away before their time
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet He filled their houses with good things
Job22:19 The righteous see and are glad
Job22:20 Saying
Job22:21 “Yield now and be at peace with Him; Thereby good will come to you.
Job22:22 Please receive instruction from His mouth And set His words in your heart.
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty
Job22:24 And put your gold in the dust
Job22:25 Then the Almighty will be your gold And choice silver to you.
Job22:26 For then you will delight in the Almighty And lift up your face to God.
Job22:27 You will entreat Him
Job22:28 You will also decree a thing
Job22:29 When some are cast down
Job22:30 He will provide escape for one who is not innocent
Job23:1 Then Job answered and said
Job23:2 “Even today my musing is rebellion; His hand is heavy despite my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh that I knew where I might find Him
Job23:4 I would arrange my case for justice before Him And fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would know the words which He would answer
Job23:6 Would He contend with me by the greatness of His power? No
Job23:7 There the upright would argue with Him; And I would have escaped forever from my Judge.
Job23:8 “Behold
Job23:9 When He acts on the left
Job23:10 But He knows the way I take; When He has tested me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to His path; I have kept His way and not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the command of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth more than my portion of food.
Job23:13 But He is unique and who can turn Him? And what His soul desires
Job23:14 For He performs what is apportioned for me
Job23:15 Therefore
Job23:16 It is God who has made my heart faint
Job23:17 But I am not silenced by the darkness
Job24:1 “Why are times not stored up by the Almighty
Job24:2 Some move the boundaries; They seize and devour flocks.
Job24:3 They drive away the donkeys of the orphans; They take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They push the needy aside from the road; The afflicted of the land are made to hide themselves altogether.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They harvest their fodder in the field And glean the vineyard of the wicked.
Job24:7 They spend the night naked
Job24:8 They are wet with the mountain rains And hug the rock for want of a shelter.
Job24:9 Others snatch the orphan from the breast
Job24:10 Those poor ones walk about naked without clothing
Job24:11 Within the walls they produce oil; They tread wine presses but thirst.
Job24:12 From the city men groan
Job24:13 “Others have been with those who rebel against the light; They do not want to recognize its ways Nor abide in its paths.
Job24:14 The murderer arises at dawn; He kills the afflicted and the needy
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer keeps watch for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig into houses; They shut themselves up by day; They do not know the light.
Job24:17 For the morning is the same to him as the shadow of death
Job24:18 “They are insignificant on the surface of the water; Their portion is cursed on the earth. They do not turn toward the vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat seize the snow waters
Job24:20 A mother will forget him; The worm feasts sweetly till he is no longer remembered. And unrighteousness will be broken like a tree.
Job24:21 He feeds on the barren woman who does not give birth And does no good for the widow.
Job24:22 But He drags off the mighty by His power; He rises
Job24:23 He provides them with security
Job24:24 They are exalted a little while
Job24:25 Now if it is not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said
Job25:2 “Rule and dread belong to Him Who makes peace in His heights.
Job25:3 Is there any number to His troops? And upon whom does His light not rise?
Job25:4 How then can mortal man be right with God? Or how can he be pure who is born of woman?
Job25:5 Behold even the moon has no brightness
Job25:6 How much less mortal man
Job26:1 Then Job answered and said
Job26:2 “What a help you are to the one without power! How you have saved the arm without strength!
Job26:3 What counsel you have given to one without wisdom! What sound wisdom you have abundantly made known!
Job26:4 To whom have you declared words? And whose breath comes out from you?
Job26:5 “The departed spirits tremble Under the waters and their inhabitants.
Job26:6 Naked is Sheol before Him
Job26:7 He stretches out the north over what is formless And hangs the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He wraps up the waters in His clouds
Job26:9 He obscures the face of His throne And spreads His cloud over it.
Job26:10 He has marked a circle on the surface of the waters At the boundary of light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble And are astonished at His rebuke.
Job26:12 He quieted the sea with His power
Job26:13 By His breath the heavens are made beautiful; His hand has pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Behold
Job27:1 Then Job continued to lift up his discourse and said
Job27:2 “As God lives
Job27:3 For as long as breath is in me
Job27:4 My lips certainly will not speak unrighteousness
Job27:5 Far be it from me that I should declare you right; Till I breathe my last I will not remove my integrity from me.
Job27:6 I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go. My heart does not reproach any of my days.
Job27:7 “May my enemy be as the wicked And the one who rises against me as the unjust.
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless when he is cut off
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry When distress comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
Job27:11 I will instruct you in the power of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 “This is the portion of a wicked man from God
Job27:14 Though his sons are many
Job27:15 His survivors will be buried because of the plague
Job27:16 Though he piles up silver like dust And prepares garments as plentiful as the clay
Job27:17 He may prepare it
Job27:18 He has built his house like the moth
Job27:19 He lies down rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like many waters; A tempest steals him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind carries him away
Job27:22 For it will hurl at him without sparing; He will surely try to flee from its power.
Job27:23 Men will clap their hands at him And will hiss him from his place.
Job28:1 “Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the dust
Job28:3 Man puts an end to darkness
Job28:4 He sinks a shaft far from habitation
Job28:5 The earth
Job28:6 Its rocks are the source of sapphires
Job28:7 The path no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it
Job28:9 He sends his hand forth to the flint; He overturns the mountains at the base.
Job28:10 He breaks out channels through the rocks
Job28:11 He dams up the streams from flowing
Job28:12 “But where can wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man does not know its worth
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 Pure gold cannot be given in place of it
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold or glass cannot meet its worth
Job28:18 Coral and crystal are not to be mentioned; And the acquisition of wisdom is above that of pearls.
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia cannot meet its worth
Job28:20 Where then does wisdom come from? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 Thus it is hidden from the eyes of all living And concealed from the birds of the sky.
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 “God understands its way
Job28:24 For He looks to the ends of the earth And sees everything under the heavens.
Job28:25 When He set weight to the wind And meted out the waters by measure
Job28:26 When He set a limit for the rain And a course for the thunderbolt
Job28:27 Then He saw it and recounted it; He established it and also searched it out.
Job28:28 So He said to man
Job29:1 And Job continued to lift up his discourse and said
Job29:2 “Oh that I were as in months gone by
Job29:3 When His lamp shone over my head
Job29:4 As I was in the prime of my days
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When my steps were bathed in butter
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate of the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me and hid
Job29:9 The princes stopped talking And put their hands on their mouths;
Job29:10 The voice of the nobles was hidden away
Job29:11 For the ear heard
Job29:12 Because I provided escape for the afflicted who cried for help
Job29:13 The blessing of the one ready to perish came upon me
Job29:14 I clothed myself with righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind And feet to the lame.
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 I broke the fangs of the unjust And snatched the prey from his teeth.
Job29:18 Then I said
Job29:19 My root is spread out to the waters
Job29:20 My glory is ever new with me
Job29:21 “To me they listened and waited
Job29:22 After my words they did not speak again
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain
Job29:24 I laughed with them as they could not believe it
Job29:25 I chose a way for them and sat as chief
Job30:1 “But now those younger than I laugh at me
Job30:2 Indeed
Job30:3 From want and famine they are gaunt
Job30:4 Who pluck mallow by the bushes
Job30:5 They are driven from the community; They shout against them as against a thief
Job30:6 So that they dwell in the slopes of the valleys
Job30:7 Among the bushes they cry out; Under the nettles they are gathered together.
Job30:8 Wicked fools
Job30:9 “And now I have become their mocking song; I have even become a taunting word to them.
Job30:10 They abhor me and keep a distance from me
Job30:11 Because He has loosed His bowstring and afflicted me
Job30:12 On the right hand their brood arises; They thrust aside my feet and build up against me their ways to disaster.
Job30:13 They break up my path; They profit from my destruction; They have no helper.
Job30:14 As through a wide breach they come
Job30:15 Terrors are turned against me; They pursue my nobility as the wind
Job30:16 “And now my soul is poured out within me; Days of affliction have seized me.
Job30:17 At night it pierces my bones within me
Job30:18 By a great force my garment is distorted; It seizes me about as the collar of my tunic.
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry out to You for help
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me; With the might of Your hand You hunted me down.
Job30:22 You lift me up to the wind and cause me to ride; And You melt me away in a storm.
Job30:23 For I know that You will bring me to death And to the house of meeting for all living.
Job30:24 “Yet does not one in a heap of ruins stretch out his hand
Job30:25 Have I not wept for the one whose life is hard? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?
Job30:26 When I hoped for good
Job30:27 I am boiling within and cannot be silent; Days of affliction confront me.
Job30:28 I go about darkened but not by the sun; I stand up in the assembly and cry out for help.
Job30:29 I have become a brother to jackals And a companion of ostriches.
Job30:30 My skin turns black on me
Job30:31 Therefore my harp is turned to mourning
Job31:1 “I have cut a covenant with my eyes; How then could I gaze at a virgin?
Job31:2 And what is the portion of God from above Or the inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
Job31:3 Is it not disaster to the unjust And misfortune to those who work iniquity?
Job31:4 Does He not see my ways And number all my steps?
Job31:5 “If I have walked with worthlessness
Job31:6 Let Him weigh me with just scales
Job31:7 If my step has turned from the way
Job31:8 Let me sow and another eat
Job31:9 “If my heart has been enticed by a woman
Job31:10 May my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For that would be lewdness; Moreover
Job31:12 For it would be fire that consumes to Abaddon
Job31:13 “If I have rejected the justice of my male or female slaves When they filed a complaint against me
Job31:14 What then could I do when God arises? And when He calls me to account
Job31:15 Did not He who made me in the womb make him
Job31:16 “If I have held back the poor from their desire
Job31:17 Or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 (But from my youth he grew up with me as with a father
Job31:19 If I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 If his loins have not blessed me
Job31:21 If I have waved my hand against the orphan Because I saw my help in the gate
Job31:22 Let my shoulder fall from the socket
Job31:23 For disaster from God is a dread to me
Job31:24 “If I have put my confidence in gold
Job31:25 If I have been glad because my wealth was great
Job31:26 If I have looked at the sun when it shone Or the moon going in splendor
Job31:27 And my heart became secretly enticed
Job31:28 That too would have been an iniquity calling for judgment
Job31:29 “If I have been glad at the upheaval of the one who hated me
Job31:30 But I have not given over my mouth to sin By asking for his life in a curse—
Job31:31 If the men of my tent have not said
Job31:32 The sojourner has not lodged outside
Job31:33 If I have covered my transgressions like Adam
Job31:34 Because I feared the great multitude
Job31:35 Oh that I had one to hear me! Behold
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; I would bind it to myself like a crown.
Job31:37 I would declare to Him the number of my steps; Like a prince I would approach Him—
Job31:38 “If my land cries out against me
Job31:39 If I have eaten its fruit without money
Job31:40 Let briars come out instead of wheat
Job32:1 Then these three men ceased answering Job because he was righteous in his own eyes.
Job32:2 But the anger of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite
Job32:3 And his anger burned against his three friends because they had found no answer
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited with his words for Job because they were years older than he.
Job32:5 Then Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of the three men
Job32:6 So Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said
Job32:7 I thought age should speak
Job32:8 But it is a spirit in man
Job32:9 The abundant in years may not be wise
Job32:10 So I say
Job32:11 “Behold
Job32:12 I even carefully considered you; And behold
Job32:13 Lest you say
Job32:14 Now he has not arranged his words against me
Job32:15 “They are dismayed; they no longer answer; Words have moved away from them.
Job32:16 Shall I wait
Job32:17 I myself will also answer my share; I also will tell my knowledge.
Job32:18 For I am full of words; The spirit within my belly presses me.
Job32:19 Behold
Job32:20 Let me speak that I may get relief; Let me open my lips and answer.
Job32:21 Let me now be partial to no one
Job32:22 For I do not know how to flatter
Job33:1 “However now
Job33:2 Behold now
Job33:3 My words are from the uprightness of my heart
Job33:4 The Spirit of God has made me
Job33:5 Respond to me if you can; Arrange yourselves before me
Job33:6 Behold
Job33:7 Behold
Job33:8 “Surely you have spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 ‘I am pure
Job33:10 Behold
Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks; He keeps watch over all my paths.’
Job33:12 Behold
Job33:13 “Why do you contend against Him? For He does not give an answer for all His doings.
Job33:14 Indeed God speaks once
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 Then He opens the ears of men
Job33:17 That He may turn man aside from his conduct
Job33:18 He holds back his soul from the pit
Job33:19 “Man is also reproved with pain on his bed
Job33:20 So that his life loathes bread
Job33:21 His flesh wastes away from sight
Job33:22 Then his soul draws near to the pit
Job33:23 “If there is an angel as mediator for him
Job33:24 Then let him be gracious to him
Job33:25 Let his flesh become fresher than in youth
Job33:26 Then he will entreat God
Job33:27 He will sing to men and say
Job33:28 He has redeemed my soul from passing into the pit
Job33:29 “Behold
Job33:30 To bring back his soul from the pit
Job33:31 Pay attention
Job33:32 Then if you have any speech
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Then Elihu answered and said
Job34:2 “Hear my speech
Job34:3 For the ear tests words As the palate tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us choose for ourselves what is just; Let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job has said
Job34:6 Should I lie concerning my justice? My wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 But he travels in company with the workers of iniquity
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 “Therefore
Job34:11 For He pays a man according to his work
Job34:12 Truly
Job34:13 Who appointed Him with authority over the earth? And who has laid on Him the whole world?
Job34:14 If He should set His heart on it
Job34:15 All flesh would breathe its last together
Job34:16 “But if you have understanding
Job34:17 Shall one who hates justice rule? And will you condemn the righteous mighty One
Job34:18 Who says to a king
Job34:19 Who shows no partiality to princes Nor recognizes the rich above the poor
Job34:20 In a moment they die
Job34:21 “For His eyes are upon the ways of a man
Job34:22 There is no darkness or shadow of death Where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
Job34:23 For He does not need to consider a man further
Job34:24 He breaks in pieces mighty men without searching anything out
Job34:25 Therefore He recognizes their labors
Job34:26 He strikes them like the wicked In a public place
Job34:27 Because they turned aside from following Him
Job34:28 So that they caused the cry of the poor to come to Him
Job34:29 So if He keeps quiet
Job34:30 So that godless men would not rule Nor be snares of the people.
Job34:31 “For has anyone said to God
Job34:32 Instruct me what I do not behold; If I have done injustice
Job34:33 Shall He repay on your terms
Job34:34 Men with a heart of wisdom will say to me
Job34:35 ‘Job speaks without knowledge
Job34:36 Job ought to be tested to the limit Because he answers like wicked men.
Job34:37 For he adds transgression to his sin; He strikes his hands together among us
Job35:1 Then Elihu answered and said
Job35:2 “Do you think this is according to justice? Do you say
Job35:3 For you say
Job35:4 I will respond to you
Job35:5 Look at the heavens and see; And perceive the clouds—they are higher than you.
Job35:6 If you have sinned
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness is for a man like yourself
Job35:9 “Because of many oppressions they cry out; They cry for help because of the arm of many oppressors.
Job35:10 But no one says
Job35:11 Who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth And makes us wiser than the birds of the heavens?’
Job35:12 There they cry out
Job35:13 Surely God will not listen to an empty cry
Job35:14 How much less when you say you do not perceive Him
Job35:15 And now
Job35:16 So Job opens his mouth vainly; He multiplies words without knowledge.”
Job36:1 Then Elihu continued and said
Job36:2 “Wait for me a little
Job36:3 I will take up my knowledge from afar
Job36:4 For truly my words are not a lie; One who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 He does not keep the wicked alive
Job36:7 He does not withdraw His eyes from the righteous; But with kings on the throne He has seated them forever
Job36:8 And if they are bound in fetters
Job36:9 Then He declares to them their work And their transgressions
Job36:10 He opens their ear to discipline
Job36:11 If they hear and serve Him
Job36:12 But if they do not hear
Job36:13 But the godless in heart lay up anger; They do not cry for help when He binds them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivers the afflicted in their affliction
Job36:16 Then indeed
Job36:17 “But you were full of judgment on the wicked; Judgment and justice take hold of you.
Job36:18 Beware lest wrath entice you to scoffing; And do not let the greatness of the atonement turn you aside.
Job36:19 Will your cries keep you from distress
Job36:20 Do not long for the night
Job36:21 Be careful
Job36:22 Behold
Job36:23 Who has appointed Him His way
Job36:24 “Remember that you should exalt His work
Job36:25 All men have beheld it; Man looks from afar.
Job36:26 Behold
Job36:27 For He draws up the drops of water
Job36:28 Which the clouds pour down
Job36:29 Can anyone discern the spreading of the clouds
Job36:30 Behold
Job36:31 For by these He judges peoples; He gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covers His hands with the lightning
Job36:33 Its thundering declares about Him; The cattle also
Job37:1 “At this also my heart trembles
Job37:2 Listen closely to the thunder of His voice
Job37:3 Under the whole heaven He lets it loose
Job37:4 After it
Job37:5 God thunders with His voice marvelously
Job37:6 For to the snow He says
Job37:7 He seals the hand of every man
Job37:8 Then the beast goes into its lair And dwells in its den.
Job37:9 Out of the south comes the storm
Job37:10 From the breath of God ice is made
Job37:11 Also with moisture He loads the thick cloud; He scatters the cloud of His lightning.
Job37:12 It changes direction
Job37:13 Whether for correction
Job37:14 “Give ear to this
Job37:15 Do you know how God establishes them
Job37:16 Do you know about the layers of the thick clouds
Job37:17 You whose garments are hot
Job37:18 Can you
Job37:19 Make us know what we shall say to Him; We cannot arrange our case because of darkness.
Job37:20 Shall it be recounted to Him that I would speak? If a man says a word
Job37:21 “So now men do not see the light which is bright in the skies; But the wind has passed and cleared them.
Job37:22 Out of the north comes golden splendor; Around God is fearsome majesty.
Job37:23 The Almighty—we cannot find Him; He is exalted in power And He will not afflict justice and abundant righteousness.
Job37:24 Therefore men fear Him; He does not regard any who are wise of heart.”
Job38:1 Then Yahweh answered Job out of the whirlwind and said
Job38:2 “Who is this that darkens counsel By words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Now gird up your loins like a man
Job38:4 Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:5 Who set its measurements? Since you know. Or who stretched the line on it?
Job38:6 On what were its bases sunk? Or who laid its cornerstone
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together And all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Job38:8 “Or who enclosed the sea with doors When
Job38:9 When I made a cloud its garment And dense gloom its swaddling band
Job38:10 And I placed boundaries on it And set a bolt and doors
Job38:11 And I said
Job38:12 “Have you ever in your life commanded the morning
Job38:13 That it might seize the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed like clay under the seal; And they stand forth like clothing.
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 “Have you entered into the springs of the sea Or walked in the recesses of the deep?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you
Job38:18 Have you carefully considered the expanse of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:19 “Where is the way to where the light dwells? And darkness
Job38:20 That you may take it to its territory And that you may discern the paths to its home?
Job38:21 You know
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouses of the snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for the time of distress
Job38:24 Where is the way that the light is divided
Job38:25 “Who has cleft a conduit for the flood
Job38:26 To bring rain on a land without people
Job38:27 To satisfy the waste and desolate land And to make the growth of grass to sprout?
Job38:28 Has the rain a father? Or who has begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 From whose womb has come the ice? And the frost of heaven
Job38:30 Water becomes hard like stone
Job38:31 “Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you lead forth a constellation in its season
Job38:33 Do you know the statutes of the heavens
Job38:34 “Can you raise your voice up to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send forth lightnings that they may go And say to you
Job38:36 Who has given wisdom in the innermost being Or given understanding to the mind?
Job38:37 Who can count the clouds by wisdom
Job38:38 When the dust hardens into a mass And the clods stick together?
Job38:39 “Can you hunt the prey for the lion
Job38:40 When they crouch in their dens And lie in wait in their lair?
Job38:41 Who prepares for the raven its provision When its young cry for help to God And wander about without food?
Job39:1 “Do you know the time the mountain goats give birth? Do you keep watch over the calving of the deer?
Job39:2 Can you count the months they fulfill
Job39:3 They kneel down; they bring forth their young; They send out their labor pains.
Job39:4 Their children become strong; they grow up in the open field; They leave and do not return to them.
Job39:5 “Who sent out the wild donkey free? And who loosed the bonds of the swift donkey
Job39:6 For whom I have set the desert plain as a home And the salt land as his dwelling place?
Job39:7 He laughs at the tumult of the city; The shoutings of the driver he does not hear.
Job39:8 He explores the mountains for his pasture And searches after every green thing.
Job39:9 Will the wild ox consent to serve you
Job39:10 Can you bind the wild ox in a furrow with ropes
Job39:11 Will you trust him because his power is great And leave your labor to him?
Job39:12 Will you believe him that he will return your seed of grain And gather it from your threshing floor?
Job39:13 “The ostriches’ wings flap joyously But are they the pinion and plumage of a stork?
Job39:14 For she leaves her eggs to the earth And warms them in the dust
Job39:15 And she forgets that a foot may crush them
Job39:16 She treats her children cruelly
Job39:17 Because God has made her forget wisdom
Job39:18 When she raises herself up high
Job39:19 “Do you give the horse his might? Do you clothe his neck with a mane?
Job39:20 Do you make him leap like the locust? His splendid snorting is terrible.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley and rejoices in his power; He goes out to meet the weapons.
Job39:22 He laughs at dread and is not dismayed; And he does not turn back from the sword.
Job39:23 The quiver rattles against him
Job39:24 With shaking and rage he races over the ground
Job39:25 As often as the trumpet sounds he says
Job39:26 “Is it by your understanding that the hawk soars
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle goes on high And raises his nest high?
Job39:28 On the cliff he dwells and lodges
Job39:29 From there he spies out food; His eyes see it from afar.
Job39:30 His young ones also suck up blood; And where the slain are
Job40:1 Then Yahweh answered Job and said
Job40:2 “Will the faultfinder contend with the Almighty? Let him who reproves God answer it.”
Job40:3 Then Job answered Yahweh and said
Job40:4 “Behold
Job40:5 Once I have spoken
Job40:6 Then Yahweh answered Job out of the whirlwind and said
Job40:7 “Now gird up your loins like a man; I will ask you
Job40:8 Will you really annul My judgment? Will you condemn Me that you may be justified?
Job40:9 Or do you have an arm like God
Job40:10 “Adorn yourself with exaltation and loftiness
Job40:11 Pour out the overflowings of your anger
Job40:12 Look on everyone who is proud
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together; Bind their faces in the hidden place.
Job40:14 Then I will also praise you
Job40:15 “Behold now
Job40:16 Behold now
Job40:17 It hangs its tail like a cedar; The sinews of its thighs are knit together.
Job40:18 Its bones are tubes of bronze; Its limbs are like bars of iron.
Job40:19 “It is the first of the ways of God; Let its Maker bring near its sword.
Job40:20 Surely the mountains bring it food
Job40:21 Under the lotus plants it lies down
Job40:22 The lotus plants cover it with shade; The willows of the brook surround it.
Job40:23 If a river oppresses
Job40:24 Can anyone capture it before its eyes
Job41:1 “Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook? Or press down its tongue with a cord?
Job41:2 Can you put a rope in its nose Or pierce its jaw with a hook?
Job41:3 Will it make many supplications to you
Job41:4 Will it cut a covenant with you? Will you take it for a slave forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with it as with a bird
Job41:6 Will the traders bargain over it? Will they divide it among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill its skin with harpoons
Job41:8 Place your hand on it; Remember the battle; you will not do that again!
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 No one is so fierce that he dares to arouse it; Who then is he that can stand before Me?
Job41:11 Who has given to Me that I should repay him? Whatever is under the whole heaven is Mine.
Job41:12 “I will not keep silence concerning its limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off its outer armor? Who can come with its doubled bridle?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of its face? Around its teeth there is dreadful terror.
Job41:15 Its strong scales are its pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another That no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They cling one to another; They are interlocked and cannot be separated.
Job41:18 Its sneezes flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of its mouth go burning torches; Sparks of fire leap forth.
Job41:20 Out of its nostrils smoke goes forth As from a boiling pot and burning reeds.
Job41:21 Its breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In its neck lodges strength
Job41:23 The folds of its flesh cling together
Job41:24 Its heart is as hard as a stone
Job41:25 When it raises itself up
Job41:26 The sword that reaches it cannot avail
Job41:27 It regards iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make it flee; Slingstones are turned into stubble for it.
Job41:29 Clubs are regarded as stubble; It laughs at the rattling of the javelin.
Job41:30 Its underparts are like sharp potsherds; It spreads out like a threshing sledge on the mire.
Job41:31 It makes the depths boil like a pot; It makes the sea like a jar of ointment.
Job41:32 Behind it
Job41:33 There is nothing upon the dust like it
Job41:34 It looks on everything that is high; It is king over all the sons of pride.”
Job42:1 Then Job answered Yahweh and said
Job42:2 “I know that You can do all things
Job42:3 ‘Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?’ Therefore I have declared that which I did not understand
Job42:4 ‘Hear
Job42:5 I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear; But now my eye sees You;
Job42:6 Therefore I reject myself
Job42:7 Now it happened after Yahweh had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 So now
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as Yahweh told them; and Yahweh accepted Job.
Job42:10 And Yahweh restored the fortunes of Job when he prayed for his friends
Job42:11 Then all his brothers and all his sisters and all who had known him before came to him
Job42:12 And Yahweh blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; and he had 14
Job42:13 He also had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he named the first Jemimah and the second Keziah and the third Keren-happuch.
Job42:15 Now in all the land no women were found so beautiful as Job’s daughters; and their father gave them inheritance among their brothers.
Job42:16 And after this
Job42:17 Then Job died
Psalms1:1 How blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked
Psalms1:2 But his delight is in the law of Yahweh
Psalms1:3 And he will be like a tree firmly planted by streams of water
Psalms1:4 The wicked are not so
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not rise in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For Yahweh knows the way of the righteous
Psalms2:1 Why do the nations rage And the peoples meditate on a vain thing?
Psalms2:2 The kings of the earth take their stand And the rulers take counsel together Against Yahweh and against His Anointed
Psalms2:3 “Let us tear their fetters apart And cast away their cords from us!”
Psalms2:4 He who sits in the heavens laughs
Psalms2:5 Then He speaks to them in His anger And terrifies them in His fury
Psalms2:6 “But as for Me
Psalms2:7 “I will surely tell of the decree of Yahweh: He said to Me
Psalms2:8 Ask of Me
Psalms2:9 You shall break them with a rod of iron
Psalms2:10 So now
Psalms2:11 Serve Yahweh with fear And rejoice with trembling.
Psalms2:12 Kiss the Son
Psalms3:1 A Psalm of David. When he fled from Absalom his son. O Yahweh
Psalms3:2 Many are saying of my soul
Psalms3:3 But You
Psalms3:4 I was calling to Yahweh with my voice
Psalms3:5 I lay down and slept; I awoke
Psalms3:6 I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people Who all around have set themselves against me.
Psalms3:7 Arise
Psalms3:8 Salvation belongs to Yahweh; Your blessing be upon Your people! Selah.
Psalms4:1 For the choir director. With stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Answer me when I call
Psalms4:2 O sons of men
Psalms4:3 But know that Yahweh has set apart the holy one for Himself; Yahweh hears when I call to Him.
Psalms4:4 Tremble
Psalms4:5 Offer the sacrifices of righteousness
Psalms4:6 Many are saying
Psalms4:7 You have put gladness in my heart
Psalms4:8 In peace I will both lie down and sleep
Psalms5:1 For the choir director. For the flutes. A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words
Psalms5:2 Give heed to the sound of my cry for help
Psalms5:3 O Yahweh
Psalms5:4 For You are not a God who delights in wickedness; Evil does not sojourn with You.
Psalms5:5 The boastful shall not stand before Your eyes; You hate all workers of iniquity.
Psalms5:6 You destroy those who speak falsehood; Yahweh abhors the man of bloodshed and deceit.
Psalms5:7 But as for me
Psalms5:8 O Yahweh
Psalms5:9 There is nothing reliable in their mouth; Their inward part is destruction itself. Their throat is an open grave; They flatter with their tongue.
Psalms5:10 Hold them guilty
Psalms5:11 But let all who take refuge in You be glad
Psalms5:12 For it is You who blesses the righteous one
Psalms6:1 For the choir director. With stringed instruments. According to the Sheminith. A Psalm of David. O Yahweh
Psalms6:2 Be gracious to me
Psalms6:3 And my soul is greatly dismayed; But You
Psalms6:4 Return
Psalms6:5 For there is no remembrance of You in death; In Sheol who will give You thanks?
Psalms6:6 I am weary with my sighing; Every night I make my bed swim
Psalms6:7 My eye has wasted away with grief; It has become old because of all my adversaries.
Psalms6:8 Depart from me
Psalms6:9 Yahweh has heard my supplication
Psalms6:10 All my enemies will be ashamed and greatly dismayed; They shall turn back
Psalms7:1 A Shiggaion of David
Psalms7:2 Lest he tear my soul like a lion
Psalms7:3 O Yahweh my God
Psalms7:4 If I have rewarded evil to him who is at peace with me
Psalms7:5 Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it; And let him trample my life down to the ground And cause my glory to dwell in the dust. Selah.
Psalms7:6 Arise
Psalms7:7 Let the congregation of the peoples encompass You
Psalms7:8 Yahweh judges the peoples; Give justice to me
Psalms7:9 O let the evil of the wicked come to an end
Psalms7:10 My shield is with God
Psalms7:11 God is a righteous judge
Psalms7:12 If a man does not repent
Psalms7:13 He has also prepared for Himself deadly weapons; He makes His arrows fiery shafts.
Psalms7:14 Behold
Psalms7:15 He has dug a pit and hollowed it out
Psalms7:16 His mischief will return upon his own head
Psalms7:17 I will give thanks to Yahweh according to His righteousness And will sing praise to the name of Yahweh Most High.

Psalms9:1 For the choir director. Almuth-labben. A Psalm of David. I will give thanks to Yahweh with all my heart; I will recount all Your wondrous deeds.
Psalms9:2 I will be glad and exult in You; I will sing praise to Your name
Psalms9:3 When my enemies turn back
Psalms9:4 For You have maintained my justice and my cause; You have sat on the throne judging righteously.
Psalms9:5 You have rebuked the nations
Psalms9:6 The enemy has come to an end in perpetual ruins
Psalms9:7 But Yahweh abides forever; He has established His throne for judgment
Psalms9:8 And He will judge the world in righteousness; He will render justice for the peoples with equity.
Psalms9:9 Yahweh also will be a stronghold for the oppressed
Psalms9:10 And those who know Your name will put their trust in You
Psalms9:11 Sing praises to Yahweh
Psalms9:12 For He who requires blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
Psalms9:13 Be gracious to me
Psalms9:14 That I may recount all Your praises
Psalms9:15 The nations have sunk down in the pit which they have made; In the net which they hid
Psalms9:16 Yahweh has made Himself known; He has executed judgment. In the work of his own hands the wicked is snared. Higgaion Selah.
Psalms9:17 The wicked will return to Sheol
Psalms9:18 For the needy will not always be forgotten

Psalms10:4 The wicked
Psalms10:5 His ways prosper at all times; Your judgments are on high
Psalms10:6 He says in his heart

Psalms10:11 He says in his heart

Psalms16:1 A Mikhtam of David. Keep me
Psalms16:2 O my soul
Psalms16:3 As for the saints who are in the earth
Psalms16:4 The pains of those who have bartered for another god will be multiplied; I shall not pour out their drink offerings of blood
Psalms16:5 Yahweh is the portion of my inheritance and my cup; You support my lot.
Psalms16:6 The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; Indeed
Psalms16:7 I will bless Yahweh who has counseled me; Indeed
Psalms16:8 I have set Yahweh continually before me; Because He is at my right hand
Psalms16:9 Therefore my heart is glad and my glory rejoices; My flesh also will dwell securely.
Psalms16:10 For You will not forsake my soul to Sheol; You will not give Your Holy One over to see corruption.
Psalms16:11 You will make known to me the path of life; In Your presence is fullness of joy; In Your right hand there are pleasures forever.
Psalms17:1 A Prayer of David. Hear a righteous cause
Psalms17:2 May my judgment come from Your presence; May Your eyes behold what is upright.
Psalms17:3 You have tested my heart; You have visited me by night; You have tried me and You find nothing; I have purposed that my mouth will not transgress.
Psalms17:4 As for the deeds of men
Psalms17:5 My steps have held fast to Your paths. My footsteps have not stumbled.
Psalms17:6 I have called upon You
Psalms17:7 Marvelously show Your lovingkindnesses
Psalms17:8 Keep me as the apple of the eye; Hide me in the shadow of Your wings
Psalms17:9 From the wicked who devastate me

Psalms17:15 As for me
Psalms18:1 For the choir director. Of the servant of Yahweh
Psalms18:2 Yahweh is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer
Psalms18:3 I call upon Yahweh
Psalms18:4 The cords of death encompassed me
Psalms18:5 The cords of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon Yahweh
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and quaked; And the foundations of the mountains were trembling And were shaken
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up out of His nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens and came down With thick darkness under His feet.
Psalms18:10 He rode upon a cherub and flew; And He sped upon the wings of the wind.
Psalms18:11 He made darkness His hiding place
Psalms18:12 From the brightness before Him passed His thick clouds
Psalms18:13 Yahweh also thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 He sent out His arrows
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of water appeared
Psalms18:16 He sent from on high
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my disaster
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into a broad place; He rescued me
Psalms18:20 Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of Yahweh
Psalms18:22 For all His judgments were before me
Psalms18:23 I was also blameless with Him
Psalms18:24 Therefore Yahweh has recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the kind You show Yourself kind; With the blameless You show Yourself blameless;
Psalms18:26 With the pure You show Yourself pure
Psalms18:27 For You save an afflicted people
Psalms18:28 For You light my lamp; Yahweh my God illumines my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by You I can run upon a troop; And by my God I can leap over a wall.
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 The God who girds me with strength And makes my way blameless?
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet like hinds’ feet
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for battle
Psalms18:35 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation
Psalms18:36 You enlarge my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued my enemies and overtook them
Psalms18:38 I crushed them
Psalms18:39 For You have girded me with strength for battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me
Psalms18:41 They cried for help
Psalms18:42 Then I beat them fine as the dust before the wind; I emptied them out as the mire of the streets.
Psalms18:43 You have delivered me from the contentions of the people; You have placed me as head of the nations; A people whom I have not known serve me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear
Psalms18:45 Foreigners fade away
Psalms18:46 Yahweh lives
Psalms18:47 The God who executes vengeance for me
Psalms18:48 Who delivers me from my enemies; Surely You lift me above those who rise up against me; You rescue me from the violent man.
Psalms18:49 Therefore I will give thanks to You among the nations
Psalms18:50 He gives great salvation to His king

Psalms19:2 Day to day pours forth speech
Psalms19:3 There is no speech
Psalms19:4 Their line has gone out through all the earth
Psalms19:5 Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber; It rejoices as a strong man to run his course.
Psalms19:6 Its rising is from one end of the heavens
Psalms19:7 The law of Yahweh is perfect
Psalms19:8 The precepts of Yahweh are right
Psalms19:9 The fear of Yahweh is clean
Psalms19:10 They are more desirable than gold
Psalms19:11 Moreover
Psalms19:12 Who can discern his errors? Acquit me of hidden faults.
Psalms19:13 Also keep back Your slave from presumptuous sins; Let them not rule over me; Then I will be blameless
Psalms19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be acceptable in Your sight

Psalms21:2 You have given him his heart’s desire
Psalms21:3 For You meet him with the blessings of good things; You set a crown of fine gold on his head.
Psalms21:4 He asked life of You
Psalms21:5 His glory is great through Your salvation
Psalms21:6 For You make him most blessed forever; You make him joyful with gladness in Your presence.
Psalms21:7 For the king trusts in Yahweh
Psalms21:8 Your hand will find out all your enemies; Your right hand will find out those who hate you.
Psalms21:9 You will make them as a fiery oven in the time of your anger; Yahweh will swallow them up in His wrath
Psalms21:10 Their offspring You will destroy from the earth
Psalms21:11 Though they intended evil against You And devised a scheme
Psalms21:12 For You will make them turn their back; You will aim with Your bowstrings at their faces.

Psalms22:1 For the choir director. According to Aijeleth Hashshahar. A Psalm of David. My God
Psalms22:2 O my God
Psalms22:3 Yet You are holy
Psalms22:4 In You our fathers trusted; They trusted and You rescued them.
Psalms22:5 To You they cried out and were granted escape; In You they trusted and were not disappointed.
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm and not a man
Psalms22:7 All who see me mock me; They smack their lip
Psalms22:8 “Commit yourself to Yahweh; let Him rescue him; Let Him deliver him
Psalms22:9 Yet You are He who brought me out of the womb; You made me trust when upon my mother’s breasts.
Psalms22:10 Upon You I was cast from birth; You have been my God from my mother’s womb.
Psalms22:11 Be not far from me
Psalms22:12 Many bulls have surrounded me; Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled me.
Psalms22:13 They open wide their mouth at me
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd
Psalms22:16 For dogs have surrounded me; A band of evildoers has encompassed me; They pierced my hands and my feet.
Psalms22:17 I count all my bones. They look
Psalms22:18 They divide my garments among them
Psalms22:19 But You
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword
Psalms22:21 Save me from the mouth of the lion; From the horns of the wild oxen You have answered me.
Psalms22:22 I will surely recount Your name to my brothers; In the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
Psalms22:23 You who fear Yahweh
Psalms22:24 For He has not despised and He has not abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; And He has not hidden His face from him; But when he cried to Him for help
Psalms22:25 Of You is my praise in the great assembly; I shall pay my vows before those who fear Him.
Psalms22:26 The afflicted will eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise Yahweh. May your heart live forever!
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to Yahweh
Psalms22:28 For the kingdom is Yahweh’s And He rules over the nations.
Psalms22:29 All the prosperous of the earth will eat and worship
Psalms22:30 Their seed will serve Him; It will be recounted about the Lord to the coming generation.
Psalms22:31 They will come and will declare His righteousness To a people who will be born

Psalms31:2 Incline Your ear to me
Psalms31:3 For You are my high rock and my fortress; For Your name’s sake You will lead me and guide me.
Psalms31:4 You will bring me out of the net which they have secretly laid for me
Psalms31:5 Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have ransomed me
Psalms31:6 I hate those who regard worthless idols
Psalms31:7 I will rejoice and be glad in Your lovingkindness
Psalms31:8 And You have not given me over into the hand of the enemy; You have set my feet in a large place.
Psalms31:9 Be gracious to me
Psalms31:10 For my life is worn down with sorrow And my years with sighing; My strength fails because of my iniquity
Psalms31:11 Among all my adversaries
Psalms31:12 I am forgotten like a dead man—out of mind— I am like a broken vessel.
Psalms31:13 For I have heard the bad report of many
Psalms31:14 But as for me
Psalms31:15 My times are in Your hand; Deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who pursue me.

Psalms31:18 Let the lying lips be mute

Psalms31:23 Oh
