Psalms32:1 Of David. A Maskil. How blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven
Psalms32:2 How blessed is the man whose iniquity Yahweh will not take into account
Psalms32:3 When I kept silent about my sin
Psalms32:4 For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; My vitality was drained away as with the heat of summer. Selah.
Psalms32:5 I acknowledged my sin to You
Psalms32:6 Therefore
Psalms32:7 You are my hiding place; You guard me from trouble; You surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.
Psalms32:8 I will give you insight and teach you in the way which you should go; I will counsel you with My eye upon you.
Psalms32:9 Do not be as the horse or as the mule which have no understanding
Psalms32:10 Many are the sorrows of the wicked
Psalms32:11 Be glad in Yahweh and rejoice
Psalms33:1 Sing for joy in Yahweh
Psalms33:2 Give thanks to Yahweh with the lyre; Sing praises to Him with a harp of ten strings.
Psalms33:3 Sing to Him a new song; Play skillfully with a loud shout.
Psalms33:4 For the word of Yahweh is upright
Psalms33:5 He loves righteousness and justice; The earth is full of the lovingkindness of Yahweh.
Psalms33:6 By the word of Yahweh the heavens were made
Psalms33:7 He gathers the waters of the sea as a heap; He lays up the deeps in storehouses.
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear Yahweh; Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
Psalms33:9 For He spoke
Psalms33:10 Yahweh nullifies the counsel of the nations; He frustrates the thoughts of the peoples.
Psalms33:11 The counsel of Yahweh stands forever
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is Yahweh
Psalms33:13 Yahweh looks from heaven; He sees all the sons of men;
Psalms33:14 From the place of His habitation He gazes On all the inhabitants of the earth
Psalms33:15 He who forms the hearts of them all
Psalms33:16 The king is not saved by a mighty army; A warrior is not delivered by great strength.
Psalms33:17 A horse is a false hope for salvation; Nor does it provide escape to anyone by its great strength.
Psalms33:18 Behold
Psalms33:19 To deliver their soul from death And to keep them alive in famine.
Psalms33:20 Our soul is patient for Yahweh; He is our help and our shield.
Psalms33:21 For our heart is glad in Him
Psalms33:22 Let Your lovingkindness
Psalms34:1 Of David. When he feigned madness before Abimelech
Psalms34:2 My soul will make its boast in Yahweh; The humble will hear it and rejoice.
Psalms34:3 O magnify Yahweh with me
Psalms34:4 I inquired of Yahweh
Psalms34:5 They looked to Him and were radiant
Psalms34:6 This poor man called out
Psalms34:7 The angel of Yahweh encamps around those who fear Him
Psalms34:8 O taste and see that Yahweh is good; How blessed is the man who takes refuge in Him!
Psalms34:9 Oh
Psalms34:10 The young lions do lack and suffer hunger; But they who inquire of Yahweh shall not be in want of any good thing.
Psalms34:11 Come
Psalms34:12 Who is the man who delights in life And loves many days that he may see good?
Psalms34:13 Guard your tongue from evil And your lips from speaking deceit.
Psalms34:14 Depart from evil and do good; Seek peace and pursue it.
Psalms34:15 The eyes of Yahweh are toward the righteous And His ears are open to their cry for help.
Psalms34:16 The face of Yahweh is against evildoers
Psalms34:17 The righteous cry
Psalms34:18 Yahweh is near to the brokenhearted And saves those who are crushed in spirit.
Psalms34:19 Many are the evils against the righteous
Psalms34:20 He keeps all his bones
Psalms34:21 Evil shall slay the wicked
Psalms34:22 Yahweh redeems the soul of His slaves
Psalms35:1 Of David. Contend
Psalms35:2 Take hold of shield and large shield And rise up for my help.
Psalms35:3 Draw also the spear and the battle‑axe to meet those who pursue me; Say to my soul
Psalms35:4 Let those be ashamed and dishonored who seek my life; Let those who devise evil against me be turned back and humiliated.
Psalms35:5 Let them be like chaff before the wind
Psalms35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery
Psalms35:7 For without cause they hid their net for me; Without cause they dug a pit for my soul.
Psalms35:8 Let destruction
Psalms35:9 And my soul shall rejoice in Yahweh; It shall be joyful in His salvation.
Psalms35:10 All my bones will say
Psalms35:11 Malicious witnesses rise up
Psalms35:12 They repay me evil for good
Psalms35:13 But as for me
Psalms35:14 I walked about as though it were my friend or brother; I bowed down mourning
Psalms35:15 But at my stumbling they were glad and gathered themselves together; The smiters whom I did not know gathered together against me
Psalms35:16 Amongst the godless jesters at a feast
Psalms35:17 Lord
Psalms35:18 I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among a mighty people.
Psalms35:19 Let those who are wrongfully my enemies not be glad over me; Nor let those who hate me without cause wink maliciously.
Psalms35:20 For they do not speak peace
Psalms35:21 They opened their mouth wide against me; They said
Psalms35:22 You have seen it
Psalms35:23 Stir up Yourself
Psalms35:24 Judge me
Psalms35:25 Do not let them say in their heart
Psalms35:26 Let those be ashamed and humiliated altogether who are glad at the evil done to me; Let those be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves over me.
Psalms35:27 Let them shout for joy and be glad
Psalms35:28 And my tongue shall utter Your righteousness And Your praise all day long.
Psalms36:1 For the choir director. Of the servant of Yahweh
Psalms36:2 For it flatters him in his eyes For one to discover his iniquity and hate it.
Psalms36:3 The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit; He has ceased to consider to do good.
Psalms36:4 He devises wickedness upon his bed; He sets himself on a path that is not good; He does not despise evil.
Psalms36:5 Your lovingkindness
Psalms36:6 Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; Your judgments are like a great deep. O Yahweh
Psalms36:7 How precious is Your lovingkindness
Psalms36:8 They are satisfied from the richness of Your house; And You give them to drink of the river of Your delights.
Psalms36:9 For with You is the fountain of life; In Your light we see light.
Psalms36:10 Continue Your lovingkindness to those who know You
Psalms36:11 Let not the foot of pride come upon me
Psalms36:12 There the workers of wickedness have fallen; They have been thrust down and cannot rise.
Psalms37:1 Of David. Do not fret because of evildoers
Psalms37:2 For they will wither quickly like the grass And fade like the green herb.
Psalms37:3 Trust in Yahweh and do good; Dwell in the land and cultivate faithfulness.
Psalms37:4 Delight yourself in Yahweh; And He will give you the desires of your heart.
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to Yahweh
Psalms37:6 He will bring forth your righteousness as the light And your judgment as the noonday.
Psalms37:7 Be still in Yahweh and wait patiently for Him; Do not fret because of him who prospers in his way
Psalms37:8 Cease from anger and forsake wrath; Do not fret; it leads only to evildoing.
Psalms37:9 For evildoers will be cut off
Psalms37:10 Yet a little while and the wicked man will be no more; You will look carefully at his place
Psalms37:11 But the lowly will inherit the land And will delight themselves in abundant peace.
Psalms37:12 The wicked schemes against the righteous And gnashes at him with his teeth.
Psalms37:13 The Lord laughs at him
Psalms37:14 The wicked have drawn the sword and bent their bow To cast down the afflicted and the needy
Psalms37:15 Their sword will enter their own heart
Psalms37:16 Better is the little of the righteous Than the abundance of many wicked.
Psalms37:17 For the arms of the wicked will be broken
Psalms37:18 Yahweh knows the days of the blameless
Psalms37:19 They will not be ashamed in the time of evil
Psalms37:20 But the wicked will perish; And the enemies of Yahweh will be like the glory of the pastures
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrows and does not pay back
Psalms37:22 For those blessed by Him will inherit the land
Psalms37:23 The footsteps of a man are established by Yahweh
Psalms37:24 When he falls
Psalms37:25 I was young and now I am old
Psalms37:26 All day long he is gracious and lends
Psalms37:27 Depart from evil and do good
Psalms37:28 For Yahweh loves justice And will not forsake His holy ones; They are kept forever
Psalms37:29 The righteous will inherit the land And dwell in it forever.
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom
Psalms37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; His steps do not slip.
Psalms37:32 The wicked spies upon the righteous And seeks to put him to death.
Psalms37:33 Yahweh will not forsake him in his hand; He will not condemn him when he is judged.
Psalms37:34 Hope for Yahweh and keep His way
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked
Psalms37:36 Then he passed away
Psalms37:37 Observe the blameless man
Psalms37:38 But transgressors will be altogether destroyed; The posterity of the wicked will be cut off.
Psalms37:39 But the salvation of the righteous is from Yahweh; He is their strength in time of distress.
Psalms37:40 Yahweh helps them and protects them; He protects them from the wicked and saves them
Psalms38:1 A Psalm of David. To bring to remembrance. O Yahweh
Psalms38:2 For Your arrows have pressed deep into me
Psalms38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of Your indignation; There is no health in my bones because of my sin.
Psalms38:4 For my iniquities go over my head; As a heavy burden they weigh too much for me.
Psalms38:5 My wounds stink and rot Because of my folly.
Psalms38:6 I am bent over and greatly bowed down; I go mourning all day long.
Psalms38:7 For my loins are filled with burning
Psalms38:8 I am faint and badly crushed; I groan because of the agitation of my heart.
Psalms38:9 Lord
Psalms38:10 My heart throbs
Psalms38:11 My loved ones and my friends stand aloof from my plague; And my kinsmen stand afar off.
Psalms38:12 Those who search for my life lay snares for me; And those who seek to do me evil have threatened destruction
Psalms38:13 But I
Psalms38:14 And I am like a man who does not hear
Psalms38:15 For I wait on You
Psalms38:16 For I said
Psalms38:17 For I am ready to fall
Psalms38:18 For I confess my iniquity; I am full of anxiety because of my sin.
Psalms38:19 But my enemies are vigorous and strong
Psalms38:20 And those who repay evil for good
Psalms38:21 Do not forsake me
Psalms38:22 Make haste to help me
Psalms39:1 For the choir director. For Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said
Psalms39:2 I was mute with silence
Psalms39:3 My heart was hot within me
Psalms39:4 “Yahweh
Psalms39:5 Behold
Psalms39:6 Surely every man walks about as a shadow; Surely they make an uproar in vain; He piles up riches and does not know who will gather them.
Psalms39:7 “And now
Psalms39:8 Deliver me from all my transgressions; Make me not the reproach of the wicked fool.
Psalms39:9 I have become mute
Psalms39:10 Remove Your plague from me; Because of the opposition of Your hand I am wasting away.
Psalms39:11 With reproofs You chasten a man for iniquity; You consume as a moth what is precious to him; Surely every man is vanity. Selah.
Psalms39:12 “Hear my prayer
Psalms39:13 Turn Your gaze away from me
Psalms40:1 For the choir director. Of David. A Psalm. I hoped earnestly for Yahweh; And He inclined to me and heard my cry for help.
Psalms40:2 He brought me up out of the pit of destruction
Psalms40:3 He put a new song in my mouth
Psalms40:4 How blessed is the man who has made Yahweh his trust
Psalms40:5 Many
Psalms40:6 Sacrifice and meal offering You have not desired; My ears You have opened; Burnt offering and sin offering You have not required.
Psalms40:7 Then I said
Psalms40:8 I desire to do Your will
Psalms40:9 I proclaim good news of righteousness in the great assembly; Behold
Psalms40:10 I do not conceal Your righteousness within my heart; I speak of Your faithfulness and Your salvation; I do not hide Your lovingkindness and Your truth from the great assembly.
Psalms40:11 You
Psalms40:12 For evils beyond number have surrounded me; My iniquities have overtaken me
Psalms40:13 Be pleased
Psalms40:14 Let those be ashamed and humiliated together Who seek my life to sweep it away; Let those be turned back and dishonored Who delight in evil against me.
Psalms40:15 Let those be appalled because of their shame Who say to me
Psalms40:16 Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; Let those who love Your salvation say continually
Psalms40:17 As for me
Psalms41:1 For the choir director. A Psalm of David. How blessed is he who considers the poor; Yahweh will provide him escape in a day of calamity.
Psalms41:2 Yahweh will keep him and keep him alive
Psalms41:3 Yahweh will sustain him upon his sickbed; In his illness
Psalms41:4 As for me
Psalms41:5 My enemies speak evil against me
Psalms41:6 And when he comes to see me
Psalms41:7 All who hate me whisper together against me; Against me
Psalms41:8 “A vile thing is poured out upon him
Psalms41:9 Even my close friend in whom I trusted
Psalms41:10 But You
Psalms41:11 By this I know that You delight in me
Psalms41:12 As for me
Psalms41:13 Blessed be Yahweh
Psalms42:1 For the choir director. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for the water brooks
Psalms42:2 My soul thirsts for God
Psalms42:3 My tears have been my food day and night
Psalms42:4 These things I remember and I pour out my soul within me. For I used to go along with the throng and lead them in procession to the house of God
Psalms42:5 Why are you in despair
Psalms42:6 O my God
Psalms42:7 Deep calls to deep at the sound of Your waterfalls; All Your breakers and Your waves have rolled over me.
Psalms42:8 By day
Psalms42:9 I say to God my rock
Psalms42:10 As a shattering of my bones
Psalms42:11 Why are you in despair
Psalms43:1 Give justice to me
Psalms43:2 For You are the God of my strength; why have You rejected me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
Psalms43:3 Oh send out Your light and Your truth
Psalms43:4 Then I will go to the altar of God
Psalms43:5 Why are you in despair
Psalms44:1 For the choir director. Of the sons of Korah. A Maskil. O God
Psalms44:2 You with Your own hand dispossessed the nations; Then You planted them; You afflicted the peoples
Psalms44:3 For by their own sword they did not possess the land
Psalms44:4 You are my King
Psalms44:5 Through You we will push back our adversaries; Through Your name we will tread down those who rise up against us.
Psalms44:6 For I will not trust in my bow
Psalms44:7 But You have saved us from our adversaries
Psalms44:8 In God we have boasted all day long
Psalms44:9 Yet You have rejected us and brought us to dishonor
Psalms44:10 You cause us to turn back from the adversary; And those who hate us have plundered us for themselves.
Psalms44:11 You give us as sheep to be eaten And have scattered us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You sell Your people for no amount
Psalms44:13 You make us a reproach to our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You make us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All day long my dishonor is before me And the shame of my face has covered me
Psalms44:16 Because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles
Psalms44:17 All this has come upon us
Psalms44:18 Our heart has not turned back
Psalms44:19 Yet You have crushed us in a place of jackals And covered us with the shadow of death.
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God Or spread our hands to a strange god
Psalms44:21 Would not God find this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
Psalms44:22 But for Your sake we are killed all day long; We are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
Psalms44:23 Arouse Yourself
Psalms44:24 Why do You hide Your face And forget our affliction and our oppression?
Psalms44:25 For our soul has sunk down into the dust; Our body cleaves to the earth.
Psalms44:26 Rise up
Psalms45:1 For the choir director. According to Shoshannim. Of the sons of Korah. A Maskil. A Song of Love. My heart overflows with a good theme; I address my verses to the King; My tongue is the pen of a skillful scribe.
Psalms45:2 You are fairer than the sons of men; Grace is poured upon Your lips; Therefore God has blessed You forever.
Psalms45:3 Gird Your sword on Your thigh
Psalms45:4 And in Your majesty ride on victoriously
Psalms45:5 Your arrows are sharp; The peoples fall under You; Your arrows are in the heart of the King’s enemies.
Psalms45:6 Your throne
Psalms45:7 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; Therefore God
Psalms45:8 All Your garments are fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia; Out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made You glad.
Psalms45:9 Kings’ daughters are among Your noble ladies; At Your right hand stands the queen in gold from Ophir.
Psalms45:10 Listen
Psalms45:11 Then the King will desire your beauty. Because He is your Lord
Psalms45:12 The daughter of Tyre will come with a present; The rich among the people will seek your favor.
Psalms45:13 The King’s daughter is all glorious within her chamber; Her clothing is interwoven with gold.
Psalms45:14 She will be led to the King in embroidered work; The virgins
Psalms45:15 They will be led forth with gladness and rejoicing; They will enter into the King’s palace.
Psalms45:16 In place of your fathers will be your sons; You shall set them up as princes in all the earth.
Psalms45:17 I will cause Your name to be remembered from generation to every generation; Therefore the peoples will give You thanks forever and ever.
Psalms46:1 For the choir director. Of the sons of Korah. According to Alamoth. A Song. God is our refuge and strength
Psalms46:2 Therefore we will not fear
Psalms46:3 Though its waters roar and foam
Psalms46:4 There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God
Psalms46:5 God is in the midst of her
Psalms46:6 The nations roar
Psalms46:7 Yahweh of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah.
Psalms46:8 Come
Psalms46:9 He makes wars to cease to the end of the earth; He breaks the bow and cuts up the spear; He burns the chariots with fire.
Psalms46:10 “Cease striving and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations
Psalms46:11 Yahweh of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah.

Psalms47:2 For Yahweh Most High is fearsome
Psalms47:3 He subdues peoples under us And nations under our feet.
Psalms47:4 He chooses our inheritance for us
Psalms47:5 God has ascended with a loud shout
Psalms47:6 Sing praises to God
Psalms47:7 For God is the King of all the earth; Sing praises with a skillful psalm.
Psalms47:8 God reigns over the nations
Psalms47:9 The nobles of the peoples have assembled themselves with the people of the God of Abraham
Psalms48:1 A Song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is Yahweh
Psalms48:2 Beautiful in elevation
Psalms48:3 God
Psalms48:4 For
Psalms48:5 They saw it
Psalms48:6 Panic seized them there
Psalms48:7 With the east wind You break the ships of Tarshish.
Psalms48:8 As we have heard
Psalms48:9 We have thought on Your lovingkindness
Psalms48:10 As is Your name
Psalms48:11 Let Mount Zion be glad
Psalms48:12 Walk about Zion and go around her; Count her towers;
Psalms48:13 Consider her ramparts; Go through her palaces
Psalms48:14 For this is God
Psalms49:1 For the choir director. Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm. Hear this
Psalms49:2 Both low and high
Psalms49:3 My mouth will speak wisdom
Psalms49:4 I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will express my riddle on the harp.
Psalms49:5 Why should I fear in days of evil
Psalms49:6 Even those who trust in their wealth And boast in the abundance of their riches?
Psalms49:7 Truly
Psalms49:8 For the redemption price for their soul is costly
Psalms49:9 That he should live on eternally
Psalms49:10 For he sees that even wise men die; The fool and the senseless alike perish And leave their wealth to others.
Psalms49:11 Their inner thought is that their houses are forever And their dwelling places from generation to generation; They have called their lands after their own names.
Psalms49:12 But man in his honor will not endure; He is like the animals that perish.
Psalms49:13 This is the way of those who are foolish
Psalms49:14 As sheep they are appointed for Sheol; Death will shepherd them; And the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning
Psalms49:15 But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol
Psalms49:16 Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich
Psalms49:17 For when he dies he will not take any of it; His glory will not descend after him.
Psalms49:18 For while he lives he blesses his soul— And men will praise you when you do well for yourself—
Psalms49:19 But his soul shall go to the generation of his fathers; They will eternally not see light.
Psalms49:20 Man in his honor
Psalms50:1 A Psalm of Asaph. The Mighty One
Psalms50:2 Out of Zion
Psalms50:3 May our God come and not be silent; Fire devours before Him
Psalms50:4 He calls the heavens above
Psalms50:5 “Gather My holy ones to Me
Psalms50:6 And the heavens declare His righteousness
Psalms50:7 “Hear
Psalms50:8 I do not reprove you for your sacrifices
Psalms50:9 I shall take no young bull out of your house Nor male goats out of your folds.
Psalms50:10 For every beast of the forest is Mine
Psalms50:11 I know every bird of the mountains
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry I would not tell you
Psalms50:13 Shall I eat the flesh of bulls Or drink the blood of male goats?
Psalms50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving And pay your vows to the Most High;
Psalms50:15 Call upon Me in the day of distress; I shall rescue you
Psalms50:16 But to the wicked God says
Psalms50:17 For you hate discipline
Psalms50:18 When you see a thief
Psalms50:19 You let your mouth loose in evil And you harness your tongue for deceit.
Psalms50:20 You sit and speak against your brother; You slander your own mother’s son.
Psalms50:21 These things you have done and I kept silent; You thought that I was just like you; I will reprove you and state the case in order before your eyes.
Psalms50:22 “Now consider this
Psalms50:23 He who offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifies Me; And he who orders his way
Psalms51:1 For the choir director. A Psalm of David. When Nathan the prophet came to him
Psalms51:2 Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity And cleanse me from my sin.
Psalms51:3 For I know my transgressions
Psalms51:4 Against You
Psalms51:5 Behold
Psalms51:6 Behold
Psalms51:7 Purify me with hyssop
Psalms51:8 Make me to hear joy and gladness
Psalms51:9 Hide Your face from my sins And blot out all my iniquities.
Psalms51:10 Create in me a clean heart
Psalms51:11 Do not cast me away from Your presence And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
Psalms51:12 Restore to me the joy of Your salvation And sustain me with a willing spirit.
Psalms51:13 Then I will teach transgressors Your ways
Psalms51:14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness
Psalms51:15 O Lord
Psalms51:16 For You do not delight in sacrifice
Psalms51:17 The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; A broken and a contrite heart
Psalms51:18 By Your favor do good to Zion; Build the walls of Jerusalem.
Psalms51:19 Then You will delight in righteous sacrifices

Psalms52:8 But as for me

Psalms53:2 God looks down from heaven upon the sons of men To see if there is anyone who has insight
Psalms53:3 Every one of them has turned back; together they have become worthless; There is no one who does good
Psalms53:4 Do the workers of iniquity not know
Psalms53:5 There they were in great dread where no dread had been; For God scattered the bones of him who encamped against you; You put them to shame
Psalms53:6 Oh
Psalms54:1 For the choir director. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. When the Ziphites came and said to Saul
Psalms54:2 O God
Psalms54:3 For strangers have risen against me And ruthless men have sought my life; They have not set God before them. Selah.
Psalms54:4 Behold
Psalms54:5 He will return the evil to my foes; Destroy them in Your truth.
Psalms54:6 With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to You; I will give thanks to Your name
Psalms54:7 For He has delivered me from all distress
Psalms55:1 For the choir director. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. Give ear to my prayer
Psalms55:2 Give heed to me and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and am surely distracted
Psalms55:3 Because of the voice of the enemy
Psalms55:4 My heart is in anguish within me
Psalms55:5 Fear and trembling come upon me
Psalms55:6 I said
Psalms55:7 Behold
Psalms55:8 I would hasten to my place of refuge From the stormy wind and tempest.”
Psalms55:9 Confuse
Psalms55:10 Day and night they go around her upon her walls
Psalms55:11 Destruction is in her midst; Oppression and deceit do not depart from her streets.
Psalms55:12 For it is not an enemy who reproaches me
Psalms55:13 But it is you
Psalms55:14 We who had sweet counsel together Walked in the house of God in the throng.
Psalms55:15 Let death come deceitfully upon them; Let them go down alive to Sheol
Psalms55:16 As for me
Psalms55:17 Evening and morning and at noon
Psalms55:18 He will redeem my soul in peace from the battle which is against me
Psalms55:19 God will hear and answer them— Even the one who sits enthroned from of old— Selah. Because they do not change
Psalms55:20 My companion has put forth his hands against those who were at peace with him; He has violated his covenant.
Psalms55:21 His speech was smoother than butter
Psalms55:22 Cast your burden upon Yahweh and He will sustain you; He will never allow the righteous to be shaken.
Psalms55:23 But You
Psalms56:1 For the choir director. According to Jonath Elem Rehokim. A Mikhtam of David. When the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be gracious to me
Psalms56:2 My foes have trampled upon me all day long
Psalms56:3 When I am afraid
Psalms56:4 In God
Psalms56:5 All day long they distort my words; All their thoughts are against me for evil.
Psalms56:6 They attack
Psalms56:7 On account of their wickedness
Psalms56:8 You have taken account of my wanderings; Put my tears in Your bottle. Are they not in Your book?
Psalms56:9 Then my enemies will turn back in the day when I call; This I know
Psalms56:10 In God
Psalms56:11 In God I trust

Psalms57:1 For the choir director. Al-tashheth. Of David. A Mikhtam. When he fled from Saul in the cave. Be gracious to me
Psalms57:2 I will call to God Most High
Psalms57:3 He will send from heaven and save me; He reproaches him who tramples upon me. Selah. God will send His lovingkindness and His truth.
Psalms57:4 My soul is among lions; I am lying down among those who breathe forth fire
Psalms57:5 Be exalted above the heavens
Psalms57:6 They have set a net for my steps; My soul is bowed down; They dug a pit before me; They themselves have fallen into the midst of it. Selah.
Psalms57:7 My heart is set
Psalms57:8 Awake
Psalms57:9 I will give thanks to You
Psalms57:10 For Your lovingkindness is great to the heavens And Your truth to the skies.
Psalms57:11 Be exalted above the heavens
Psalms58:1 For the choir director. Al-tashheth. Of David. A Mikhtam. Do you indeed speak righteousness
Psalms58:2 No
Psalms58:3 The wicked are estranged from the womb; These who speak falsehood wander in error from birth.
Psalms58:4 They have venom like the venom of a serpent; Like a deaf cobra that stops up its ear
Psalms58:5 So that it does not hear the voice of charmers
Psalms58:6 O God
Psalms58:7 Let them flow away like water that runs off; When he aims his arrows
Psalms58:8 Let them be as a snail which melts away as it goes along
Psalms58:9 Before your pots can feel the fire of thorns He will sweep them away with a whirlwind
Psalms58:10 The righteous will be glad when he beholds the vengeance; He will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
Psalms58:11 And men will say
Psalms59:1 For the choir director. Al-tashheth. Of David. A Mikhtam. When Saul sent men and they watched the house in order to put him to death. Deliver me from my enemies
Psalms59:2 Deliver me from workers of iniquity And save me from men of bloodshed.
Psalms59:3 For behold
Psalms59:4 For no guilt of mine
Psalms59:5 You
Psalms59:6 They return at evening
Psalms59:7 Behold
Psalms59:8 But You
Psalms59:9 Because of his strength I will watch for You
Psalms59:10 My God in His lovingkindness will approach me; God will let me look triumphantly upon my foes.
Psalms59:11 Do not slay them
Psalms59:12 On account of the sin of their mouth and the word of their lips
Psalms59:13 Destroy them in wrath
Psalms59:14 They return at evening
Psalms59:15 They wander about for food And growl if they are not satisfied.
Psalms59:16 But as for me
Psalms59:17 O my strength
Psalms60:1 For the choir director. According to Shushan Eduth. A Mikhtam of David. For teaching. When he struggled with Aram-naharaim and with Aram-zobah
Psalms60:2 You have made the land quake
Psalms60:3 You have caused Your people to see hardship; You have given us wine to drink that causes reeling.
Psalms60:4 You have given a banner to those who fear You
Psalms60:5 That Your beloved may be rescued
Psalms60:6 God has spoken in His holiness: “I will exult
Psalms60:7 Gilead is Mine
Psalms60:8 Moab is My washbowl; Over Edom I shall throw My shoe; Make a loud shout
Psalms60:9 Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Psalms60:10 Have You Yourself
Psalms60:11 Oh give us help against the adversary
Psalms60:12 Through God we shall do valiantly
Psalms61:1 For the choir director. On a stringed instrument. Of David. Hear my cry of lamentation
Psalms61:2 From the end of the earth I call to You when my heart is faint; Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
Psalms61:3 For You have been a refuge for me
Psalms61:4 Let me sojourn in Your tent forever; Let me take refuge in the shelter of Your wings. Selah.
Psalms61:5 For You
Psalms61:6 You will add days to the king’s life; His years will be from generation to generation.
Psalms61:7 He will sit enthroned before God forever; Appoint lovingkindness and truth that they may guard him.
Psalms61:8 So I will sing praise to Your name forever
Psalms62:1 For the choir director. According to Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. Surely my soul waits in silence for God; From Him is my salvation.
Psalms62:2 Surely He is my rock and my salvation
Psalms62:3 How long will you assail a man
Psalms62:4 Surely they have counseled to thrust him down from his high position; They find pleasure in falsehood; They bless with their mouth
Psalms62:5 Surely wait in silence for God
Psalms62:6 Surely He is my rock and my salvation
Psalms62:7 On God my salvation and my glory rest; The rock of my strength
Psalms62:8 Trust in Him at all times
Psalms62:9 Surely men of low degree are merely vanity and men of rank are a lie; In the balances they go up; They are together lighter than a breath of vanity.
Psalms62:10 Do not trust in oppression And do not put vain hope in robbery; If riches increase
Psalms62:11 Once God has spoken; Twice I have heard this: That strength belongs to God;
Psalms62:12 And that to You
Psalms63:1 A Psalm of David. When he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God
Psalms63:2 Thus I have beheld You in the sanctuary
Psalms63:3 Because Your lovingkindness is better than life
Psalms63:4 Thus I will bless You as long as I live; I will lift up my hands in Your name.
Psalms63:5 My soul is satisfied as with fatness and richness
Psalms63:6 When I remember You on my bed
Psalms63:7 For You have been my help
Psalms63:8 My soul clings to You; Your right hand upholds me.
Psalms63:9 But those who seek my life to destroy it
Psalms63:10 They will be delivered over to the power of the sword; They will be a portion for foxes.
Psalms63:11 But the king will be glad in God; Everyone who swears by Him will boast
Psalms64:1 For the choir director. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice
Psalms64:2 Hide me from the secret counsel of evildoers
Psalms64:3 Who have sharpened their tongue like a sword. They aimed bitter speech as their arrow
Psalms64:4 To shoot from places of hiding at the blameless; Suddenly they shoot at him
Psalms64:5 They hold fast to themselves an evil purpose; They talk of laying snares secretly; They say
Psalms64:6 They searched out unrighteousness
Psalms64:7 But God will shoot them with an arrow; Suddenly they will be wounded.
Psalms64:8 So they will cause their own tongue to turn against them; All who see them will shake their head.
Psalms64:9 Then all men will fear
Psalms64:10 The righteous man will be glad in Yahweh and will take refuge in Him; And all the upright in heart will boast.
Psalms65:1 For the choir director. A Psalm of David. A Song. To You
Psalms65:2 O You who hear prayer
Psalms65:3 Words of iniquity prevail against me; As for our transgressions
Psalms65:4 How blessed is the one whom You choose and bring near to You That he would dwell in Your courts. We will be satisfied with the goodness of Your house
Psalms65:5 By fearsome deeds You answer us in righteousness
Psalms65:6 Who establishes the mountains by His strength
Psalms65:7 Who stills the rumbling of the seas
Psalms65:8 They who inhabit the ends of the earth are in fear on account of Your signs; You make the dawn and the sunset shout for joy.
Psalms65:9 You visit the earth and cause it to overflow; You greatly enrich it; The stream of God is full of water; You establish their grain
Psalms65:10 You water its furrows abundantly
Psalms65:11 You crown the year with Your goodness
Psalms65:12 The pastures of the wilderness drip
Psalms65:13 The meadows are clothed with flocks And the valleys are covered with grain; They make a loud shout
Psalms66:1 For the choir director. A Song. A Psalm. Make a loud shout to God
Psalms66:2 Sing praise for the glory of His name; Establish His praise as glorious.
Psalms66:3 Say to God
Psalms66:4 All the earth will worship You
Psalms66:5 Come and see the works of God
Psalms66:6 He turned the sea into dry land; They passed through the river on foot; There let us be glad in Him!
Psalms66:7 He rules by His might forever; His eyes keep watch on the nations; Let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
Psalms66:8 Bless our God
Psalms66:9 Who establishes us among the living And does not allow our feet to stumble.
Psalms66:10 For You have tested us
Psalms66:11 You brought us into the net; You established an oppressive burden upon our loins.
Psalms66:12 You made men ride over our heads; We went through fire and through water
Psalms66:13 I shall come into Your house with burnt offerings; I shall pay You my vows
Psalms66:14 Which my lips uttered And my mouth spoke when I was in distress.
Psalms66:15 I shall offer to You burnt offerings of fat beasts
Psalms66:16 Come and hear
Psalms66:17 I called out to Him with my mouth
Psalms66:18 If I see wickedness in my heart
Psalms66:19 But certainly God has heard; He has given heed to the voice of my prayer.
Psalms66:20 Blessed be God

Psalms68:1 For the choir director. A Psalm of David. A Song. Let God arise
Psalms68:2 As smoke is driven away
Psalms68:3 But let the righteous be glad; let them exult before God; And let them rejoice with gladness.
Psalms68:4 Sing to God
Psalms68:5 A father of the fatherless and a judge for the widows
Psalms68:6 God causes the lonely to inhabit a home; He leads out the prisoners into prosperity
Psalms68:7 O God
Psalms68:8 The earth quaked; The heavens also dripped rain at the presence of God; Sinai itself quaked at the presence of God
Psalms68:9 You caused abundant rain to sprinkle down
Psalms68:10 Your creatures inhabited it; You established it in Your goodness for the poor
Psalms68:11 The Lord gives the word; The women who proclaim the good news are a great host:
Psalms68:12 “Kings of armies retreat
Psalms68:13 If you men lie down among the sheepfolds
Psalms68:14 When the Almighty dispersed the kings there
Psalms68:15 A mountain of God is the mountain of Bashan; A mountain of many peaks is the mountain of Bashan.
Psalms68:16 Why do you look with envy
Psalms68:17 The chariots of God are myriads
Psalms68:18 You have ascended on high
Psalms68:19 Blessed be the Lord
Psalms68:20 God is to us a God of salvation; And to Yahweh the Lord belong escapes from death.
Psalms68:21 Surely God will crush the head of His enemies
Psalms68:22 The Lord said
Psalms68:23 That your foot may crush them in blood
Psalms68:24 They have seen Your procession
Psalms68:25 The singers went on
Psalms68:26 Bless God in the congregations
Psalms68:27 There is Benjamin
Psalms68:28 Your God has commanded your strength; Show Yourself strong
Psalms68:29 Because of Your temple at Jerusalem Kings will bring gifts to You.
Psalms68:30 Rebuke the beast in the reeds
Psalms68:31 Envoys will come out of Egypt; Ethiopia will quickly stretch out her hands to God.
Psalms68:32 Sing to God
Psalms68:33 To Him who rides upon the highest heavens
Psalms68:34 Ascribe strength to God; His majesty is over Israel And His strength is in the skies.
Psalms68:35 O God
Psalms69:1 For the choir director. According to Shoshannim. Of David. Save me
Psalms69:2 I have sunk in deep clay
Psalms69:3 I am weary with my calling out; my throat is parched; My eyes fail while I wait for my God.
Psalms69:4 Those who hate me without cause are more than the hairs of my head; Those who would destroy me are powerful
Psalms69:5 O God
Psalms69:6 May those who hope for You not be ashamed through me
Psalms69:7 Because for Your sake I have borne reproach; Dishonor has covered my face.
Psalms69:8 I have become estranged from my brothers And a foreigner to my mother’s sons.
Psalms69:9 For zeal for Your house has consumed me
Psalms69:10 When I wept in my soul with fasting
Psalms69:11 When I made sackcloth my clothing
Psalms69:12 Those who dwell at the gate moan about me
Psalms69:13 But as for me
Psalms69:14 Deliver me from the mire and do not let me sink; May I be delivered from my foes and from the deep waters.
Psalms69:15 May the flood of water not overflow me Nor the deep swallow me up
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 And do not hide Your face from Your slave
Psalms69:18 Oh draw near to my soul and redeem it; Ransom me because of my enemies!
Psalms69:19 You know my reproach and my shame and my dishonor; All my adversaries are before You.
Psalms69:20 Reproach has broken my heart and I am so sick. And I hoped for sympathy
Psalms69:21 They also gave me gall for my food And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Psalms69:22 May their table before them become a snare; And when they are in peace
Psalms69:23 May their eyes darken so that they cannot see
Psalms69:24 Pour out Your indignation on them
Psalms69:25 May their camp be desolate; May none dwell in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they have persecuted him whom You Yourself have struck down
Psalms69:27 Add iniquity to their iniquity
Psalms69:28 May they be blotted out of the book of life And may they not be recorded with the righteous.
Psalms69:29 But I am afflicted and in pain; May Your salvation
Psalms69:30 I will praise the name of God with song And magnify Him with thanksgiving.
Psalms69:31 And this will please Yahweh better than an ox Or a young bull with horns and hoofs.
Psalms69:32 The humble see it and are glad; You who seek God
Psalms69:33 For Yahweh hears the needy And does not despise His who are prisoners.
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise Him
Psalms69:35 For God will save Zion and build the cities of Judah
Psalms69:36 The seed of His slaves will inherit it

Psalms70:2 Let those be ashamed and humiliated Who seek my life; Let those be turned back and dishonored Who delight in evil against me.
Psalms70:3 Let those turn back because of their shame Who say
Psalms70:4 Let those be joyful and glad in You All who seek You And let them say continually
Psalms70:5 But I am afflicted and needy; Hasten to me
Psalms71:1 In You
Psalms71:2 In Your righteousness deliver me and protect me; Incline Your ear to me and save me.
Psalms71:3 Be to me a rock of habitation to which I may continually come; You have given the command to save me
Psalms71:4 Protect me
Psalms71:5 For You are my hope; O Lord Yahweh
Psalms71:6 By You I have been sustained from my birth; You are He who took me from my mother’s womb; My praise is continually of You.
Psalms71:7 I have become a marvel to many
Psalms71:8 My mouth is filled with Your praise And with Your beauty all day long.
Psalms71:9 Do not cast me off in the time of old age; Do not forsake me when my strength fails.
Psalms71:10 For my enemies have spoken against me; And those who watch my life have counseled together
Psalms71:11 Saying
Psalms71:12 O God
Psalms71:13 Let those who accuse my soul be ashamed and consumed; Let them be wrapped up with reproach and dishonor
Psalms71:14 But as for me
Psalms71:15 My mouth shall recount Your righteousness And Your salvation all day long; For I do not know the sum of them.
Psalms71:16 I will come with the mighty deeds of Lord Yahweh; I will bring to remembrance Your righteousness
Psalms71:17 O God
Psalms71:18 And even when I am old and gray
Psalms71:19 For Your righteousness
Psalms71:20 You
Psalms71:21 May You increase my greatness And turn to comfort me.
Psalms71:22 I will also praise You with a harp
Psalms71:23 My lips will sing for joy when I sing praises to You; And my soul
Psalms71:24 My tongue also will utter Your righteousness all day long; For they are ashamed
Psalms72:1 Of Solomon. O God
Psalms72:2 May he render judgment to Your people with righteousness And Your afflicted with justice.
Psalms72:3 Let the mountains lift up peace to the people
Psalms72:4 May he give justice to the afflicted of the people
Psalms72:5 Let them fear You while the sun endures
Psalms72:6 May he come down like rain upon the mown grass
Psalms72:7 May the righteous flourish in his days
Psalms72:8 May he also have dominion from sea to sea And from the River to the ends of the earth.
Psalms72:9 Let the desert creatures kneel before him
Psalms72:10 Let the kings of Tarshish and of the coastlands bring a present; The kings of Sheba and Seba offer tribute.
Psalms72:11 And let all kings bow down to him
Psalms72:12 For he will deliver the needy when he cries for help
Psalms72:13 He will have compassion on the poor and needy
Psalms72:14 He will redeem their life from oppression and violence
Psalms72:15 So may he live! And may they give to him the gold of Sheba; And let each pray for him continually; Let each bless him all day long.
Psalms72:16 May there be abundance of grain in the earth on top of the mountains; May its fruit wave like the cedars of Lebanon; And may those from the city blossom like vegetation of the earth.
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever; May his name increase as long as the sun shines; Let all nations be blessed in him; Let all nations call him blessed.
Psalms72:18 Blessed be Yahweh God
Psalms72:19 And blessed be His glorious name forever; And may the whole earth be filled with His glory. Amen
Psalms72:20 The prayers of David
Psalms73:1 A Psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I was envious of the boastful
Psalms73:4 For there are no pains in their death
Psalms73:5 They are not in trouble as other men
Psalms73:6 Therefore lofty pride is their necklace; The garment of violence covers them.
Psalms73:7 Their eye bulges from fatness; The delusions of their heart overflow.
Psalms73:8 They scoff and wickedly speak of oppression; They speak from on high.
Psalms73:9 They have set their mouth against the heavens
Psalms73:10 Therefore his people return here
Psalms73:11 They say
Psalms73:12 Behold
Psalms73:13 Surely in vain I have kept my heart pure And washed my hands in innocence;
Psalms73:14 For I have been stricken all day long And reproved every morning.
Psalms73:15 If I had said
Psalms73:16 When I gave thought to know this
Psalms73:17 Until I came into the sanctuary of God; Then I understood their end.
Psalms73:18 Surely You set them in slippery places; You cause them to fall to destruction.
Psalms73:19 How they become desolate in a moment! They are completely swept away by terrors!
Psalms73:20 Like a dream when one awakes
Psalms73:21 When my heart was embittered And I was pierced within
Psalms73:22 Then I was senseless and ignorant; I was like an animal before You.
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless I am continually with You; You have taken hold of my right hand.
Psalms73:24 With Your counsel You will lead me
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but You? And besides You
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart fail
Psalms73:27 For
Psalms73:28 But as for me
Psalms74:1 A Maskil of Asaph. Why
Psalms74:2 Remember Your congregation
Psalms74:3 Lift up Your steps toward the perpetual ruins; The enemy has damaged everything within the sanctuary.
Psalms74:4 Your adversaries have roared in the midst of Your meeting place; They have set up their own signs for signs.
Psalms74:5 Each seems like one who lifts up An axe against the undergrowth of trees.
Psalms74:6 And now its carved work altogether They smash with hatchet and hammers.
Psalms74:7 They have set Your sanctuary on fire; By bringing it to the ground
Psalms74:8 They said in their heart
Psalms74:9 We do not see our signs; There is no longer any prophet
Psalms74:10 How long
Psalms74:11 Why do You turn back Your hand
Psalms74:12 Yet God is my King from of old
Psalms74:13 You divided the sea by Your strength; You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.
Psalms74:14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You gave him as food for the creatures of the desert.
Psalms74:15 You split open spring and river; You dried up ever‑flowing rivers.
Psalms74:16 Yours is the day
Psalms74:17 You have caused all the boundaries of the earth to stand firm; You have formed summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember this
Psalms74:19 Do not deliver the soul of Your turtledove to the wild beast; Do not forget the life of Your afflicted forever.
Psalms74:20 Look to the covenant; For the dark places of the land are full of the haunts of violence.
Psalms74:21 Let not the oppressed return dishonored; Let the afflicted and needy praise Your name.
Psalms74:22 Arise
Psalms74:23 Do not forget the voice of Your adversaries

Psalms75:2 “For I select an appointed time
Psalms75:3 The earth and all who dwell in it melt; It is I who have firmly set its pillars. Selah.
Psalms75:4 I said to the boastful
Psalms75:5 Do not raise up your horn on high
Psalms75:6 For one’s rising up does not come from the east
Psalms75:7 But God is the Judge; He puts down one and raises up another.
Psalms75:8 For a cup is in the hand of Yahweh
Psalms75:9 But as for me
Psalms75:10 And all the horns of the wicked I will cut off
Psalms76:1 For the choir director. With stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. God is known in Judah; His name is great in Israel.
Psalms76:2 So His tabernacle is in Salem; His dwelling place is in Zion.
Psalms76:3 There He broke the flaming arrows
Psalms76:4 You are shining
Psalms76:5 The stouthearted were plundered
Psalms76:6 At Your rebuke
Psalms76:7 But You
Psalms76:8 You made Your cause to be heard from heaven; The earth feared and was quiet
Psalms76:9 When God arose to judgment
Psalms76:10 For the wrath of man shall praise You; With a remnant of wrath You will gird Yourself.
Psalms76:11 Make vows to Yahweh your God and pay them; Let all who are around Him bring gifts to the Fearsome One.
Psalms76:12 He will cut off the spirit of princes; He is feared by the kings of the earth.
Psalms77:1 For the choir director. According to Jeduthun. Of Asaph. A Psalm. My voice rises to God
Psalms77:2 In the day of my distress I sought the Lord; In the night my hand was stretched out without weariness; My soul refused to be comforted.
Psalms77:3 I remember God and I am disturbed; I muse and my spirit faints. Selah.
Psalms77:4 You have held my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I give thought to the days of old
Psalms77:6 I remember my music in the night; I am musing with my heart
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord reject evermore? And will He not be favorable again?
Psalms77:8 Has His lovingkindness ceased forever? Has His word ended from generation to generation?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be gracious
Psalms77:10 Then I said
Psalms77:11 I shall remember the deeds of Yah; Surely I will remember Your wonders of old.
Psalms77:12 I will meditate on all Your work And muse on Your deeds.
Psalms77:13 O God
Psalms77:14 You are the God who works wonders; You have made known Your strength among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 You have by Your arm redeemed Your people
Psalms77:16 The waters saw You
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured out water; The skies gave forth a sound; Your arrows went here and there.
Psalms77:18 The sound of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; The lightnings lit up the world; The earth trembled and shook.
Psalms77:19 Your way was in the sea And Your paths in the mighty waters
Psalms77:20 You led Your people like a flock By the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Psalms78:1 A Maskil of Asaph. Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will pour forth dark sayings of old
Psalms78:3 Which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not conceal them from their children
Psalms78:5 For He established a testimony in Jacob And set a law in Israel
Psalms78:6 That the generation to come might know
Psalms78:7 That they should set their confidence in God And not forget the deeds of God
Psalms78:8 And not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The sons of Ephraim were archers equipped with bows
Psalms78:10 They did not keep the covenant of God And refused to walk in His law;
Psalms78:11 So they forgot His acts And His wondrous deeds that He had shown them.
Psalms78:12 He did wonders before their fathers In the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He split the sea and caused them to pass through
Psalms78:14 Then He led them with the cloud by day And all the night with a light of fire.
Psalms78:15 He was splitting the rocks in the wilderness And so gave them abundant drink like the ocean depths.
Psalms78:16 He brought forth streams also from the cliff face And caused waters to run down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 Yet they still continued to sin against Him
Psalms78:18 And in their heart they put God to the test By asking for food according to their desire.
Psalms78:19 Then they spoke against God; They said
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore Yahweh heard and was full of wrath; And a fire was kindled against Jacob And anger also mounted against Israel
Psalms78:22 Because they did not believe in God And did not trust in His salvation.
Psalms78:23 Yet He commanded the skies above And opened the doors of heaven;
Psalms78:24 He rained down manna upon them to eat And gave them grain from heaven.
Psalms78:25 Man ate the bread of angels; He sent them provision to satisfy.
Psalms78:26 He led forth the east wind in the heavens And by His strength He guided the south wind.
Psalms78:27 Then He rained meat upon them like the dust
Psalms78:28 He caused them to fall in the midst of His camp
Psalms78:29 So they ate and were very satisfied
Psalms78:30 Before they had satisfied their desire
Psalms78:31 The anger of God rose against them And killed some of their stoutest ones
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this they still sinned And did not believe in His wondrous deeds.
Psalms78:33 So He brought their days to an end in futility And their years in sudden terror.
Psalms78:34 When He killed them
Psalms78:35 And they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they deceived Him with their mouth And lied to Him with their tongue.
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not prepared to remain with Him
Psalms78:38 But He
Psalms78:39 Thus He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against Him in the wilderness And grieved Him in the wasteland!
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God
Psalms78:42 They did not remember His power
Psalms78:43 When He performed His signs in Egypt And His miracles in the field of Zoan
Psalms78:44 And turned their rivers to blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies which devoured them
Psalms78:46 He gave also their crops to the grasshopper And the fruit of their labor to the locust.
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hailstones And their sycamore trees with frost.
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle also to the hailstones And their herds to bolts of lightning.
Psalms78:49 He sent upon them His burning anger
Psalms78:50 He leveled a path for His anger; He did not hold back their soul from death
Psalms78:51 So He struck all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But He led forth His own people like sheep And guided them in the wilderness like a flock;
Psalms78:53 He led them safely
Psalms78:54 So He brought them to His holy land
Psalms78:55 He also drove out the nations before them And apportioned them for an inheritance by measurement
Psalms78:56 Yet they tested and rebelled against the Most High God And did not keep His testimonies
Psalms78:57 But turned back and acted treacherously like their fathers; They turned aside like a treacherous bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked Him with their high places And aroused His jealousy with their graven images.
Psalms78:59 God heard and was filled with wrath And greatly rejected Israel;
Psalms78:60 So that He abandoned the dwelling place at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 And gave up His strength to captivity And His beauty into the hand of the adversary.
Psalms78:62 He also gave over His people to the sword
Psalms78:63 Fire devoured His choice men
Psalms78:64 His priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as if from sleep
Psalms78:66 He struck His adversaries backward; He put on them an everlasting reproach.
Psalms78:67 He also rejected the tent of Joseph
Psalms78:68 But chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 And He built His sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 He also chose David His servant And took him from the sheepfolds;
Psalms78:71 From following the nursing ewes He brought him To shepherd Jacob His people
Psalms78:72 So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart

Psalms88:1 A Song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. For the choir director. According to Mahalath Leannoth. A Maskil of Heman the Ezrahite. O Yahweh
Psalms88:2 Let my prayer come before You; Incline Your ear to my cry of lamentation!
Psalms88:3 For my soul has been saturated with calamities
Psalms88:4 I am counted among those who go down to the pit; I am like a man without strength
Psalms88:5 Released among the dead
Psalms88:6 You have put me in the pit far below
Psalms88:7 Your wrath lies upon me
Psalms88:8 You have removed my acquaintances far from me; You have set me as an abomination to them; I am shut up and cannot go out.
Psalms88:9 My eye has wasted away because of affliction; I have called upon You every day
Psalms88:10 Will You do wonders for the dead? Will the departed spirits rise and praise You? Selah.
Psalms88:11 Will Your lovingkindness be recounted in the grave
Psalms88:12 Will Your wonders be known in the darkness? And Your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
Psalms88:13 But as for me
Psalms88:14 O Yahweh
Psalms88:15 I have been afflicted and about to breathe my last from my youth on; I bear Your terrors; I am overcome.
Psalms88:16 Your burning anger has passed over me; Your horrors have destroyed me.
Psalms88:17 They have surrounded me like water all day long; They have encompassed me altogether.
Psalms88:18 You have removed lover and friend far from me; My acquaintances are in darkness.
Psalms89:1 A Maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the lovingkindnesses of Yahweh forever; From generation to generation I will make known Your faithfulness with my mouth.

Psalms89:4 I will establish your seed forever And build up your throne from generation to generation.” Selah.
Psalms89:5 The heavens will praise Your wonders
Psalms89:6 For who in the sky is comparable to Yahweh? Who among the sons of the mighty is like Yahweh
Psalms89:7 A God greatly dreaded in the council of the holy ones
Psalms89:8 O Yahweh God of hosts
Psalms89:9 You rule the swelling of the sea; When its waves rise
Psalms89:10 You Yourself crushed Rahab like one who is slain; You scattered Your enemies with Your strong arm.
Psalms89:11 The heavens are Yours
Psalms89:12 The north and the south
Psalms89:13 You have a mighty arm; Your hand is strong
Psalms89:14 Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne; Lovingkindness and truth go before You.
Psalms89:15 How blessed are the people who know the loud shout of joy! O Yahweh
Psalms89:16 In Your name they rejoice all the day
Psalms89:17 For You are the beauty of their strength
Psalms89:18 For our shield belongs to Yahweh
Psalms89:19 Formerly You spoke in vision to Your holy ones
Psalms89:20 I have found David My servant; With My holy oil I have anointed him
Psalms89:21 With whom My hand will be established; My arm also will strengthen him.
Psalms89:22 The enemy will not deceive him
Psalms89:23 But I shall crush his adversaries before him
Psalms89:24 My faithfulness and My lovingkindness will be with him
Psalms89:25 I shall also set his hand on the sea And his right hand on the rivers.
Psalms89:26 He will call to Me
Psalms89:27 I also shall make him My firstborn
Psalms89:28 My lovingkindness I will keep for him forever
Psalms89:29 So I will set up his seed to endure forever And his throne as the days of heaven.
Psalms89:30 “If his sons forsake My law And do not walk in My judgments
Psalms89:31 If they profane My statutes And do not keep My commandments
Psalms89:32 Then I will punish their transgression with the rod And their iniquity with striking.
Psalms89:33 But I will not break off My lovingkindness from him
Psalms89:34 My covenant I will not profane
Psalms89:35 Once I have sworn by My holiness; I will not lie to David.
Psalms89:36 His seed shall endure forever And his throne as the sun before Me.
Psalms89:37 It shall be established forever like the moon
Psalms89:38 But You have cast off and rejected
Psalms89:39 You have spurned the covenant of Your slave; You have profaned his crown to the ground.
Psalms89:40 You have broken down all his walls; You have beset his strongholds with ruin.
Psalms89:41 All who pass along the way plunder him; He has become a reproach to his neighbors.
Psalms89:42 You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries; You have made all his enemies be glad.
Psalms89:43 You also turn back the edge of his sword And have not made him arise in battle.
Psalms89:44 You have made his splendor to cease And cast his throne to the ground.
Psalms89:45 You have shortened the days of his youth; You have wrapped him up with shame. Selah.
Psalms89:46 How long
Psalms89:47 Remember what my span of life is; For what vanity You have created all the sons of men!
Psalms89:48 What man can live and not see death? Can he provide his soul escape from the power of Sheol? Selah.
Psalms89:49 Where are Your former lovingkindnesses
Psalms89:50 Remember
Psalms89:51 With which Your enemies have reproached
Psalms89:52 Blessed be Yahweh forever! Amen and Amen.

Psalms103:8 Yahweh is compassionate and gracious
Psalms103:9 He will not always contend with us
Psalms103:10 He has not dealt with us according to our sins
Psalms103:11 For as high as the heavens are above the earth
Psalms103:12 As far as the east is from the west
Psalms103:13 As a father has compassion on his children
Psalms103:14 For He Himself knows our form; He remembers that we are but dust.
Psalms103:15 As for man

Psalms103:17 But the lovingkindness of Yahweh is from everlasting to everlasting on those who fear Him
Psalms103:18 To those who keep His covenant And remember His precepts to do them.

Psalms104:1 Bless Yahweh
Psalms104:2 Wrapping Yourself with light as with a cloak
Psalms104:3 He lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters; He sets up the clouds to be His chariot; He walks upon the wings of the wind;
Psalms104:4 He makes His angels the winds
Psalms104:5 He founded the earth upon its place
Psalms104:6 You covered it with the deep as with a garment; The waters were standing above the mountains.
Psalms104:7 At Your rebuke they fled
Psalms104:8 The mountains went up; the valleys went down To the place which You founded for them.
Psalms104:9 You set a boundary that they may not pass over
Psalms104:10 He sends forth springs in the valleys; They flow between the mountains;
Psalms104:11 They give water to every beast of the field; The wild donkeys quench their thirst.
Psalms104:12 Above them the birds of the heavens dwell; They give forth their voices among the branches.
Psalms104:13 He gives water to the mountains from His upper chambers; The earth is satisfied with the fruit of His works.
Psalms104:14 He causes the grass to grow for the cattle
Psalms104:15 And wine which makes man’s heart glad
Psalms104:16 The trees of Yahweh are satisfied
Psalms104:17 Where the birds build their nests
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for the wild goats; The cliffs are a refuge for the shephanim.
Psalms104:19 He made the moon for the seasons; The sun knows the place of its setting.
Psalms104:20 You appoint darkness so that it becomes night
Psalms104:21 The young lions roar to go after their prey And to seek their food from God.
Psalms104:22 When the sun rises they gather together And lie down in their dens.
Psalms104:23 Man goes forth to his work And to his labor until evening.
Psalms104:24 How numerous are Your works
Psalms104:25 This is the sea
Psalms104:26 There the ships move along
Psalms104:27 They all wait for You To give them their food in due season.
Psalms104:28 You give to them
Psalms104:29 You hide Your face
Psalms104:30 You send forth Your Spirit
Psalms104:31 Let the glory of Yahweh endure forever; Let Yahweh be glad in His works;
Psalms104:32 He looks at the earth
Psalms104:33 I will sing to Yahweh throughout my life; I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
Psalms104:34 Let my musing be pleasing to Him; As for me
Psalms104:35 Let sinners be consumed from the earth And let the wicked be no more. Bless Yahweh
Psalms105:1 Oh give thanks to Yahweh
Psalms105:2 Sing to Him
Psalms105:3 Boast in His holy name; Let the heart of those who seek Yahweh be glad.
Psalms105:4 Inquire of Yahweh and His strength; Seek His face continually.
Psalms105:5 Remember His wondrous deeds which He has done
Psalms105:6 O seed of Abraham
Psalms105:7 He is Yahweh our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
Psalms105:8 He has remembered His covenant forever
Psalms105:9 Which He cut with Abraham
Psalms105:10 Then He confirmed it to Jacob for a statute
Psalms105:11 Saying
Psalms105:12 When they were only a few men in number
Psalms105:13 And they wandered about from nation to nation
Psalms105:14 He permitted no man to oppress them
Psalms105:15 “Do not touch My anointed ones
Psalms105:16 And He called for a famine upon the land; He broke the whole staff of bread.
Psalms105:17 He sent a man before them
Psalms105:18 They afflicted his feet with fetters
Psalms105:19 Until the time that his word came to pass
Psalms105:20 The king sent and released him
Psalms105:21 He set him up as lord of his house And ruler over all his possessions
Psalms105:22 To imprison his princes at will
Psalms105:23 Then Israel came to Egypt; And Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
Psalms105:24 And He caused His people to be very fruitful
Psalms105:25 He turned their heart to hate His people
Psalms105:26 He sent Moses His servant
Psalms105:27 They set forth the words of His signs among them
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and made it dark; And they did not rebel against His words.
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood And caused their fish to die.
Psalms105:30 Their land swarmed with frogs Even in the chambers of their kings.
Psalms105:31 He spoke
Psalms105:32 He gave them hail for rain
Psalms105:33 He also struck down their vines and their fig trees
Psalms105:34 He spoke
Psalms105:35 And they ate up all vegetation in their land
Psalms105:36 He also struck down all the firstborn in their land
Psalms105:37 Then He brought them out with silver and gold
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad when they went out
Psalms105:39 He spread a cloud for a covering
Psalms105:40 They asked
Psalms105:41 He opened the rock and water flowed out; It ran in the dry places like a river.
Psalms105:42 For He remembered His holy word With Abraham His servant;
Psalms105:43 And He brought His people out with joy
Psalms105:44 He gave them also the lands of the nations
Psalms105:45 So that they might keep His statutes And observe His laws
Psalms106:1 Praise Yah! Oh give thanks to Yahweh
Psalms106:2 Who can speak of the mighty deeds of Yahweh
Psalms106:3 How blessed are those who keep justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 That I may see the goodness of Your chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We have sinned with our fathers
Psalms106:7 Our fathers in Egypt did not consider Your wondrous deeds; They did not remember Your abundant lovingkindnesses
Psalms106:8 Yet He saved them for the sake of His name
Psalms106:9 Thus He rebuked the Red Sea and it dried up
Psalms106:10 So He saved them from the hand of the one who hated them
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their adversaries; Not one of them was left.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed His words; They sang His praise.
Psalms106:13 They quickly forgot His works; They did not wait for His counsel
Psalms106:14 But craved intensely in the wilderness
Psalms106:15 So He gave them their request
Psalms106:16 Then they became envious of Moses in the camp
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan
Psalms106:18 And a fire burned up in their company; The flame consumed the wicked.
Psalms106:19 They made a calf in Horeb And worshiped a molten image.
Psalms106:20 Thus they exchanged their glory For the image of an ox that eats grass.
Psalms106:21 They forgot God their Savior
Psalms106:22 Wondrous deeds in the land of Ham And awesome things by the Red Sea.
Psalms106:23 Therefore He said that He would destroy them
Psalms106:24 Then they despised the pleasant land; They did not believe in His word
Psalms106:25 But grumbled in their tents; They did not listen to the voice of Yahweh.
Psalms106:26 So He swore to them To make them fall in the wilderness
Psalms106:27 And to make their seed fall among the nations And to scatter them in the lands.
Psalms106:28 They then joined themselves to Baal‑peor
Psalms106:29 Thus they provoked Him to anger with their actions
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas stood up and interceded
Psalms106:31 And it was counted to him for righteousness
Psalms106:32 They also provoked Him to wrath at the waters of Meribah
Psalms106:33 Because they were rebellious against His Spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples
Psalms106:35 But they mingled with the nations And learned their works
Psalms106:36 And served their idols
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons
Psalms106:38 And they shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 Thus they became unclean in their works
Psalms106:40 So the anger of Yahweh was kindled against His people And He abhorred His inheritance.
Psalms106:41 Then He gave them into the hand of the nations
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times He would deliver them; But they were rebellious in their counsel
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless He looked upon their distress When He heard their cry of lamentation;
Psalms106:45 And He remembered for them His covenant
Psalms106:46 He also made them objects of compassion In the presence of all their captors.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Blessed be Yahweh

Psalms107:2 Let the redeemed of Yahweh say so
Psalms107:3 And gathered from the lands

Psalms107:6 Then they cried out to Yahweh in their trouble; He delivered them out of their distresses.
Psalms107:7 He led them by a straight way
Psalms107:8 Let them give thanks to Yahweh for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:9 For He has satisfied the thirsty soul
Psalms107:10 There were those who inhabited darkness and the shadow of death
Psalms107:11 Because they had rebelled against the words of God And spurned the counsel of the Most High.
Psalms107:12 So He subdued their heart with labor; They stumbled and there was none to help.
Psalms107:13 Then they cried out to Yahweh in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
Psalms107:14 He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death And broke their bands apart.
Psalms107:15 Let them give thanks to Yahweh for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:16 For He has shattered the doors of bronze And cut through the bars of iron.
Psalms107:17 Ignorant fools
Psalms107:18 Their soul abhorred all kinds of food

Psalms107:22 Let them also offer sacrifices of thanksgiving
Psalms107:23 Those who go down to the sea in ships
Psalms107:24 They have seen the works of Yahweh
Psalms107:25 He spoke and set up a stormy wind
Psalms107:26 They went up to the heavens
Psalms107:27 They staggered and swayed like a drunken man
Psalms107:28 Then they cried to Yahweh in their trouble
Psalms107:29 He caused the storm to stand still
Psalms107:30 Then they were glad because they were quiet
Psalms107:31 Let them give thanks to Yahweh for His lovingkindness
Psalms107:32 Let them exalt Him also in the assembly of the people
Psalms107:33 He makes rivers into a wilderness And springs of water into a thirsty ground;
Psalms107:34 A fruitful land into a salt waste
Psalms107:35 He makes a wilderness into a pool of water And a dry land into springs of water;
Psalms107:36 And there He causes the hungry to inhabit
Psalms107:37 And sow fields and plant vineyards
Psalms107:38 Also He blesses them and they multiply greatly
Psalms107:39 But when they decrease and are bowed down Through oppression
Psalms107:40 He pours contempt upon nobles And makes them wander in a pathless void.
Psalms107:41 But He sets the needy securely on high away from affliction
Psalms107:42 The upright see it and are glad; But all unrighteousness shuts its mouth.
Psalms107:43 Who is wise? Let him keep these things

Psalms109:1 For the choir director. Of David. A Psalm. O God of my praise
Psalms109:2 For they have opened a wicked mouth and a deceitful mouth against me; They have spoken to me with a lying tongue.
Psalms109:3 They have also surrounded me with words of hatred
Psalms109:4 In return for my love they accuse me; But I am in prayer.
Psalms109:5 Thus they have set upon me evil for good And hatred for my love.
Psalms109:6 Appoint a wicked man over him
Psalms109:7 When he is judged
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few; Let another take his office.
Psalms109:9 Let his sons be orphans And his wife a widow.
Psalms109:10 Let his sons wander aimlessly and beg; And let them search for food from their ruined homes.
Psalms109:11 Let the creditor seize all that he has
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend lovingkindness to him
Psalms109:13 Let those who follow him be cut off; In a following generation let their name be blotted out.
Psalms109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before Yahweh
Psalms109:15 Let them be before Yahweh continually
Psalms109:16 Because he did not remember to show lovingkindness
Psalms109:17 He also loved cursing
Psalms109:18 But he clothed himself with cursing as his garment
Psalms109:19 Let it be to him as a garment with which he wraps himself
Psalms109:20 This is the reward of my accusers from Yahweh
Psalms109:21 But as for You
Psalms109:22 For I am afflicted and needy
Psalms109:23 I am passing like a shadow when it is stretched out; I am shaken off like the locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees are feeble from fasting
Psalms109:25 As for me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 And let them know that this is Your hand; You
Psalms109:28 Let them curse
Psalms109:29 Let my accusers be clothed with dishonor
Psalms109:30 With my mouth I will give thanks abundantly to Yahweh; And in the midst of many I will praise Him.
Psalms109:31 For He stands at the right hand of the needy

Psalms110:5 The Lord is at Your right hand; He will crush kings in the day of His anger.

Psalms111:6 He has declared to His people the power of His works
Psalms111:7 The works of His hands are truth and justice; All His precepts are faithful.
Psalms111:8 They are upheld forever and ever; They are done in truth and uprightness.
Psalms111:9 He has sent redemption to His people; He has commanded His covenant forever; Holy and fearsome is His name.
Psalms111:10 The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom; Good insight belongs to all those who do His commandments; His praise stands forever.
