Psalms119:1 How blessed are those whose way is blameless
Psalms119:2 How blessed are those who observe His testimonies
Psalms119:3 They also do not work unrighteousness; They walk in His ways.
Psalms119:4 You have commanded us
Psalms119:5 Oh may my ways be established To keep Your statutes!
Psalms119:6 Then I shall not be ashamed When I look upon all Your commandments.
Psalms119:7 I shall give thanks to You with uprightness of heart
Psalms119:8 I shall keep Your statutes; Do not forsake me utterly!
Psalms119:9 How can a young man keep his way pure? By keeping it according to Your word.
Psalms119:10 With all my heart I have sought You; Do not let me stray from Your commandments.
Psalms119:11 Your word I have treasured in my heart
Psalms119:12 Blessed are You
Psalms119:13 With my lips I have recounted All the judgments of Your mouth.
Psalms119:14 I have rejoiced in the way of Your testimonies
Psalms119:15 I will muse upon Your precepts And look upon Your ways.
Psalms119:16 I shall delight in Your statutes; I shall not forget Your word.
Psalms119:17 Deal bountifully with Your slave
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes
Psalms119:19 I am a sojourner in the earth; Do not hide Your commandments from me.
Psalms119:20 My soul is crushed with longing For Your judgments at all times.
Psalms119:21 You rebuke the arrogant
Psalms119:22 Take away reproach and contempt from me
Psalms119:23 Even though princes sit and talk against me
Psalms119:24 Your testimonies also are my delight; They are my counselors.
Psalms119:25 My soul clings to the dust; Revive me according to Your word.
Psalms119:26 I have recounted my ways
Psalms119:27 Make me understand the way of Your precepts
Psalms119:28 My soul weeps because of grief; Raise me up according to Your word.
Psalms119:29 Remove the false way from me
Psalms119:30 I have chosen the faithful way; I have placed Your judgments before me.
Psalms119:31 I cling to Your testimonies; O Yahweh
Psalms119:32 I shall run the way of Your commandments
Psalms119:33 Instruct me
Psalms119:34 Cause me to understand
Psalms119:35 Cause me to walk in the path of Your commandments
Psalms119:36 Cause my heart to incline to Your testimonies And not to dishonest gain.
Psalms119:37 Cause my eyes to turn away from looking at worthlessness
Psalms119:38 Cause Your word to be established for Your slave
Psalms119:39 Cause my reproach which I dread to pass away
Psalms119:40 Behold
Psalms119:41 May Your lovingkindnesses also come to me
Psalms119:42 So I will have an answer for him who reproaches me
Psalms119:43 And do not take away the word of truth utterly from my mouth
Psalms119:44 So I will keep Your law continually
Psalms119:45 And I will walk in a wide place
Psalms119:46 I will also speak of Your testimonies before kings And I shall not be ashamed.
Psalms119:47 I shall delight in Your commandments
Psalms119:48 And I shall lift up my hands to Your commandments
Psalms119:49 Remember the word to Your slave
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in my affliction
Psalms119:51 The arrogant utterly scoff at me
Psalms119:52 I have remembered Your judgments from of old
Psalms119:53 Burning indignation has seized me because of the wicked
Psalms119:54 Your statutes have become my songs In the house of my sojourning.
Psalms119:55 I remember in the night Your name
Psalms119:56 This has become mine
Psalms119:57 Yahweh is my portion; I have promised to keep Your words.
Psalms119:58 I have sought to please Your face with all my heart; Be gracious to me according to Your word.
Psalms119:59 I thought upon my ways And I turned my feet to Your testimonies.
Psalms119:60 I hastened and did not delay To keep Your commandments.
Psalms119:61 The cords of the wicked have encircled me
Psalms119:62 At midnight I shall rise to give thanks to You Because of Your righteous judgments.
Psalms119:63 I am a companion of all those who fear You
Psalms119:64 The earth
Psalms119:65 You have dealt well with Your slave
Psalms119:66 Teach me good discernment and knowledge
Psalms119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray
Psalms119:68 You are good and do good; Teach me Your statutes.
Psalms119:69 The arrogant have smeared me with lying; With all my heart I will observe Your precepts.
Psalms119:70 Their heart is covered with fat
Psalms119:71 It is good for me that I was afflicted
Psalms119:72 The law of Your mouth is better to me Than thousands of gold and silver pieces.
Psalms119:73 Your hands made me and established me; Give me understanding
Psalms119:74 May those who fear You see me and be glad
Psalms119:75 I know
Psalms119:76 Oh may Your lovingkindness comfort me
Psalms119:77 May Your compassion come to me that I may live
Psalms119:78 May the arrogant be ashamed
Psalms119:79 May those who fear You turn to me
Psalms119:80 May my heart be blameless in Your statutes
Psalms119:81 My soul fails with longing for Your salvation; I wait for Your word.
Psalms119:82 My eyes fail with longing for Your word
Psalms119:83 For I am like a wineskin in the smoke
Psalms119:84 How many are the days of Your slave? When will You execute judgment on those who persecute me?
Psalms119:85 The arrogant have dug pits for me
Psalms119:86 All Your commandments are faithful; They have persecuted me with lying; help me!
Psalms119:87 They almost made an end of me on the earth
Psalms119:88 Revive me according to Your lovingkindness
Psalms119:89 Forever
Psalms119:90 Your faithfulness endures from generation to generation; You established the earth
Psalms119:91 They stand this day according to Your judgments
Psalms119:92 If Your law had not been my delight
Psalms119:93 I will never forget Your precepts
Psalms119:94 I am Yours
Psalms119:95 The wicked hope for me—to destroy me; I shall perceive Your testimonies.
Psalms119:96 I have seen a limit to all perfection; Your commandment is exceedingly broad.
Psalms119:97 Oh how I love Your law! It is my meditation all the day.
Psalms119:98 Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies
Psalms119:99 I have more insight than all my teachers
Psalms119:100 I perceive more than the aged
Psalms119:101 I have restrained my feet from every evil way
Psalms119:102 I have not turned aside from Your judgments
Psalms119:103 How sweet is Your word to my taste! Sweeter than honey to my mouth!
Psalms119:104 From Your precepts I get perception; Therefore I hate every false way.
Psalms119:105 Your word is a lamp to my feet And a light to my path.
Psalms119:106 I have sworn and I have confirmed
Psalms119:107 I am exceedingly afflicted; O Yahweh
Psalms119:108 Oh be pleased with the freewill offerings of my mouth
Psalms119:109 My soul is continually in my hand
Psalms119:110 The wicked have laid a snare for me
Psalms119:111 I have inherited Your testimonies forever
Psalms119:112 I have inclined my heart to do Your statutes Forever
Psalms119:113 I hate those who are double‑minded
Psalms119:114 You are my hiding place and my shield; I wait for Your word.
Psalms119:115 Depart from me
Psalms119:116 Sustain me according to Your word
Psalms119:117 Uphold me that I may be saved
Psalms119:118 You have rejected all those who stray from Your statutes
Psalms119:119 You remove all the wicked of the earth like dross; Therefore I love Your testimonies.
Psalms119:120 My flesh quakes for dread of You
Psalms119:121 I have done justice and righteousness; Do not leave me to my oppressors.
Psalms119:122 Be for Your slave a guarantee for good; Do not let the arrogant oppress me.
Psalms119:123 My eyes fail with longing for Your salvation And for Your righteous word.
Psalms119:124 Deal with Your slave according to Your lovingkindness And teach me Your statutes.
Psalms119:125 I am Your slave; give me understanding
Psalms119:126 It is time for Yahweh to act
Psalms119:127 Therefore I love Your commandments Above gold
Psalms119:128 Therefore I deem all Your precepts concerning everything to be right
Psalms119:129 Your testimonies are wonderful; Therefore my soul observes them.
Psalms119:130 The unfolding of Your words gives light; It gives understanding to the simple.
Psalms119:131 I opened my mouth wide and panted
Psalms119:132 Turn to me and be gracious to me
Psalms119:133 Establish my steps in Your word
Psalms119:134 Redeem me from the oppression of man
Psalms119:135 Make Your face shine upon Your slave
Psalms119:136 My eyes shed streams of water
Psalms119:137 Righteous are You
Psalms119:138 In righteousness
Psalms119:139 My zeal has consumed me
Psalms119:140 Your word is exceedingly refined
Psalms119:141 I am small and despised
Psalms119:142 Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness
Psalms119:143 Trouble and anguish have found me
Psalms119:144 Righteous are Your testimonies forever; Give me understanding that I may live.
Psalms119:145 I called with all my heart; answer me
Psalms119:146 I called to You; save me And I shall keep Your testimonies.
Psalms119:147 I eagerly greet the dawn and cry for help; I wait for Your words.
Psalms119:148 My eyes eagerly greet the night watches
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice according to Your lovingkindness; O Yahweh
Psalms119:150 Near are those who pursue wickedness; They are far from Your law.
Psalms119:151 Near are You
Psalms119:152 Of old I have known from Your testimonies That You have founded them forever.
Psalms119:153 See my affliction and rescue me
Psalms119:154 Plead my cause and redeem me; Revive me according to Your word.
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from the wicked
Psalms119:156 Many are Your compassions
Psalms119:157 Many are my persecutors and my adversaries
Psalms119:158 I see the treacherous and loathe them
Psalms119:159 See how I love Your precepts; O Yahweh
Psalms119:160 The sum of Your word is truth
Psalms119:161 Princes persecute me without cause
Psalms119:162 I rejoice at Your word
Psalms119:163 I hate and abhor lying
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day I praise You
Psalms119:165 Those who love Your law have much peace
Psalms119:166 I hope for Your salvation
Psalms119:167 My soul keeps Your testimonies
Psalms119:168 I keep Your precepts and Your testimonies
Psalms119:169 Let my cry of lamentation come near before You
Psalms119:170 Let my supplication come before You; Deliver me according to Your word.
Psalms119:171 Let my lips pour forth praise
Psalms119:172 Let my tongue answer with Your word
Psalms119:173 Let Your hand be ready to help me
Psalms119:174 I long for Your salvation
Psalms119:175 Let my soul live that it may praise You
Psalms119:176 I have wandered off like a lost sheep; search for Your slave

Psalms139:11 If I say

Psalms147:2 Yahweh builds up Jerusalem; He gathers the outcasts of Israel.
Psalms147:3 He is the One who heals the brokenhearted And who binds up their wounds
Psalms147:4 Who counts the number of the stars; He gives names to all of them.
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord and abundant in power; His discernment is infinite.
Psalms147:6 Yahweh helps up the afflicted; He brings down the wicked to the ground.
Psalms147:7 Sing to Yahweh with thanksgiving; Sing praises to our God on the lyre.
Psalms147:8 He is the One who covers the heavens with clouds
Psalms147:9 Who gives to the animal its food
Psalms147:10 He does not delight in the might of the horse; He does not take pleasure in the legs of a man.
Psalms147:11 Yahweh is pleased with those who fear Him
Psalms147:12 Laud Yahweh
Psalms147:13 For He strengthened the bars of your gates; He blessed your sons within you.
Psalms147:14 He is the One who sets peace in your borders
Psalms147:15 The One who sends forth His command to the earth; His word runs very swiftly
Psalms147:16 The One who gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes
Psalms147:17 Who casts forth His ice as fragments; Who can stand before His cold?
Psalms147:18 He sends forth His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow and so the waters flow
Psalms147:19 Who declares His words to Jacob
Psalms147:20 He has not done so with any nation; So as for His judgments

Psalms149:1 Praise Yah! Sing to Yahweh a new song
Psalms149:2 Let Israel be glad in his Maker; Let the sons of Zion rejoice in their King.
Psalms149:3 Let them praise His name with dancing; With tambourine and lyre let them sing praises to Him.
Psalms149:4 For Yahweh takes pleasure in His people; He will beautify the afflicted ones with salvation.
Psalms149:5 Let the holy ones exult in glory; Let them sing for joy on their beds.
Psalms149:6 Let the exaltations of God be in their throats
Psalms149:7 To execute vengeance on the nations And punishments on the peoples
Psalms149:8 To bind their kings with chains And their honored men with fetters of iron
Psalms149:9 To execute on them the judgment written; This is the majesty of all His holy ones. Praise Yah!
Psalms150:1 Praise Yah! Praise God in His sanctuary; Praise Him in His mighty expanse.
Psalms150:2 Praise Him for His mighty deeds; Praise Him according to the abundance of His greatness.
Psalms150:3 Praise Him with trumpet blast; Praise Him with harp and lyre.
Psalms150:4 Praise Him with tambourine and dancing; Praise Him with stringed instruments and pipe.
Psalms150:5 Praise Him with resounding cymbals; Praise Him with clashing cymbals.
Psalms150:6 Let everything that has breath praise Yah. Praise Yah!
Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon the son of David
Proverbs1:2 To know wisdom and discipline
Proverbs1:3 To receive discipline that leads to insight
Proverbs1:4 To give prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 Let the wise man hear and increase in learning
Proverbs1:6 To understand a proverb and an enigma
Proverbs1:7 The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of knowledge; Ignorant fools despise wisdom and discipline.
Proverbs1:8 Hear
Proverbs1:9 For they are a garland of grace for your head And ornaments about your neck.
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them alive like Sheol
Proverbs1:13 We will find all kinds of precious wealth
Proverbs1:14 Cast in your lot with us
Proverbs1:15 My son
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil And they hasten to shed blood.
Proverbs1:17 For it is no use that a net is spread In the sight of any bird;
Proverbs1:18 But they lie in wait for their own blood; They ambush their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 So are the paths of everyone who is greedy for gain; It takes away the life of its possessors.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom shouts in the street
Proverbs1:21 At the head of the noisy streets she calls out; At the entrance of the gates in the city she utters her sayings:
Proverbs1:22 “How long
Proverbs1:23 Turn to my reproof
Proverbs1:24 Because I called and you refused
Proverbs1:25 And you neglected all my counsel And were not willing to accept my reproof;
Proverbs1:26 I will also laugh at your disaster; I will mock when your dread comes
Proverbs1:27 When your dread comes like a storm And your disaster comes like a whirlwind
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call on me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge And did not choose the fear of Yahweh.
Proverbs1:30 They were not willing to accept my counsel
Proverbs1:31 So they shall eat of the fruit of their way And be satisfied with their own devices.
Proverbs1:32 For the turning away of the simple will kill them
Proverbs1:33 But he who listens to me shall dwell securely And will be at ease from the dread of evil.”
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 To make your ear pay attention to wisdom
Proverbs2:3 For if you call out for understanding
Proverbs2:4 If you seek her as silver And search for her as for hidden treasures;
Proverbs2:5 Then you will understand the fear of Yahweh And find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For Yahweh gives wisdom; From His mouth come knowledge and discernment.
Proverbs2:7 He stores up sound wisdom for the upright
Proverbs2:8 To guard the paths of justice
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice And equity—every good track.
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart And knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will keep you
Proverbs2:12 To deliver you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 From those who forsake the paths of uprightness To walk in the ways of darkness;
Proverbs2:14 Who are glad to do evil And they rejoice in the perversity of evil;
Proverbs2:15 Whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 To deliver you from the strange woman
Proverbs2:17 Who forsakes the close companion of her youth And forgets the covenant of her God;
Proverbs2:18 For her house sinks down to death And her tracks descend to the dead;
Proverbs2:19 All who go to her will not return
Proverbs2:20 So that you will walk in the way of good men And keep to the paths of the righteous.
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will dwell in the land And the blameless will remain in it;
Proverbs2:22 But the wicked will be cut off from the land And the treacherous will be torn away from it.
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 For length of days and years of life And peace they will add to you.
Proverbs3:3 Do not let lovingkindness and truth forsake you; Bind them around your neck
Proverbs3:4 So you will find favor and good insight In the eyes of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in Yahweh with all your heart And do not lean on your own understanding.
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways acknowledge Him
Proverbs3:7 Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear Yahweh and turn away from evil.
Proverbs3:8 It will be healing to your body And refreshment to your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor Yahweh from your wealth And from the first of all your produce;
Proverbs3:10 So your barns will be filled with plenty And your vats will burst with new wine.
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 For whom Yahweh loves He reproves
Proverbs3:13 How blessed is the man who finds wisdom And the man who obtains discernment.
Proverbs3:14 For her profit is better than the profit of silver And her produce better than fine gold.
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than pearls; And nothing you desire compares with her.
Proverbs3:16 Length of days is in her right hand; In her left hand are riches and glory.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are pleasant ways And all her pathways are peace.
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who seize her
Proverbs3:19 Yahweh by wisdom founded the earth
Proverbs3:20 By His knowledge the deeps were split up And the skies drip with dew.
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 So they will be life for your soul And grace for your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will walk in your way securely And your foot will not stumble.
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden dread Nor of the storm of the wicked when it comes;
Proverbs3:26 For Yahweh will be your confidence And will keep your foot from being caught.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not devise harm against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Do not contend with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy a man of violence And do not choose any of his ways.
Proverbs3:32 For the devious one is an abomination to Yahweh; But His secret council is with the upright.
Proverbs3:33 The curse of Yahweh is on the house of the wicked one
Proverbs3:34 Though He scoffs at the scoffers
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit glory
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 For I give you sound learning; Do not forsake my instruction.
Proverbs4:3 When I was a son to my father
Proverbs4:4 Then he instructed me and said to me
Proverbs4:5 Acquire wisdom! Acquire understanding! Do not forget and do not turn away from the sayings of my mouth.
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake her
Proverbs4:7 The beginning of wisdom is: Acquire wisdom; And with all your acquiring
Proverbs4:8 Prize her
Proverbs4:9 She will give for your head a garland of grace; She will present you with a crown of beauty.”
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I have instructed you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in upright tracks.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Seize discipline; do not let go. Guard her
Proverbs4:14 Do not enter the path of wicked men And do not step into the way of evil men.
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they do not sleep unless they do evil; And they are robbed of sleep unless they make someone stumble.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness And drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like thick darkness; They do not know over what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Do not let them deviate from your eyes; Keep them in the midst of your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them And healing to all his flesh.
Proverbs4:23 Guard your heart with all diligence
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you a perverse mouth And put devious lips far from you.
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look directly ahead And even let your eyelids be fixed straight in front of you.
Proverbs4:26 Watch the track of your feet And all your ways will be established.
Proverbs4:27 Do not turn to the right nor to the left; Turn your foot from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 That you may keep discretion And that your lips may guard knowledge.
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of a strange woman drip honey And smoother than oil is her speech;
Proverbs5:4 But her end is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death
Proverbs5:6 Lest she watch the path of life; Her tracks are unstable
Proverbs5:7 So now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her And do not go near the door of her house
Proverbs5:9 Lest you give your splendor to others And your years to the cruel one;
Proverbs5:10 Lest strangers be satisfied by your strength And by your painful labor
Proverbs5:11 And you groan at your end
Proverbs5:12 And you say
Proverbs5:13 I have not listened to the voice of my instructors
Proverbs5:14 I was almost in utter ruin In the midst of the assembly and congregation.”
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern And fresh water from your own well.
Proverbs5:16 Should your springs be dispersed abroad
Proverbs5:17 Let them be for you alone
Proverbs5:18 Let your fountain be blessed
Proverbs5:19 As a loving hind and a graceful doe
Proverbs5:20 So why should you
Proverbs5:21 For the ways of a man are before the eyes of Yahweh
Proverbs5:22 His own iniquities will capture him who is the wicked one
Proverbs5:23 He will die for lack of discipline
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 If you have been snared with the words of your mouth
Proverbs6:3 Do this then
Proverbs6:4 Give no sleep to your eyes
Proverbs6:5 Deliver yourself like a gazelle from the hunter’s hand And like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 Which
Proverbs6:8 Prepares her food in the summer And gathers her provision in the harvest.
Proverbs6:9 How long will you lie down
Proverbs6:10 “A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 Your poverty will come in like a vagabond And your want like an armed man.
Proverbs6:12 A vile person
Proverbs6:13 Who winks with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 Who with perversity in his heart continually devises evil
Proverbs6:15 Therefore his disaster will come suddenly; Instantly he will be broken and there will be no healing.
Proverbs6:16 There are six things which Yahweh hates
Proverbs6:17 Haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 A heart that devises wicked thoughts
Proverbs6:19 A false witness who breathes out lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them continually on your heart; Tie them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you walk about
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp and the law is light; And reproofs for discipline are the way of life
Proverbs6:24 To keep you from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Do not desire her beauty in your heart
Proverbs6:26 For on account of a harlot one is reduced to a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man take fire in his bosom And his clothes not be burned?
Proverbs6:28 Or can a man walk on hot coals And his feet not be scorched?
Proverbs6:29 So is the one who goes in to his neighbor’s wife; Whoever touches her will not go unpunished.
Proverbs6:30 Men do not despise a thief if he steals To fill himself when he is hungry;
Proverbs6:31 But when he is found
Proverbs6:32 The one who commits adultery with a woman is lacking a heart of wisdom; He who would destroy his soul does it.
Proverbs6:33 Wounds and disgrace he will find
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy enrages a man
Proverbs6:35 He will not accept any ransom
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commandments and live
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your fingers; Write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 In order to keep you from the strange woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I looked out through my lattice
Proverbs7:7 And I saw among the simple
Proverbs7:8 Passing through the street near her corner; And he strides along the way to her house
Proverbs7:9 In the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And behold
Proverbs7:11 She is boisterous and rebellious
Proverbs7:12 Stepping in the streets
Proverbs7:13 So she seizes him and kisses him And with a brazen face she says to him:
Proverbs7:14 “The sacrifices of peace offerings are with me; Today I paid my vows.
Proverbs7:15 Therefore I have come out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have spread my couch with coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have sprinkled my bed With myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home
Proverbs7:20 He took a bag of silver in his hand
Proverbs7:21 With her abundant persuasions she entices him; With her flattering lips she drives him to herself.
Proverbs7:22 He suddenly follows her As an ox goes to the slaughter
Proverbs7:23 Until an arrow pierces through his liver; As a bird hastens to the snare
Proverbs7:24 So now
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart go astray into her ways
Proverbs7:26 For many are the slain whom she has cast down
Proverbs7:27 The ways to Sheol are in her house
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom call
Proverbs8:2 At the top of the heights upon the way
Proverbs8:3 Beside the gates
Proverbs8:4 “To you
Proverbs8:5 O simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 For my mouth will utter truth; And wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; There is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all straightforward to him who understands
Proverbs8:10 Take my discipline and not silver
Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is better than pearls; And all desirable things cannot compare with her.
Proverbs8:12 “I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of Yahweh is to hate evil; Pride and arrogance and the evil way And the mouth of perverted words
Proverbs8:14 Counsel is mine and sound wisdom; I am understanding
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 By me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me; And those who earnestly seek me will find me.
Proverbs8:18 Riches and glory are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than fine gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the path of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 To give those who love me an inheritance of wealth
Proverbs8:22 “Yahweh possessed me at the beginning of His way
Proverbs8:23 From everlasting I was installed
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 While He had not yet made the earth and the fields outside
Proverbs8:27 When He established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 When He made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 When He set for the sea its boundary So that the water would not pass over His command
Proverbs8:30 Then I was beside Him
Proverbs8:31 Rejoicing in the world
Proverbs8:32 “So now
Proverbs8:33 Hear discipline and be wise
Proverbs8:34 How blessed is the man who hears me
Proverbs8:35 For he who finds me finds life And obtains favor from Yahweh.
Proverbs8:36 But he who sins against me does violence to his own soul; All those who hate me love death.”
Proverbs9:1 Wisdom has built her house
Proverbs9:2 She has slaughtered her cattle
Proverbs9:3 She has sent out her maidens
Proverbs9:4 “Whoever is simple
Proverbs9:5 “Come
Proverbs9:6 Forsake your simplicity and live
Proverbs9:7 He who disciplines a scoffer receives disgrace for himself
Proverbs9:8 Do not reprove a scoffer
Proverbs9:9 Give knowledge to a wise man and he will be still wiser
Proverbs9:10 The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom
Proverbs9:11 For by me your days will become many
Proverbs9:12 If you are wise
Proverbs9:13 The woman of foolishness is boisterous
Proverbs9:14 She sits at the doorway of her house
Proverbs9:15 To call to those who pass by that way
Proverbs9:16 “Whoever is simple
Proverbs9:17 “Stolen water is sweet; And bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
Proverbs9:18 But he does not know that the dead are there
Proverbs10:1 The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a father glad
Proverbs10:2 Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit
Proverbs10:3 Yahweh will not allow the soul of the righteous to hunger
Proverbs10:4 Poor is he who works with a slack hand
Proverbs10:5 He who gathers in summer is a son who acts insightfully
Proverbs10:6 Blessings are on the head of the righteous
Proverbs10:7 The remembrance of the righteous is blessed
Proverbs10:8 The wise of heart will receive commandments
Proverbs10:9 He who walks in integrity walks securely
Proverbs10:10 He who winks the eye causes pain
Proverbs10:11 The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life
Proverbs10:12 Hatred stirs up strife
Proverbs10:13 On the lips of the one who has understanding
Proverbs10:14 Wise men store up knowledge
Proverbs10:15 The rich man’s wealth is his strong city
Proverbs10:16 The wages of the righteous is life
Proverbs10:17 He is on the path of life who keeps discipline
Proverbs10:18 He who covers up hatred has lying lips
Proverbs10:19 When there are many words
Proverbs10:20 The tongue of the righteous is as choice silver
Proverbs10:21 The lips of the righteous feed many
Proverbs10:22 It is the blessing of Yahweh that makes rich
Proverbs10:23 Doing wickedness is like laughing to a fool
Proverbs10:24 What the wicked dreads will come upon him
Proverbs10:25 When the whirlwind passes
Proverbs10:26 Like vinegar to the teeth and like smoke to the eyes
Proverbs10:27 The fear of Yahweh prolongs life
Proverbs10:28 The expectation of the righteous is gladness
Proverbs10:29 The way of Yahweh is a stronghold to the one with integrity
Proverbs10:30 The righteous will never be shaken
Proverbs10:31 The mouth of the righteous bears wisdom
Proverbs10:32 The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable

Proverbs11:2 When arrogance comes
Proverbs11:3 The integrity of the upright will lead them
Proverbs11:4 Wealth will not profit in the day of wrath
Proverbs11:5 The righteousness of the blameless will make his way straight
Proverbs11:6 The righteousness of the upright will deliver them
Proverbs11:7 When a wicked man dies
Proverbs11:8 The righteous is rescued from distress
Proverbs11:9 With his mouth the godless man corrupts his neighbor
Proverbs11:10 When it goes well with the righteous
Proverbs11:11 By the blessing of the upright a city is raised up
Proverbs11:12 He who despises his neighbor lacks a heart of wisdom
Proverbs11:13 He who goes about as a slanderer reveals secrets
Proverbs11:14 Where there is no guidance the people fall
Proverbs11:15 If one becomes a guarantor for a stranger
Proverbs11:16 A gracious woman holds fast to glory
Proverbs11:17 The man of lovingkindness deals bountifully with his soul
Proverbs11:18 The wicked earns deceptive wages
Proverbs11:19 He who is steadfast in righteousness will attain to life
Proverbs11:20 Those with a crooked heart are an abomination to Yahweh
Proverbs11:21 Assuredly
Proverbs11:22 As a ring of gold in a swine’s snout So is a beautiful woman who turns away from discretion.
Proverbs11:23 The desire of the righteous is only good
Proverbs11:24 There is one who scatters
Proverbs11:25 The soul that blesses will be enriched
Proverbs11:26 He who withholds grain
Proverbs11:27 He who earnestly seeks good seeks favor
Proverbs11:28 He who trusts in his riches will fall
Proverbs11:29 He who troubles his own house will inherit wind
Proverbs11:30 The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life
Proverbs11:31 If the righteous will be repaid in the earth

Proverbs12:7 The wicked are overthrown and are no more

Proverbs13:2 From the fruit of a man’s mouth he eats what is good

Proverbs15:8 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh
Proverbs15:9 The way of the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh
Proverbs15:10 Grievous discipline is for him who forsakes the way; He who hates reproof will die.

Proverbs15:12 A scoffer does not love one who reproves him
Proverbs15:13 A glad heart makes a face look good
Proverbs15:14 The heart of the one who has understanding seeks knowledge
Proverbs15:15 All the days of the afflicted are evil
Proverbs15:16 Better is a little with the fear of Yahweh Than great treasure and turmoil with it.
Proverbs15:17 Better is a dish of vegetables where there is love Than a fattened ox and hatred in it.
Proverbs15:18 A hot‑tempered man stirs up strife

Proverbs15:22 Without consultation
Proverbs15:23 A man has gladness in an apt answer

Proverbs15:26 Evil thoughts are an abomination to Yahweh

Proverbs15:29 Yahweh is far from the wicked
Proverbs15:30 Bright eyes gladden the heart; A good report puts fat on the bones.

Proverbs15:32 He who neglects discipline despises his soul

Proverbs16:2 All the ways of a man are pure in his own sight
Proverbs16:3 Commit your works to Yahweh And your plans will be established.
Proverbs16:4 Yahweh has made everything for its own purpose
Proverbs16:5 Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to Yahweh; Assuredly
Proverbs16:6 By lovingkindness and truth iniquity is atoned for
Proverbs16:7 When a man’s ways are pleasing to Yahweh

Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur the son of Jakeh
Proverbs30:2 Surely I am more senseless than any man
Proverbs30:3 Neither have I learned wisdom
Proverbs30:4 Who has ascended into heaven and descended? Who has gathered the wind in His fists? Who has wrapped the waters in His garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name? And what is His Son’s name? Surely you know!
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God is tested; He is a shield to those who take refuge in Him.
Proverbs30:6 Do not add to His words Lest He reprove you

Proverbs30:8 Keep worthlessness and every false word far from me
Proverbs30:9 Lest I be full and deny You and say
Proverbs30:10 Do not slander a slave to his master
Proverbs30:11 There is a generation that curses its father And does not bless its mother.
Proverbs30:12 There is a generation that is clean in its own eyes
Proverbs30:13 There is a generation—oh how haughty are its eyes! And its eyelids are lifted up.
Proverbs30:14 There is a generation whose teeth are like swords And its fangs like knives
Proverbs30:15 The leech has two daughters
Proverbs30:16 Sheol
Proverbs30:17 The eye that mocks a father And despises obedience to a mother
Proverbs30:18 There are three things which are too wonderful for me
Proverbs30:19 The way of an eagle in the sky
Proverbs30:20 This is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth
Proverbs30:21 Under three things the earth quakes
Proverbs30:22 Under a slave when he becomes king
Proverbs30:23 Under an unloved woman when she gets a husband
Proverbs30:24 Four things are small on the earth
Proverbs30:25 The ants are not a strong people
Proverbs30:26 The shephanim are not a mighty people
Proverbs30:27 The locusts have no king
Proverbs30:28 The lizard you may grasp with the hands
Proverbs30:29 There are three things which are stately in their march
Proverbs30:30 The lion which is mighty among the animals And does not turn back before any
Proverbs30:31 The strutting rooster
Proverbs30:32 If you have been wickedly foolish in lifting yourself up
Proverbs30:33 For pressing milk brings forth butter
Proverbs31:1 The words of King Lemuel
Proverbs31:2 What
Proverbs31:3 Do not give your excellence to women
Proverbs31:4 It is not for kings
Proverbs31:5 Lest he drink and forget what is decreed
Proverbs31:6 Give strong drink to him who is perishing
Proverbs31:7 Let him drink and forget his poverty And he will not remember his trouble any longer.
Proverbs31:8 Open your mouth for the mute
Proverbs31:9 Open your mouth
Proverbs31:10 An excellent wife
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her
Proverbs31:12 She deals bountifully with him for good and not evil All the days of her life.
Proverbs31:13 She searches for wool and flax And works with her hands in delight.
Proverbs31:14 She is like merchant ships; She brings her food from afar.
Proverbs31:15 And she rises while it is still night
Proverbs31:16 She makes plans for a field and buys it; From the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She girds herself with strength And makes her arms strong.
Proverbs31:18 She senses that her gain is good; Her lamp does not go out at night.
Proverbs31:19 She stretches out her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She extends her hand to the poor
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of the snow for her household
Proverbs31:22 She makes coverings for herself; Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the gates
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them
Proverbs31:25 Strength and majesty are her clothing
Proverbs31:26 She opens her mouth in wisdom
Proverbs31:27 She watches over the ways of her household
Proverbs31:28 Her children rise up and bless her; As for her husband
Proverbs31:29 “Many daughters have done excellently
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceitful and beauty is vain
Proverbs31:31 Give to her from the fruit of her hands
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 “Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What advantage does man have in all his labor In which he labors under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation goes and a generation comes
Ecclesiastes1:5 Also
Ecclesiastes1:6 Going toward the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers go into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are wearisome; Man is not able to speak of it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 That which has been is that which will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there anything of which one might say
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of earlier things; And also of the later things which will be
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I gave my heart to seek and explore by wisdom concerning all that has been done under heaven. It is a grievous endeavor which God has given to the sons of men with which to occupy themselves.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the works which have been done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is bent cannot be straightened
Ecclesiastes1:16 I spoke within my heart
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I gave my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and simpleminded folly; I came to know that this also is striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 Because in much wisdom there is much vexation
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I explored with my heart how to stimulate my body with wine—while my heart was guiding me wisely—and how to seize simpleminded folly
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made my works great: I built houses for myself; I planted vineyards for myself;
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made for myself gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made for myself pools of water from which to water a forest of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 Also
Ecclesiastes2:9 Then I became great and increased more than all who preceded me in Jerusalem. My wisdom also stood by me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 All that my eyes asked for I did not refuse them. I did not withhold my heart from any gladness
Ecclesiastes2:11 Thus I turned to all my works which my hands had done and the labor which I had labored to do
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I turned to see wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that there is an advantage in wisdom over simpleminded folly as light has an advantage over darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man’s eyes are in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there is no remembrance of the wise man along with the fool forever
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 Thus I hated all the fruit of my labor for which I had labored under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be a wise man or a man of simpleminded folly? Yet he will have power over all the fruit of my labor for which I have labored and for which I have acted wisely under the sun. This too is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 Therefore I turned my heart to despair of all my labor for which I had labored under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 When there is a man who has labored with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what does a man get in all his labor and in the striving of his heart with which he labors under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 Because all his days his endeavor is painful and vexing; even at night his heart does not lie down. This too is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a man than to eat and drink and have his soul see good in his labor. This also I have seen that it is from the hand of God.
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat and who can have enjoyment outside of Him?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to a man who is good before Him
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is an appointed time for everything. And there is a time for every matter under heaven—
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to give birth and a time to die; A time to plant and a time to uproot what is planted.
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill and a time to heal; A time to tear down and a time to build up.
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep and a time to laugh; A time to mourn and a time to dance.
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to throw stones and a time to gather stones; A time to embrace and a time to shun embracing.
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to search and a time to lose; A time to keep and a time to throw away.
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to tear apart and a time to sew together; A time to be silent and a time to speak.
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love and a time to hate; A time for war and a time for peace.
Ecclesiastes3:9 What advantage is there to the worker from that in which he labors?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the endeavor which God has given the sons of men with which to occupy themselves.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in their heart
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to be glad and to do good in one’s lifetime;
Ecclesiastes3:13 moreover
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that everything God does will be forever; there is nothing to add to it and there is nothing to take from it—God has so worked that men should fear Him.
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is has been already and that which will be has already been
Ecclesiastes3:16 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart concerning the sons of men
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the fate of the sons of men and the fate of beasts is the same fate for each of them. As one dies so dies the other
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to the same place. All came from the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows that the breath of man ascends upward and the breath of the beast descends downward to the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 I have seen that nothing is better than that man should be glad in his works
Ecclesiastes4:1 Then I looked again at all the acts of oppression which were being done under the sun. And behold
Ecclesiastes4:2 So I lauded the dead who are already dead more than the living who are still living.
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better off than both of them is the one who never has been
Ecclesiastes4:4 I have seen that every labor and every success of the work is the result of jealousy between a man and his neighbor. This too is vanity and striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands in embrace and consumes his own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 One hand full of rest is better than two fists full of labor and striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Then I looked again at vanity under the sun.
Ecclesiastes4:8 There was a certain man without a second man
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one because they have good wages for their labor.
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if either of them falls
Ecclesiastes4:11 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes4:12 And if one can overpower him who is alone
Ecclesiastes4:13 A poor yet wise lad is better than an old and foolish king who no longer knows how to receive warning.
Ecclesiastes4:14 For he has come out of prison to become king
Ecclesiastes4:15 I have seen all the living who walk about under the sun go along with the second lad who stands in place of him.
Ecclesiastes4:16 There is no end to all the people
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps as you go to the house of God and draw near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools; for they do not know they are doing evil.
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be hasty with your mouth or impulsive in your heart to bring up a matter in the presence of God. For God is in heaven but you are on the earth; therefore let your words be few.
Ecclesiastes5:3 For the dream comes through abundant endeavor and the voice of a fool through abundant words.
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not allow your mouth to cause your flesh to sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For in many dreams and vanities are many words. Rather
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see oppression of the poor and robbery of justice and righteousness in the province
Ecclesiastes5:9 But the advantage of the land in everything is this—a king committed to a cultivated field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 He who loves money will not be satisfied with money
Ecclesiastes5:11 When good things increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of the laboring man is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a sickening evil which I have seen under the sun: riches being hoarded by their master to his own evil demise.
Ecclesiastes5:14 And those riches were lost through a bad endeavor; and he became the father of a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he had come naked from his mother’s womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This also is a sickening evil—exactly as a man came
Ecclesiastes5:17 Also
Ecclesiastes5:18 Here is what I have seen to be good
Ecclesiastes5:19 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will not remember much the days of his life because God allows him to occupy himself with the gladness of his heart.
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil which I have seen under the sun and it is prevalent among men—
Ecclesiastes6:2 a man to whom God gives riches and wealth and honor so that his soul lacks nothing of all that he desires; yet God does not empower him to eat from them
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man becomes the father of one hundred children and lives many years
Ecclesiastes6:4 for that one comes in vanity and goes into darkness; and that one’s name is covered in darkness.
Ecclesiastes6:5 Indeed
Ecclesiastes6:6 Even if the other man lives one thousand years twice and does not see good things—do not all go to the same place?”
Ecclesiastes6:7 All a man’s labor is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage does the wise man have over the fool? What advantage does the afflicted man have
Ecclesiastes6:9 What the eyes see is better than what the soul goes after. This too is vanity and striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever exists has already been named
Ecclesiastes6:11 For there are many words which increase vanity. What then is the advantage to a man?
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for a man during his lifetime
Ecclesiastes7:1 Better is a good name than good oil
Ecclesiastes7:2 Better to go to a house of mourning Than to go to a house of feasting Because that is the end of all mankind
Ecclesiastes7:3 Better is vexation than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 Better to listen to the rebuke of a wise man Than for one to listen to the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling sound of thorn bushes under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 For oppression gives a wise man over to madness
Ecclesiastes7:8 Better is the end of a matter than its beginning; Better is patience of spirit than haughtiness of spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not be eager in your spirit to be vexed
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom along with an inheritance is good And an advantage to those who see the sun.
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom is a shadow of protection as money is a shadow of protection
Ecclesiastes7:13 See the work of God
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day when there is good be of good cheer
Ecclesiastes7:15 I have seen everything during my days of vanity; there is a righteous man who perishes in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be excessively righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be excessively wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you seize one thing and also not let go of the other; for the one who fears God comes forth with both of them.
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom strengthens a wise man more than ten men with power who are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Indeed
Ecclesiastes7:21 Also
Ecclesiastes7:22 For your heart also knows that you likewise have many times cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 I tested all this with wisdom
Ecclesiastes7:24 What has been is far away and exceedingly deep. Who can find it?
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned my heart to know
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I found more bitter than death the woman whose heart is snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 “See
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul still seeks but has not found. I have found one man out of a thousand
Ecclesiastes7:29 See
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise man and who knows the interpretation of a matter? A man’s wisdom illumines his face and causes his stern face to beam.
Ecclesiastes8:2 I say
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not be in a hurry to go from his presence. Do not stand in an evil matter
Ecclesiastes8:4 Since the word of the king is powerful
Ecclesiastes8:5 He who keeps a royal command experiences no evil thing
Ecclesiastes8:6 For there is a proper time and custom for every matter
Ecclesiastes8:7 If no one knows what will happen
Ecclesiastes8:8 There is no man who has power to restrain the wind with the wind
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I have seen and given my heart to every work that has been done under the sun wherein a man has power over another man to his calamity.
Ecclesiastes8:10 So then
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against an evil work is not executed quickly
Ecclesiastes8:12 Although a sinner does evil a hundred times and may prolong his life
Ecclesiastes8:13 But it will not be well for the wicked man
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is vanity which is done on the earth
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I laud gladness
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I gave my heart to know wisdom and to see the endeavor which has been done on the earth (even though one never sees sleep with his eyes day or night)
Ecclesiastes8:17 and I saw every work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 For I have given all this to my heart and explain it that righteous men
Ecclesiastes9:2 It is the same for all. There is one fate for the righteous and for the wicked; for the good
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 For whoever is joined with all the living
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know they will die; but the dead do not know anything
Ecclesiastes9:6 Indeed their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go then
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your clothes be white all the time
Ecclesiastes9:9 See life with the woman whom you love all the days of your vain life
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I again saw under the sun that the race is not to the swift and the battle is not to the mighty
Ecclesiastes9:12 Moreover
Ecclesiastes9:13 Also this I came to see as wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small city with few men in it
Ecclesiastes9:15 But there was found in it a poor wise man
Ecclesiastes9:16 So I said
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise heard in restfulness are better than the shouting of a ruler among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies make a perfumer’s oil stink
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man’s heart directs him toward the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when the simpleminded fool walks along the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the ruler’s temper rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 folly is set in many exalted places while rich men sit in humble places.
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves riding on horses and princes walking like slaves on the land.
Ecclesiastes10:8 He who digs a pit may fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 He who quarries stones may be hurt by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the axe is dull and he does not sharpen its edge
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bites before being charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 Words from the mouth of a wise man are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 the beginning of the words of his mouth is simpleminded folly
Ecclesiastes10:14 Yet the simpleminded fool multiplies words. No man knows what will happen
Ecclesiastes10:15 The labor of a fool so wearies him that he does not even know how to go to a city.
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Blessed are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Through indolence the beams sag
Ecclesiastes10:19 Men prepare bread for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast your bread on the surface of the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Divide your portion to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full
Ecclesiastes11:4 He who watches the wind will not sow
Ecclesiastes11:5 Just as you do not know the path of the wind and how bones are formed in the womb of the pregnant woman
Ecclesiastes11:6 Sow your seed in the morning and do not put your hands down in the evening
Ecclesiastes11:7 The light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 Indeed
Ecclesiastes11:9 Be glad
Ecclesiastes11:10 So
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember also your Creator in the days of your youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun and the light
Ecclesiastes12:3 in the day that the watchmen of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 and the doors on the street are shut as the sound of the grinding mill is low
Ecclesiastes12:5 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember Him before the silver cord is snapped and the golden bowl is crushed
Ecclesiastes12:7 then the dust will return to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 “Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes12:9 In addition to being a wise man
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Preacher sought to find delightful words and words of truth written uprightly.
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of wise men are like goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 But in addition to this
Ecclesiastes12:13 The end of the matter
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every work to judgment

SongOfSongs3:3 The watchmen who go about the city found me

SongOfSongs5:16 His mouth is full of sweetness. And he is wholly desirable. This is my beloved and this is my friend

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz concerning Judah and Jerusalem
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 An ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Alas
Isaiah1:5 Where will you be stricken again
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head There is nothing sound in it
Isaiah1:7 Your land is desolate; Your cities are burned with fire; Your fields—strangers are devouring them in your presence; It is desolate
Isaiah1:8 The daughter of Zion is left like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Unless Yahweh of hosts Had left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of Yahweh
Isaiah1:11 “What are your multiplied sacrifices to Me?” Says Yahweh. “I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams And the fat of fed cattle; And in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before Me
Isaiah1:13 Bring your worthless offerings no longer
Isaiah1:14 My soul hates your new moon festivals and your appointed times
Isaiah1:15 So when you spread out your hands in prayer
Isaiah1:16 “Wash yourselves
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do good; Seek justice
Isaiah1:18 “Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obey
Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a harlot
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels And companions of thieves; Everyone loves a bribe And pursues rewards. They do not execute justice for the orphan
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord
Isaiah1:25 I will also turn My hand against you
Isaiah1:26 Then I will have your judges return as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion will be redeemed with justice And her repentant ones with righteousness.
Isaiah1:28 But transgressors and sinners will be broken together
Isaiah1:29 For you will be ashamed of the oaks which you have desired
Isaiah1:30 For you will be like an oak whose leaf withers away Or as a garden that has no water.
Isaiah1:31 And the strong man will become tinder
Isaiah2:1 The word which Isaiah the son of Amoz beheld concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
Isaiah2:2 Now it will be that In the last days The mountain of the house of Yahweh Will be established as the head of the mountains
Isaiah2:3 And many peoples will come and say
Isaiah2:4 And He will judge between the nations
Isaiah2:5 Come
Isaiah2:6 For You have abandoned Your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land has also been filled with silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land has also been filled with idols; They worship the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So the common man has been bowed down
Isaiah2:10 Enter the rock and hide in the dust From the dread of Yahweh and from the splendor of His majesty.
Isaiah2:11 The lofty look of man will be made low
Isaiah2:12 For Yahweh of hosts will have a day of reckoning Against everyone who is proud and high And against everyone who is lifted up
Isaiah2:13 And it will be against all the cedars of Lebanon that are high and lifted up
Isaiah2:14 Against all the high mountains
Isaiah2:15 Against every lofty tower
Isaiah2:16 Against all the ships of Tarshish And against all the desirable craft.
Isaiah2:17 The loftiness of man will be bowed down
Isaiah2:18 But the idols will completely vanish.
Isaiah2:19 Men will go into caves of the rocks And into holes of the ground Before the dread of Yahweh And the splendor of His majesty
Isaiah2:20 In that day men will cast away to the moles and the bats Their idols of silver and their idols of gold
Isaiah2:21 In order to go into the caverns of the rocks and the clefts of the cliffs Before the dread of Yahweh and the splendor of His majesty
Isaiah2:22 Stop regarding man
Isaiah3:1 For behold
Isaiah3:2 The mighty man and the man of war
Isaiah3:3 The commander of fifty and the highly respected man
Isaiah3:4 And I will make young men their princes
Isaiah3:5 And the people will be oppressed
Isaiah3:6 When a man grasps his brother in his father’s house
Isaiah3:7 He will protest on that day
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen
Isaiah3:9 The expression of their faces answers against them
Isaiah3:10 Say to the righteous that it will go well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked! It will go badly with him
Isaiah3:12 O My people! Their taskmasters are infants
Isaiah3:13 Yahweh takes His stand to contend
Isaiah3:14 Yahweh enters into judgment with the elders of His people and His princes
Isaiah3:15 What do you mean by crushing My people And grinding the face of the afflicted?” Declares Lord Yahweh of hosts.
Isaiah3:16 Moreover
Isaiah3:17 Therefore the Lord will smite the skull of the daughters of Zion with scabs
Isaiah3:18 In that day the Lord will remove the beauty of their anklets
Isaiah3:19 dangling earrings
Isaiah3:20 headdresses
Isaiah3:21 finger rings
Isaiah3:22 festal robes
Isaiah3:23 hand mirrors
Isaiah3:24 Now it will be that instead of sweet perfume there will be the smell of rot; Instead of a belt
Isaiah3:25 Your men will fall by the sword And your mighty ones in battle.
Isaiah3:26 And her gates will lament and mourn
Isaiah4:1 And seven women will take hold of one man in that day
Isaiah4:2 In that day the Branch of Yahweh will be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 It will be that he who remains in Zion and is left in Jerusalem will be called holy—everyone who is written down for life in Jerusalem.
Isaiah4:4 When the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and rinsed away the bloodshed of Jerusalem from her midst
Isaiah4:5 then Yahweh will create over the whole area of Mount Zion and over her convocation a cloud by day
Isaiah4:6 And there will be a booth to give shade from the heat by day
Isaiah5:1 Let me sing now for my well‑beloved A song of my beloved concerning His vineyard. My well‑beloved had a vineyard on a fertile hill.
Isaiah5:2 He dug it all around
Isaiah5:3 “So now
Isaiah5:4 What more was there to do for My vineyard that I have not done in it? Why
Isaiah5:5 So now let Me tell you what I am going to do to My vineyard: I will remove its hedge
Isaiah5:6 I will lay it waste; It will not be pruned or hoed
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of Yahweh of hosts is the house of Israel And the men of Judah His delightful plant. Thus He hoped for justice
Isaiah5:8 Woe to those who add house to house and join field to field
Isaiah5:9 In my ears Yahweh of hosts has sworn
Isaiah5:10 For ten acres of vineyard will yield only one bath of wine
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning that they may pursue strong drink
Isaiah5:12 And their banquets are accompanied by lyre and harp
Isaiah5:13 Therefore My people go into exile for their lack of knowledge; And their honorable men are famished
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol has enlarged its throat and opened its mouth without limit; And Jerusalem’s majesty
Isaiah5:15 So the common man will be bowed down and the man of importance will be made low
Isaiah5:16 But Yahweh of hosts will be lofty in judgment
Isaiah5:17 Then the lambs will graze as in their pasture
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who drag iniquity with the cords of worthlessness
Isaiah5:19 Who say
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good and good evil
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes And understanding in their own sight!
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are mighty men in drinking wine And valiant men in mixing strong drink
Isaiah5:23 Who declare the wicked righteous for a bribe
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 On this account
Isaiah5:26 He will also lift up a standard to the distant nations
Isaiah5:27 No one in it is weary or stumbles
Isaiah5:28 Its arrows are sharp and all its bows are bent; The hoofs of its horses seem like flint and its chariot wheels like a whirlwind.
Isaiah5:29 Its roaring is like a lioness
Isaiah5:30 And it will growl over it in that day like the roaring of the sea. If one looks to the land
Isaiah6:1 In the year of King Uzziah’s death I saw the Lord sitting on a throne
Isaiah6:2 Seraphim stood above Him
Isaiah6:3 And one called out to another and said
Isaiah6:4 And the foundations of the thresholds shook at the voice of him who called out
Isaiah6:5 Then I said
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphim flew to me with a burning coal in his hand
Isaiah6:7 And he touched my mouth with it and said
Isaiah6:8 Then I heard the voice of the Lord
Isaiah6:9 He said
Isaiah6:10 Render the hearts of this people insensitive
Isaiah6:11 Then I said
Isaiah6:12 And Yahweh has removed men far away
Isaiah6:13 Yet there will be a tenth portion in it
Isaiah7:1 Now it happened in the days of Ahaz
Isaiah7:2 When it was told to the house of David
Isaiah7:3 Then Yahweh said to Isaiah
Isaiah7:4 and say to him
Isaiah7:5 Because Aram
Isaiah7:6 “Let us go up against Judah and terrorize it
Isaiah7:7 thus says Lord Yahweh: “It shall not stand
Isaiah7:8 For the head of Aram is Damascus and the head of Damascus is Rezin (now within another sixty-five years Ephraim will be shattered
Isaiah7:9 and the head of Ephraim is Samaria and the head of Samaria is the son of Remaliah. If you do not establish your faith in Yahweh
Isaiah7:10 Then Yahweh spoke again to Ahaz
Isaiah7:11 “Ask a sign for yourself from Yahweh your God; make it deep as Sheol or high as heaven.”
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz said
Isaiah7:13 Then he said
Isaiah7:14 Therefore the Lord Himself will give you a sign: Behold
Isaiah7:15 He will eat curds and honey in order that He will know to refuse evil and choose good.
Isaiah7:16 For before the boy will know to refuse evil and choose good
Isaiah7:17 Yahweh will bring on you
Isaiah7:18 And it will be in that day
Isaiah7:19 And they will all come and rest upon the steep ravines
Isaiah7:20 In that day
Isaiah7:21 And it will be in that day
Isaiah7:22 and because of the abundance of the milk produced
Isaiah7:23 And it will be in that day
Isaiah7:24 People will come there with bows and arrows because all the land will be briars and thorns.
Isaiah7:25 As for all the hills which used to be cultivated with the hoe
Isaiah8:1 Then Yahweh said to me
Isaiah8:2 And I will take to Myself faithful witnesses for testimony
Isaiah8:3 Then I drew near to the prophetess
Isaiah8:4 for before the boy knows how to cry out ‘My father’ or ‘My mother
Isaiah8:5 Again Yahweh spoke to me further
Isaiah8:6 “Inasmuch as these people have rejected the gently flowing waters of Shiloah And rejoice in Rezin and the son of Remaliah;
Isaiah8:7 Now therefore
Isaiah8:8 Then it will sweep on into Judah; it will overflow and pass through; It will reach even to the neck; And the spread of its wings will fill the breadth of Your land
Isaiah8:9 “Be broken
Isaiah8:10 Devise counsel
Isaiah8:11 For thus Yahweh spoke to me with a strong hand and disciplined me not to walk in the way of this people
Isaiah8:12 “You are not to say
Isaiah8:13 It is Yahweh of hosts whom you should regard as holy. And He shall be your fear
Isaiah8:14 Then He shall become a sanctuary; But to both the houses of Israel
Isaiah8:15 And many will stumble over them; Then they will fall and be broken; They will even be snared and caught.”
Isaiah8:16 Bind up the testimony
Isaiah8:17 And I will wait for Yahweh who is hiding His face from the house of Jacob; and I will hope for Him.
Isaiah8:18 Behold
Isaiah8:19 Now when they say to you
Isaiah8:20 To the law and to the testimony! If they do not speak according to this word
Isaiah8:21 And they will pass through the land hard-pressed and hungry
Isaiah8:22 Then they will look to the earth
Isaiah9:1 But there will be no more gloom for her who was in anguish; in earlier times He treated the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali with contempt
Isaiah9:2 The people who walk in darkness Will see a great light; Those who live in the land of the shadow of death
Isaiah9:3 You shall multiply the nation
Isaiah9:4 For You shall shatter the yoke of their burden and the staff on their shoulders
Isaiah9:5 For every boot of the booted warrior in the rumbling of battle
Isaiah9:6 For a child will be born to us
Isaiah9:7 There will be no end to the increase of His government or of peace
Isaiah9:8 The Lord sends a message against Jacob
Isaiah9:9 And all the people know it
Isaiah9:10 “The bricks have fallen down
Isaiah9:11 Therefore Yahweh exalts against them adversaries from Rezin And incites their enemies
Isaiah9:12 The Arameans on the east and the Philistines on the west; And they devour Israel with gaping jaws. In spite of all this
Isaiah9:13 Yet the people do not turn back to Him who struck them
Isaiah9:14 So Yahweh cuts off head and tail from Israel
Isaiah9:15 The head is the elder and the highly respected man
Isaiah9:16 For those who guide this people are leading them astray; And those who are guided by them are brought to confusion.
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord is not glad in their choice men
Isaiah9:18 For wickedness burns like a fire; It consumes briars and thorns; It even sets the thickets of the forest aflame
Isaiah9:19 By the fury of Yahweh of hosts the land is burned up
Isaiah9:20 They slice off what is on the right hand but still are hungry
Isaiah9:21 Manasseh devours Ephraim
Isaiah10:1 Woe to those who enact evil statutes And to those who constantly record mischief
Isaiah10:2 So as to turn the poor away from their cause And rob the afflicted of My people of their justice
Isaiah10:3 Now what will you do in the day of visitation
Isaiah10:4 Nothing remains but to crouch among the captives Or fall among those killed. In spite of all this
Isaiah10:5 Woe to Assyria
Isaiah10:6 I send it against a godless nation And command it against the people of My fury To capture spoil and to seize plunder
Isaiah10:7 But it does not intend to act in this way
Isaiah10:8 For it says
Isaiah10:9 Is not Calno like Carchemish
Isaiah10:10 As my hand has reached to the kingdoms of the idols
Isaiah10:11 Shall I not do to Jerusalem and her images Just as I have done to Samaria and her idols?”
Isaiah10:12 So it will be that when the Lord has completed all His work on Mount Zion and on Jerusalem
Isaiah10:13 For he has said
Isaiah10:14 And my hand reached for the wealth of the peoples like a nest
Isaiah10:15 Is the axe to boast itself over the one who chops with it? Is the saw to magnify itself over the one who wields it? That would be like a rod wielding those who lift it
Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord
Isaiah10:17 And the light of Israel will become a fire and his Holy One a flame
Isaiah10:18 And He will bring to an end the glory of his forest and of his fruitful orchard
Isaiah10:19 And the rest of the trees of his forest will be so small in number That a child could write them down.
Isaiah10:20 Now it will be in that day
Isaiah10:21 A remnant will return
Isaiah10:22 For though your people
Isaiah10:23 For a complete destruction
Isaiah10:24 Therefore thus says Lord Yahweh of hosts
Isaiah10:25 For in a very little while My indignation against you will end and My anger will be directed to their destruction.”
Isaiah10:26 And Yahweh of hosts will waken a scourge against him like the slaughter of Midian at the rock of Oreb; and His staff will be over the sea and He will lift it up the way He did in Egypt.
Isaiah10:27 So it will be in that day
Isaiah10:28 He has come against Aiath
Isaiah10:29 They have gone through the pass
Isaiah10:30 Cry aloud with your voice
Isaiah10:31 Madmenah has fled. The inhabitants of Gebim have sought refuge.
Isaiah10:32 Yet today he will stand at Nob; He shakes his fist at the mountain of the daughter of Zion
Isaiah10:33 Behold
Isaiah10:34 He will cut down the thickets of the forest with an iron axe

Isaiah11:2 The Spirit of Yahweh will rest on Him
Isaiah11:3 And He will delight in the fear of Yahweh
Isaiah11:4 But with righteousness He will judge the poor
Isaiah11:5 Also righteousness will be the belt about His loins
Isaiah11:6 And the wolf will dwell with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 Also the cow and the bear will graze
Isaiah11:8 And the nursing baby will play by the hole of the cobra
Isaiah11:9 They will do no evil nor act corruptly in all My holy mountain
Isaiah11:10 Then it will be in that day
Isaiah11:11 Then it will be in that day
Isaiah11:12 And He will lift up a standard for the nations And assemble the banished ones of Israel
Isaiah11:13 Then the jealousy of Ephraim will depart
Isaiah11:14 And they will swoop down on the slopes of the Philistines on the west; Together they will plunder the sons of the east; They will stretch out their hands over Edom and Moab
Isaiah11:15 And Yahweh will devote to destruction The tongue of the Sea of Egypt; And He will wave His hand over the River With His scorching wind; And He will strike it into seven streams And make men walk over dry‑shod.
Isaiah11:16 And there will be a highway from Assyria For the remnant of His people who will remain

Isaiah12:2 Behold

Isaiah13:1 The oracle concerning Babylon which Isaiah the son of Amoz beheld.
Isaiah13:2 Lift up a standard on the bare hill
Isaiah13:3 I have commanded My set apart ones
Isaiah13:4 A sound of tumult on the mountains
Isaiah13:5 They are coming from a far country
Isaiah13:6 Wail
Isaiah13:7 Therefore all hands will fall limp
Isaiah13:8 They will be terrified
Isaiah13:9 Behold
Isaiah13:10 For the stars of heaven and their constellations Will not flash forth their light; The sun will be dark when it rises And the moon will not shed its light.
Isaiah13:11 Thus I will punish the world for its evil And the wicked for their iniquity; I will also put an end to the pride of the arrogant And bring low the lofty pride of the ruthless.
Isaiah13:12 I will make mortal man scarcer than fine gold And mankind than the gold of Ophir.
Isaiah13:13 Therefore I will make the heavens tremble
Isaiah13:14 And it will be that like a hunted gazelle
Isaiah13:15 Anyone who is found will be pierced through
Isaiah13:16 Their infants also will be dashed to pieces Before their eyes; Their houses will be plundered And their wives ravished.
Isaiah13:17 Behold
Isaiah13:18 And their bows will dash the young men to pieces
Isaiah13:19 And it will be that Babylon
Isaiah13:20 It will never be inhabited or dwelt in from generation to generation; Nor will the Arab pitch his tent there
Isaiah13:21 But desert creatures will lie down there
Isaiah13:22 And hyenas will howl in their fortified towers And jackals in their luxurious palaces. Her fateful time also will soon come And her days will not be prolonged.
Isaiah14:1 When Yahweh will have compassion on Jacob and again choose Israel
Isaiah14:2 And the peoples will take them along and bring them to their place
Isaiah14:3 And it will be in the day when Yahweh gives you rest from your pain and turmoil and harsh slavery in which you have been enslaved
Isaiah14:4 that you will take up this taunt against the king of Babylon
Isaiah14:5 Yahweh has broken the staff of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 Which used to strike the peoples in fury with unceasing strokes
Isaiah14:7 The whole earth is at rest and is quiet; They break forth into shouts of joy.
Isaiah14:8 Even the cypress trees are glad over you
Isaiah14:9 Sheol from beneath trembles excitedly over you to meet you when you come; It wakens for you the spirits of the dead
Isaiah14:10 They will all answer and say to you
Isaiah14:11 Your pride and the music of your harps Have been brought down to Sheol; Maggots are spread out as your bed beneath you And worms are your covering.’
Isaiah14:12 How you have fallen from heaven
Isaiah14:13 But you said in your heart
Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the heights of the clouds; I will make myself like the Most High.’
Isaiah14:15 Nevertheless you will be brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:16 Those who see you will gaze at you
Isaiah14:17 Who made the world like a wilderness And pulled down its cities
Isaiah14:18 All the kings of the nations lie in glory
Isaiah14:19 But you have been cast out of your grave Like an abhorred branch
Isaiah14:20 You will not be united with them in burial
Isaiah14:21 Prepare for his sons a place of slaughter Because of the iniquity of their fathers. They must not arise and take possession of the earth And fill the face of the world with cities.”
Isaiah14:22 “I will rise up against them
Isaiah14:23 “I will also make it a possession for the hedgehog and swamps of water
Isaiah14:24 Yahweh of hosts has sworn saying
Isaiah14:25 to break Assyria in My land
Isaiah14:26 This is the counsel that is counseled against the whole earth; and this is the hand that is stretched out against all the nations.
Isaiah14:27 For Yahweh of hosts has counseled

Isaiah14:29 “Do not be glad
Isaiah14:30 And the foremost of the poor will eat
Isaiah14:31 Wail
Isaiah14:32 How then will one answer the messengers of the nation? That Yahweh has founded Zion
Isaiah15:1 The oracle concerning Moab. Surely in a night Ar of Moab is destroyed and ruined; Surely in a night Kir of Moab is destroyed and ruined.
Isaiah15:2 They have gone up to the temple and to Dibon
Isaiah15:3 In their streets they have girded themselves with sackcloth; On their rooftops and in their squares Everyone is wailing
Isaiah15:4 Heshbon and Elealeh also cry out
Isaiah15:5 My heart cries out for Moab; Those who flee from her are as far as Zoar and Eglath‑shelishiyah
Isaiah15:6 For the waters of Nimrim are desolate. Surely the grass is dried up
Isaiah15:7 Therefore the abundance they have made and stored up They carry off over the brook of Arabim.
Isaiah15:8 For the cry has gone around the territory of Moab
Isaiah15:9 For the waters of Dimon are full of blood; Surely I will put added woes upon Dimon

Isaiah16:2 Then
Isaiah16:3 “Bring us counsel
Isaiah16:4 Let the banished of Moab sojourn with you; Be a hiding place to them from the destroyer.” For the extortioner has come to an end
Isaiah16:5 And a throne will be established in lovingkindness
Isaiah16:6 We have heard of the pride of Moab
Isaiah16:7 Therefore Moab will wail; everyone of Moab will wail. You will moan for the raisin cakes of Kir‑hareseth As those who are utterly stricken.
Isaiah16:8 For the fields of Heshbon have languished
Isaiah16:9 Therefore I will weep bitterly for Jazer
Isaiah16:10 Gladness and joy are taken away from the fruitful orchard; In the vineyards also there will be no cries of joy or shouts of jubilation
Isaiah16:11 Therefore my inner being moans like a harp for Moab And my inward feelings for Kir‑hareseth.
Isaiah16:12 So it will be when Moab appears
Isaiah16:13 This is the word which Yahweh spoke earlier concerning Moab.
Isaiah16:14 But now Yahweh speaks

Isaiah17:2 The cities of Aroer are forsaken; They will be for flocks
Isaiah17:3 And the fortified city will cease from Ephraim
Isaiah17:4 Now it will be in that day
Isaiah17:5 And it will be even like the reaper gathering the standing grain
Isaiah17:6 Yet gleanings will remain in it like the shaking of an olive tree
Isaiah17:7 In that day man will have regard for his Maker And his eyes will look to the Holy One of Israel.
Isaiah17:8 He will not have regard for the altars
Isaiah17:9 In that day their strong cities will be like forsaken places in the forest
Isaiah17:10 For you have forgotten the God of your salvation And have not remembered the rock of your strong defense. Therefore you plant delightful plants And set them with vine branches of a strange god.
Isaiah17:11 In the day that you plant it you carefully fence it in
Isaiah17:12 Alas
Isaiah17:13 The nations rumble on like the rumbling of many waters

Isaiah18:2 Which sends envoys by the sea

Isaiah18:4 For thus Yahweh has told me
Isaiah18:5 For before the harvest
