Isaiah19:1 The oracle concerning Egypt. Behold
Isaiah19:2 “So I will incite Egyptians against Egyptians; And they will each fight against his brother and each against his neighbor
Isaiah19:3 Then the spirit of the Egyptians will be emptied to destruction within them; And I will confuse their counsel
Isaiah19:4 Moreover
Isaiah19:5 The waters from the sea will dry up
Isaiah19:6 The rivers will emit a stench
Isaiah19:7 The bulrushes by the Nile
Isaiah19:8 And the fishermen will lament
Isaiah19:9 Moreover
Isaiah19:10 And the pillars of Egypt will be crushed; All the hired laborers will be grieved in soul.
Isaiah19:11 The princes of Zoan are merely ignorant fools; The counsel of Pharaoh’s wisest counselors has become senseless. How can you men say to Pharaoh
Isaiah19:12 Well then
Isaiah19:13 The princes of Zoan have acted as ignorant fools
Isaiah19:14 Yahweh has mixed within her a spirit of distortion; They have led Egypt astray in all that it does
Isaiah19:15 There will be no work for Egypt Which its head or tail
Isaiah19:16 In that day the Egyptians will become like women
Isaiah19:17 And the land of Judah will become a terror to Egypt; everyone to whom it is mentioned will be in dread of it because of the counsel of Yahweh of hosts which He is counseling against them.
Isaiah19:18 In that day five cities in the land of Egypt will be speaking the language of Canaan and swearing allegiance to Yahweh of hosts; one will be called the City of Destruction.
Isaiah19:19 In that day there will be an altar to Yahweh in the midst of the land of Egypt
Isaiah19:20 And it will become a sign and a witness to Yahweh of hosts in the land of Egypt; for they will cry to Yahweh because of oppressors
Isaiah19:21 Thus Yahweh will make Himself known to Egypt
Isaiah19:22 And Yahweh will smite Egypt
Isaiah19:23 In that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria
Isaiah19:24 In that day Israel will be the third party with Egypt and Assyria
Isaiah19:25 whom Yahweh of hosts has blessed

Isaiah20:4 so the king of Assyria will lead away the captives of Egypt and the exiles of Ethiopia
Isaiah20:5 Then they will be dismayed and ashamed because of Ethiopia their hope and Egypt their boast.
Isaiah20:6 So the inhabitants of this coastland will say in that day
Isaiah21:1 The oracle concerning the wilderness of the sea. As whirlwinds in the Negev sweep on
Isaiah21:2 A harsh vision has been declared to me; The treacherous one still deals treacherously
Isaiah21:3 For this reason my loins are full of anguish; Pains have seized me like the pains of a woman in labor. I am so bewildered I cannot hear
Isaiah21:4 My heart reels; horror terrorizes me; The twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.
Isaiah21:5 They set the table
Isaiah21:6 For thus the Lord says to me
Isaiah21:7 Indeed
Isaiah21:8 Then the lookout called
Isaiah21:9 Now behold
Isaiah21:10 O my trampled people and my afflicted of the threshing floor! What I have heard from Yahweh of hosts
Isaiah21:11 The oracle concerning Dumah. One keeps calling to me from Seir
Isaiah21:12 The watchman says
Isaiah21:13 The oracle about Arabia. In the thickets of Arabia you must spend the night
Isaiah21:14 Bring water to meet the thirsty
Isaiah21:15 For they have fled from the swords
Isaiah21:16 For thus the Lord said to me

Isaiah22:1 The oracle concerning the valley of vision. What is the matter with you now
Isaiah22:2 You who were full of noise
Isaiah22:3 All your rulers have fled together
Isaiah22:4 Therefore I say
Isaiah22:5 For Lord Yahweh of hosts has a day of confusion
Isaiah22:6 Now Elam took up the quiver With the chariots
Isaiah22:7 Then your choicest valleys were full of chariots
Isaiah22:8 And He revealed the defense of Judah. In that day you looked to the weapons of the house of the forest
Isaiah22:9 And you saw that the breaches In the wall of the city of David were many; And you collected the waters of the lower pool.
Isaiah22:10 Then you counted the houses of Jerusalem And tore down houses to fortify the wall.
Isaiah22:11 And you made a reservoir between the two walls For the waters of the old pool. But you did not look to Him who made it

Isaiah42:18 Hear
Isaiah42:19 Who is blind but My servant
Isaiah42:20 You have seen many things
Isaiah42:21 Yahweh was pleased for His righteousness’ sake That He make the law great and majestic.
Isaiah42:22 But this is a people plundered and pillaged as spoil; All of them are trapped in caves
Isaiah42:23 Who among you will give ear to this? Who will give heed and hear hereafter?
Isaiah42:24 Who gave Jacob up for spoil
Isaiah42:25 So He poured out on him the heat of His anger And the fierceness of battle; And it set him aflame all around
Isaiah43:1 But now
Isaiah43:2 When you pass through the waters
Isaiah43:3 For I am Yahweh your God
Isaiah43:4 Since you are precious in My sight
Isaiah43:5 Do not fear
Isaiah43:6 I will say to the north
Isaiah43:7 Everyone who is called by My name
Isaiah43:8 Bring out the people who are blind
Isaiah43:9 All the nations have gathered together So that the peoples may be assembled. Who among them can declare this And make us hear the former things? Let them present their witnesses that they may be justified
Isaiah43:10 “You are My witnesses
Isaiah43:11 I
Isaiah43:12 It is I who have declared and saved and caused it to be heard
Isaiah43:13 Even from eternity I am He
Isaiah43:14 Thus says Yahweh your Redeemer
Isaiah43:15 I am Yahweh
Isaiah43:16 Thus says Yahweh
Isaiah43:17 Who brings forth the chariot and the horse
Isaiah43:18 “Do not remember the former things
Isaiah43:19 Behold
Isaiah43:20 The beasts of the field will glorify Me
Isaiah43:21 The people whom I formed for Myself Will recount My praise.
Isaiah43:22 “Yet you have not called on Me
Isaiah43:23 You have not brought to Me the sheep of your burnt offerings
Isaiah43:24 You have not bought Me sweet cane with money
Isaiah43:25 “I
Isaiah43:26 Bring Me to remembrance
Isaiah43:27 Your first father sinned
Isaiah43:28 So I will profane the princes of the sanctuary
Isaiah44:1 “But now hear
Isaiah44:2 Thus says Yahweh who made you And formed you from the womb
Isaiah44:3 For I will pour out water on the thirsty ground And streams on the dry land; I will pour out My Spirit on your seed And My blessing on your offspring;
Isaiah44:4 And they will spring up among the grass Like poplars by streams of water.’
Isaiah44:5 This one will say
Isaiah44:6 “Thus says Yahweh
Isaiah44:7 Who is like Me? Let him call out and declare it; And let him tell it to Me in order
Isaiah44:8 Do not be in dread and do not be afraid; Have I not long since caused it to be heard to you and declared it? And you are My witnesses. Is there any God besides Me
Isaiah44:9 Those who form a graven image are all of them futile
Isaiah44:10 Who has formed a god or cast a graven image to no profit?
Isaiah44:11 Behold
Isaiah44:12 The man crafts iron into a cutting tool and does his work over the coals
Isaiah44:13 Another crafts wood
Isaiah44:14 In order to cut cedars for himself
Isaiah44:15 Then it becomes something for a man to burn
Isaiah44:16 Half of it he burns in the fire; over this half he eats meat as he roasts a roast and is satisfied. He also warms himself and says
Isaiah44:17 But the rest of it he makes into a god
Isaiah44:18 They do not know
Isaiah44:19 No one causes this to return to his heart
Isaiah44:20 He feeds on ashes; a deceived heart has turned him aside. And he cannot deliver his soul
Isaiah44:21 “Remember these things
Isaiah44:22 I have wiped out your transgressions like a thick cloud And your sins like a cloud. Return to Me
Isaiah44:23 Shout for joy
Isaiah44:24 Thus says Yahweh
Isaiah44:25 Causing the omens of boasters to be annulled
Isaiah44:26 Confirming the word of His servant— And the counsel of His messengers He will complete— And being the One who says of Jerusalem
Isaiah44:27 It is I who says to the depth of the sea
Isaiah44:28 It is I who says of Cyrus

Isaiah45:5 I am Yahweh

Isaiah45:15 Truly

Isaiah47:7 Yet you said
Isaiah47:8 “So now
Isaiah47:9 But these two things will come on you suddenly in one day: Loss of children and widowhood. They will come on you in full measure In spite of your many sorceries
Isaiah47:10 You felt secure in your evil and said
Isaiah47:11 But evil will come on you Which you will not know how to charm away; And disaster will fall on you For which you cannot atone; And destruction about which you do not know Will come on you suddenly.
Isaiah47:12 “Stand fast now in your spells And in your many sorceries With which you have labored from your youth; Perhaps you will be able to profit; Perhaps you may cause trembling.

Isaiah53:1 Who has believed our report? And to whom has the arm of Yahweh been revealed?
Isaiah53:2 For He grew up before Him like a tender shoot
Isaiah53:3 He was despised and forsaken of men
Isaiah53:4 Surely our griefs He Himself bore
Isaiah53:5 But He was pierced through for our transgressions
Isaiah53:6 All of us like sheep have gone astray
Isaiah53:7 He was oppressed and He was afflicted
Isaiah53:8 By oppression and judgment He was taken away; And as for His generation
Isaiah53:9 So His grave was assigned with wicked men
Isaiah53:10 But Yahweh was pleased To crush Him
Isaiah53:11 As a result of the anguish of His soul
Isaiah53:12 Therefore
Isaiah54:1 “Shout for joy
Isaiah54:2 “Enlarge the place of your tent; Stretch out the curtains of your dwellings
Isaiah54:3 For you will spread abroad to the right and to the left. And your seed will possess nations And will resettle the desolate cities.
Isaiah54:4 “Do not be afraid
Isaiah54:5 For your husband is your Maker
Isaiah54:6 For Yahweh has called you
Isaiah54:7 “For a brief moment I forsook you
Isaiah54:8 In a flood of fury I hid My face from you for a moment
Isaiah54:9 “For this is like the days of Noah to Me
Isaiah54:10 For the mountains may be removed and the hills may shake
Isaiah54:11 “O afflicted one
Isaiah54:12 Moreover
Isaiah54:13 All your sons will be taught of Yahweh; And the peace of your sons will be great.
Isaiah54:14 In righteousness you will be established; You will be far from oppression
Isaiah54:15 If anyone fiercely attacks you it will not be from Me. Whoever attacks you will fall because of you.
Isaiah54:16 Behold
Isaiah54:17 No weapon that is formed against you will succeed; And every tongue that accuses you in judgment you will condemn. This is the inheritance of the slaves of Yahweh

Isaiah56:9 All you beasts of the field
Isaiah56:10 His watchmen are blind; All of them know nothing. All of them are mute dogs unable to bark
Isaiah56:11 And the dogs have a strong appetite; they do not know satisfaction. And they are shepherds who do not know understanding; They have all turned to their own way

Isaiah57:2 He enters into peace; They rest in their beds
Isaiah57:3 “But draw near
Isaiah57:4 Against whom do you jest? Against whom do you open wide your mouth And stick out your tongue? Are you not children of transgression
Isaiah57:5 Who inflame yourselves among the oaks
Isaiah57:6 Among the smooth stones of the ravine Is your portion
Isaiah57:7 Upon a mountain lofty and lifted up You have made your bed. You also went up there to offer sacrifice.
Isaiah57:8 Behind the door and the doorpost You have set up your memorial; Indeed
Isaiah57:9 You have journeyed to the king with oil And increased your perfumes; You have sent your envoys a great distance And made them go down to Sheol.
Isaiah57:10 You were tired out by the length of your road
Isaiah57:11 “Of whom were you anxious and fearful When you lied and did not remember Me
Isaiah57:12 I will declare your righteousness and your deeds
Isaiah57:13 When you cry out
Isaiah57:14 And it will be said

Isaiah59:2 But your iniquities have made a separation between you and your God
Isaiah59:3 For your hands are defiled with blood And your fingers with iniquity; Your lips have spoken a lie; Your tongue mutters unrighteousness.
Isaiah59:4 No one calls in righteousness
Isaiah59:5 They break open vipers’ eggs and weave the spider’s web; He who eats of their eggs dies
Isaiah59:6 Their webs will not become a garment
Isaiah59:7 Their feet run to evil
Isaiah59:8 They do not know the way of peace
Isaiah59:9 Therefore justice is far from us
Isaiah59:10 We grope along the wall like blind men; We grope like those who have no eyes; We stumble at midday as in the twilight
Isaiah59:11 All of us growl like bears
Isaiah59:12 For our transgressions are multiplied before You
Isaiah59:13 Transgressing and denying Yahweh

Isaiah65:18 But be joyful and rejoice forever in what I create; For behold
Isaiah65:19 I will also rejoice in Jerusalem and be joyful in My people; And there will no longer be heard in her The voice of weeping and the voice of crying.
Isaiah65:20 No longer will there be in it an infant who lives but a few days
Isaiah65:21 They will build houses and inhabit them; They will also plant vineyards and eat their fruit.

Isaiah66:1 Thus says Yahweh
Isaiah66:2 For My hand made all these things
Isaiah66:3 “But he who slaughters an ox is the one who strikes down a man; He who sacrifices a lamb is the one who breaks a dog’s neck; He who offers a grain offering is the one who offers swine’s blood; He who offers a memorial offering of frankincense is the one who blesses wickedness. As they have chosen their own ways
Isaiah66:4 So I will choose their punishments And will bring on them what they dread
Isaiah66:5 Hear the word of Yahweh
Isaiah66:6 A voice of rumbling from the city
Isaiah66:7 “Before she was in labor pains
Isaiah66:8 Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen such things? Can a land be brought forth through labor pains in one day? Can a nation be born all at once? As soon as Zion was in labor pains
Isaiah66:9 Shall I bring to the point of breaking forth and not cause birth?” says Yahweh. “Or shall I who causes birth shut the womb?” says your God.
Isaiah66:10 “Be glad with Jerusalem and rejoice for her
Isaiah66:11 That you may nurse and be satisfied with her comforting breasts
Isaiah66:12 For thus says Yahweh
Isaiah66:13 As one whom his mother comforts
Isaiah66:14 Then you will see this
Isaiah66:15 For behold
Isaiah66:16 For Yahweh will execute judgment by fire And by His sword on all flesh
Isaiah66:17 “Those who sanctify and purify themselves to go to the gardens
Isaiah66:18 “And I know their works and their thoughts; the time is coming to gather all nations and tongues. And they shall come and see My glory.
Isaiah66:19 I will set a sign among them and will send those who have escaped from them to the nations: Tarshish
Isaiah66:20 Then they shall bring all your brothers from all the nations as a grain offering to Yahweh
Isaiah66:21 I will also take some of them for priests and for Levites
Isaiah66:22 “For just as the new heavens and the new earth Which I make will endure before Me
Isaiah66:23 And it shall be from new moon to new moon And from sabbath to sabbath
Isaiah66:24 “Then they will go forth and look On the corpses of the men Who have transgressed against Me. For their worm will not die And their fire will not be quenched; And they will be an object of contempt to all mankind.”
Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 to whom the word of Yahweh came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon
Jeremiah1:3 And it came in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah1:4 Now the word of Yahweh came to me saying
Jeremiah1:5 “Before I formed you in the innermost parts I knew you
Jeremiah1:6 Then I said
Jeremiah1:7 But Yahweh said to me
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of them
Jeremiah1:9 Then Yahweh sent forth His hand and touched my mouth
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 Now the word of Yahweh came to me saying
Jeremiah1:12 Then Yahweh said to me
Jeremiah1:13 The word of Yahweh came to me a second time saying
Jeremiah1:14 Then Yahweh said to me
Jeremiah1:15 For
Jeremiah1:16 I will speak My judgments on them concerning all their evil
Jeremiah1:17 Now
Jeremiah1:18 Now behold
Jeremiah1:19 And they will fight against you
Jeremiah2:1 Now the word of Yahweh came to me saying
Jeremiah2:2 “Go and call out in the ears of Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holy to Yahweh
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of Yahweh
Jeremiah2:5 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah2:6 They did not say
Jeremiah2:7 I brought you into the fruitful land To eat its fruit and its good things. But you came and defiled My land
Jeremiah2:8 The priests did not say
Jeremiah2:9 “Therefore I will yet contend with you
Jeremiah2:10 For cross to the coastlands of Kittim and see
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation changed gods Though they were not gods? But My people have changed their glory For that which does not profit.
Jeremiah2:12 Be appalled
Jeremiah2:13 “For My people have done two evils: They have forsaken Me
Jeremiah2:14 “Is Israel a slave? Or is he a homeborn slave? Why has he become plunder?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions have roared at him; They have given forth their voice. And they have made his land a desolation; His cities have been turned into ruin
Jeremiah2:16 Also the men of Memphis and Tahpanhes Have shaved the top of your head.
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not done this to yourself By your forsaking Yahweh your God When He led you in the way?
Jeremiah2:18 But now what are you doing on the road to Egypt
Jeremiah2:19 Your own evil will chastise you
Jeremiah2:20 “For long ago I broke your yoke And tore off your bonds; But you said
Jeremiah2:21 Yet I planted you a choice vine
Jeremiah2:22 Although you wash yourself with lye And use much soap
Jeremiah2:23 “How can you say
Jeremiah2:24 A wild donkey accustomed to the wilderness
Jeremiah2:25 Keep your feet from being barefoot And your throat from thirst; But you said
Jeremiah2:26 “As the thief is shamed when he is found
Jeremiah2:27 Who say to a tree
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods Which you made for yourself? Let them arise
Jeremiah2:29 “Why do you contend with Me? You have all transgressed against Me
Jeremiah2:30 “In vain I have struck your sons; They received no discipline. Your sword has devoured your prophets Like a destroying lion.
Jeremiah2:31 O generation
Jeremiah2:32 Can a virgin forget her ornaments
Jeremiah2:33 How well you prepare your way To seek love! Therefore even the wicked women You have taught your ways.
Jeremiah2:34 Also on your skirts is found The lifeblood of the innocent needy; You did not find them breaking in. But in spite of all these things
Jeremiah2:35 Yet you said
Jeremiah2:36 Why do you despise Him so much In changing your way? Also
Jeremiah2:37 From this place also you will go out With your hands on your head; For Yahweh has rejected those in whom you trust
Jeremiah3:1 God says

Jeremiah3:4 Have you not just now called to Me
Jeremiah3:5 Will He be angry forever? Will He keep it to the end?’ Behold

Jeremiah4:20 Destruction upon destruction is called out

Jeremiah5:6 Therefore a lion from the forest will strike them down; A wolf of the deserts will devastate them; A leopard is watching their cities. Everyone who goes out of them will be torn in pieces Because their transgressions are many; Their acts of faithlessness are mighty.
Jeremiah5:7 “Why should I pardon you? Your sons have forsaken Me And sworn by those who are not gods. I had satisfied them to the full
Jeremiah5:8 They were well‑fed lusty horses
Jeremiah5:9 Shall I not punish these people

Jeremiah5:11 For the house of Israel and the house of Judah Have dealt very treacherously with Me
Jeremiah5:12 They have dealt falsely about Yahweh And said
Jeremiah5:13 The prophets are as wind
Jeremiah5:14 Therefore
Jeremiah5:15 Behold
Jeremiah5:16 Their quiver is like an open grave; All of them are mighty men.
Jeremiah5:17 They will devour your harvest and your food; They will devour your sons and your daughters; They will devour your flocks and your herds; They will devour your vines and your fig trees; They will demolish with the sword your fortified cities in which you trust.

Jeremiah5:24 They do not say in their heart
Jeremiah5:25 Your iniquities have turned these away

Jeremiah6:1 “Flee for safety
Jeremiah6:2 The comely and delicate one
Jeremiah6:3 Shepherds and their flocks will come to her; They will pitch their tents around her; They will pasture each in his place.
Jeremiah6:4 Set yourselves apart for war against her; Arise
Jeremiah6:5 Arise
Jeremiah6:6 For thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah6:7 Like a well keeps its waters fresh
Jeremiah6:8 Heed discipline
Jeremiah6:9 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah6:10 To whom shall I speak and give warning That they may hear? Behold
Jeremiah6:11 But I am full of the wrath of Yahweh; I am weary of holding it in. “Pour it out on the infants in the street And on the gathering of choice men together; For both husband and wife shall be captured
Jeremiah6:12 Their houses shall be turned over to others
Jeremiah6:13 “For from the least of them even to the greatest of them
Jeremiah6:14 They have healed the brokenness of My people superficially
Jeremiah6:15 Were they ashamed because of the abomination they have done? They were not even ashamed at all; They did not even know how to feel dishonor. Therefore they shall fall among those who fall; At the time that I punish them
Jeremiah6:16 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah6:17 And I set watchmen over you
Jeremiah6:18 Therefore hear
Jeremiah6:19 Hear
Jeremiah6:20 For what purpose does frankincense come to Me from Sheba And the sweet cane from a distant land? Your burnt offerings are not acceptable
Jeremiah6:21 Therefore
Jeremiah6:22 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah6:23 They take hold of bow and spear; They are cruel and have no compassion; Their voice roars like the sea
Jeremiah6:24 We have heard the report of it; Our hands are limp. Distress has taken hold of us
Jeremiah6:25 Do not go out into the field And do not walk on the road
Jeremiah6:26 O daughter of my people
Jeremiah6:27 “I have made you a tester of metals and an assayer among My people
Jeremiah6:28 All of them are rebelliously stubborn
Jeremiah6:29 The bellows blow fiercely; The lead is consumed by the fire; In vain the refining goes on
Jeremiah6:30 They call them rejected silver Because Yahweh has rejected them.
Jeremiah7:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from Yahweh
Jeremiah7:2 “Stand in the gate of the house of Yahweh
Jeremiah7:3 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah7:4 Do not trust in lying words
Jeremiah7:5 For if you truly make your ways and your deeds good
Jeremiah7:6 if you do not oppress the sojourner
Jeremiah7:7 then I will let you dwell in this place
Jeremiah7:8 “Behold
Jeremiah7:9 Will you steal

Jeremiah7:11 Has this house

Jeremiah7:13 And now
Jeremiah7:14 therefore
Jeremiah7:15 I will cast you out of My presence
Jeremiah7:16 “As for you
Jeremiah7:17 Are you not seeing what they are doing in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?
Jeremiah7:18 The children gather wood
Jeremiah7:19 Do they provoke Me?” declares Yahweh. “Is it not themselves they provoke to the shame of their own face?”
Jeremiah7:20 Therefore thus says Lord Yahweh
Jeremiah7:21 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah7:22 For I did not speak to your fathers or command them in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt concerning burnt offerings and sacrifices.
Jeremiah7:23 But this is what I commanded them
Jeremiah7:24 Yet they did not listen or incline their ear
Jeremiah7:25 Since the day that your fathers came out of the land of Egypt until this day
Jeremiah7:26 Yet they did not listen to Me or incline their ear
Jeremiah7:27 “You shall speak all these words to them
Jeremiah7:28 You shall say to them
Jeremiah7:29 Cut off your hair and cast it away
Jeremiah7:30 For the sons of Judah have done that which is evil in My sight
Jeremiah7:31 They have built the high places of Topheth
Jeremiah7:32 “Therefore
Jeremiah7:33 The dead bodies of this people will be food for the birds of the sky and for the beasts of the earth; and no one will frighten them away.
Jeremiah7:34 Then I will make to cease from the cities of Judah and from the streets of Jerusalem the voice of joy and the voice of gladness

Jeremiah8:2 And they will spread them out to the sun
Jeremiah8:3 And death will be chosen rather than life by all the remnant that remains of this evil family

Jeremiah8:5 Why then has this people
Jeremiah8:6 I have given heed and heard
Jeremiah8:7 Even the stork in the sky Knows her seasons; And the turtledove and the swift and the crane Keep the time of their migration; But My people do not know The legal judgment of Yahweh.

Jeremiah8:9 The wise men are put to shame; They are dismayed and captured; Behold

Jeremiah8:12 Were they ashamed because of the abomination they had done? They certainly were not ashamed
Jeremiah8:13 “I will surely gather them up
Jeremiah8:14 Why are we sitting still? Gather yourselves
Jeremiah8:15 We waited for peace
Jeremiah8:16 From Dan is heard the snorting of his horses; At the sound of the neighing of his valiant steeds The whole land quakes; And they come and devour the land as well as its fullness
Jeremiah8:17 “For behold
Jeremiah8:18 My sorrow is beyond healing; My heart is faint within me!
Jeremiah8:19 Behold

Jeremiah8:21 For the brokenness of the daughter of my people I am broken; I mourn
Jeremiah8:22 Is there no balm in Gilead? Is there no physician there? Why then has not the health of the daughter of my people gone up?

Jeremiah9:2 Oh that I had in the desert A travelers’ lodging place

Jeremiah9:4 “Let everyone beware of his neighbor
Jeremiah9:5 Everyone deceives his neighbor And does not speak the truth; They have taught their tongue to speak lies; They weary themselves committing iniquity.
Jeremiah9:6 Your habitation is in the midst of deceit; Through deceit they refuse to know Me
Jeremiah9:7 Therefore thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah9:8 Their tongue is a slaughtering arrow; It speaks deceit; With his mouth one speaks peace to his neighbor
Jeremiah9:9 Shall I not punish them for these things?” declares Yahweh. “On a nation such as this Shall I not avenge Myself?

Jeremiah10:1 Hear the word which Yahweh speaks to you
Jeremiah10:2 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah10:3 For the statutes of the peoples are vanity Because it is wood cut from the forest
Jeremiah10:4 They make it beautiful with silver and with gold; They strengthen it with nails and with hammers So that it will not totter.
Jeremiah10:5 Like a scarecrow in a cucumber field are they
Jeremiah10:6 There is none like You
Jeremiah10:7 Who would not fear You
Jeremiah10:8 But they are altogether senseless and foolish; They are in a discipline of vanities—it is mere wood!
Jeremiah10:9 Beaten silver is brought from Tarshish
Jeremiah10:10 But Yahweh is the true God; He is the living God and the everlasting King. At His wrath the earth quakes
Jeremiah10:11 Thus you shall say to them
Jeremiah10:12 It is He who made the earth by His power
Jeremiah10:13 When He gives forth His voice
Jeremiah10:14 Every man is senseless
Jeremiah10:15 They are vanity
Jeremiah10:16 The portion of Jacob is not like these; For the Maker of all is He

Jeremiah10:18 For thus says Yahweh

Jeremiah10:20 My tent is destroyed

Jeremiah10:23 I know

Jeremiah12:6 For even your brothers and the household of your father

Jeremiah12:8 My inheritance has become to Me Like a lion in the forest; She has given forth its voice against Me; Therefore I have come to hate her.
Jeremiah12:9 Is My inheritance like a speckled bird of prey to Me? Are the birds of prey against her on every side? Go
Jeremiah12:10 Many shepherds have ruined My vineyard; They have trampled down My portion; They have made My desired portion A desolate wilderness.

Jeremiah12:12 On all the bare heights in the wilderness Destroyers have come

Jeremiah14:1 That which came as the word of Yahweh to Jeremiah in regard to the drought:
Jeremiah14:2 “Judah mourns
Jeremiah14:3 Their mighty ones have sent their underlings for water; They have come to the trenches and found no water. They have returned with their vessels empty; They have been put to shame and dishonored
Jeremiah14:4 Because the ground is dismayed
Jeremiah14:5 For even the doe in the field has given birth only to forsake her young Because there is no grass.
Jeremiah14:6 The wild donkeys stand on the bare heights; They pant for air like jackals; Their eyes fail For there is no vegetation.

Jeremiah14:9 Why are You like a man confused

Jeremiah14:20 We know our wickedness
Jeremiah14:21 Do not despise us

Jeremiah15:2 And it will be that when they say to you
Jeremiah15:3 I will appoint over them four kinds of doom
Jeremiah15:4 I will give them over to be an example of terror among all the kingdoms of the earth because of Manasseh
Jeremiah15:5 “Indeed
Jeremiah15:6 You who have abandoned Me
Jeremiah15:7 And I will winnow them with a winnowing fork At the gates of the land; I will bereave them of children; I will make My people perish; They did not repent of their ways.
Jeremiah15:8 Their widows will be more numerous before Me Than the sand of the seas; I will bring against them
Jeremiah15:9 She who bore seven sons languishes; Her breathing is labored. Her sun has set while it was yet day; She has been shamed and humiliated. So I will give over their survivors to the sword Before their enemies
Jeremiah15:10 Woe to me
Jeremiah15:11 Yahweh said

Jeremiah15:15 You who know
Jeremiah15:16 Your words were found
Jeremiah15:17 I did not sit in the circle of merrymakers

Jeremiah15:19 Therefore thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah15:20 Then I will make you to this people A fortified wall of bronze; And they will fight against you

Jeremiah16:1 The word of Yahweh also came to me saying
Jeremiah16:2 “You shall not take a wife for yourself nor have sons or daughters in this place.”
Jeremiah16:3 For thus says Yahweh concerning the sons and daughters born in this place and concerning their mothers who bear them and their fathers who beget them in this land:
Jeremiah16:4 “They will die of deadly diseases; they will not be lamented or buried; they will be as dung on the surface of the ground and come to an end by sword and famine
Jeremiah16:5 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah16:6 Both great men and small will die in this land; they will not be buried; they will not be lamented
Jeremiah16:7 Men will not break bread in mourning for them
Jeremiah16:8 Moreover you shall not go into a house of feasting to sit with them to eat and drink.”
Jeremiah16:9 For thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah16:10 “Now when you tell this people all these words
Jeremiah16:11 Then you are to say to them
Jeremiah16:12 You too have done evil
Jeremiah16:13 So I will hurl you out of this land into the land which you have not known
Jeremiah16:14 “Therefore behold
Jeremiah16:15 but
Jeremiah16:16 “Behold
Jeremiah16:17 For My eyes are on all their ways; they are not hidden from My face
Jeremiah16:18 I will first doubly repay their iniquity and their sin because they have profaned My land; they have filled My inheritance with the carcasses of their detestable idols and with their abominations.”
Jeremiah16:19 O Yahweh
Jeremiah16:20 Can man make gods for himself? Yet they are not gods!
Jeremiah16:21 “Therefore behold
Jeremiah17:1 The sin of Judah is written down with an iron stylus; With a diamond point it is engraved upon the tablet of their heart And on the horns of their altars
Jeremiah17:2 As they remember their children
Jeremiah17:3 O mountain of Mine in the countryside
Jeremiah17:4 And you will
Jeremiah17:5 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah17:6 And he will be like a juniper in the desert And will not see when prosperity comes
Jeremiah17:7 Blessed is the man who trusts in Yahweh And whose trust is Yahweh.
Jeremiah17:8 And he will be like a tree planted by the water
Jeremiah17:9 “The heart is more deceitful than all else And is desperately sick; Who can know it?
Jeremiah17:10 I
Jeremiah17:11 As a partridge that hatches eggs which it has not laid
Jeremiah17:12 A glorious throne on high from the beginning Is the place of our sanctuary.
Jeremiah17:13 O Yahweh
Jeremiah17:14 Heal me
Jeremiah17:15 Behold
Jeremiah17:16 But as for me
Jeremiah17:17 Do not be a terror to me; You are my refuge in the day of calamity.
Jeremiah17:18 Let those who pursue me be put to shame
Jeremiah17:19 Thus Yahweh said to me
Jeremiah17:20 and say to them
Jeremiah17:21 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah17:22 You shall not bring a load out of your houses on the sabbath day nor do any work
Jeremiah17:23 Yet they did not listen or incline their ears
Jeremiah17:24 “But it will be
Jeremiah17:25 then there will come in through the gates of this city kings and princes sitting on the throne of David
Jeremiah17:26 And they will come in from the cities of Judah and from all around Jerusalem
Jeremiah17:27 But if you do not listen to Me to keep the sabbath day holy by not carrying a load and coming in through the gates of Jerusalem on the sabbath day
Jeremiah18:1 The word which came to Jeremiah from Yahweh saying
Jeremiah18:2 “Arise and go down to the potter’s house
Jeremiah18:3 Then I went down to the potter’s house
Jeremiah18:4 But the vessel that he was making of clay was ruined in the hand of the potter
Jeremiah18:5 Then the word of Yahweh came to me saying
Jeremiah18:6 “Can I not
Jeremiah18:7 At one moment I might speak concerning a nation or concerning a kingdom to uproot
Jeremiah18:8 but if that nation against which I have spoken turns from its evil
Jeremiah18:9 Or at another moment I might speak concerning a nation or concerning a kingdom to build up or to plant it;
Jeremiah18:10 but if it does evil in My sight by not listening to My voice
Jeremiah18:11 So now then
Jeremiah18:12 But they will say
Jeremiah18:13 “Therefore thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah18:14 Does the snow of Lebanon forsake the rock of the open country? Or is the cold flowing water from a strange land ever uprooted?
Jeremiah18:15 For My people have forgotten Me; They burn incense to worthless gods
Jeremiah18:16 To make their land an object of horror
Jeremiah18:17 Like an east wind I will scatter them Before the enemy; I will show them My back and not My face In the day of their disaster.’”
Jeremiah18:18 Then they said
Jeremiah18:19 Do give heed to me
Jeremiah18:20 Should good be repaid with evil? For they have dug a pit for me. Remember how I stood before You To speak good on their behalf
Jeremiah18:21 Therefore
Jeremiah18:22 May an outcry be heard from their houses
Jeremiah18:23 Yet You
Jeremiah19:1 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah19:2 Then go out to the valley of Ben-hinnom
Jeremiah19:3 and say
Jeremiah19:4 Because they have forsaken Me and have made this a foreign place and have burned incense in it to other gods
Jeremiah19:5 and have built the high places of Baal to burn their sons in the fire as burnt offerings to Baal
Jeremiah19:6 therefore
Jeremiah19:7 I will empty out the counsel of Judah and Jerusalem to the point of destruction in this place
Jeremiah19:8 I will also make this city an object of horror and of hissing; everyone who passes by it will be horrified and hiss because of all its slaughtering.
Jeremiah19:9 I will make them eat the flesh of their sons and the flesh of their daughters
Jeremiah19:10 “Then you are to break the jar in the sight of the men who accompany you
Jeremiah19:11 and you shall say to them
Jeremiah19:12 This is what I will do to this place and its inhabitants
Jeremiah19:13 And the houses of Jerusalem and the houses of the kings of Judah will be defiled like the place Topheth because of all the houses on whose rooftops they burned incense to all the heavenly host and poured out drink offerings to other gods.”’”
Jeremiah19:14 Then Jeremiah came from Topheth
Jeremiah19:15 “Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah20:1 Then Pashhur the priest
Jeremiah20:2 and Pashhur had Jeremiah the prophet struck and put him in the stocks that were at the upper Benjamin Gate
Jeremiah20:3 Now it happened that on the next day
Jeremiah20:4 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah20:5 I will also give over all the wealth of this city
Jeremiah20:6 And you
Jeremiah20:7 O Yahweh
Jeremiah20:8 For each time I speak
Jeremiah20:9 But if I say
Jeremiah20:10 For I have heard the bad report of many
Jeremiah20:11 But Yahweh is with me like a ruthless mighty one; Therefore my persecutors will stumble and not prevail. They will be utterly ashamed
Jeremiah20:12 Yet
Jeremiah20:13 Sing to Yahweh
Jeremiah20:14 Cursed be the day when I was born; Let the day not be blessed when my mother bore me!
Jeremiah20:15 Cursed be the man who brought the good news To my father
Jeremiah20:16 But let that man be like the cities Which Yahweh overthrew without relenting
Jeremiah20:17 Because he did not put me to death from the womb
Jeremiah20:18 Why did I ever come forth from the womb To look on trouble and sorrow

Jeremiah21:2 “Please inquire of Yahweh on our behalf
Jeremiah21:3 Then Jeremiah said to them
Jeremiah21:4 ‘Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah21:5 I Myself will war against you with an outstretched hand and a strong arm
Jeremiah21:6 I will also strike down the inhabitants of this city
Jeremiah21:7 Then afterwards
Jeremiah21:8 “You shall also say to this people
Jeremiah21:9 He who inhabits this city will die by the sword and by famine and by pestilence; but he who goes out and falls away to the Chaldeans who are besieging you will live
Jeremiah21:10 For I have set My face against this city for evil and not for good
Jeremiah21:11 “Then say to the household of the king of Judah
Jeremiah21:12 O house of David

Jeremiah22:2 and say
Jeremiah22:3 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah22:4 For if you men will indeed do this thing
Jeremiah22:5 But if you will not obey these words
Jeremiah22:6 For thus says Yahweh concerning the house of the king of Judah: “You are like Gilead to Me
Jeremiah22:7 For I will set apart destroyers against you
Jeremiah22:8 “Many nations will pass by this city; and they will say to one another
Jeremiah22:9 Then they will say
Jeremiah22:10 Do not weep for the dead or console him
Jeremiah22:11 For thus says Yahweh in regard to Shallum the son of Josiah
Jeremiah22:12 but in the place where they took him away into exile
Jeremiah22:13 “Woe to him who builds his house without righteousness And his upper rooms without justice
Jeremiah22:14 Who says
Jeremiah22:15 Do you become a king because you are competing in cedar? Did not your father eat and drink And do justice and righteousness? Then it was well with him.
Jeremiah22:16 He pled the cause of the afflicted and needy; Then it was well. Is not that what it means to know Me?” Declares Yahweh.
Jeremiah22:17 “But your eyes and your heart Are set on nothing except your own greedy gain And on shedding innocent blood And on doing oppression and extortion.”
Jeremiah22:18 Therefore thus says Yahweh in regard to Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah22:19 He will be buried with a donkey’s burial
Jeremiah22:20 Go up to Lebanon and cry out
Jeremiah22:21 I spoke to you in your prosperity
Jeremiah22:22 The wind will sweep away all your shepherds
Jeremiah22:23 You who inhabit Lebanon
Jeremiah22:24 “As I live
Jeremiah22:25 and I will give you over into the hand of those who are seeking your life
Jeremiah22:26 I will hurl you and your mother who bore you into another land—there you were not born
Jeremiah22:27 But as for the land to which their soul desires to return
Jeremiah22:28 Is this man Coniah a despised
Jeremiah22:29 O land
Jeremiah22:30 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah23:1 “Woe to the shepherds who are destroying and scattering the sheep of My pasture!” declares Yahweh.
Jeremiah23:2 Therefore thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah23:3 “Then I Myself will gather the remnant of My flock out of all the land where I have banished them and cause them to return to their pasture
Jeremiah23:4 I will also raise up shepherds over them
Jeremiah23:5 “Behold
Jeremiah23:6 In His days Judah will be saved
Jeremiah23:7 “Therefore behold
Jeremiah23:8 but
Jeremiah23:9 As for the prophets: My heart is broken within me; All my bones tremble; I have become like a drunken man
Jeremiah23:10 For the land is full of adulterers; For the land mourns because of the curse. The pastures of the wilderness have dried up. Their course also is evil
Jeremiah23:11 “For both prophet and priest are polluted; Even in My house I have found their evil
Jeremiah23:12 “Therefore their way will be like slippery paths to them; They will be driven away into the thick darkness and fall down in it; For I will bring their evil upon them
Jeremiah23:13 “Moreover
Jeremiah23:14 Also among the prophets of Jerusalem I have seen an appalling thing: The committing of adultery and walking in lying; And they strengthen the hands of evildoers
Jeremiah23:15 Therefore thus says Yahweh of hosts concerning the prophets
Jeremiah23:16 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah23:17 They keep saying to those who spurn Me
Jeremiah23:18 But who has stood in the council of Yahweh
Jeremiah23:19 Behold
Jeremiah23:20 The anger of Yahweh will not turn back Until He has done and established the purposes of His heart; In the last days you will clearly understand it.
Jeremiah23:21 I did not send these prophets
Jeremiah23:22 But if they had stood in My council
Jeremiah23:23 “Am I a God who is near
Jeremiah23:24 Can a man hide himself in hiding places So I do not see him?” declares Yahweh. “Do I not fill the heavens and the earth?” declares Yahweh.
Jeremiah23:25 “I have heard what the prophets have said who prophesy a lie in My name
Jeremiah23:26 How long? Is there anything in the hearts of the prophets who prophesy a lie
Jeremiah23:27 who intend to make My people forget My name by their dreams which they recount to one another
Jeremiah23:28 The prophet who has a dream may recount his dream
Jeremiah23:29 “Is not My word like fire?” declares Yahweh
Jeremiah23:30 Therefore behold
Jeremiah23:31 Behold
Jeremiah23:32 Behold
Jeremiah23:33 “Now when this people or the prophet or a priest asks you
Jeremiah23:34 Then as for the prophet or the priest or the people who say
Jeremiah23:35 Thus will each of you say to his neighbor and to his brother
Jeremiah23:36 For you will no longer remember the oracle of Yahweh because every man’s own word will become the oracle
Jeremiah23:37 Thus you will say to that prophet
Jeremiah23:38 For if you all say
Jeremiah23:39 Therefore behold
Jeremiah23:40 I will put an everlasting reproach on you and an everlasting humiliation which will not be forgotten.”
Jeremiah24:1 After Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had taken away into exile Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim
Jeremiah24:2 One basket had very good figs
Jeremiah24:3 Then Yahweh said to me
Jeremiah24:4 Then the word of Yahweh came to me
Jeremiah24:5 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah24:6 For I will set My eyes on them for good
Jeremiah24:7 I will give them a heart to know Me
Jeremiah24:8 ‘But like the rotten figs which cannot be eaten due to rottenness—indeed
Jeremiah24:9 I will give them over to be a terror and an evil for all the kingdoms of the earth
Jeremiah24:10 I will send the sword
Jeremiah25:1 The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah25:2 which Jeremiah the prophet spoke to all the people of Judah and to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem
Jeremiah25:3 “From the thirteenth year of Josiah the son of Amon
Jeremiah25:4 And Yahweh has sent to you all His slaves the prophets
Jeremiah25:5 saying
Jeremiah25:6 and do not walk after other gods to serve them and to worship them
Jeremiah25:7 Yet you have not listened to Me
Jeremiah25:8 “Therefore thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah25:9 behold
Jeremiah25:10 Moreover
Jeremiah25:11 This whole land will be a waste place and an object of horror
Jeremiah25:12 ‘Then it will be when seventy years are fulfilled
Jeremiah25:13 I will bring upon that land all My words which I have spoken against it
Jeremiah25:14 —For many nations and great kings will make slaves of them
Jeremiah25:15 For thus Yahweh
Jeremiah25:16 They will drink and stagger and go mad because of the sword that I will send among them.”
Jeremiah25:17 Then I took the cup from the hand of Yahweh and made all the nations to whom Yahweh sent me drink it:
Jeremiah25:18 Jerusalem and the cities of Judah and its kings and its princes
Jeremiah25:19 Pharaoh king of Egypt
Jeremiah25:20 and all the foreign people
Jeremiah25:21 Edom
Jeremiah25:22 and all the kings of Tyre
Jeremiah25:23 and Dedan
Jeremiah25:24 and all the kings of Arabia and all the kings of the foreign people who dwell in the desert
Jeremiah25:25 and all the kings of Zimri
Jeremiah25:26 and all the kings of the north
Jeremiah25:27 “You shall say to them
Jeremiah25:28 And it will be
Jeremiah25:29 For behold
Jeremiah25:30 “Therefore you shall prophesy against them all these words
Jeremiah25:31 A rumbling has come to the end of the earth Because Yahweh has a contention against the nations. He is entering into judgment with all flesh; As for the wicked
Jeremiah25:32 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah25:33 “Those slain by Yahweh on that day will be from one end of the earth to the other. They will not be lamented
Jeremiah25:34 Wail
Jeremiah25:35 Refuge will perish from the shepherds And escape from the mighty ones of the flock.
Jeremiah25:36 Hear the sound of the cry of the shepherds
Jeremiah25:37 And the peaceful pastures are made silent Because of the burning anger of Yahweh.
Jeremiah25:38 He has forsaken His hiding place like the lion; For their land has become an object of horror Because of the burning desire of the oppressor And because of His burning anger.”

Jeremiah26:11 Then the priests and the prophets spoke to the officials and to all the people

Jeremiah26:13 So now
Jeremiah26:14 But as for me
Jeremiah26:15 Only know for certain that if you put me to death

Jeremiah26:20 Indeed
Jeremiah26:21 And King Jehoiakim and all his mighty men and all the officials heard his words. So the king sought to put him to death; but Uriah heard it

Jeremiah27:4 And you shall command them to go to their masters
Jeremiah27:5 “I have made the earth
Jeremiah27:6 So now

Jeremiah29:5 ‘Build houses and live in them; and plant gardens and eat their fruit.

Jeremiah29:11 For I know the plans that I have for you
Jeremiah29:12 Then you will call upon Me and come and pray to Me
Jeremiah29:13 You will seek Me and find Me when you search for Me with all your heart.

Jeremiah30:2 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah30:3 For behold
Jeremiah30:4 Now these are the words which Yahweh spoke concerning Israel and concerning Judah:
Jeremiah30:5 “For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah30:6 Ask now
Jeremiah30:7 Alas! For that day is great
Jeremiah30:8 ‘And it will be in that day
Jeremiah30:9 But they shall be a slave to Yahweh their God and David their king
Jeremiah30:10 Fear not
Jeremiah30:11 For I am with you
Jeremiah30:12 “For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah30:13 There is no one to plead your cause; No healing for your sore
Jeremiah30:14 All your lovers have forgotten you; They do not seek you; For I have wounded you with the wound of an enemy
Jeremiah30:15 Why do you cry out over your injury? Your pain is incurable. Because your iniquity is numerous And your sins are mighty
Jeremiah30:16 Therefore all who devour you will be devoured; And all your adversaries
Jeremiah30:17 For I will restore you to health
Jeremiah30:18 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah30:19 From them will come forth thanksgiving And the voice of those who celebrate; And I will multiply them
Jeremiah30:20 Their children also will be as formerly
Jeremiah30:21 And their mighty one shall be one of them
Jeremiah30:22 ‘You shall be My people
Jeremiah30:23 Behold
Jeremiah30:24 The burning anger of Yahweh will not turn back Until He has done and until He has established The intent of His heart; In the last days you will understand this.

Jeremiah31:2 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah31:3 Yahweh appeared to him from afar
Jeremiah31:4 Again I will build you
Jeremiah31:5 Again you will plant vineyards On the hills of Samaria; The planters will plant And will enjoy them.
Jeremiah31:6 For there will be a day when watchmen On the hills of Ephraim call out
Jeremiah31:7 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah31:8 Behold
Jeremiah31:9 With weeping they will come
Jeremiah31:10 Hear the word of Yahweh
Jeremiah31:11 For Yahweh has ransomed Jacob And redeemed him from the hand of him who was stronger than he.
Jeremiah31:12 “They will come and sing for joy on the height of Zion
Jeremiah31:13 Then the virgin will be glad in the dance
Jeremiah31:14 I will fill the soul of the priests with richness
Jeremiah31:15 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah31:16 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah31:17 And there is hope for your future
Jeremiah31:18 I have surely heard Ephraim grieving
Jeremiah31:19 For after I turned away
Jeremiah31:20 Is Ephraim My dear son? Is he a delightful child? Indeed
Jeremiah31:21 “Set up roadmarks for yourself
Jeremiah31:22 How long will you go here and there
Jeremiah31:23 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah31:24 And Judah and all its cities will inhabit it together
Jeremiah31:25 For I satisfy the weary soul and fill up every soul who wastes away.”
Jeremiah31:26 At this I awoke and looked
Jeremiah31:27 “Behold
Jeremiah31:28 And it will be that as I have watched over them to uproot
Jeremiah31:29 “In those days they will not say again
Jeremiah31:30 But everyone will die for his own iniquity; each man who eats the sour grapes

Jeremiah31:32 not like the covenant which I cut with their fathers in the day I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah31:33 “But this is the covenant which I will cut with the house of Israel after those days
Jeremiah31:34 And they will not teach again
Jeremiah31:35 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah31:36 “If these statutes are removed From before Me

Jeremiah31:40 And the whole valley of the dead bodies and of the ashes
Jeremiah32:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from Yahweh in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah32:2 Now at that time the military force of the king of Babylon was besieging Jerusalem
Jeremiah32:3 because Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up
Jeremiah32:4 and Zedekiah king of Judah will not escape out of the hand of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah32:5 and he will lead Zedekiah to Babylon
Jeremiah32:6 And Jeremiah said
Jeremiah32:7 ‘Behold
Jeremiah32:8 Then Hanamel my uncle’s son came to me in the court of the guard according to the word of Yahweh and said to me
Jeremiah32:9 “I bought the field which was at Anathoth from Hanamel my uncle’s son
Jeremiah32:10 And I signed and sealed the deed and called in witnesses and weighed out the silver on the scales.
Jeremiah32:11 Then I took the deeds of purchase
Jeremiah32:12 and I gave the deed of purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:13 And I commanded Baruch in their sight
Jeremiah32:14 ‘Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah32:15 For thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah32:16 “After I had given the deed of purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:17 ‘Ah Lord Yahweh! Behold
Jeremiah32:18 who shows lovingkindness to thousands
Jeremiah32:19 great in counsel and abundant in deeds
Jeremiah32:20 who has set signs and wonders in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah32:21 You brought Your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs and with wonders
Jeremiah32:22 and gave them this land
Jeremiah32:23 They came in and took possession of it
Jeremiah32:24 Behold
Jeremiah32:25 And You have said to me
Jeremiah32:26 Then the word of Yahweh came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah32:27 “Behold
Jeremiah32:28 Therefore thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah32:29 And the Chaldeans who are fighting against this city will enter and set this city on fire and burn it
Jeremiah32:30 Indeed the sons of Israel and the sons of Judah have been doing only evil in My sight from their youth; for the sons of Israel have been only provoking Me to anger by the work of their hands
Jeremiah32:31 “Indeed this city has been to Me a provocation of My anger and My wrath from the day that they built it
Jeremiah32:32 because of all the evil of the sons of Israel and the sons of Judah which they have done to provoke Me to anger—they
Jeremiah32:33 And they have turned their back to Me and not their face; though I taught them
Jeremiah32:34 But they put their detestable things in the house which is called by My name
Jeremiah32:35 And they built the high places of Baal that are in the valley of Ben-hinnom to cause their sons and their daughters to pass through the fire to Molech
Jeremiah32:36 “So now
Jeremiah32:37 Behold
Jeremiah32:38 And they shall be My people
Jeremiah32:39 and I will give them one heart and one way
Jeremiah32:40 And I will cut an everlasting covenant with them that I will not turn away from them
Jeremiah32:41 And I will rejoice over them to do them good and will truly plant them in this land with all My heart and with all My soul.
Jeremiah32:42 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah32:43 Thus fields will be bought in this land of which you all are saying
Jeremiah32:44 Men will buy fields for money
Jeremiah33:1 Then the word of Yahweh came to Jeremiah the second time
Jeremiah33:2 “Thus says Yahweh who made the earth
Jeremiah33:3 ‘Call to Me and I will answer you
Jeremiah33:4 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah33:5 ‘While they are coming to fight with the Chaldeans and to fill them with the corpses of men whom I have struck down in My anger and in My wrath
Jeremiah33:6 Behold
Jeremiah33:7 I will return the fortunes of Judah and the fortunes of Israel and will rebuild them as they were at first.
Jeremiah33:8 And I will cleanse them from all their iniquity by which they have sinned against Me
Jeremiah33:9 And it will be to Me a name of joy
Jeremiah33:10 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah33:11 the voice of joy and the voice of gladness
Jeremiah33:12 “Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah33:13 In the cities of the hill country
Jeremiah33:14 ‘Behold
Jeremiah33:15 In those days and at that time I will cause a righteous Branch of David to branch forth; and He shall do justice and righteousness on the earth.
Jeremiah33:16 In those days Judah will be saved
Jeremiah33:17 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah33:18 and the Levitical priests shall not have a man cut off from before Me who is to offer burnt offerings
Jeremiah33:19 Then the word of Yahweh came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:20 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah33:21 then My covenant may also be broken with David My servant so that he will not have a son to reign on his throne
Jeremiah33:22 As the host of heaven cannot be counted and the sand of the sea cannot be measured
Jeremiah33:23 And the word of Yahweh came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:24 “Have you not seen what this people have spoken
Jeremiah33:25 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah33:26 then I would reject the seed of Jacob and David My servant
Jeremiah34:1 The word which came to Jeremiah from Yahweh
Jeremiah34:2 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah34:3 And you will not escape from his hand
Jeremiah34:4 Yet hear the word of Yahweh
Jeremiah34:5 You will die in peace; and as spices were burned for your fathers
Jeremiah34:6 Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah in Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:7 when the military force of the king of Babylon was fighting against Jerusalem and against all the cities of Judah that were left
Jeremiah34:8 The word which came to Jeremiah from Yahweh after King Zedekiah had cut a covenant with all the people who were in Jerusalem to proclaim release to them:
Jeremiah34:9 that each man should let his male slave go free and each man his female slave
Jeremiah34:10 And all the officials and all the people obeyed who had entered into the covenant that each man should let his male slave go free and each man his female slave
Jeremiah34:11 But afterwards they turned around and returned to themselves the male slaves and the female slaves
Jeremiah34:12 Then the word of Yahweh came to Jeremiah from Yahweh
Jeremiah34:13 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah34:14 “At the end of seven years each of you shall let his Hebrew brother go who has been sold to you and has served you six years
Jeremiah34:15 Although recently you had turned and done what is right in My sight
Jeremiah34:16 Yet you turned and profaned My name
Jeremiah34:17 “Therefore thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah34:18 And I will give the men who have trespassed against My covenant
Jeremiah34:19 the officials of Judah and the officials of Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:20 And I will give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life. And their dead bodies will be food for the birds of the sky and the beasts of the earth.
Jeremiah34:21 And as for Zedekiah king of Judah and his officials
Jeremiah34:22 Behold

Jeremiah36:1 Now it happened that in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:2 “Take a scroll of a book and write on it all the words which I have spoken to you concerning Israel and concerning Judah and concerning all the nations
Jeremiah36:3 Perhaps the house of Judah will hear all the evil which I devise to bring on them
Jeremiah36:4 Then Jeremiah called Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah36:5 And Jeremiah commanded Baruch
Jeremiah36:6 So you shall go and read from the scroll
Jeremiah36:7 Perhaps their supplication will come before Yahweh
Jeremiah36:8 So Baruch the son of Neriah did according to all that Jeremiah the prophet commanded him
Jeremiah36:9 Now it happened in the fifth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:10 Then Baruch read from the book the words of Jeremiah in the house of Yahweh in the chamber of Gemariah the son of Shaphan the scribe
Jeremiah36:11 Then Micaiah the son of Gemariah
Jeremiah36:12 And he went down to the king’s house
Jeremiah36:13 And Micaiah declared to them all the words that he had heard when Baruch read from the book in the hearing of the people.
Jeremiah36:14 Then all the officials sent Jehudi the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah36:15 And they said to him
Jeremiah36:16 Now it happened that when they heard all the words
Jeremiah36:17 And they asked Baruch
Jeremiah36:18 Then Baruch said to them
Jeremiah36:19 Then the officials said to Baruch
Jeremiah36:20 So they went to the king in the court
Jeremiah36:21 Then the king sent Jehudi to get the scroll
Jeremiah36:22 Now the king was sitting in the winter house in the ninth month
Jeremiah36:23 And it happened that when Jehudi had read three or four columns
Jeremiah36:24 Yet the king and all his servants who heard all these words were not in dread
Jeremiah36:25 Even though Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah interceded with the king not to burn the scroll
Jeremiah36:26 And the king commanded Jerahmeel the king’s son
Jeremiah36:27 Then the word of Yahweh came to Jeremiah after the king had burned the scroll and the words which Baruch had written at the dictation of Jeremiah
Jeremiah36:28 “Take again another scroll and write on it all the former words that were on the first scroll which Jehoiakim the king of Judah burned.
Jeremiah36:29 And concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah you shall say
Jeremiah36:30 Therefore thus says Yahweh concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah
Jeremiah36:31 And I will also punish him and his seed and his servants for their iniquity
Jeremiah36:32 Then Jeremiah took another scroll and gave it to Baruch the son of Neriah

Jeremiah37:4 Now Jeremiah was still coming in and going out among the people; they had not yet put him in the prison.

Jeremiah37:12 that Jeremiah went out from Jerusalem to go to the land of Benjamin in order to obtain his portion of some property there among the people.
Jeremiah37:13 Now it happened that while he was at the Gate of Benjamin
Jeremiah37:14 But Jeremiah said

Jeremiah37:16 For Jeremiah had come into the pit

Jeremiah37:18 Moreover
Jeremiah37:19 Where then are your prophets who prophesied to you
Jeremiah37:20 But now

Jeremiah38:2 “Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah38:3 Thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah38:4 Then the officials said to the king

Jeremiah38:7 But Ebed-melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah38:8 and Ebed-melech went out from the king’s house and spoke to the king
Jeremiah38:9 “My lord the king
Jeremiah38:10 Then the king commanded Ebed-melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah38:11 So Ebed-melech took the men in his hand and went into the king’s palace to a place beneath the storeroom and took from there worn-out clothes and worn-out rags and let them down by ropes into the cistern to Jeremiah.
Jeremiah38:12 Then Ebed-melech the Ethiopian said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:13 So they pulled Jeremiah up with the ropes and brought him up from the cistern
Jeremiah38:14 Then King Zedekiah sent and had Jeremiah the prophet brought to him at the third entrance that is in the house of Yahweh; and the king said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:15 Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah
Jeremiah38:16 But King Zedekiah swore to Jeremiah in secret saying
Jeremiah38:17 Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah

Jeremiah38:19 Then King Zedekiah said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:20 But Jeremiah said
Jeremiah38:21 But if you keep refusing to go out
Jeremiah38:22 ‘And behold
Jeremiah38:23 And they will also bring out all your wives and your sons to the Chaldeans
Jeremiah38:24 Then Zedekiah said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:25 But if the officials hear that I have talked with you and come to you and say to you
Jeremiah38:26 then you are to say to them
Jeremiah38:27 Then all the officials came to Jeremiah and questioned him. So he told them in accordance with all these words which the king had commanded; and they ceased speaking with him since the conversation had not been overheard.
Jeremiah38:28 So Jeremiah stayed in the court of the guard until the day that Jerusalem was captured.
Jeremiah39:1 Now when Jerusalem was captured in the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah39:2 in the eleventh year of Zedekiah
Jeremiah39:3 Then all the officials of the king of Babylon came in and sat down at the Middle Gate: Nergal-sar-ezer
Jeremiah39:4 Now it happened that when Zedekiah the king of Judah and all the men of war saw them
Jeremiah39:5 But the military force of the Chaldeans pursued them and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and they took him and brought him up to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah39:6 Then the king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes at Riblah; the king of Babylon also slaughtered all the nobles of Judah.
Jeremiah39:7 He then blinded Zedekiah’s eyes and bound him in fetters of bronze to bring him to Babylon.
Jeremiah39:8 The Chaldeans also burned with fire the house of the king and the houses of the people
Jeremiah39:9 And as for the rest of the people who remained in the city
Jeremiah39:10 But some of the poorest people who had nothing
Jeremiah39:11 Now Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon gave a command about Jeremiah through Nebuzaradan the captain of the bodyguard
Jeremiah39:12 “Take him and set your eyes to look after him and do nothing harmful to him
Jeremiah39:13 So Nebuzaradan the captain of the bodyguard sent word
Jeremiah39:14 they even sent and took Jeremiah out of the court of the guard and gave him over to Gedaliah
Jeremiah39:15 Now the word of Yahweh had come to Jeremiah while he was confined in the court of the guard
Jeremiah39:16 “Go and speak to Ebed-melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah39:17 But I will deliver you on that day
Jeremiah39:18 For I will certainly provide you escape

Jeremiah41:2 Then Ishmael the son of Nethaniah and the ten men who were with him arose and struck down Gedaliah the son of Ahikam
Jeremiah41:3 Ishmael also struck down all the Jews who were with him
Jeremiah41:4 Now it happened on the next day after putting Gedaliah to death
Jeremiah41:5 that eighty men came from Shechem
Jeremiah41:6 Then Ishmael the son of Nethaniah went out from Mizpah to meet them
Jeremiah41:7 But it happened that as soon as they came inside the city
Jeremiah41:8 But ten men who were found among them said to Ishmael
Jeremiah41:9 Now as for the cistern where Ishmael had cast all the corpses of the men whom he had struck down because of Gedaliah
Jeremiah41:10 Then Ishmael took captive all the remnant of the people who were in Mizpah
Jeremiah41:11 But Johanan the son of Kareah and all the commanders of the military forces that were with him heard of all the evil that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah had done.
Jeremiah41:12 So they took all the men and went to fight with Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:13 Now it happened as soon as all the people who were with Ishmael saw Johanan the son of Kareah and all the commanders of the military forces that were with him
Jeremiah41:14 So all the people whom Ishmael had taken captive from Mizpah turned around and returned and went to Johanan the son of Kareah.
Jeremiah41:15 But Ishmael the son of Nethaniah escaped from Johanan with eight men and went to the sons of Ammon.
Jeremiah41:16 Then Johanan the son of Kareah and all the commanders of the military forces that were with him took from Mizpah all the remnant of the people whom he had returned from Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:17 And they went and stayed in Geruth Chimham
Jeremiah41:18 because of the Chaldeans; for they were afraid of them

Jeremiah44:1 The word that came to Jeremiah for all the Jews living in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:2 “Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah44:3 because of their evil which they did so as to provoke Me to anger by continuing to burn incense and to serve other gods whom they had not known
Jeremiah44:4 Yet I sent you all My slaves the prophets
Jeremiah44:5 But they did not listen or incline their ears to turn from their evil
Jeremiah44:6 Therefore My wrath and My anger were poured out and burned in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:7 So now
Jeremiah44:8 provoking Me to anger with the works of your hands
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten the evil of your fathers
Jeremiah44:10 But they have not become contrite even to this day
Jeremiah44:11 “Therefore thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah44:12 And I will take away the remnant of Judah who have set their face on entering the land of Egypt to sojourn there
Jeremiah44:13 And I will punish those who live in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:14 So there will be no one who escapes or any survivors for the remnant of Judah who have entered the land of Egypt to sojourn there and then to return to the land of Judah

Jeremiah44:16 “As for the message that you have spoken to us in the name of Yahweh
Jeremiah44:17 But rather we will certainly carry out every word that has proceeded from our mouths
Jeremiah44:18 But since we stopped burning incense to the queen of heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her
Jeremiah44:19 “And
Jeremiah44:20 Then Jeremiah said to all the people
Jeremiah44:21 “As for the smoking sacrifices that you offered in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:22 So Yahweh was no longer able to bear it
Jeremiah44:23 Because you have burned incense and have sinned against Yahweh and not listened to the voice of Yahweh or walked in His law
Jeremiah44:24 Then Jeremiah said to all the people
Jeremiah44:25 thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah44:26 Nevertheless
Jeremiah44:27 Behold

Jeremiah44:29 This will be the sign to you
Jeremiah44:30 Thus says Yahweh

Jeremiah46:2 To Egypt

Jeremiah46:4 Harness the horses
Jeremiah46:5 Why have I seen it? They are shattered; They are drawing back
Jeremiah46:6 Let not the swift man flee
Jeremiah46:7 Who is this that rises like the Nile
Jeremiah46:8 Egypt rises like the Nile
Jeremiah46:9 Go up
Jeremiah46:10 Indeed
Jeremiah46:11 Go up to Gilead and obtain balm
Jeremiah46:12 The nations have heard of your disgrace

Jeremiah46:14 “Declare in Egypt and cause it to be heard in Migdol; Cause it to be heard also in Memphis and Tahpanhes; Say
Jeremiah46:15 Why are your mighty ones thrown down? They do not stand because Yahweh has thrust them down.
Jeremiah46:16 They have repeatedly stumbled; Indeed
Jeremiah46:17 They called out there
Jeremiah46:18 As I live
Jeremiah46:19 Make your baggage ready for exile
Jeremiah46:20 Egypt is a pretty heifer
Jeremiah46:21 Also her mercenaries in her midst Are like fattened calves
Jeremiah46:22 Its sound moves along like a serpent; For they move on like a military force And come to her as woodcutters with axes.
Jeremiah46:23 They have cut down her forest
Jeremiah46:24 The daughter of Egypt has been put to shame
Jeremiah46:25 Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah46:26 I shall give them into the hand of those who are seeking their lives
Jeremiah46:27 “But as for you
Jeremiah46:28 O Jacob My servant
