Jeremiah47:6 Ah
Jeremiah47:7 How can it be quiet? Yahweh has given it a command; Against Ashkelon and against the seacoast— There He has assigned it.”
Jeremiah48:1 Concerning Moab. Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah48:2 There is praise for Moab no longer; In Heshbon they have devised calamity against her: ‘Come and let us cut her off from being a nation!’ You too
Jeremiah48:3 The sound of an outcry from Horonaim
Jeremiah48:4 Moab is broken; Her little ones have made their cry of distress heard.
Jeremiah48:5 For by the ascent of Luhith They will ascend with continual weeping; For at the descent of Horonaim They have heard the distressed cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 Flee
Jeremiah48:7 For because of your trust in your own works and treasures
Jeremiah48:8 A destroyer will come to every city
Jeremiah48:9 Give wings to Moab
Jeremiah48:10 Cursed be the one who does the work of Yahweh with a slack hand
Jeremiah48:11 “Moab has been at ease since his youth; He has also had quiet
Jeremiah48:12 Therefore behold
Jeremiah48:13 And Moab will be ashamed of Chemosh
Jeremiah48:14 How can you say
Jeremiah48:15 Moab has been destroyed
Jeremiah48:16 “The disaster of Moab will soon come
Jeremiah48:17 Mourn for him
Jeremiah48:18 Come down from your glory And inhabit the parched ground
Jeremiah48:19 Stand by the road and keep watch
Jeremiah48:20 Moab has been put to shame
Jeremiah48:21 “Judgment has also come upon the plain
Jeremiah48:22 and against Dibon
Jeremiah48:23 and against Kiriathaim
Jeremiah48:24 and against Kerioth
Jeremiah48:25 The horn of Moab has been cut in pieces and his arm broken
Jeremiah48:26 “Make him drunk
Jeremiah48:27 Now was not Israel a laughingstock to you? Or was he caught among thieves? For each time you speak about him you shake your head in scorn.
Jeremiah48:28 Leave the cities and dwell among the crags
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of the pride of Moab—he is very proud— Of his haughtiness
Jeremiah48:30 I know his fury
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I will wail for Moab
Jeremiah48:32 More than the weeping for Jazer I will weep for you
Jeremiah48:33 So gladness and joy are gathered up From the fruitful orchard
Jeremiah48:34 From the outcry at Heshbon even to Elealeh
Jeremiah48:35 I will make Moab cease
Jeremiah48:36 “Therefore My heart moans for Moab like flutes; My heart also moans like flutes for the men of Kir-heres. Therefore they have lost the abundance it produced.
Jeremiah48:37 For every head is bald and every beard cut short; there are gashes on all the hands and sackcloth on the loins.
Jeremiah48:38 On all the rooftops of Moab and in its open squares there is lamentation everywhere; for I have broken Moab like an undesirable vessel
Jeremiah48:39 “How shattered it is! How they have wailed! How Moab has turned his back—he is ashamed! So Moab will become a laughingstock and an object of terror to all around him.”
Jeremiah48:40 For thus says Yahweh: “Behold
Jeremiah48:41 Kerioth has been captured
Jeremiah48:42 Moab will be destroyed from being a people Because he has magnified himself against Yahweh.
Jeremiah48:43 Panic and pit and pitfall are coming upon you
Jeremiah48:44 “The one who flees from the panic Will fall into the pit
Jeremiah48:45 “In the shadow of Heshbon Those who flee stand without strength; For a fire has gone forth from Heshbon And a flame from the midst of Sihon
Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
Jeremiah48:47 Yet I will return the fortunes of Moab In the last days
Jeremiah49:1 Concerning the sons of Ammon. Thus says Yahweh: “Does Israel have no sons? Or has he no one who can take possession? Why then has Malcam taken possession of Gad And his people settled in its cities?
Jeremiah49:2 Therefore behold
Jeremiah49:3 “Wail
Jeremiah49:4 How boastful you are about the valleys! Your valley is flowing away
Jeremiah49:5 Behold
Jeremiah49:6 But afterward I will return The fortunes of the sons of Ammon
Jeremiah49:7 Concerning Edom. Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah49:8 Flee away
Jeremiah49:9 If grape gatherers came to you
Jeremiah49:10 But I have stripped Esau bare; I have uncovered his hiding places
Jeremiah49:11 Leave your orphans behind
Jeremiah49:12 For thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah49:13 For I have sworn by Myself
Jeremiah49:14 I have heard a message from Yahweh
Jeremiah49:15 “For behold
Jeremiah49:16 As for the terror of you
Jeremiah49:17 “Edom will become an object of horror; everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss at all its wounds.
Jeremiah49:18 Like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah with its neighbors
Jeremiah49:19 Behold
Jeremiah49:20 Therefore hear the counsel of Yahweh which He has counseled against Edom
Jeremiah49:21 The earth has quaked at the noise of their downfall. There is an outcry! The noise of it has been heard at the Red Sea.
Jeremiah49:22 Behold
Jeremiah49:23 Concerning Damascus. “Hamath and Arpad are put to shame
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become limp in their hands; She has turned away to flee
Jeremiah49:25 How the city of praise has not been forsaken
Jeremiah49:26 Therefore
Jeremiah49:27 “I will set fire to the wall of Damascus
Jeremiah49:28 Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor
Jeremiah49:29 They will take away their tents and their flocks; They will carry off for themselves Their tent curtains
Jeremiah49:30 Run away
Jeremiah49:31 Arise
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels will become plunder
Jeremiah49:33 “Hazor will become a haunt of jackals
Jeremiah49:34 That which came as the word of Yahweh to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam
Jeremiah49:35 “Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah49:36 I will bring upon Elam the four winds From the four ends of heaven
Jeremiah49:37 So I will shatter Elam before their enemies And before those who seek their lives; And I will bring calamity upon them
Jeremiah49:38 Then I will set My throne in Elam And cause king and princes from there to perish
Jeremiah49:39 ‘But it will be in the last days That I will return the fortunes of Elam
Jeremiah50:1 The word which Yahweh spoke concerning Babylon
Jeremiah50:2 “Declare and make it heard among the nations. Make it heard and lift up a standard. Do not conceal it but say
Jeremiah50:3 For a nation has come up against her out of the north; it will make her land an object of horror
Jeremiah50:4 “In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask for the way to Zion—turning their faces in its direction and saying
Jeremiah50:6 “My people have become lost sheep; Their shepherds have led them astray. They have made them turn away on the mountains; They have gone along from mountain to hill And have forgotten their resting place.
Jeremiah50:7 All who came out against them have devoured them; And their adversaries have said
Jeremiah50:8 “Wander away from the midst of Babylon And go forth from the land of the Chaldeans; Be also like male goats at the head of the flock.
Jeremiah50:9 For behold
Jeremiah50:10 Chaldea will become spoil; All who take her spoil will have enough
Jeremiah50:11 “Because you are glad
Jeremiah50:12 Your mother will be greatly ashamed; She who gave you birth will be humiliated. Behold
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of Yahweh she will not be inhabited
Jeremiah50:14 Arrange your battle lines against Babylon on every side
Jeremiah50:15 Raise a loud shout against her on every side! She has given herself up
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon And the one who seizes the sickle at the time of harvest; From before the sword of the oppressor They will each turn back to his own people
Jeremiah50:17 “Israel is a scattered flock; the lions have banished them away. The first one who devoured him was the king of Assyria
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah50:19 And I will return Israel back to his pasture
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:21 “Against the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 The noise of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth Has been cut in pieces and broken! How Babylon has become An object of horror among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I set a snare for you
Jeremiah50:25 Yahweh has opened His armory And has brought forth the weapons of His indignation
Jeremiah50:26 Come to her from the farthest border; Open up her barns; Pile her up like heaps
Jeremiah50:27 Put all her young bulls to the sword; Let them go down to the slaughter! Woe be upon them
Jeremiah50:28 There is a sound of those who flee and escape from the land of Babylon
Jeremiah50:29 “Summon many against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore her young men will fall in her open squares
Jeremiah50:31 “Behold
Jeremiah50:32 The arrogant one will stumble and fall With no one to raise him up; And I will set fire to his cities
Jeremiah50:33 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong
Jeremiah50:35 A sword against the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword against the oracle priests
Jeremiah50:37 A sword against their horses and against their chariots And against all the foreigners who are in the midst of her
Jeremiah50:38 A drought on her waters
Jeremiah50:39 “Therefore the desert creatures will live there along with the jackals; The ostriches also will live in it
Jeremiah50:40 As when God overthrew Sodom And Gomorrah with its neighbors
Jeremiah50:41 “Behold
Jeremiah50:42 They seize their bow and javelin; They are cruel and have no compassion. Their voice roars like the sea; And they ride on horses
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard the report about them
Jeremiah50:44 “Behold
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear the counsel of Yahweh which He has counseled against Babylon
Jeremiah50:46 At the sound
Jeremiah51:1 Thus says Yahweh: “Behold
Jeremiah51:2 I will send strangers to Babylon that they may winnow her And may empty her land to destruction; For on every side they will be against her In the day of her calamity.
Jeremiah51:3 Let not him who bends his bow bend it
Jeremiah51:4 They will fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For neither Israel nor Judah has been widowed By his God
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon has been a golden cup in the hand of Yahweh
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been broken; Wail over her! Take balm for her pain; Perhaps she may be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We applied healing to Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 Yahweh has brought about our righteousness; Come and let us recount in Zion The work of Yahweh our God!
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows
Jeremiah51:12 Lift up a standard against the walls of Babylon; Make strong the watch; Raise up watchmen; Establish men in ambush! For Yahweh has both purposed and performed What He spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 O you who dwell by abundant waters
Jeremiah51:14 Yahweh of hosts has sworn by Himself: “Surely I will fill you with a population like locusts
Jeremiah51:15 It is He who made the earth by His power
Jeremiah51:16 When He gives forth His voice
Jeremiah51:17 All mankind is senseless
Jeremiah51:18 They are vanity
Jeremiah51:19 The portion of Jacob is not like these; For the Maker of all is He
Jeremiah51:20 He says
Jeremiah51:21 With you I shatter the horse and his rider
Jeremiah51:22 And with you I shatter man and woman
Jeremiah51:23 And with you I shatter the shepherd and his flock
Jeremiah51:24 “But I will repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea for all their evil that they have done in Zion before your eyes
Jeremiah51:25 “Behold
Jeremiah51:26 They will not take from you even a stone for a corner Nor a stone for foundations
Jeremiah51:27 Lift up a standard in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Set apart the nations against her
Jeremiah51:29 So the land quakes and writhes
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty men of Babylon have ceased fighting; They inhabit the strongholds; Their might is dried up; They are becoming like women; Their dwelling places are set on fire; The bars of her gates are broken.
Jeremiah51:31 One runner runs to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 The fords also have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 “Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has devoured me and brought me into confusion; He has set me down like an empty vessel; He has swallowed me like a sea monster; He has filled his stomach with my delicacies; He has rinsed me away.
Jeremiah51:35 May the violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says Yahweh
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon will become a heap of ruins
Jeremiah51:38 They will roar together like young lions; They will growl like lions’ cubs.
Jeremiah51:39 When they become heated up
Jeremiah51:40 “I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 “How Sheshak has been captured
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up over Babylon; She has been covered with its tumultuous waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become an object of horror
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 “Come forth from her midst
Jeremiah51:46 Now lest your heart grow faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:48 Then heaven and earth and all that is in them Will shout for joy over Babylon
Jeremiah51:49 Indeed Babylon is to fall for the slain of Israel
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are ashamed because we have heard reproach; Dishonor has covered our faces
Jeremiah51:52 “Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should ascend to the heavens
Jeremiah51:54 The sound of an outcry from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 For Yahweh is going to destroy Babylon
Jeremiah51:56 For the destroyer is coming against her
Jeremiah51:57 “I will make her princes and her wise men drunk
Jeremiah51:58 Thus says Yahweh of hosts
Jeremiah51:59 The message which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 So Jeremiah wrote in a single scroll all the calamity which would come upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 Then Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 and then you will say
Jeremiah51:63 And as soon as you finish reading this scroll
Jeremiah51:64 and then you will say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
Jeremiah52:2 And he did what was evil in the sight of Yahweh
Jeremiah52:3 For through the anger of Yahweh this came about in Jerusalem and Judah until He cast them out from His presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
Jeremiah52:4 Now it happened in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city came under siege until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was breached
Jeremiah52:8 But the military force of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho
Jeremiah52:9 Then they seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 Then the king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 Then he blinded the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him with bronze fetters and brought him to Babylon and put him in prison until the day of his death.
Jeremiah52:12 Now on the tenth day of the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 And he burned the house of Yahweh
Jeremiah52:14 So all the military force of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard tore down all the walls around Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took away into exile some of the poorest of the people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard had some of the poorest of the land remain to be vinedressers and plowmen.
Jeremiah52:17 Now the bronze pillars which belonged to the house of Yahweh and the stands and the bronze sea
Jeremiah52:18 They also took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 And the captain of the guard also took away the cups
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 As for the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 Now a capital of bronze was on it; and the height of each capital was five cubits
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six exposed pomegranates; all the pomegranates numbered one hundred on the network all around.
Jeremiah52:24 Then the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah the second priest
Jeremiah52:25 And from the city he took one official who was overseer of the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and led them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 Then the king of Babylon struck them down and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah went into exile from its land.
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people whom Nebuchadnezzar took away into exile: in the seventh year 3
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar 832 persons from Jerusalem;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:31 Now it happened in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 and he spoke to him good words
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin changed his prison clothes and had his meals in the king’s presence continually all the days of his life.
Jeremiah52:34 For his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city That was great with people! She has become like a widow Who was once great among the nations! She who was a princess among the provinces Has become a forced laborer!
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile because of affliction And because of great slavery; She sits among the nations
Lamentations1:4 The roads of Zion are in mourning Because no one comes to the appointed times. All her gates are desolate; Her priests are sighing
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become her masters; Her enemies are complacent; For Yahweh has caused her grief Because of the greatness of her transgressions; Her infants have gone away As captives before the adversary.
Lamentations1:6 So all her majesty Has gone out from the daughter of Zion; Her princes have become like deer That have found no pasture; So they have fled without strength Before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 In the days of her affliction and homelessness Jerusalem remembers all her precious things That were from the days of old
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem sinned greatly; Therefore she has become an impure thing. All who honored her despise her Because they have seen her nakedness; Even she herself sighs and turns away.
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness was in her skirts; She did not remember her future. Therefore she has gone down astonishingly; She has no comforter. “See
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has stretched out his hand Over all her desirable things
Lamentations1:11 All her people are sighing
Lamentations1:12 “Is it nothing to all you who pass this way? Look and see if there is any pain like my pain Which was dealt severely to me
Lamentations1:13 From on high He sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions is bound; By His hand they are knit together. They have come upon my neck; He has made my strength stumble. The Lord has given me into the hands Of those against whom I am not able to stand.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has rejected all my mighty men In my midst; He has called an appointed time against me To break my young men; The Lord has trodden as in a wine press The virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I am weeping; My eyes run down with water; Because far from me is a comforter
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands; There is no one to comfort her; Yahweh has commanded concerning Jacob That the ones round about him should be his adversaries; Jerusalem has become an impure thing among them.
Lamentations1:18 “Yahweh is righteous; For I have rebelled against His command; Hear now
Lamentations1:19 I called to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 See
Lamentations1:21 They have heard that I am sighing; There is no one to comfort me; All my enemies have heard of the evil done to me; They rejoice that You have done it. You have brought the day which You have proclaimed
Lamentations1:22 Let all their evil come before You; And deal severely with them as You have dealt severely with me For all my transgressions; For my groans are great and my heart is faint.”
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has covered the daughter of Zion With a cloud in His anger! He has cast from heaven to earth The beauty of Israel
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up; He has not spared All the habitations of Jacob. In His wrath He has pulled down The strongholds of the daughter of Judah; He has brought them down to the ground; He has profaned the kingdom and its princes.
Lamentations2:3 In hot anger He has cut in pieces All the strength of Israel; He has turned back His right hand From before the enemy. And He has burned in Jacob like a flaming fire Devouring round about.
Lamentations2:4 He has bent His bow like an enemy; He has set His right hand like an adversary And killed all that were desirable to the eye; In the tent of the daughter of Zion He has poured out His wrath like fire.
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy. He has swallowed up Israel; He has swallowed up all its palaces; He has brought its strongholds to ruin And multiplied in the daughter of Judah Mourning and moaning.
Lamentations2:6 And He has violently treated His tabernacle like a garden booth; He has brought His appointed meeting place to ruin. Yahweh has caused to be forgotten The appointed time and sabbath in Zion
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected His altar; He has abandoned His sanctuary; He has delivered into the hand of the enemy The walls of her palaces. They have made a noise in the house of Yahweh As in the day of an appointed time.
Lamentations2:8 Yahweh determined to bring to ruin The wall of the daughter of Zion. He has stretched out a line; He has not turned His hand back from swallowing up
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; He has destroyed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are among the nations; The law is no more. Also
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion Sit on the ground; they are silent. They have thrown dust up on their heads; They have girded themselves with sackcloth. The virgins of Jerusalem Have bowed their heads down to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes fail because of tears; My inmost being is greatly disturbed; My heart is poured out on the earth Because of the destruction of the daughter of my people
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What shall I testify about you? To what shall I equate you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have beheld for you Worthless and ineffective visions; And they have not uncovered your iniquity So as to return you from captivity
Lamentations2:15 All who pass along the way Clap their hands in derision at you; They hiss and shake their heads At the daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies Have opened their mouths wide against you; They hiss and gnash their teeth. They say
Lamentations2:17 Yahweh has done what He purposed; He has completed His word Which He commanded from days of old. He has pulled down without sparing
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried out to the Lord
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 See
Lamentations2:21 On the ground in the streets Lie young and old; My virgins and my young men Have fallen by the sword. You have killed them in the day of Your anger; You have slaughtered
Lamentations2:22 You called as in the day of an appointed time My terrors on every side; And there was no one who escaped or survived In the day of Yahweh’s anger. Those whom I gave birth to and reared
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction Because of the rod of His wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has driven me and made me walk In darkness and not in light.
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me He has turned His hand Repeatedly all the day.
Lamentations3:4 He has caused my flesh and my skin to waste away; He has broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged and encompassed me with gall and hardship.
Lamentations3:6 Dark places He has made me inhabit
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in so that I cannot go out; He has made my chain heavy.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry out and call for help
Lamentations3:9 He has blocked my ways with cut stone; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is to me like a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He has turned aside my ways and torn me to pieces; He has made me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He bent His bow And set me as a target for the arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He made the arrows of His quiver To enter into my inward parts.
Lamentations3:14 I have become a laughingstock to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He has saturated me with bitterness; He has sated me with wormwood.
Lamentations3:16 He has broken my teeth with gravel; He has made me cower in the dust.
Lamentations3:17 My soul has been rejected from peace; I have forgotten goodness.
Lamentations3:18 So I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my homelessness
Lamentations3:20 Surely my soul remembers And is bowed down within me.
Lamentations3:21 This I will return to my heart; Therefore I will wait in hope.
Lamentations3:22 The lovingkindnesses of Yahweh indeed never cease
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; Great is Your faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 “Yahweh is my portion
Lamentations3:25 Yahweh is good to those who hope in Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that he waits silently For the salvation of Yahweh.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he should bear The yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and be silent Since He has laid it on him.
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust; Perhaps there is hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him give his cheek to the one who strikes him; Let him be saturated with reproach.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not reject forever
Lamentations3:32 For if He causes grief
Lamentations3:33 For He does not afflict from His heart Or grieve the sons of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush under His feet All the prisoners of the land
Lamentations3:35 To deprive a man of justice In the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 To defraud a man in his legal case— These things the Lord does not see with approval.
Lamentations3:37 Who is there who speaks and it happens
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High That both calamities and good go forth?
Lamentations3:39 Why should any living person or any man Complain because of his sins?
Lamentations3:40 Let us search out and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 We lift up our heart to our hands Toward God in heaven;
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled; You have not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself with anger And pursued us; You have killed and have not spared.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud So that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us mere scum and refuse In the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 Panic and pitfall have befallen us
Lamentations3:48 My eyes run down with streams of water Because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes pour down unceasingly
Lamentations3:50 Until Yahweh looks down And sees from heaven.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes deal severely with my soul Because of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies without cause Hunted me down like a bird;
Lamentations3:53 They have silenced my life in the pit And have placed a stone on me.
Lamentations3:54 Waters flowed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called on Your name
Lamentations3:56 You have heard my voice
Lamentations3:57 You drew near when I called on You; You said
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 O Yahweh
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 The lips of those who rise against me and their whispering Are against me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 Look on their sitting and their rising; I am their mocking song.
Lamentations3:64 You will recompense them
Lamentations3:65 You will give them dullness of heart; Your curse will be on them.
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue them in anger and destroy them From under the heavens of Yahweh!
Lamentations4:1 How dark the gold has become
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals offer the breast; They nurse their young; But the daughter of my people has become cruel Like ostriches in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the nursing baby cleaves To the roof of its mouth because of thirst; The infants ask for bread
Lamentations4:5 Those who ate delicacies Are desolate in the streets; Those reared in crimson Embrace ash pits.
Lamentations4:6 So the iniquity of the daughter of my people Is greater than the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her Nazirites were purer than snow; They were whiter than milk; They were more ruddy in body than corals
Lamentations4:8 Their form is blacker than soot; They are not recognized in the streets; Their skin is shriveled on their bones; It is withered
Lamentations4:9 Better are those pierced through with the sword Than those pierced through with hunger; For their life flows away
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women Boiled their own children; They became food for them Because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 Yahweh has spent His wrath; He has poured out His burning anger; And He has kindled a fire in Zion Which has devoured its foundations.
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe
Lamentations4:13 Because of the sins of her prophets And the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered
Lamentations4:15 “Depart! Unclean!” they cried of themselves. “Depart
Lamentations4:16 The presence of Yahweh has eradicated them; He will not continue to look at them; They did not honor the priests; They did not favor the elders.
Lamentations4:17 Yet our eyes were spent
Lamentations4:18 They hunted our steps So that we could not walk in our open squares; Our end drew near
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter Than the eagles of the sky; They hotly pursued us on the mountains; They waited in ambush for us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity has come to an end
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans without a father; Our mothers are like widows.
Lamentations5:4 We drink our water by means of silver; Our wood comes to us at a price.
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are at our necks; We are worn out; there is no rest for us.
Lamentations5:6 We have given over our hands to Egypt and Assyria to get enough bread.
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned; they are no more; It is we who have borne their iniquities.
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; There is no one to tear us away from their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the risk of our lives Because of the sword in the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin has become as hot as an oven Because of the burning heat of famine.
Lamentations5:11 They violated the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes were hung by their hands; Elders were not respected.
Lamentations5:13 Young men lifted up the stone at the grinding mill
Lamentations5:14 Elders have ceased from being at the gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased; Our dancing has been turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head; Woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this our heart is faint; Because of these things our eyes are dim;
Lamentations5:18 Because of Mount Zion which lies desolate
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why do You forget us forever? Why do You forsake us so long?
Lamentations5:21 Cause us to return to You
Lamentations5:22 Even if You have utterly rejected us And are exceedingly angry with us.
Ezekiel1:1 Now it happened in the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 (On the fifth of the month in the fifth year of King Jehoiachin’s exile
Ezekiel1:3 the word of Yahweh came expressly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 Then I looked
Ezekiel1:5 And within it there were figures with the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: they had the likeness of a man.
Ezekiel1:6 And each of them had four faces and four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 And their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 And under their wings on their four sides were hands of a man. As for the faces and wings of the four of them
Ezekiel1:9 their wings touched one another; their faces did not turn as they went; each went straight forward.
Ezekiel1:10 And as for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 And such were their faces. Their wings were spread out above; each had two touching another being
Ezekiel1:12 And each went straight forward; wherever the spirit was about to go
Ezekiel1:13 As for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures ran to and fro with the appearance of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 Then I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their workmanship was like the gleam of beryl
Ezekiel1:17 Whenever they went
Ezekiel1:18 And as for their rims
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went

Ezekiel1:24 I also heard the sound of their wings like the sound of many waters as they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there came a sound from above the expanse that was over their heads; whenever they stood still
Ezekiel1:26 Now above the expanse that was over their heads there was something in the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 Then I saw from the appearance of His loins and upward something like the gleam of glowing metal with the appearance of fire all around within it
Ezekiel1:28 As the appearance of the rainbow in the clouds on a rainy day

Ezekiel16:17 You also took your splendid jewelry made of My gold and of My silver

Ezekiel16:19 Also My bread which I gave you
Ezekiel16:20 “Moreover

Ezekiel16:23 “Now it happened after all your evil—‘Woe
Ezekiel16:24 that you built yourself a shrine and made yourself a high place in every square.
Ezekiel16:25 You built yourself a high place at the head of every street and made your beauty abominable
Ezekiel16:26 You also played the harlot with the Egyptians
Ezekiel16:27 Behold now
Ezekiel16:28 Moreover
Ezekiel16:29 You also multiplied your harlotry with the land of merchants
Ezekiel16:30 “How languishing is your heart
Ezekiel16:31 When you built your shrine at the head of every way and made your high place in every square
Ezekiel16:32 You adulteress wife
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all harlots
Ezekiel16:34 Thus you are the opposite of those women in your harlotries
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 Thus says Lord Yahweh
Ezekiel16:37 therefore
Ezekiel16:38 Thus I will judge you like women who commit adultery or shed blood are judged; and I will bring on you the blood of wrath and jealousy.
Ezekiel16:39 I will also give you into the hands of your lovers
Ezekiel16:40 They will bring up an assembly against you
Ezekiel16:41 They will burn your houses with fire and execute judgments on you in the sight of many women. Then I will make you cease from playing the harlot
Ezekiel16:42 So I will cause My wrath against you to be at rest
Ezekiel16:43 Because you have not remembered the days of your youth but have enraged Me by all these things
Ezekiel16:44 “Behold
Ezekiel16:45 You are the daughter of your mother
Ezekiel16:46 Now your older sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Yet you have not merely walked in their ways or done according to their abominations; but
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 Behold
Ezekiel16:50 Then they were haughty and committed abominations before Me. So I removed them when I saw it.
Ezekiel16:51 Furthermore
Ezekiel16:52 Also bear your dishonor in that you have made judgment favorable for your sisters. Because of your sins in which you acted more abominably than they
Ezekiel16:53 “Nevertheless
Ezekiel16:54 in order that you may bear your dishonor and feel dishonor for all that you have done when you become a comfort to them.
Ezekiel16:55 Your sisters
Ezekiel16:56 As the name of your sister Sodom was not heard in your mouth in your day of lofty pride
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was uncovered
Ezekiel16:58 You have borne the penalty of your lewdness and abominations
Ezekiel16:59 For thus says Lord Yahweh
Ezekiel16:60 “Nevertheless
Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember your ways and feel dishonor when you receive your sisters
Ezekiel16:62 Thus I will establish My covenant with you
Ezekiel16:63 so that you may remember and be ashamed and never open your mouth anymore because of your dishonor

Ezekiel18:2 “What do you mean by using this proverb concerning the land of Israel
Ezekiel18:3 As I live
Ezekiel18:4 Behold
Ezekiel18:5 “But if a man is righteous and does justice and righteousness
Ezekiel18:6 and does not eat at the mountain shrines or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel
Ezekiel18:7 if a man does not mistreat anyone
Ezekiel18:8 if he does not lend money on interest or take increase
Ezekiel18:9 if he walks in My statutes and My judgments and is careful to do the truth—he is righteous and will surely live
Ezekiel18:10 “Then he may have a violent son who sheds blood and who does any of these things to a brother
Ezekiel18:11 (though he himself did not do any of these things)
Ezekiel18:12 he mistreats the afflicted and needy
Ezekiel18:13 he lends money on interest and takes increase; will he live? He will not live! He has done all these abominations; he will surely be put to death; his blood will be on himself.
Ezekiel18:14 “Now behold
Ezekiel18:15 He does not eat at the mountain shrines or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel
Ezekiel18:16 or mistreat anyone
Ezekiel18:17 he turns his hand away from the afflicted
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 “Yet you say
Ezekiel18:20 The soul who sins will die. The son will not bear the iniquity of the father
Ezekiel18:21 “But if the wicked man turns from all his sins which he has done and keeps all My statutes and does justice and righteousness
Ezekiel18:22 All his transgressions which he has done will not be remembered against him; because of his righteousness which he has done
Ezekiel18:23 Do I have any pleasure in the death of the wicked
Ezekiel18:24 “But when a righteous man turns away from his righteousness
Ezekiel18:25 Yet you say
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous man turns away from his righteousness
Ezekiel18:27 Again
Ezekiel18:28 Again
Ezekiel18:29 But the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 “Therefore I will judge you
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from yourselves all your transgressions which you have committed and make yourselves a new heart and a new spirit! Now why will you die
Ezekiel18:32 For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone who dies
Ezekiel19:1 “As for you
Ezekiel19:2 and say
Ezekiel19:3 So she brought up one of her cubs; He became a lion
Ezekiel19:4 Then nations heard about him; He was captured in their pit
Ezekiel19:5 Then she saw
Ezekiel19:6 And he walked about among the lions; He became a young lion; He learned to tear his prey; He devoured men.
Ezekiel19:7 He knew how to destroy their fortified towers And laid waste their cities; And the land was in desolation
Ezekiel19:8 Then nations put themselves against him All around from their provinces
Ezekiel19:9 They put him in a cage with hooks And brought him to the king of Babylon; They brought him in hunting nets So that his voice would be heard no more On the mountains of Israel.
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine in your vineyard
Ezekiel19:11 And it had strong thick branches fit for scepters of rulers
Ezekiel19:12 But it was uprooted in wrath; It was cast down to the ground; And the east wind dried up its fruit. Its strong thick branch was torn off So that it dried up; The fire consumed it.
Ezekiel19:13 So now it is planted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 And fire has gone out from its thick branch; It has consumed its shoots and fruit

Ezekiel20:2 And the word of Yahweh came to me saying
Ezekiel20:3 “Son of man

Ezekiel20:5 and say to them

Ezekiel20:7 I said to them
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against Me and were not willing to listen to Me; each one did not cast away the detestable things of their eyes
Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:10 So I took them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:11 I gave them My statutes and made them know My judgments
Ezekiel20:12 And I also gave them My sabbaths to be a sign between Me and them
Ezekiel20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against Me in the wilderness. They did not walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:14 But I acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:15 Also I swore to them in the wilderness that I would not bring them into the land which I had given them
Ezekiel20:16 because they rejected My judgments
Ezekiel20:17 Yet My eye had pity on them rather than bringing them to ruin

Ezekiel20:19 I am Yahweh your God; walk in My statutes and keep My judgments and do them.
Ezekiel20:20 Keep My sabbaths holy; and they shall be a sign between Me and you
Ezekiel20:21 But the children rebelled against Me; they did not walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:22 But I turned back My hand and acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:23 Also I swore to them in the wilderness that I would scatter them among the nations and disperse them among the lands
Ezekiel20:24 because they had not done My judgments

Ezekiel20:28 Indeed

Ezekiel20:30 Therefore

Ezekiel22:2 “Now as for you
Ezekiel22:3 And you shall say
Ezekiel22:4 You have become guilty by the blood which you have shed

Ezekiel22:6 “Behold
Ezekiel22:7 They have treated father and mother with contempt within you. The sojourner they have oppressed in your midst; the fatherless and the widow they have mistreated in you.
Ezekiel22:8 You have despised My holy things and profaned My sabbaths.
Ezekiel22:9 Slanderous men have been in you for the purpose of shedding blood
Ezekiel22:10 In you they have uncovered their fathers’ nakedness; in you they have humbled her who was unclean in her menstrual impurity.
Ezekiel22:11 One has also done what is an abomination with his neighbor’s wife
Ezekiel22:12 In you they have taken bribes for the purpose of shedding blood; you have taken interest and profits
Ezekiel22:13 “Now behold
Ezekiel22:14 Can your heart stand
Ezekiel22:15 I will scatter you among the nations
Ezekiel22:16 You will profane yourself in the sight of the nations

Ezekiel22:18 “Son of man
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore
Ezekiel22:20 As they gather silver and bronze and iron and lead and tin into the furnace to blow fire on it in order to melt it
Ezekiel22:21 And I will collect you together and blow on you with the fire of My fury
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is melted in the furnace
Ezekiel22:23 And the word of Yahweh came to me
Ezekiel22:24 “Son of man
Ezekiel22:25 There is a conspiracy of her prophets in her midst like a roaring lion tearing the prey. They have devoured lives; they have taken treasure and precious things; they have made many widows in the midst of her.
Ezekiel22:26 Her priests have done violence to My law and have profaned My holy things; they have made no separation between the holy and the profane
Ezekiel22:27 Her princes within her are like wolves tearing the prey by shedding blood and destroying lives in order to get greedy gain.
Ezekiel22:28 And her prophets have smeared whitewash for them
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have practiced oppression and committed robbery
Ezekiel22:30 And I searched for a man among them who would build up the wall and stand in the breach before Me for the land
Ezekiel22:31 Thus I have poured out My indignation on them; I have consumed them with the fire of My fury; their way I have brought upon their heads
Ezekiel23:1 The word of Yahweh came to me again
Ezekiel23:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel23:3 and they played the harlot in Egypt. They played the harlot in their youth; there their breasts were pressed
Ezekiel23:4 Their names were Oholah the elder and Oholibah her sister. And they became Mine
Ezekiel23:5 “Oholah played the harlot while she was Mine; and she lusted after her lovers
Ezekiel23:6 who were clothed in purple
Ezekiel23:7 She gave her harlotries to them
Ezekiel23:8 She did not forsake her harlotries from the time in Egypt; for in her youth men had lain with her
Ezekiel23:9 Therefore
Ezekiel23:10 They uncovered her nakedness; they took her sons and her daughters
Ezekiel23:11 “Now her sister Oholibah saw this
Ezekiel23:12 She lusted after the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:13 And I saw that she had defiled herself; they both took the same way.
Ezekiel23:14 So she increased her harlotries. And she saw men portrayed on the wall
Ezekiel23:15 girded with belts on their loins
Ezekiel23:16 And she saw them and lusted after them and sent messengers to them in Chaldea.
Ezekiel23:17 So the Babylonians came to her to the bed of love and defiled her with their harlotry. Then she became defiled by them
Ezekiel23:18 And she uncovered her harlotries and uncovered her nakedness; then My soul became disgusted with her
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she multiplied her harlotries
Ezekiel23:20 She lusted after their paramours
Ezekiel23:21 Thus you longed for the lewdness of your youth
Ezekiel23:22 “Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans
Ezekiel23:24 They will come against you with weapons
Ezekiel23:25 And I will set My jealousy against you
Ezekiel23:26 They will also strip you of your clothes and take away your beautiful jewelry.
Ezekiel23:27 Thus I will make your lewdness and your harlotry brought from the land of Egypt to cease from you
Ezekiel23:28 For thus says Lord Yahweh
Ezekiel23:29 They will deal with you in hatred
Ezekiel23:30 These things will be done to you because you have played the harlot with the nations
Ezekiel23:31 You have walked in the way of your sister; therefore I will give her cup into your hand.’
Ezekiel23:32 Thus says Lord Yahweh
Ezekiel23:33 You will be filled with drunkenness and sorrow
Ezekiel23:34 You will drink it and drain it. Then you will gnaw its fragments And tear your breasts; for I have spoken
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore
Ezekiel23:36 Moreover
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 Again
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slaughtered their children for their idols
Ezekiel23:40 “Furthermore
Ezekiel23:41 and you sat on a splendid couch with a table arranged before it on which you had set My incense and My oil.
Ezekiel23:42 The sound of a multitude at ease was with her; and drunkards were brought from the wilderness with men of the common sort. And they put bracelets on the hands of the women and beautiful crowns on their heads.
Ezekiel23:43 “Then I said concerning her who was worn out by adulteries
Ezekiel23:44 But they went in to her as they would go in to a harlot. Thus they went in to Oholah and to Oholibah

Ezekiel27:1 Moreover
Ezekiel27:2 “Now as for you
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the heart of the seas; Your builders have perfected your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They have made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; They have taken a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
Ezekiel27:6 Of oaks from Bashan they have made your oars; With ivory they have inlaid your deck of boxwood from the coastlands of Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:7 Your sail was of fine embroidered linen from Egypt So that it became your standard; Your awning was blue and purple from the coastlands of Elishah.
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; Your wise men
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Gebal and her wise men were with you repairing your seams; All the ships of the sea and their sailors were with you in order to deal in your merchandise.
Ezekiel27:10 “Persia and Lud and Put were in your military force
Ezekiel27:11 The sons of Arvad and your military force were on your walls
Ezekiel27:12 “Tarshish was your customer because of the abundance of all kinds of wealth; with silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 Those from Beth-togarmah gave horses and war horses and mules for your wares.
Ezekiel27:15 The sons of Dedan were your traders. Many coastlands were customers at your hand; ivory tusks and ebony they brought as your payment.
Ezekiel27:16 Aram was your customer because of the abundance of your goods; they paid for your wares with emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus was your customer because of the abundance of your goods
Ezekiel27:19 Also Vedan and Javan paid for your wares from Uzal; wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar
Ezekiel27:22 The traders of Sheba and Raamah
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 They traded with you in choice garments
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish were the carriers for your merchandise. And you were filled and were very glorious In the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 “Your rowers have brought you Into great waters; The east wind has broken you In the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your wealth
Ezekiel27:28 At the sound of the cry of your pilots The pasture lands will shake.
Ezekiel27:29 All who handle the oar
Ezekiel27:30 And they will make their voice heard over you And will cry bitterly. They will cast dust on their heads; They will wallow in ashes.
Ezekiel27:31 Also they will make themselves bald for you And gird themselves with sackcloth; And they will weep for you in bitterness of soul With bitter mourning.

Ezekiel27:33 When your wares went out from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 Now that you are broken by the seas In the depths of the waters
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the coastlands Are appalled at you
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; You have become terrified

Ezekiel31:1 Now it happened in the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Behold
Ezekiel31:4 The waters made it grow; the deep made it high. With its rivers it was going all around its planting place And sent out its conduits to all the trees of the field.
Ezekiel31:5 Therefore its height was loftier than all the trees of the field
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the sky nested in its boughs
Ezekiel31:7 So it was beautiful in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in God’s garden could not match it; The cypresses could not liken themselves with its boughs
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful with the multitude of its foliage
Ezekiel31:10 ‘Therefore thus says Lord Yahweh
Ezekiel31:11 therefore I will give it into the hand of a dominant one of the nations; he will thoroughly deal with it. According to its wickedness I have driven it away.
Ezekiel31:12 Strangers—ruthless ones of the nations have cut it down and abandoned it; on the mountains and in all the valleys its foliage have fallen
Ezekiel31:13 On its downfall all the birds of the sky will dwell
Ezekiel31:14 so that all the trees by the waters may not be lofty in their height
Ezekiel31:15 ‘Thus says Lord Yahweh
Ezekiel31:16 I made the nations quake at the sound of its downfall when I made it go down to Sheol with those who go down to the pit; and all the trees of Eden
Ezekiel31:17 They also went down with it to Sheol to those who were slain by the sword; and those who were its strength lived under its shade among the nations.

Ezekiel44:31 The priests shall not eat any bird or beast that has died of itself or has been torn to pieces.

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 Then the king said for Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 youths in whom was no defect
Daniel1:5 And the king appointed for them a daily ration from the king’s choice food and from the wine which he drank
Daniel1:6 Now among them from the sons of Judah were Daniel
Daniel1:7 Then the commander of the officials set names for them; and for Daniel he set the name Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 But Daniel set in his heart that he would not defile himself with the king’s choice food or with the wine which he drank; so he sought permission from the commander of the officials that he might not defile himself.
Daniel1:9 Now God granted Daniel lovingkindness and compassion before the commander of the officials
Daniel1:10 and the commander of the officials said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 But Daniel said to the overseer whom the commander of the officials had appointed over Daniel
Daniel1:12 “Please test your servants for ten days
Daniel1:13 Then let our appearance be observed before you and the appearance of the youths who are eating the king’s choice food; and deal with your servants according to what you see.”
Daniel1:14 So he listened to them in this matter and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 At the end of ten days it was seen that their appearance was better and that they were fatter than all the youths who had been eating the king’s choice food.
Daniel1:16 So the overseer continued to withhold their choice food and the wine they were to drink
Daniel1:17 And as for these four youths
Daniel1:18 Then at the end of the days which the king had spoken of for bringing them in
Daniel1:19 And the king talked with them
Daniel1:20 And as for every matter of wisdom in understanding which the king sought from them
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued until the first year of Cyrus the king.

Daniel2:8 The king answered and said

Daniel2:11 Moreover

Daniel10:6 His body also was like beryl
Daniel10:7 Now I
Daniel10:8 So I alone remained and saw this great vision that appeared; yet no might remained in me
Daniel10:9 But I heard the sound of his words; and as soon as I heard the sound of his words
Daniel10:10 Then behold
Daniel10:11 And he said to me
Daniel10:12 Then he said to me
Daniel10:13 But the prince of the kingdom of Persia was standing against me for twenty-one days; then behold
Daniel10:14 Now I have come to give you an understanding of what will happen to your people in the last days
Daniel10:15 Now when he had spoken to me according to these words
Daniel10:16 And behold
Daniel10:17 Now how can such a servant of my lord talk with such as my lord? As for me
Daniel10:18 Then this one with the appearance of a man touched me again and strengthened me.
Daniel10:19 And he said
Daniel10:20 Then he said
Daniel10:21 However
Daniel11:1 “Now I
Daniel11:2 So now I will tell you the truth. Behold
Daniel11:3 And a mighty king will stand
Daniel11:4 But as soon as he stands
Daniel11:5 “Then the king of the South will grow strong
Daniel11:6 And after some years they will form an alliance
Daniel11:7 But one of the descendants of her line will stand in his place
Daniel11:8 And also their gods with their metal images and their desirable vessels of silver and gold he will bring into captivity to Egypt
Daniel11:9 Then the latter will enter the kingdom of the king of the South
Daniel11:10 “And his sons will wage war. So they will gather a multitude of great forces; and one of them will keep on coming and overflow and pass through
Daniel11:11 And the king of the South will be enraged and go forth and fight with the king of the North. Then the latter will cause a great multitude to stand
Daniel11:12 Then the multitude will be carried away
Daniel11:13 And the king of the North will again cause a much greater multitude than the former to stand
Daniel11:14 “Now in those times many will stand against the king of the South; the violent ones among your people will also lift themselves up in order to cause the vision to stand
Daniel11:15 Then the king of the North will come
Daniel11:16 But he who comes against him will do as he pleases
Daniel11:17 And he will set his face to come with the authority of his whole kingdom
Daniel11:18 Then he will turn his face to the coastlands and capture many. But a ruler will make his reproach against him cease; moreover
Daniel11:19 So he will turn his face toward the fortresses of his own land
Daniel11:20 “Then in his place one will stand who will have an oppressor pass through the Jewel of his kingdom; yet within a few days he will be broken
Daniel11:21 And in his place a despised person will stand
Daniel11:22 But the overflowing might will be flooded away before him and broken
Daniel11:23 And after an alliance is made with him
Daniel11:24 In a time of ease he will enter the richest parts of the province
Daniel11:25 And he will stir up his strength and heart against the king of the South with a great military force; so the king of the South will wage war with an extremely large and mighty military force for war; but he will not stand
Daniel11:26 And those who eat his choice food will break him
Daniel11:27 And as for both kings
Daniel11:28 Then he will return to his land with great possessions; but his heart will be set against the holy covenant
Daniel11:29 “At the appointed time he will return and come into the South
Daniel11:30 Indeed
Daniel11:31 Mighty forces from him will stand
Daniel11:32 And by smooth words he will turn to godlessness those who act wickedly toward the covenant
Daniel11:33 And those who have insight among the people will give understanding to the many; yet they will fall by sword and by flame
Daniel11:34 Now when they fall
Daniel11:35 And some of those who have insight will fall
Daniel11:36 “Then the king will do as he pleases
Daniel11:37 He will show no regard for the gods of his fathers or for the desire of women
Daniel11:38 But instead he will honor a god of fortresses
Daniel11:39 And he will take action against the strongest of fortresses with the help of a foreign god; he will give great honor to those who recognize him and will cause them to rule over the many and will apportion land for a price.
Daniel11:40 “And at the time of the end
Daniel11:41 And he will also enter the Beautiful Land
Daniel11:42 Then he will send forth his hand against other countries
Daniel11:43 But he will rule over the hidden treasures of gold and silver and over all the desirable things of Egypt; and Libyans and Ethiopians will follow at his heels.
Daniel11:44 But reports from the East and from the North will dismay him
Daniel11:45 And he will pitch the tents of his royal pavilion between the seas and the beautiful Holy Mountain; yet he will come to his end
Daniel12:1 “Now at that time
Daniel12:2 And many of those who sleep in the dust of the ground will awake
Daniel12:3 And those who have insight will shine brightly like the brightness of the expanse of heaven
Daniel12:4 But as for you
Daniel12:5 Then I
Daniel12:6 And one said to the man dressed in linen
Daniel12:7 And I heard the man dressed in linen
Daniel12:8 As for me
Daniel12:9 Then he said
Daniel12:10 Many will be purged
Daniel12:11 But from the time that the regular sacrifice is abolished and the abomination of desolation is set up
Daniel12:12 How blessed is he who keeps waiting and reaches the 1
Daniel12:13 But as for you
Hosea1:1 The word of Yahweh which came to Hosea the son of Beeri
Hosea1:2 When Yahweh first spoke through Hosea
Hosea1:3 So he went and took Gomer the daughter of Diblaim
Hosea1:4 And Yahweh said to him
Hosea1:5 And it will be in that day
Hosea1:6 Then she conceived again and gave birth to a daughter. And Yahweh said to him
Hosea1:7 But I will have compassion on the house of Judah and save them by Yahweh their God
Hosea1:8 Then she weaned Lo-ruhamah. And she conceived and gave birth to a son.
Hosea1:9 And Yahweh said
Hosea1:10 Yet the number of the sons of Israel Will be like the sand of the sea
Hosea1:11 And the sons of Judah and the sons of Israel will be gathered together
Hosea2:1 Say to your brothers
Hosea2:2 “Contend with your mother
Hosea2:3 Lest I strip her naked And set her forth as on the day when she was born And make her like a wilderness And make her like dry land And put her to death with thirst.
Hosea2:4 Also
Hosea2:5 For their mother has played the harlot; She who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said
Hosea2:6 Therefore
Hosea2:7 So she will pursue her lovers
Hosea2:8 “Now she does not know that it was I who gave her the grain
Hosea2:9 Therefore
Hosea2:10 So now I will uncover her lewdness In the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will also cease all her joy
Hosea2:12 And I will make desolate her vines and fig trees
Hosea2:13 So I will visit the days of the Baals upon her When she used to offer offerings in smoke to them And adorn herself with her earrings and jewelry And go after her lovers
Hosea2:14 “Therefore
Hosea2:15 Then I will give her her vineyards from there And the valley of Achor as a door of hope. And she will sing there as in the days of her youth
Hosea2:16 And it will be in that day
Hosea2:17 So I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth So that they will be remembered by their names no more.
Hosea2:18 And in that day I will cut a covenant for them With the beasts of the field
Hosea2:19 And I will betroth you to Me forever; Indeed
Hosea2:20 And I will betroth you to Me in faithfulness. Then you will know Yahweh.
Hosea2:21 “And it will be in that day
Hosea2:22 And the earth will answer the grain
Hosea2:23 And I will sow her for Myself in the land. I will also have compassion on her who had not obtained compassion
Hosea3:1 Then Yahweh said to me
Hosea3:2 So I bargained for her for myself for fifteen shekels of silver and a homer and a half of barley.
Hosea3:3 Then I said to her
Hosea3:4 For the sons of Israel will remain for many days without king or prince
Hosea3:5 Afterward the sons of Israel will return and seek Yahweh their God and David their king; and they will come in dread to Yahweh and to His goodness in the last days.
Hosea4:1 Listen to the word of Yahweh
Hosea4:2 There is swearing of oaths
Hosea4:3 Therefore the land mourns
Hosea4:4 Yet let no man contend
Hosea4:5 So you will stumble by day
Hosea4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge
Hosea4:7 The more they multiplied
Hosea4:8 They eat the sin of My people And lift up their soul toward their iniquity.
Hosea4:9 And it will be like people
Hosea4:10 They will eat
Hosea4:11 Harlotry
Hosea4:12 My people ask their wooden idol
Hosea4:13 They offer sacrifices on the tops of the mountains And burn incense on the hills
Hosea4:14 I will not punish your daughters when they play the harlot Or your brides when they commit adultery
Hosea4:15 Though you
Hosea4:16 Since Israel is stubborn Like a stubborn heifer
Hosea4:17 Ephraim is joined to idols; Let him alone.
Hosea4:18 Their drink gone; They play the harlot continually; Their rulers dearly love disgrace.
Hosea4:19 The wind binds them up in its wings
Hosea5:1 Hear this
Hosea5:2 And the revolters have dug deep into slaughtering
Hosea5:3 I know Ephraim
Hosea5:4 Their deeds will not allow them To return to their God. For a spirit of harlotry is within them
Hosea5:5 Moreover
Hosea5:6 They will go with their flocks and herds To seek Yahweh
Hosea5:7 They have dealt treacherously against Yahweh
Hosea5:8 Blow the horn in Gibeah
Hosea5:9 Ephraim will become a desolation in the day of reproof; Among the tribes of Israel I will make known what is true.
Hosea5:10 The princes of Judah have become like those who move a boundary; On them I will pour out My wrath like water.
Hosea5:11 Ephraim is oppressed
Hosea5:12 Therefore I am like a moth to Ephraim And like rottenness to the house of Judah.
Hosea5:13 Then Ephraim saw his sickness
Hosea5:14 For I will be like a lion to Ephraim And like a young lion to the house of Judah. I
Hosea5:15 I will go away and return to My place Until they acknowledge their guilt and seek My face; In their affliction they will seek Me earnestly.
Hosea6:1 “Come
Hosea6:2 He will make us alive after two days; He will raise us up on the third day
Hosea6:3 So let us know
Hosea6:4 What shall I do with you
Hosea6:5 Therefore I have hewn them in pieces by the prophets; I have killed them by the words of My mouth; And the judgments on you are like the light that goes forth.
Hosea6:6 For I delight in lovingkindness rather than sacrifice
Hosea6:7 But like Adam they have trespassed against the covenant; There they have dealt treacherously against Me.
Hosea6:8 Gilead is a city of workers of iniquity
Hosea6:9 And as raiders wait for a man
Hosea6:10 In the house of Israel I have seen an appalling thing; Ephraim’s harlotry is there; Israel has defiled itself.
Hosea6:11 Also
Hosea7:1 When I would heal Israel
Hosea7:2 And they do not say to their hearts That I remember all their evil. Now their deeds are all around them; They are before My face.
Hosea7:3 With their evil they make the king glad
Hosea7:4 They are all adulterers
Hosea7:5 On the day of our king
Hosea7:6 For their hearts are like an oven As they draw near in their plotting; Their anger smolders all night; In the morning it burns like a flaming fire.
Hosea7:7 All of them are hot like an oven
Hosea7:8 Ephraim mixes himself with the peoples; Ephraim has become a cake not turned.
Hosea7:9 Strangers devour his power
Hosea7:10 So the pride of Israel answers against him
Hosea7:11 So Ephraim has become like a silly dove
Hosea7:12 When they go
Hosea7:13 Woe to them
Hosea7:14 And they do not cry out to Me in their heart When they wail on their beds; For the sake of grain and new wine they gather together as sojourners; They depart from Me.
Hosea7:15 Although I disciplined and strengthened their arms
Hosea7:16 They turn
Hosea8:1 Put the trumpet to your mouth! Like an eagle the enemy comes against the house of Yahweh Because they have trespassed against My covenant And transgressed against My law.
Hosea8:2 They cry out to Me
Hosea8:3 Israel has rejected the good; The enemy will pursue him.
Hosea8:4 They have set up kings
Hosea8:5 He has rejected your calf
Hosea8:6 For from Israel is even this! A craftsman made it
Hosea8:7 For they sow the wind
Hosea8:8 Israel is swallowed up; They are now among the nations Like a vessel in which no one delights.
Hosea8:9 For they have gone up to Assyria Like a wild donkey all alone; Ephraim has hired lovers.
Hosea8:10 Even though they hire allies among the nations
Hosea8:11 Since Ephraim has multiplied altars for sin
Hosea8:12 Though I wrote for him ten thousand precepts of My law
Hosea8:13 As for My sacrificial gifts
Hosea8:14 So Israel has forgotten his Maker and built palaces; And Judah has multiplied fortified cities
Hosea9:1 Do not be glad
Hosea9:2 Threshing floor and wine press will not feed them
Hosea9:3 They will not remain in the land of Yahweh
Hosea9:4 They will not pour out drink offerings of wine to Yahweh; Their sacrifices will not please Him. Their bread will be like mourners’ bread; All who eat of it will be defiled
Hosea9:5 What will you do on the day of the appointed festival And on the day of the feast of Yahweh?
Hosea9:6 For behold
Hosea9:7 The days of punishment have come; The days of recompense have come; Let Israel know this! The prophet is an ignorant fool
Hosea9:8 Ephraim was a watchman with my God
Hosea9:9 They have dug deep in corruption As in the days of Gibeah; He will remember their iniquity; He will punish their sins.
Hosea9:10 I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness; I saw your fathers as the earliest fruit on the fig tree in its first season. But they came to Baal‑peor and devoted themselves to shame
Hosea9:11 As for Ephraim
Hosea9:12 Though they bring up their children
Hosea9:13 Ephraim
Hosea9:14 Give them
Hosea9:15 All their evil is at Gilgal; Indeed
Hosea9:16 Ephraim is stricken; their root is dried up; They will bear no fruit. Even though they bear children
Hosea9:17 My God will despise them Because they have not listened to Him; And they will be those who flee among the nations.
Hosea10:1 Israel is a luxuriant vine; He produces fruit for himself. The more abundant his fruit
Hosea10:2 Their heart is faithless; Now they must bear their guilt. Yahweh will break down their altars And destroy their sacred pillars.
Hosea10:3 Surely now they will say
Hosea10:4 They speak mere words; With worthless oaths they cut covenants; And judgment flourishes like gall in the furrows of the field.
Hosea10:5 The dweller of Samaria will fear For the calf of Beth‑aven. Indeed
Hosea10:6 The thing itself will be carried to Assyria As tribute to King Jareb; Ephraim will receive shame
Hosea10:7 Samaria will be ruined with her king Like a stick on the surface of the water.
Hosea10:8 Also the high places of Aven
Hosea10:9 From the days of Gibeah you have sinned
Hosea10:10 When it is My desire
Hosea10:11 And Ephraim is a trained heifer that loves to thresh
Hosea10:12 Sow with a view to righteousness
Hosea10:13 You have plowed wickedness; you have reaped injustice; You have eaten the fruit of deception. Because you have trusted in your way
Hosea10:14 Therefore a rumbling will arise among your people
Hosea10:15 Thus it will be done to you at Bethel because of your evil of evils. At dawn the king of Israel will be completely ruined.

Hosea11:2 The more they called them
Hosea11:3 Yet it is I who taught Ephraim to walk; I took them in My arms; But they did not know that I healed them.
Hosea11:4 I led them with cords of a man
Hosea11:5 They will not return to the land of Egypt; But Assyria—he will be their king Because they refused to return to Me.

Hosea11:8 How can I give you up
Hosea11:9 I will not execute My burning anger; I will not make Ephraim a ruin again. For I am God and not man

Hosea11:11 They will come trembling like birds from Egypt And like doves from the land of Assyria; And I will settle them in their houses

Hosea12:13 But by a prophet Yahweh brought Israel up from Egypt
Hosea12:14 Ephraim has provoked to bitter anger; So his Lord will leave his bloodguilt on him And cause his reproach to return to him.

Joel1:1 The word of Yahweh that came to Joel
Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Recount about it to your sons
Joel1:4 What the gnawing locust has left
Joel1:5 Awake
Joel1:6 For a nation has come up against my land
Joel1:7 It has made my vine a desolation And my fig tree splinters. It has stripped them bare and cast them away; Their branches have become white.
Joel1:8 Wail like a virgin girded with sackcloth For the bridegroom of her youth.
Joel1:9 The grain offering and the drink offering are cut off From the house of Yahweh. The priests mourn
Joel1:10 The field is destroyed; The land mourns
Joel1:11 Be ashamed
Joel1:12 The vine dries up
Joel1:13 Gird yourselves with sackcloth And lament
Joel1:14 Set apart a fast as holy
Joel1:15 Alas for the day! For the day of Yahweh is near
Joel1:16 Has not food been cut off before our eyes
Joel1:17 The seeds shrivel under their clods; The storehouses are desolate; The barns are pulled down
Joel1:18 How the beasts groan! The herds of cattle wander aimlessly Because there is no pasture for them; Even the flocks of sheep suffer.
Joel1:19 To You
Joel1:20 Even the beasts of the field pant for You; For the water brooks are dried up
Joel2:1 Blow a trumpet in Zion
Joel2:2 A day of darkness and thick darkness
Joel2:3 A fire consumes before them
Joel2:4 Their appearance is like the appearance of horses; And like war horses
Joel2:5 With a noise as of chariots They leap on the tops of the mountains
Joel2:6 Before them the peoples are writhing; All faces turn pale.
Joel2:7 They run like mighty men; They climb up the wall like men of war; And they each march in line
Joel2:8 They do not crowd each other; They march everyone in his path; When they fall against the defending weapons
Joel2:9 They rush on the city; They run on the wall; They climb up into the houses; They enter through the windows like a thief.
Joel2:10 Before them the earth trembles; The heavens quake; The sun and the moon grow dark
Joel2:11 But Yahweh gives forth His voice before His military force; Surely His camp is very numerous
Joel2:12 “Yet even now
Joel2:13 And tear your heart and not your garments.” Now return to Yahweh your God

Joel2:16 Gather the people
Joel2:17 Let the priests
Joel2:18 Then Yahweh will be zealous for His land And will spare His people.
Joel2:19 Then Yahweh will answer and say to His people
Joel2:20 But I will remove the northern military force far from you
Joel2:21 Do not fear
Joel2:22 Do not fear
Joel2:23 So rejoice
Joel2:24 The threshing floors will be full of grain
Joel2:25 “Then I will pay back to you in full for the years That the swarming locust has consumed
Joel2:26 And you will have plenty to consume and be satisfied And praise the name of Yahweh your God
Joel2:27 Thus you will know that I am in the midst of Israel
Joel2:28 “And it will be afterwards That I will pour out My Spirit on all mankind; And your sons and your daughters shall prophesy; Your old men will dream dreams; Your young men will see visions.
Joel2:29 Even on the male slaves and female slaves I will in those days pour out My Spirit.
Joel2:30 And I will put wonders in the sky and on the earth
Joel2:31 The sun will be turned into darkness And the moon into blood Before the great and awesome day of Yahweh comes.
Joel2:32 And it will be that everyone who calls on the name of Yahweh Will be delivered; For on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem There will be those who escape

Joel3:4 Moreover
Joel3:5 Since you have taken My silver and My gold

Amos1:2 And he said
Amos1:3 Thus says Yahweh
Amos1:4 So I will send fire upon the house of Hazael
Amos1:5 I will also break the gate bar of Damascus And cut off the inhabitant from the valley of Aven And him who holds the scepter from Beth‑eden; So the people of Aram will go into exile to Kir

Amos1:7 So I will send fire upon the wall of Gaza
Amos1:8 I will also cut off the inhabitant from Ashdod And him who holds the scepter from Ashkelon; I will even turn My hand against Ekron
Amos1:9 Thus says Yahweh
Amos1:10 So I will send fire upon the wall of Tyre
Amos1:11 Thus says Yahweh
Amos1:12 So I will send fire upon Teman
Amos1:13 Thus says Yahweh
Amos1:14 So I will kindle a fire on the wall of Rabbah
Amos1:15 And their king will go into exile
Amos2:1 Thus says Yahweh
Amos2:2 So I will send fire upon Moab
Amos2:3 I will also cut off the judge from her midst And kill all her princes with him
Amos2:4 Thus says Yahweh
Amos2:5 So I will send fire upon Judah
Amos2:6 Thus says Yahweh
Amos2:7 These who pant after the very dust of the earth on the head of the poor Also turn aside the way of the humble; And a man and his father go to the same young woman In order to profane My holy name.
Amos2:8 On garments taken as pledges they stretch out beside every altar
Amos2:9 “Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 And it was I who brought you up from the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 Then I raised up some of your sons to be prophets And some of your choice men to be Nazirites. Is this not so
Amos2:12 “But you made the Nazirites drink wine
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 So flight will perish from the swift
Amos2:15 He who grasps the bow will not stand his ground
Amos2:16 Even the most courageous of heart among the mighty men will flee naked in that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word which Yahweh has spoken against you
Amos3:2 “You only have I known among all the families of the earth; Therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.”
Amos3:3 Do two men walk together unless they have made an appointment?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest when it has no prey? Does a young lion give forth its voice from its den unless it has captured something?
Amos3:5 Does a bird fall into a trap on the ground when there is no bait in it? Does a trap spring up from the earth when it captures nothing at all?
Amos3:6 If a trumpet is blown in a city will not the people tremble? If a calamity happens in a city has not Yahweh done it?
Amos3:7 Surely Lord Yahweh does nothing Unless He reveals His secret counsel To His slaves the prophets.
Amos3:8 A lion has roared! Who will not fear? Lord Yahweh has spoken! Who can but prophesy?
Amos3:9 Make it heard on the citadels in Ashdod and on the citadels in the land of Egypt and say
Amos3:10 But they do not know how to do what is right
Amos3:11 Therefore
Amos3:12 Thus says Yahweh
Amos3:13 Hear and testify against the house of Jacob
Amos3:14 “For on the day that I punish Israel’s transgressions
Amos3:15 I will also strike the winter house together with the summer house; The houses of ivory will also perish
Amos4:1 Hear this word
Amos4:2 Lord Yahweh has sworn by His holiness that
Amos4:3 And you will go out through breaches in the walls
Amos4:4 “Enter Bethel and transgress; In Gilgal multiply transgression! Bring your sacrifices every morning
Amos4:5 And offer a thank offering also from that which is leavened
Amos4:6 “But I gave you also cleanness of teeth in all your cities And lack of bread in all your places
Amos4:7 “And I also withheld the rain from you While there were still three months until harvest. Then I would send rain on one city
Amos4:8 So two or three cities would wander around to another city to drink water
Amos4:9 “I struck you with scorching wind and mildew; And the gnawing locust was devouring Your many gardens and vineyards
Amos4:10 “I sent a pestilence among you after the manner of Egypt; I killed your choice men by the sword along with your captured horses
Amos4:11 “I overthrew you as God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah
Amos4:12 “Therefore thus I will do to you

Amos5:2 She has fallen; she will not rise again— The virgin Israel. She lies abandoned on her land; There is none to raise her up.

Amos5:4 For thus says Yahweh to the house of Israel
Amos5:5 But do not seek Bethel
Amos5:6 Seek Yahweh that you may live
Amos5:7 For those who overturn justice into wormwood And put righteousness down to the earth.”
Amos5:8 He who made the Pleiades and Orion And overturns the shadow of death into morning
Amos5:9 It is He who flashes forth with devastation upon the strong So that devastation comes upon the fortification.
Amos5:10 They hate him who reproves in the gate
Amos5:11 Therefore because you impose heavy rent on the poor And take a tribute of grain from them
Amos5:12 For I know your transgressions are many and your sins are mighty
Amos5:13 Therefore at such a time the one with insight keeps silent
Amos5:14 Seek good and not evil
Amos5:15 Hate evil
Amos5:16 Therefore thus says Yahweh God of hosts
Amos5:17 And in all the vineyards there is wailing Because I will pass through the midst of you
Amos5:18 Woe
Amos5:19 As when a man flees from a lion And a bear meets him; Or he goes home
Amos5:20 Will not the day of Yahweh be darkness instead of light
Amos5:21 “I hate
Amos5:22 Even though you offer up to Me burnt offerings and your grain offerings
Amos5:23 Remove from Me the tumult of your songs; I will not even listen to the melody of your harps.
Amos5:24 But let justice roll down like waters And righteousness like an ever‑flowing stream.
Amos5:25 “Did you present Me with sacrifices and grain offerings in the wilderness for forty years
Amos5:26 You also carried along Sikkuth your king and Kiyyun
Amos5:27 Therefore

Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus says Lord Yahweh concerning Edom— We have heard a report from Yahweh

Obadiah1:4 Though you build loftily like the eagle
Obadiah1:5 “If thieves came to you

Obadiah1:8 Will I not on that day
Obadiah1:9 Then your mighty men will be dismayed
Obadiah1:10 “Because of violence to your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day that you stood aloof
Obadiah1:12 Now do not look on your brother’s day with triumph
Obadiah1:13 Do not enter the gate of My people In the day of their disaster. Indeed
Obadiah1:14 Do not stand at the fork of the road To cut down those among them who escape; And do not deliver over their survivors In the day of their distress.
Obadiah1:15 “For the day of Yahweh draws near on all the nations. As you have done
Obadiah1:16 Because just as you all drank on My holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But on Mount Zion there will be those who escape
Obadiah1:18 Then the house of Jacob will be a fire And the house of Joseph a flame; But the house of Esau will be as stubble. And they will set them on fire and consume them So that there will be no survivor of the house of Esau
