Obadiah1:20 And the exiles of this military force of the sons of Israel
Obadiah1:21 And the saviors will ascend Mount Zion To judge the mountain of Esau

Micah1:3 For behold
Micah1:4 The mountains will melt under Him

Micah1:7 And all of her graven images will be smashed
Micah1:8 Because of this I must lament and wail; I must go barefoot and naked; I must make a lament like the jackals And a mourning like the ostriches.

Nahum1:1 The oracle of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Nahum1:2 A jealous and avenging God is Yahweh; Yahweh is avenging and wrathful. Yahweh is avenging against His adversaries
Nahum1:3 Yahweh is slow to anger and great in power
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and makes it dry; He dries up all the rivers. Bashan and Carmel languish; The blossoms of Lebanon languish.
Nahum1:5 Mountains quake because of Him
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before His indignation? Who can endure the burning of His anger? His wrath is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 Yahweh is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overflowing flood He will make a complete destruction of its place And will pursue His enemies into darkness.
Nahum1:9 Whatever you devise against Yahweh
Nahum1:10 Like tangled thorns
Nahum1:11 From you has gone forth One who devised evil against Yahweh
Nahum1:12 Thus says Yahweh
Nahum1:13 So now
Nahum1:14 And Yahweh has commanded concerning you: “There will no longer be seed from your name. From the house of your gods
Nahum1:15 Behold
Nahum2:1 The one who scatters has come up against you. Guard the fortification
Nahum2:2 For Yahweh will restore the majesty of Jacob Like the majesty of Israel
Nahum2:3 The shields of his mighty men are colored red
Nahum2:4 The chariots race madly in the streets; They rush wildly in the squares; Their appearance is like torches; They dash to and fro like lightning flashes.
Nahum2:5 He remembers his mighty ones; They stumble in their march; They hurry to her wall
Nahum2:6 The gates of the rivers are opened
Nahum2:7 So it stands fixed: She is exiled
Nahum2:8 Though Nineveh was like a pool of water throughout her days
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold! And there is no limit to the treasure— Wealth from every kind of desirable object.
Nahum2:10 She is emptied! Yes
Nahum2:11 Where is the den of the lions And the feeding place of the young lions
Nahum2:12 The lion tore enough for its cubs And strangled enough for its lionesses And filled its lairs with torn up prey And its dens with torn up flesh.
Nahum2:13 “Behold

Nahum3:2 The sound of the whip
Nahum3:3 Horsemen charging
Nahum3:4 All because of the many harlotries of the harlot

Nahum3:8 Are you better than No‑amon
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia was her might
Nahum3:10 Yet she became an exile; She went into captivity; Also her infants were dashed to pieces At the head of every street; They cast lots for her honorable men
Nahum3:11 You too will become drunk; You will be hidden. You too will search for a strong defense from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortifications are fig trees with ripe fruit— When shaken
Nahum3:13 Behold

Nahum3:15 There
Nahum3:16 You have increased your traders more than the stars of heaven— The creeping locust strips and flies away.

Nahum3:18 Your shepherds are sleeping
Nahum3:19 There is no relief for your breakdown
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle which Habakkuk the prophet beheld.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do You make me see wickedness And cause me to look on trouble? Indeed
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore the law is ignored
Habakkuk1:5 “See among the nations! And look! Be also astonished! Be astounded! Because I am doing something in your days— You would not believe if it was recounted to you.
Habakkuk1:6 For behold
Habakkuk1:7 They are dreaded and feared; Their justice and exaltation come forth from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards And sharper than wolves in the evening. Their horsemen come galloping; Their horsemen come from afar; They fly like an eagle swooping down to devour.
Habakkuk1:9 All of them come for violence. Their horde of faces moves forward. And they gather captives like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 And they mock at kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then they will sweep through like the wind and pass on. But they will be held guilty
Habakkuk1:12 Are You not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to see evil
Habakkuk1:14 And You have made men like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The Chaldeans bring all of them up with a hook
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore they offer a sacrifice to their net And burn incense to their fishing net Because through these things their portion is rich And their food is fat.
Habakkuk1:17 Will they therefore empty their net And continually kill nations without sparing?
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand on my guard post And station myself on the fortification; And I will keep watch to see what He will speak to me And how I may respond when I am reproved.
Habakkuk2:2 Then Yahweh answered me and said
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision is yet for the appointed time; It pants toward its end
Habakkuk2:4 “Behold
Habakkuk2:5 And indeed
Habakkuk2:6 “Will not all of these lift up a taunt‑song against him
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your creditors rise up suddenly
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have taken many nations as spoil
Habakkuk2:9 “Woe to him who is greedy for evil gain for his house To put his nest on high
Habakkuk2:10 You have counseled a shameful thing for your house By cutting off many peoples; So you are sinning against your own soul.
Habakkuk2:11 Surely the stone will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 “Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed And founds a town with injustice!
Habakkuk2:13 Is it not
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled With the knowledge of the glory of Yahweh
Habakkuk2:15 “Woe to you who make your neighbors drink
Habakkuk2:16 You will be filled with disgrace rather than glory. Now you yourself drink and expose your own nakedness. The cup in Yahweh’s right hand will come around to you
Habakkuk2:17 For the violence done to Lebanon will cover you
Habakkuk2:18 “What profit is the graven image when its maker has engraved it
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to a piece of wood
Habakkuk2:20 But Yahweh is in His holy temple. Let all the earth be silent before Him.”
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet
Habakkuk3:2 O Yahweh
Habakkuk3:3 God comes from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His brightness is like the sunlight; He has rays flashing from His hand
Habakkuk3:5 Before Him goes pestilence
Habakkuk3:6 He stood and measured out the earth; He looked and startled the nations. So the perpetual mountains were shattered; The ancient hills collapsed. His ways are everlasting.
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan under wickedness; The tent curtains of the land of Midian were trembling.
Habakkuk3:8 Did Yahweh’s fury burn against the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 Your bow was made bare; Rods were sworn unto battle by word. Selah. You split the earth with rivers.
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw You and writhed; The downpour of waters passed by. The deep gave forth its voice; It lifted high its hands.
Habakkuk3:11 Sun and moon stood in their lofty places; They went away at the light of Your arrows
Habakkuk3:12 In indignation You marched through the earth; In anger You trampled the nations.
Habakkuk3:13 You went forth for the salvation of Your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced with his own sharpened rods The head of his throngs. They stormed in to scatter us; Their exultation was like those Who devour the afflicted in secret.
Habakkuk3:15 You tread on the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree should not blossom And there be no produce on the vines
Habakkuk3:18 Yet I will exult in Yahweh; I will rejoice in the God of my salvation.
Habakkuk3:19 Yahweh
Zephaniah1:1 The word of Yahweh which came to Zephaniah son of Cushi
Zephaniah1:2 “I will completely end all things From the face of the ground
Zephaniah1:3 “I will end man and beast; I will end the birds of the sky And the fish of the sea And the ruins along with the wicked; And I will cut off man from the face of the ground
Zephaniah1:4 “So I will stretch out My hand against Judah And against all the inhabitants of Jerusalem. And I will cut off the remnant of Baal from this place
Zephaniah1:5 And those who worship on the housetops the host of heaven
Zephaniah1:6 And those who have turned back from following Yahweh
Zephaniah1:7 Be silent before Lord Yahweh! For the day of Yahweh is near
Zephaniah1:8 “Then it will be on the day of Yahweh’s sacrifice That I will punish the princes
Zephaniah1:9 And I will punish on that day all who leap on the temple threshold
Zephaniah1:10 And it will be in that day
Zephaniah1:11 Wail
Zephaniah1:12 And it will be at that time That I will search Jerusalem with lamps
Zephaniah1:13 And it will be that their wealth will become spoil And their houses desolate; Indeed
Zephaniah1:14 Near is the great day of Yahweh
Zephaniah1:15 A day of fury is that day
Zephaniah1:16 A day of trumpet and loud shouting Against the fortified cities And the high corner towers.
Zephaniah1:17 I will bring distress on men So that they will walk like the blind Because they have sinned against Yahweh; And their blood will be poured out like dust And their flesh like dung.
Zephaniah1:18 Neither their silver nor their gold Will be able to deliver them On the day of the fury of Yahweh; And all the earth will be devoured In the fire of His jealousy
Zephaniah2:1 Gather yourselves together
Zephaniah2:2 Before the decree takes effect— The day passes like the chaff— Before the burning anger of Yahweh comes upon you
Zephaniah2:3 Seek Yahweh
Zephaniah2:4 For Gaza will be forsaken And Ashkelon a desolation; Ashdod will be driven out at noon

Zephaniah2:6 So the seacoast will be pastures
Zephaniah2:7 And the coast will be For the remnant of the house of Judah; They will feed upon it. In the houses of Ashkelon they will lie down at evening; For Yahweh their God will care for them And restore their fortune.

Zephaniah2:9 Therefore
Zephaniah2:10 This they will have in return for their pride because they have reproached and magnified themselves against the people of Yahweh of hosts.
Zephaniah2:11 Yahweh will be fearsome to them
Zephaniah2:12 “You also

Zephaniah2:15 This is the exultant city Which inhabits securely
Zephaniah3:1 Woe to her who is rebellious and defiled
Zephaniah3:2 She did not listen to any voice; She did not receive discipline. She did not trust in Yahweh; She did not draw near to her God.
Zephaniah3:3 Her princes in her midst are roaring lions; Her judges are wolves at evening; They leave nothing to gnaw for the morning.
Zephaniah3:4 Her prophets are reckless
Zephaniah3:5 Yahweh is righteous in her midst; He will do no injustice. Every morning He brings His justice to light; He does not fail. But the unjust knows no shame.
Zephaniah3:6 “I have cut off nations; Their corner towers are desolate. I have made their streets a waste
Zephaniah3:7 I said
Zephaniah3:8 “Therefore wait for Me
Zephaniah3:9 For then I will change them to peoples with purified lips
Zephaniah3:10 From beyond the rivers of Ethiopia My worshipers
Zephaniah3:11 In that day you will feel no shame Because of all your deeds By which you have transgressed against Me; For then I will remove from your midst Your proud
Zephaniah3:12 But I will cause to remain in your midst A lowly and poor people
Zephaniah3:13 The remnant of Israel will do no injustice And not speak falsehood
Zephaniah3:14 Sing for joy
Zephaniah3:15 Yahweh has taken away His judgments against you; He has cleared away your enemies. The King of Israel
Zephaniah3:16 In that day it will be said to Jerusalem: “Do not fear
Zephaniah3:17 Yahweh your God is in your midst
Zephaniah3:18 I will assemble those who grieve about the appointed feasts— They were from you
Zephaniah3:19 Behold
Zephaniah3:20 At that time I will bring you in

Malachi3:6 “For I

Malachi3:9 You are cursed with a curse

Malachi3:13 “Your words have been strong against Me
Malachi3:14 You have said
Malachi3:15 So now we call the arrogant blessed; not only are the doers of wickedness built up

Matthew1:1 The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 And Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar
Matthew1:4 And Ram was the father of Amminadab
Matthew1:5 And Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab
Matthew1:6 And Jesse was the father of David the king. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah.
Matthew1:7 And Solomon was the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 And Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 And Uzziah was the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 And Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 And Josiah was the father of Jeconiah and his brothers
Matthew1:12 And after the deportation to Babylon: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel
Matthew1:13 And Zerubbabel was the father of Abihud
Matthew1:14 And Azor was the father of Zadok
Matthew1:15 And Eliud was the father of Eleazar
Matthew1:16 And Jacob was the father of Joseph the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 Therefore all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David to the deportation to Babylon
Matthew1:18 Now the birth of Jesus Christ was as follows: when His mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph
Matthew1:19 And Joseph her husband
Matthew1:20 But when he had considered this
Matthew1:21 And she will bear a Son; and you shall call His name Jesus
Matthew1:22 Now all this took place in order that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet would be fulfilled
Matthew1:23 “Behold
Matthew1:24 And Joseph got up from his sleep and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him
Matthew1:25 but kept her a virgin until she gave birth to a Son; and he called His name Jesus.
Matthew2:1 Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king
Matthew2:2 “Where is He who has been born King of the Jews? For we saw His star in the east and have come to worship Him.”
Matthew2:3 And when Herod the king heard this
Matthew2:4 And gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people
Matthew2:5 And they said to him
Matthew2:6 ‘And you
Matthew2:7 Then Herod secretly called the magi and carefully determined from them the time the star appeared.
Matthew2:8 And he sent them to Bethlehem and said
Matthew2:9 Now after hearing the king
Matthew2:10 And when they saw the star
Matthew2:11 And after coming into the house they saw the Child with Mary His mother; and they fell to the ground and worshiped Him. Then
Matthew2:12 And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod
Matthew2:13 Now when they had departed
Matthew2:14 So Joseph got up and took the Child and His mother while it was still night
Matthew2:15 And he remained there until the death of Herod
Matthew2:16 Then when Herod saw that he had been tricked by the magi
Matthew2:17 Then what had been spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled
Matthew2:18 “A voice was heard in Ramah
Matthew2:19 But when Herod died
Matthew2:20 “Get up
Matthew2:21 So Joseph got up
Matthew2:22 But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod
Matthew2:23 and came and lived in a city called Nazareth
Matthew3:1 Now in those days John the Baptist came
Matthew3:2 “Repent
Matthew3:3 For this is the one referred to by Isaiah the prophet
Matthew3:4 Now John himself had a garment of camel’s hair and a leather belt around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
Matthew3:5 Then Jerusalem was going out to him
Matthew3:6 and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River
Matthew3:7 But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for his baptism
Matthew3:8 Therefore bear fruit in keeping with repentance;
Matthew3:9 and do not suppose that you can say to yourselves

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “Behold
Mark1:3 The voice of one crying in the wilderness
Mark1:4 John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
Mark1:5 And all the region of Judea was going out to him
Mark1:6 And John was clothed with camel’s hair and wore a leather belt around his waist and was eating locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 And he was preaching
Mark1:8 I baptized you with water; but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
Mark1:9 Now it happened that in those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Mark1:10 And immediately coming up out of the water
Mark1:11 and a voice came out of the heavens: “You are My beloved Son
Mark1:12 And immediately the Spirit drove Him to go out into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 And He was in the wilderness forty days being tempted by Satan; and He was with the wild beasts
Mark1:14 Now after John had been delivered up into custody
Mark1:15 and saying
Mark1:16 As He was going along by the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 And Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 And immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
Mark1:19 And going on a little farther
Mark1:20 And immediately He called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants
Mark1:21 And they went into Capernaum; and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and began to teach.
Mark1:22 And they were astonished at His teaching; for He was teaching them as one having authority
Mark1:23 And immediately there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit; and he cried out
Mark1:24 saying

1Corinthians12:25 so that there may be no division in the body
1Corinthians12:26 And if one member suffers
1Corinthians12:27 Now you are Christ’s body

1Corinthians12:30 Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with tongues? Do all translate?
1Corinthians12:31 But you earnestly desire the greater gifts. And I will yet show you a more excellent way.

1Corinthians13:5 it does not act unbecomingly

1Corinthians14:2 For one who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men but to God
1Corinthians14:3 But one who prophesies speaks to men for edification and exhortation and encouragement.

1Corinthians14:10 There are
1Corinthians14:11 If then I do not know the meaning of the sound
1Corinthians14:12 So also you
1Corinthians14:13 Therefore let one who speaks in a tongue pray that he may translate.
1Corinthians14:14 For if I pray in a tongue
1Corinthians14:15 What is the outcome then? I will pray with the spirit and I will pray with the mind also; I will sing with the spirit and I will sing with the mind also.
1Corinthians14:16 Otherwise if you bless in the spirit only
1Corinthians14:17 For you are giving thanks well enough
1Corinthians14:18 I thank God that I speak in tongues more than you all;
1Corinthians14:19 however

1Corinthians14:25 the secrets of his heart are disclosed; and so he will fall on his face and worship God

1Corinthians14:28 but if there is no translator

1Corinthians14:30 But if a revelation is made to another who is seated

1Corinthians14:32 And the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets;
1Corinthians14:33 for God is not a God of confusion but of peace

1Corinthians15:12 Now if Christ is preached
1Corinthians15:13 But if there is no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 And if Christ has not been raised

1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead are not raised
1Corinthians15:17 And if Christ has not been raised
1Corinthians15:18 Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
1Corinthians15:19 If we have hoped in Christ in this life only
1Corinthians15:20 But now Christ has been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since by a man came death
1Corinthians15:22 For as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits
1Corinthians15:24 Then comes the end

1Corinthians15:27 For He has put all things in subjection under His feet. But when He says
1Corinthians15:28 And when all things are subjected to Him
1Corinthians15:29 Otherwise
1Corinthians15:30 Why are we also in danger every hour?
1Corinthians15:31 I affirm
1Corinthians15:32 If from human motives I fought with wild beasts at Ephesus
1Corinthians15:33 Do not be deceived: “Bad company corrupts good morals.”
1Corinthians15:34 Become righteously sober-minded
1Corinthians15:35 But someone will say
1Corinthians15:36 You fool! That which you sow does not come to life unless it dies;
1Corinthians15:37 and that which you sow
1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it a body just as He wished
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh
1Corinthians15:40 There are also heavenly bodies and earthly bodies
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown a corruptible body
1Corinthians15:43 it is sown in dishonor
1Corinthians15:44 it is sown a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 So also it is written
1Corinthians15:46 However
1Corinthians15:47 The first man is from the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As is the earthy
1Corinthians15:49 And just as we have borne the image of the earthy
1Corinthians15:50 Now I say this
1Corinthians15:51 Behold
1Corinthians15:52 in a moment
1Corinthians15:53 For this corruptible must put on the incorruptible
1Corinthians15:54 But when this corruptible puts on the incorruptible
1Corinthians15:55 O death
1Corinthians15:56 Now the sting of death is sin
1Corinthians15:57 but thanks be to God
1Corinthians15:58 Therefore

Galatians2:1 Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas
Galatians2:2 And I went up because of a revelation
Galatians2:3 But not even Titus
Galatians2:4 But this was because of the false brothers secretly brought in
Galatians2:5 But we did not yield in subjection to them for even a moment
Galatians2:6 But from those who were of high reputation (what they were makes no difference to me; God shows no partiality)—well
Galatians2:7 But on the contrary
Galatians2:8 (for He who worked in Peter unto his apostleship to the circumcised worked in me also unto the Gentiles)
Galatians2:9 and recognizing the grace that had been given to me
Galatians2:10 Only they asked us to remember the poor—the very thing I also was eager to do.
Galatians2:11 But when Cephas came to Antioch
Galatians2:12 For prior to the coming of certain men from James
Galatians2:13 And the rest of the Jews joined him in hypocrisy
Galatians2:14 But when I saw that they were not straightforward about the truth of the gospel
Galatians2:15 “We are Jews by nature and not sinners from among the Gentiles;
Galatians2:16 nevertheless knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the Law but through faith in Jesus Christ
Galatians2:17 But if
Galatians2:18 For if I rebuild what I have once destroyed
Galatians2:19 For through the Law I died to the Law
Galatians2:20 I have been crucified with Christ
Galatians2:21 I do not set aside the grace of God
Galatians3:1 O foolish Galatians
Galatians3:2 This is the only thing I want to learn from you: did you receive the Spirit by the works of the Law
Galatians3:3 Are you so foolish? Having begun by the Spirit
Galatians3:4 Did you suffer so many things for nothing—if indeed it was for nothing?
Galatians3:5 So then
Galatians3:6 Just as Abraham believed God and it was counted to him as righteousness
Galatians3:7 so know that those who are of faith
Galatians3:8 And the Scripture
Galatians3:9 So then those who are of faith are blessed with Abraham
Galatians3:10 For as many as are of the works of the Law are under a curse
Galatians3:11 Now that no one is justified by the Law before God is evident
Galatians3:12 However
Galatians3:13 Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law
Galatians3:14 in order that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might come to the Gentiles
Galatians3:15 Brothers
Galatians3:16 Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. He does not say
Galatians3:17 And what I am saying is this: the Law
Galatians3:18 For if the inheritance is by law
Galatians3:19 Why the Law then? It was added because of trespasses
Galatians3:20 Now a mediator is not for one person only
Galatians3:21 Is the Law then contrary to the promises of God? May it never be! For if a law had been given which was able to impart life
Galatians3:22 But the Scripture has shut up everyone under sin
Galatians3:23 But before faith came
Galatians3:24 Therefore the Law has become our tutor unto Christ
Galatians3:25 But now that faith has come
Galatians3:26 For you are all sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.
Galatians3:27 For all of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
Galatians3:28 There is neither Jew nor Greek
Galatians3:29 And if you belong to Christ
Galatians4:1 Now I say
Galatians4:2 but he is under guardians and stewards until the date set by the father.
Galatians4:3 So also we
Galatians4:4 But when the fullness of the time came
Galatians4:5 so that He might redeem those who were under the Law
Galatians4:6 And because you are sons
Galatians4:7 Therefore you are no longer a slave
Galatians4:8 However at that time
Galatians4:9 But now
Galatians4:10 You observe days and months and seasons and years.
Galatians4:11 I fear for you
Galatians4:12 I beg of you
Galatians4:13 But you know that it was because of a bodily illness that I proclaimed the gospel to you the first time;
Galatians4:14 and that which was a trial to you in my bodily condition you did not despise or loathe
Galatians4:15 Where then is that sense of blessing you had? For I testify to you that
Galatians4:16 So have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
Galatians4:17 They zealously seek you
Galatians4:18 But it is good always to be zealously sought in a commendable manner
Galatians4:19 My children
Galatians4:20 but I could wish to be present with you now and to change my tone
Galatians4:21 Tell me
Galatians4:22 For it is written that Abraham had two sons
Galatians4:23 But the son by the servant-woman had been born according to the flesh
Galatians4:24 This is spoken with allegory
Galatians4:25 Now this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem
Galatians4:26 But the Jerusalem above is free; she is our mother.
Galatians4:27 For it is written
Galatians4:28 And you brothers
Galatians4:29 But as at that time he who was born according to the flesh was persecuting him who was born according to the Spirit
Galatians4:30 But what does the Scripture say? “Cast out the servant‑woman and her son
Galatians4:31 So then
Galatians5:1 It was for freedom that Christ set us free. Therefore
Galatians5:2 Behold I
Galatians5:3 And I testify again to every man who receives circumcision
Galatians5:4 You have been severed from Christ
Galatians5:5 For we through the Spirit
Galatians5:6 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything
Galatians5:7 You were running well; who hindered you from obeying the truth?
Galatians5:8 This persuasion is not from Him who calls you.
Galatians5:9 A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
Galatians5:10 I have confidence in you in the Lord that you will adopt no other view. But the one who is disturbing you will bear his judgment
Galatians5:11 But I
Galatians5:12 I wish that those who are upsetting you would even mutilate themselves.
Galatians5:13 For you were called to freedom
Galatians5:14 For the whole Law is fulfilled in one word
Galatians5:15 But if you bite and devour one another
Galatians5:16 But I say
Galatians5:17 For the flesh sets its desire against the Spirit
Galatians5:18 But if you are led by the Spirit
Galatians5:19 Now the deeds of the flesh are evident
Galatians5:20 idolatry
Galatians5:21 envying
Galatians5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love
Galatians5:23 gentleness
Galatians5:24 Now those who belong to Christ Jesus crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
Galatians5:25 If we live by the Spirit
Galatians5:26 Let us not become those with vain glory
Galatians6:1 Brothers
Galatians6:2 Bear one another’s burdens
Galatians6:3 For if anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing
Galatians6:4 But each one must examine his own work
Galatians6:5 For each one will bear his own load.
Galatians6:6 And the one who is instructed in the word is to share in all good things with the one who instructs him.
Galatians6:7 Do not be deceived
Galatians6:8 For the one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption
Galatians6:9 And let us not lose heart in doing good
Galatians6:10 So then
Galatians6:11 See with what large letters I am writing to you with my own hand!
Galatians6:12 As many as are wanting to make a good showing in the flesh
Galatians6:13 For those who are circumcised do not even keep the Law themselves
Galatians6:14 But may it never be that I would boast
Galatians6:15 For neither is circumcision anything
Galatians6:16 And those who will walk in step with this rule
Galatians6:17 From now on let no one cause trouble for me
Galatians6:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit
Ephesians1:1 Paul
Ephesians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Ephesians1:3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:4 just as He chose us in Him before the foundation of the world
Ephesians1:5 by predestining us to adoption as sons through Jesus Christ to Himself
Ephesians1:6 to the praise of the glory of His grace
Ephesians1:7 In Him we have redemption through His blood
Ephesians1:8 which He caused to abound to us in all wisdom and insight
Ephesians1:9 making known to us the mystery of His will
Ephesians1:10 for an administration of the fullness of the times
Ephesians1:11 In Him
Ephesians1:12 to the end that we who first have hoped in Christ would be to the praise of His glory.
Ephesians1:13 In Him
Ephesians1:14 who is given as a pledge of our inheritance
Ephesians1:15 For this reason I too
Ephesians1:16 do not cease giving thanks for you
Ephesians1:17 that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:18 so that you—the eyes of your heart having been enlightened—will know what is the hope of His calling
Ephesians1:19 and what is the surpassing greatness of His power toward us who believe according to the working of the might of His strength
Ephesians1:20 which He worked in Christ
Ephesians1:21 far above all rule and authority and power and dominion
Ephesians1:22 And He put all things in subjection under His feet
Ephesians1:23 which is His body
Ephesians2:1 And you were dead in your transgressions and sins
Ephesians2:2 in which you formerly walked according to the course of this world
Ephesians2:3 among whom we all also formerly conducted ourselves in the lusts of our flesh
Ephesians2:4 But God
Ephesians2:5 even when we were dead in our transgressions
Ephesians2:6 and raised us up with Him
Ephesians2:7 so that in the ages to come He might show the surpassing riches of His grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
Ephesians2:8 For by grace you have been saved through faith
Ephesians2:9 not of works
Ephesians2:10 For we are His workmanship
Ephesians2:11 Therefore
Ephesians2:12 remember that you were at that time without Christ
Ephesians2:13 But now in Christ Jesus you who formerly were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
Ephesians2:14 For He Himself is our peace
Ephesians2:15 by abolishing in His flesh the enmity
Ephesians2:16 and might reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross
Ephesians2:17 And He came and preached the good news of peace to you who were far away
Ephesians2:18 for through Him we both have our access in one Spirit to the Father.
Ephesians2:19 So then you are no longer strangers and sojourners
Ephesians2:20 having been built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets
Ephesians2:21 in whom the whole building
Ephesians2:22 in whom you also are being built together into a dwelling of God in the Spirit.
Ephesians3:1 For this reason I
Ephesians3:2 if indeed you heard of the stewardship of God’s grace which was given to me for you;
Ephesians3:3 that by revelation there was made known to me the mystery
Ephesians3:4 About which
Ephesians3:5 which in other generations was not made known to the sons of men
Ephesians3:6 that the Gentiles are fellow heirs and fellow members of the body
Ephesians3:7 of which I was made a minister
Ephesians3:8 To me
Ephesians3:9 and to bring to light for all what is the administration of the mystery which for ages has been hidden in God who created all things;
Ephesians3:10 so that the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known through the church to the rulers and the authorities in the heavenly places.
Ephesians3:11 This was in accordance with the eternal purpose which He carried out in Christ Jesus our Lord
Ephesians3:12 in whom we have boldness and confident access through faith in Him.
Ephesians3:13 Therefore I ask you not to lose heart at my afflictions on your behalf
Ephesians3:14 For this reason I bow my knees before the Father
Ephesians3:15 from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named
Ephesians3:16 that He would give you
Ephesians3:17 so that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith; and that you
Ephesians3:18 may be able to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth
Ephesians3:19 and to know the love of Christ which surpasses knowledge
Ephesians3:20 Now to Him who is able to do far more abundantly beyond all that we ask or understand
Ephesians3:21 to Him be the glory in the church and in Christ Jesus to all generations forever and ever. Amen.
Ephesians4:1 Therefore I
Ephesians4:2 with all humility and gentleness
Ephesians4:3 being diligent to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
Ephesians4:4 There is one body and one Spirit
Ephesians4:5 one Lord
Ephesians4:6 one God and Father of all who is over all and through all and in all.
Ephesians4:7 But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of Christ’s gift.

Ephesians4:10 He who descended is Himself also He who ascended far above all the heavens
Ephesians4:11 And He Himself gave some as apostles
Ephesians4:12 for the equipping of the saints for the work of service
Ephesians4:13 until we all attain to the unity of the faith

Ephesians4:15 but speaking the truth in love
Ephesians4:16 from whom the whole body
Ephesians4:17 Therefore this I say

Ephesians4:19 And they

Ephesians4:21 if indeed you heard Him and were taught in Him
Ephesians4:22 to lay aside

Ephesians5:1 Therefore be imitators of God
Ephesians5:2 and walk in love
Ephesians5:3 But sexual immorality or any impurity or greed must not even be named among you
Ephesians5:4 nor filthiness and foolish talk
Ephesians5:5 For this you know with certainty
Ephesians5:6 Let no one deceive you with empty words
Ephesians5:7 Therefore do not be partakers with them
Ephesians5:8 for you were formerly darkness
Ephesians5:9 (for the fruit of that light consists in all goodness and righteousness and truth)
Ephesians5:10 trying to learn what is pleasing to the Lord.
Ephesians5:11 And do not participate in the unfruitful works of darkness
Ephesians5:12 For it is disgraceful even to speak of the things which are done by them in secret.
Ephesians5:13 But all things become visible when they are exposed by the light
Ephesians5:14 For this reason it says
Ephesians5:15 Therefore look carefully how you walk
Ephesians5:16 redeeming the time
Ephesians5:17 On account of this
Ephesians5:18 And do not get drunk with wine
Ephesians5:19 speaking to one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs
Ephesians5:20 always giving thanks for all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to God
Ephesians5:21 and being subject to one another in the fear of Christ.
Ephesians5:22 Wives
Ephesians5:23 For the husband is the head of the wife
Ephesians5:24 But as the church is subject to Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 so that He might sanctify her
Ephesians5:27 that He might present to Himself the church in all her glory
Ephesians5:28 So husbands ought also to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself;
Ephesians5:29 for no one ever hated his own flesh
Ephesians5:30 because we are members of His body.
Ephesians5:31 For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife
Ephesians5:32 This mystery is great
Ephesians5:33 Nevertheless

Ephesians6:3 so that it may be well with you

Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God in all my remembrance of you
Philippians1:4 always offering prayer with joy in my every prayer for you all
Philippians1:5 because of your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now.
Philippians1:6 For I am confident of this very thing
Philippians1:7 For it is only right for me to think this way about you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness
Philippians1:9 And this I pray
Philippians1:10 so that you may approve the things that are excellent
Philippians1:11 having been filled with the fruit of righteousness which comes through Jesus Christ
Philippians1:12 Now I want you to know
Philippians1:13 so that my chains in Christ have become well known throughout the whole praetorian guard and to everyone else
Philippians1:14 and that most of the brothers
Philippians1:15 Some
Philippians1:16 the latter do it out of love
Philippians1:17 the former proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition rather than from pure motives
Philippians1:18 What then? Only that in every way
Philippians1:19 for I know that this will turn out for my salvation through your prayers and the provision of the Spirit of Jesus Christ
Philippians1:20 according to my earnest expectation and hope
Philippians1:21 For to me
Philippians1:22 But if I am to live on in the flesh
Philippians1:23 But I am hard-pressed between the two
Philippians1:24 yet to remain on in the flesh is more necessary for your sake.
Philippians1:25 And convinced of this
Philippians1:26 so that your reason for boasting may abound in Christ Jesus in me
Philippians1:27 Only live your lives in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ
Philippians1:28 in no way alarmed by your opponents—which is a sign of destruction for them
Philippians1:29 For to you it has been granted for Christ’s sake
Philippians1:30 having the same struggle which you saw in me
Philippians2:1 Therefore if there is any encouragement in Christ
Philippians2:2 fulfill my joy
Philippians2:3 doing nothing from selfish ambition or vain glory
Philippians2:4 not merely looking out for your own personal interests
Philippians2:5 Have this way of thinking in yourselves which was also in Christ Jesus
Philippians2:6 who
Philippians2:7 but emptied Himself
Philippians2:8 Being found in appearance as a man
Philippians2:9 Therefore
Philippians2:10 so that at the name of Jesus every knee will bow
Philippians2:11 and that every tongue will confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 So then
Philippians2:13 for it is God who is at work in you
Philippians2:14 Do all things without grumbling or disputing
Philippians2:15 so that you will be blameless and innocent
Philippians2:16 holding fast the word of life
Philippians2:17 But even if I am being poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrifice and service of your faith
Philippians2:18 And you also
Philippians2:19 But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you shortly
Philippians2:20 For I have no one else of kindred spirit who will genuinely be concerned about your circumstances.
Philippians2:21 For they all seek after their own interests
Philippians2:22 But you know of his proven worth
Philippians2:23 Therefore I hope to send him immediately
Philippians2:24 and I am confident in the Lord that I myself also will be coming shortly.
Philippians2:25 But I regarded it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus
Philippians2:26 because he was longing for you all and was distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
Philippians2:27 For indeed he was sick to the point of death
Philippians2:28 Therefore I have sent him all the more eagerly so that when you see him again you may rejoice and I may be less concerned.
Philippians2:29 Receive him then in the Lord with all joy
Philippians2:30 because he came close to death for the work of Christ

Philippians3:5 circumcised the eighth day
Philippians3:6 as to zeal
Philippians3:7 But whatever things were gain to me
Philippians3:8 More than that

Philippians3:11 in order that I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already obtained it or have already become perfect

Philippians3:19 whose end is destruction

Philippians4:11 Not that I speak from want

Colossians1:2 To the saints and faithful brothers in Christ in Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.

Colossians1:4 since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and the love which you have for all the saints
Colossians1:5 because of the hope laid up for you in heaven
Colossians1:6 which has come to you
Colossians1:7 just as you learned it from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 who also informed us of your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this reason also
Colossians1:10 so that you may walk in a manner worthy of the Lord
Colossians1:11 being strengthened with all power

Colossians1:13 Who rescued us from the authority of darkness
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption
Colossians1:15 Who is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 For in Him all things were created
Colossians1:17 And He is before all things

Colossians1:22 but now He reconciled you in the body of His flesh through death
Colossians1:23 if indeed you continue in the faith firmly grounded and steadfast

Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Devote yourselves to prayer
Colossians4:3 praying at the same time for us as well
Colossians4:4 that I may make it manifest in the way I ought to speak.
Colossians4:5 Walk in wisdom toward outsiders
Colossians4:6 Let your words always be with grace
Colossians4:7 Tychicus
Colossians4:8 whom I have sent to you for this very purpose
Colossians4:9 and with him Onesimus
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus
Colossians4:11 and also Jesus who is called Justus. These are the only fellow workers for the kingdom of God who are from the circumcision
Colossians4:12 Epaphras
Colossians4:13 For I testify for him that he has a deep concern for you and for those who are in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
Colossians4:14 Luke
Colossians4:15 Greet the brothers who are in Laodicea and also Nympha and the church that is in her house.
Colossians4:16 And when this letter is read among you
Colossians4:17 And say to Archippus
Colossians4:18 The greeting is in my own hand—Paul. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.
1Thessalonians1:1 Paul and Silvanus and Timothy
1Thessalonians1:2 We give thanks to God always for all of you
1Thessalonians1:3 remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ before our God and Father
1Thessalonians1:4 knowing
1Thessalonians1:5 for our gospel did not come to you in word only
1Thessalonians1:6 You also became imitators of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 so that you became a model to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For the word of the Lord has sounded forth from you
1Thessalonians1:9 For they themselves report about us what kind of an entrance we had with you
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for His Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For you yourselves know
1Thessalonians2:2 but after we had already suffered and been mistreated in Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For our exhortation does not come from error or impurity or by way of deceit;
1Thessalonians2:4 but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For we never came with a flattering word
1Thessalonians2:6 nor seeking glory from men
1Thessalonians2:7 But we proved to be gentle among you
1Thessalonians2:8 In this way
1Thessalonians2:9 For you remember
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 just as you know how we were exhorting and encouraging and bearing witness to each one of you as a father would his own children
1Thessalonians2:12 so that you would walk in a manner worthy of the God who calls you into His own kingdom and glory.
1Thessalonians2:13 And for this reason we also thank God without ceasing that when you received the word of God which you heard from us
1Thessalonians2:14 For you
1Thessalonians2:15 who both killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved; with the result that they always fill up the measure of their sins. But wrath has come upon them to the utmost.
1Thessalonians2:17 But we
1Thessalonians2:18 For we wanted to come to you—I
1Thessalonians2:19 For who is our hope or joy or crown of boasting? Is it not even you
1Thessalonians2:20 For you are our glory and joy.
1Thessalonians3:1 Therefore when we could endure it no longer
1Thessalonians3:2 and we sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 so that no one would be shaken by these afflictions
1Thessalonians3:4 For indeed when we were with you
1Thessalonians3:5 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:6 But now that Timothy has come to us from you
1Thessalonians3:7 for this reason
1Thessalonians3:8 for now we really live
1Thessalonians3:9 For what thanks can we render to God for you in return for all the joy with which we rejoice before our God because of you
1Thessalonians3:10 as we night and day keep praying most earnestly that we may see your face
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may our God and Father Himself and Jesus our Lord direct our way to you
1Thessalonians3:12 and may the Lord cause you to increase and abound in love for one another
1Thessalonians3:13 so that He may strengthen your hearts blameless in holiness
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally then
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know what commandments we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 that each of you know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not in lustful passion
1Thessalonians4:6 and that no man transgress and defraud his brother in the matter because the Lord is the avenger in all these things
1Thessalonians4:7 For God did not call us to impurity
1Thessalonians4:8 Consequently
1Thessalonians4:9 Now concerning love of the brothers
1Thessalonians4:10 for indeed you do practice it toward all the brothers who are in all Macedonia. But we urge you
1Thessalonians4:11 and to make it your ambition to lead a quiet life and attend to your own business and work with your hands
1Thessalonians4:12 so that you will walk properly toward outsiders and not be in any need.
1Thessalonians4:13 But we do not want you to be uninformed
1Thessalonians4:14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we say to you by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout
1Thessalonians4:17 Then we who are alive and remain will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore comfort one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 Now concerning the times and the seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For you yourselves know full well that the day of the Lord will come just like a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 While they are saying
1Thessalonians5:4 But you
1Thessalonians5:5 for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We are not of night nor of darkness;
1Thessalonians5:6 so then let us not sleep as others do
1Thessalonians5:7 For those who sleep
1Thessalonians5:8 But since we are of the day
1Thessalonians5:9 For God has not appointed us for wrath
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us
1Thessalonians5:11 Therefore
1Thessalonians5:12 But we ask of you
1Thessalonians5:13 and that you regard them very highly in love because of their work. Live in peace with one another.
1Thessalonians5:14 And we urge you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that no one repays another with evil for evil
1Thessalonians5:16 Rejoice always;
1Thessalonians5:17 pray without ceasing;
1Thessalonians5:18 in everything give thanks
1Thessalonians5:19 Do not quench the Spirit;
1Thessalonians5:20 do not despise prophecies
1Thessalonians5:21 but examine all things; hold fast to that which is good;
1Thessalonians5:22 abstain from every form of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you entirely
1Thessalonians5:24 Faithful is He who calls you
1Thessalonians5:25 Brothers
1Thessalonians5:26 Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
1Thessalonians5:27 I implore you by the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
2Thessalonians1:1 Paul and Silvanus and Timothy
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We ought always to give thanks to God for you
2Thessalonians1:4 so that we ourselves boast about you among the churches of God for your perseverance and faith in the midst of all your persecutions and afflictions which you endure.
2Thessalonians1:5 This is a plain indication of God’s righteous judgment so that you will be considered worthy of the kingdom of God
2Thessalonians1:6 Since it is right for God to repay with affliction those who afflict you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to give rest to you who are afflicted and to us as well at the revelation of the Lord Jesus from heaven with His mighty angels in flaming fire
2Thessalonians1:8 executing vengeance on those who do not know God and to those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
2Thessalonians1:9 These will pay the penalty of eternal destruction
2Thessalonians1:10 when He comes to be glorified in His saints on that day
2Thessalonians1:11 To this end also we pray for you always
2Thessalonians1:12 so that the name of our Lord Jesus will be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now we ask you
2Thessalonians2:2 that you not be quickly shaken in your mind or be alarmed whether by a spirit or a word or a letter as if from us
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no one in any way deceive you
2Thessalonians2:4 who opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship
2Thessalonians2:5 Do you not remember that while I was still with you
2Thessalonians2:6 And you know what restrains him now
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only he who now restrains will do so until he is taken out of the way.
2Thessalonians2:8 And then that lawless one will be revealed—whom the Lord Jesus will slay with the breath of His mouth and bring to an end by the appearance of His coming—
2Thessalonians2:9 whose coming is in accord with the working of Satan
2Thessalonians2:10 and with all the deception of unrighteousness for those who perish
2Thessalonians2:11 And for this reason God sends upon them a deluding influence so that they will believe what is false
2Thessalonians2:12 in order that they all may be judged who did not believe the truth
2Thessalonians2:13 But we should always give thanks to God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 It was for this He called you through our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself and God our Father
2Thessalonians2:17 encourage your hearts and strengthen them in every good work and word.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we will be rescued from perverse and evil men
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord about you
2Thessalonians3:5 And may the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the steadfastness of Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 Now we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us
2Thessalonians3:8 nor did we eat anyone’s bread without paying for it
2Thessalonians3:9 not because we do not have the authority
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that some among you are walking in an unruly manner
2Thessalonians3:12 Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ
2Thessalonians3:13 But as for you
2Thessalonians3:14 And if anyone does not obey our word in this letter
2Thessalonians3:15 And yet do not regard him as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace Himself continually give you peace in every circumstance. The Lord be with you all!
2Thessalonians3:17 The greeting is in my own hand—Paul
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I exhorted you when going to Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 nor to pay attention to myths and endless genealogies
1Timothy1:5 But the goal of our command is love from a pure heart and a good conscience and an unhypocritical faith.
1Timothy1:6 For some
1Timothy1:7 wanting to be teachers of the Law
1Timothy1:8 But we know that the Law is good
1Timothy1:9 knowing this
1Timothy1:10 for sexually immoral persons
1Timothy1:11 according to the gospel of the glory of the blessed God
1Timothy1:12 I am grateful to Christ Jesus our Lord
1Timothy1:13 even though I was formerly a blasphemer and a persecutor and a violent aggressor. Yet I was shown mercy because I acted ignorantly in unbelief;
1Timothy1:14 and the grace of our Lord was more than abundant
1Timothy1:15 It is a trustworthy saying and deserving full acceptance: that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners
1Timothy1:16 Yet for this reason I was shown mercy
1Timothy1:17 Now to the King of the ages
1Timothy1:18 This command I entrust to you
1Timothy1:19 keeping faith and a good conscience
1Timothy1:20 Among these are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all who are in authority
1Timothy2:3 This is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior
1Timothy2:4 who desires all men to be saved and to come to the full knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 who gave Himself as a ransom for all
1Timothy2:7 For this I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth
1Timothy2:8 Therefore I want the men in every place to pray
1Timothy2:9 Likewise
1Timothy2:10 but rather by means of good works
1Timothy2:11 A woman must learn in quietness
1Timothy2:12 But I do not allow a woman to teach or exercise authority over a man
1Timothy2:13 For it was Adam who was first formed
1Timothy2:14 And it was not Adam who was deceived
1Timothy2:15 But she will be saved through the bearing of children
1Timothy3:1 It is a trustworthy saying: if any man aspires to the office of overseer
1Timothy3:2 An overseer
1Timothy3:3 not addicted to wine or pugnacious
1Timothy3:4 leading his own household well
1Timothy3:5 (but if a man does not know how to lead his own household
1Timothy3:6 and not a new convert
1Timothy3:7 And he must have a good reputation with those outside the church
1Timothy3:8 Deacons likewise must be dignified
1Timothy3:9 but holding to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And these men must also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons if they are beyond reproach.
1Timothy3:11 Women must likewise be dignified
1Timothy3:12 Deacons must be husbands of only one wife
1Timothy3:13 For those who have served well as deacons obtain for themselves a high standing and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 I am writing these things to you
1Timothy3:15 but in case I am delayed
1Timothy3:16 And by common confession
1Timothy4:1 But the Spirit explicitly says that in later times some will fall away from the faith
1Timothy4:2 by the hypocrisy of liars
1Timothy4:3 who forbid marriage and advocate abstaining from foods which God created to be shared in with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
1Timothy4:4 For everything created by God is good
1Timothy4:5 for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
1Timothy4:6 In pointing out these things to the brothers
1Timothy4:7 But refuse godless myths fit only for old women. On the other hand
1Timothy4:8 for bodily training is only of little profit
1Timothy4:9 It is a trustworthy saying and deserving full acceptance.
1Timothy4:10 For it is for this we labor and strive
1Timothy4:11 Command and teach these things.
1Timothy4:12 Let no one look down on your youthfulness
1Timothy4:13 Until I come
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the gift within you
1Timothy4:15 Take pains with these things; be absorbed in them
1Timothy4:16 Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching; persevere in these things
1Timothy5:1 Do not sharply rebuke an older man
1Timothy5:2 the older women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows who are widows indeed
1Timothy5:4 but if any widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 Now she who is a widow indeed and who has been left alone
1Timothy5:6 But she who lives in self-indulgence is dead even while she lives.
1Timothy5:7 And command these things as well
1Timothy5:8 But if anyone does not provide for his own
1Timothy5:9 A widow is to be put on the list only if she is not less than sixty years old
1Timothy5:10 having a reputation for good works; if she has brought up children
1Timothy5:11 But refuse to put younger widows on the list
1Timothy5:12 thus incurring condemnation
1Timothy5:13 And at the same time they also learn to be idle
1Timothy5:14 Therefore
1Timothy5:15 for some have already turned aside after Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any believing woman has widows
1Timothy5:17 The elders who lead well are to be considered worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 For the Scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Do not receive an accusation against an elder except on the basis of two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 Those who continue in sin
1Timothy5:21 I solemnly charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of His elect angels
1Timothy5:22 Do not lay hands upon anyone hastily and thereby share responsibility for the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
1Timothy5:23 No longer drink water only
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some men are quite evident
1Timothy5:25 So also good works are quite evident
1Timothy6:1 All who are under the yoke as slaves are to regard their own masters as worthy of all honor so that the name of God and our doctrine will not be slandered.
1Timothy6:2 But those who have believers as their masters must not be disrespectful to them because they are brothers
1Timothy6:3 If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with sound words—those of our Lord Jesus Christ—and with the doctrine conforming to godliness
1Timothy6:4 he is conceited
1Timothy6:5 and constant friction between men of depraved mind and deprived of the truth
1Timothy6:6 But godliness actually is a means of great gain
1Timothy6:7 For we have brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 And if we have food and covering
1Timothy6:9 But those who want to get rich fall into temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful desires which plunge men into ruin and destruction.
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a root of all sorts of evils
1Timothy6:11 But you
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called
1Timothy6:13 I charge you in the presence of God
1Timothy6:14 that you keep the commandment without stain or reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ
1Timothy6:15 which He will bring about at the proper time—He who is the blessed and only Sovereign
1Timothy6:16 who alone has immortality and dwells in unapproachable light
1Timothy6:17 Command those who are rich in this present age not to be haughty or to set their hope on the uncertainty of riches
1Timothy6:18 Command them to do good
1Timothy6:19 storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 which some

2Timothy1:7 For God has not given us a spirit of timidity
2Timothy1:8 Therefore do not be ashamed of either the witness about our Lord or me His prisoner
2Timothy1:9 who has saved us and called us with a holy calling
2Timothy1:10 but now has been manifested by the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:11 for which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher.

2Timothy1:13 Hold to the standard of sound words which you have heard from me

2Timothy2:1 You therefore
2Timothy2:2 And the things which you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses
2Timothy2:3 Suffer hardship with me
2Timothy2:4 No soldier in active service entangles himself in the affairs of everyday life
2Timothy2:5 And also if anyone competes as an athlete
2Timothy2:6 The hard-working farmer ought to be the first to receive his share of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Understand what I say
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 for which I endure hardship even to chains as a criminal. But the word of God has not been chained.
2Timothy2:10 For this reason I endure all things for the sake of the elect

2Timothy2:12 If we endure
2Timothy2:13 If we are faithless

2Timothy2:18 who have gone astray from the truth saying that the resurrection has already taken place
2Timothy2:19 Nevertheless

2Timothy2:21 Therefore
2Timothy2:22 Now flee from youthful lusts and pursue righteousness
2Timothy2:23 But refuse foolish and ignorant speculations
2Timothy2:24 And the Lord’s slave must not be quarrelsome
2Timothy2:25 with gentleness correcting those who are in opposition
2Timothy2:26 and they may come to their senses and escape from the snare of the devil

Titus1:2 in the hope of eternal life
Titus1:3 but at the proper time manifested His word in preaching
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 For this reason I left you in Crete
Titus1:6 namely
Titus1:7 For the overseer must be beyond reproach as God’s steward
Titus1:8 but hospitable
Titus1:9 holding fast the faithful word which is in accordance with the teaching
Titus1:10 For there are many rebellious men
Titus1:11 who must be silenced because they are upsetting whole families
Titus1:12 One of themselves
Titus1:13 This testimony is true. For this reason reprove them severely so that they may be sound in the faith
Titus1:14 not paying attention to Jewish myths and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.
Titus1:15 To the pure
Titus1:16 They profess to know God
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 Older men are to be temperate
Titus2:3 Older women likewise are to be reverent in their behavior
Titus2:4 so that they may instruct the young women in sensibility: to love their husbands
Titus2:5 to be sensible
Titus2:6 Likewise urge the younger men to be sensible;
Titus2:7 in all things show yourself to be a model of good works
Titus2:8 sound in word which is irreproachable
Titus2:9 Urge slaves to be subject to their own masters in everything
Titus2:10 not pilfering
Titus2:11 For the grace of God has appeared
Titus2:12 instructing us that
Titus2:13 looking for the blessed hope and the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior
Titus2:14 who gave Himself for us that He might redeem us from all lawlessness
Titus2:15 These things speak and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one disregard you.
Titus3:1 Remind them to be subject to rulers
Titus3:2 to slander no one
Titus3:3 For we ourselves also once were foolish
Titus3:4 But when the kindness and affection of God our Savior appeared
Titus3:5 He saved us
Titus3:6 whom He poured out upon us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior
Titus3:7 so that having been justified by His grace
Titus3:8 This is a trustworthy saying. And concerning these things I want you to speak confidently
Titus3:9 But avoid foolish controversies and genealogies and strife and conflicts about the Law
Titus3:10 Reject a factious man after a first and second warning
Titus3:11 knowing that such a man is perverted and is sinning

Titus3:13 Diligently help send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way so that nothing is lacking for them.
Titus3:14 And our people must also learn to lead in good works to meet pressing needs
Titus3:15 All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.
Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 and to Apphia our sister
Philemon1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philemon1:4 I thank my God
Philemon1:5 because I hear of your love and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints;
Philemon1:6 and I pray that the fellowship of your faith may become effective through the full knowledge of every good thing which is in you for the sake of Christ.
Philemon1:7 For I have come to have much joy and comfort in your love
Philemon1:8 Therefore
Philemon1:9 yet for love’s sake I rather plead with you—since I am such a person as Paul
Philemon1:10 I plead with you for my child Onesimus
Philemon1:11 who formerly was useless to you
Philemon1:12 I have sent him back to you in person
Philemon1:13 whom I intended to keep with me
Philemon1:14 but without your consent I did not want to do anything
Philemon1:15 For perhaps he was for this reason separated from you for a while
Philemon1:16 no longer as a slave
Philemon1:17 If then you regard me a partner

Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the men of old gained approval.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the worlds were prepared by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a better sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he would not see death; and he was not found because God took him up; for prior to being taken up
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please Him
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:9 By faith he sojourned in the land of promise
Hebrews11:10 for he was looking for the city which has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith even Sarah herself received ability to conceive
Hebrews11:12 Therefore there were born even of one man
Hebrews11:13 All these died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.
Hebrews11:15 And indeed if they had been remembering that country from which they went out
Hebrews11:16 But now
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 to whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 He considered that God is able to raise people even from the dead
Hebrews11:20 By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to be mistreated with the people of God than to enjoy the passing pleasures of sin
Hebrews11:26 regarding the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he was looking to the reward.
Hebrews11:27 By faith he left Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of the blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed through the Red Sea as though they were passing through dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the harlot did not perish along with those who were disobedient
Hebrews11:32 And what more shall I say? For time will fail me if I recount Gideon
Hebrews11:33 who through faith conquered kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched the power of fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received back their dead by resurrection; and others were tortured
Hebrews11:36 and others experienced mockings and floggings
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 (of whom the world was not worthy)
Hebrews11:39 And all these
Hebrews11:40 because God had provided something better for us

Hebrews12:2 fixing our eyes on Jesus
Hebrews12:3 For consider Him who has endured such hostility by sinners against Himself

Hebrews12:6 For those whom the Lord loves He disciplines

Hebrews12:8 But if you are without discipline
Hebrews12:9 Furthermore
Hebrews12:10 For they disciplined us for a short time as seemed best to them
Hebrews12:11 And all discipline for the moment seems not to be joyful

Hebrews12:13 and make straight paths for your feet

Hebrews12:17 For you know that even afterwards
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come to a mountain that can be touched and to a blazing fire

Hebrews12:20 For they could not bear what was being commanded
Hebrews12:21 And so terrible was what appeared

Hebrews12:24 and to Jesus
Hebrews12:25 See to it that you do not refuse Him who is speaking. For if those did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth
Hebrews12:26 And His voice shook the earth then
Hebrews12:27 Now this expression
Hebrews12:28 Therefore
Hebrews12:29 for our God is a consuming fire.

James1:1 James
James1:2 Consider it all joy
James1:3 knowing that the testing of your faith brings about perseverance.
James1:4 And let perseverance have its perfect work
James1:5 But if any of you lacks wisdom
James1:6 But he must ask in faith
James1:7 For that man ought not to expect that he will receive anything from the Lord
James1:8 being a double-minded man
James1:9 But the brother of humble circumstances is to boast in his high position;
James1:10 and the rich man is to boast in his humiliation
James1:11 For the sun rises with a scorching heat and withers the grass; and its flower falls off and the beauty of its appearance is destroyed; so too the rich man in the midst of his pursuits will fade away.
James1:12 Blessed is a man who perseveres under trial; for once he has been approved
James1:13 Let no one say when he is tempted
James1:14 But each one is tempted when he is carried away and enticed by his own lust.
James1:15 Then when lust has conceived
James1:16 Do not be deceived
James1:17 Every good thing given and every perfect gift is from above
James1:18 In the exercise of His will He brought us forth by the word of truth
James1:19 Know this
James1:20 for the anger of man does not achieve the righteousness of God.
James1:21 Therefore
James1:22 But become doers of the word
James1:23 For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer
James1:24 for once he looked at himself and has gone away
James1:25 But one who looks intently at the perfect law
James1:26 If anyone thinks himself to be religious while not bridling his tongue but deceiving his own heart
James1:27 Pure and undefiled religion before our God and Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their affliction

James3:2 For we all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in what he says

James3:5 So also the tongue is a small part of the body
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire
James3:7 For every kind of beasts and birds
James3:8 But no one can tame the tongue; it is a restless evil and full of deadly poison.
James3:9 With it we bless our Lord and Father

James3:11 Does a fountain pour forth from the same opening fresh and bitter water?
James3:12 Can a fig tree

James3:15 This wisdom is not coming down from above
James3:16 For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist
James3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure

James5:2 Your riches have rotted and your garments have become moth-eaten.
James5:3 Your gold and your silver have rusted
James5:4 Behold
James5:5 You have lived luxuriously on the earth and lived in self-indulgence. You have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.

James5:9 Do not groan

James5:11 Behold
James5:12 But above all

James5:15 And the prayer offered in faith will save the one who is sick
James5:16 Therefore

James5:18 Then he prayed again
